Because nature created us all like islands... separate on the surface but deeply connected in ways we cannot see but we can intuitively feel. I learned to trust my instincts even when they sometimes appear wrong... tune into nature’s frequency. “Nature is the music and we are the dancer. Nature is constantly playing music. Her melodies are crafted by hidden colors and those colors are disguised as waves filled with the bounty of life enclosed. We are built as a servant to the music of the electromagnetic force and all the "notes" they can become. We are not a maestro for teaching Nature. We are meant to dance and sing to her songs not our own.”
>
> The universe is constantly speaking to us, sending us messages, signs, synchronicities, reminding us that is will always provide what we need when we need it if we are listening. That is specifically “where” you need to move too.

[@5G Canary](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/22671/), thank you from the bottom of my heart for everything you've shared with me. Time is our most precious asset, and you invested a lot of thought and time to this response, which I sincerely appreciate.

You said "the universe is constantly speaking to us, sending us messages, signs, synchronicities, reminding us that it will always provide what we need when we need it if we are listening." About six months ago, I was driving on the highway with my niece when I realized I was having tire troubles. Thankfully, we made it to a tire shop. While we were there, another female customer eventually came in. We struck up a brief conversation. Before she left, she timidly asked if she could give me something. Feeling nothing "untowards" about her, I said "yes." She pulled out a big red garden rock that she'd painted with the words "I AM SAFE." She said she didn't know why she'd made it, but she wanted to give it to me. After some of the things I've experienced in the past few years, I needed that message from the universe.

Usually when I don't know what to do, then I don't do anything. I don't know "where" I need to go, and unfortunately it's been awhile since I've gotten a clear message from the universe. Maybe if I take a few steps on my own, then I will.

I've decided my time here on the forum is coming to an end. My answers will be found in nature, not here.

Once again, I just want to thank you for the time you've taken to help me "get" what you had to learn along your own healing journey.

Being dark skinned at a high altitide is giving yourself oxygen toxicity.

Oxygen “Allergy”: Dehydrated tissues struggle to handle oxygen’s paramagnetic properties, leading to an “allergy” to O₂. This manifests as increased reactive oxygen species (ROS) production, as cytochrome c oxidase fails to efficiently reduce O₂ to water (step 9). The energy cascade diagram shows this as a failure to maintain the proton gradient (ΔμH⁺, step 8), reducing ATP synthesis and causing mitochondrial dysfunction.

Any additional oxygen you use will make WORSE. You can use DDW to help, but sunlight is cheaper and way better option. Living at a lower latituide where no nnEMF is best for you. I know funds are problem but if you want that brain to come back on line SUN is your best answer.

WHY TEXAS SUCKS FOR YOU SPECIFICALLY?

-   Sleep apnea results from the body downregulating oxygen needs under poor light exposure. Sunlight’s red and UV components drive water production for cytochrome c oxidase and melanin synthesis via POMC, respectively, reducing oxygen toxicity in well-hydrated tissues.
-   Without sunlight, the brain reduces breathing to avoid oxygen’s toxicity to dehydrated, dysfunctional tissues, where cytochrome c oxidase struggles as the terminal electron acceptor, aligning with the physics-based explanation.
-   Functional medicine, food gurus, and MAHA advocates pushing hyperbaric oxygen (HBO), supplemental oxygen, ozone, and ECMO overlook the risks seen in ARDS, space missions, and ventilated COVID-19 patients, where acute oxygen therapy spiked mitochondrial heteroplasmy—damaging mutations in mitochondrial DNA.
-   Low dopamine and melatonin levels, tied to poor light choices and dehydrated melanin, impair cognition and consciousness, as these neurotransmitters depend on sunlight-driven circadian rhythms and hydrated cellular environments.
-   The public’s inability to critically assess health advice or elect competent leaders mirrors this cognitive decline rooted in disrupted dopamine-melatonin biology and poor light exposure, which exacerbates societal decision-making issues.

SUNLIGHT FIXES IT ALL WHEN GROUNDED TO EARTH because it contains 43% red light to make water at cytochrome c oxidase, and it makes melanin from UV light. # Thread: Hello from London
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-london.17472/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nomad — 2016-02-03T20:41:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-london.17472/post-188096>

Hi guys, I've been lurking around the forum for a while and thought it was time to introduce myself.

After picking up a few injuries I've been unable to train as much as I used to, and started getting out of shape. I decided to embark on a ketogenic diet for a month or so to try and lose some fat. I was previously lead to believe that Ketogenic diets were unhealthy and not something that should be continued long term. I expected to feel lethargic as I always assumed that less carbs = less energy, but by the end of the first week of going ketogenic I had double the amount of energy, increased mental clarity, and generally felt a lot healthier.

I decided to do a bit of research on why this might be, and by doing so stumbled upon a Jack Kruse interview on Primal Health's YouTube channel. Although not directly linked to ketogenic diets, it put me on another path, and from that video I started seeing everything I thought I knew about health in a completely different light. Many things Jack spoke about really resonated with me, I had often thought our modern lifestyles go against what evolution had built us for, but my own ignorance decided not to listen - as I thought 'the professionals' must know better!

I have since read the Epi Paleo RX, watched most/if not all video's featuring Jack on Youtube, and have tried to take in as much as I can from this site. Unfortunately, I'm not as educated as many of you are, and find it a bit difficult to get my head around some subjects! But I am getting there slowly

From what I do understand, the improvement I felt from the ketogenic diet is the very tip of the iceberg, with diet being a small part of the jigsaw. I'm employing most of what I understand from the Epi Paleo RX. I'm also having cold showers every day, 3 cold baths a week (time permitting) and using blue-blocker glasses at night.

There's still a lot I need to improve on. My main problems are living in London.. An EMF hell hole, working shifts, and living in a country that offers little UV light. I know Jack will say I need to move and change my job, but neither are realistically possible at this moment in time.

I already feel healthier than I ever did before, but why settle for mediocrity? I know theres a lot more potential.. looking forward to learning more from this forum.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-03T22:23:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-london.17472/post-188107>

You settled because of low dopamine.........London = that issue. Dopamine = UV = CT .........all in the time series.

As dopamine rises so does your mind and your muscles everywhere.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-03T22:23:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-london.17472/post-188108>

Leave a review on Amazon for the book......thanks in advance.

### Nomad — 2016-02-04T02:15:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-london.17472/post-188130>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=188107)
>
> You settled because of low dopamine.........London = that issue. Dopamine = UV = CT .........all in the time series.
>
> As dopamine rises so does your mind and your muscles everywhere.

I fully intend to correct my circadian rhythm, which will mean changing my job (due to shift work), but that will be a few years down the line. As much as I would prefer to work things out naturally - in the mean time can I lessen the negative affects from working at night with gadgets such as intranasal laser therapy? And are there any other gadgets that may assist me with this? When I can afford it I intend to purchase the Quantlet, and a magneto amongst other things.

I have always struggled to wake up in the morning, its an issue that hasn't seen any improvement since implementing the changes I've made so far. Since UV is probably the main factor in this, I guess thats no big surprise.. I used to believe that I was naturally a 'night owl' as I seem to function better a night. I seem to wake up mentally, physically and have more 'drive' at night whilst daytimes are a bit of a drag. Now I understand its probably just because I'm a bit messed up!

The only time I seem to naturally wake up at a decent time is when I'm in warmer countries with an abundance of UV, I just never realised UV was probably the major influence before.

Well when we do member live events we do still do educational consults. I just did one last week as a make up. # Thread: 14 Day Fasting Experiment

**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jason Prall — 2016-02-04T01:43:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/14-day-fasting-experiment.17476/post-188128>

In about a week, I plan on doing a 10-14 day fast to find out my own personal physiologic effects.I'll be measuring some biomarkers before and after. In prep, I plan to get into nutritional ketosis for a few days prior.During the fast, I will be doing the following:

-   drinking shit loads of water
-   getting a ton of SoCal sun
-   doing daily cold thermogenesis (in ocean when possible)
-   grounding as much as possible (on beach sand & ocean water when possible)
-   blocking blue as usual
-   doing some Wim Hof style breathe work

Any thoughts or cautions from physicians or those who've gone through multiple-day fast? Any advice if you've tried something similar?Note: this is not a food/no food experiment. This is an electron-flow, mitochondria, and light experiment.

### seanb4 — 2016-02-04T13:43:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/14-day-fasting-experiment.17476/post-188167>

I did a 30 day fast a couple years back which was a big FAIL. I wouldn't recommend it however things I did which no doubt made it 20x harder:
No sun light.
Massively limited water intake.
No avoidance of nnEMF.
No grounding.

Maybe you will have a better experience with the following however things to be prepared for:
Being cold all the time. (will make CT very hard).
Feeling very sick and having disgusting taste in mouth all the time.
Feeling dizzy constantly.
Not "hunger" but strong desire for food.
Misery / depression.

Like I say I wouldn't recommend it but if it's only 2 week it shouldn't be too bad.

### Jason Prall — 2016-02-04T17:02:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/14-day-fasting-experiment.17476/post-188180>

Interesting. Thanks for the reply.

I'm actually looking at this from an electron flow, lighting, and mitochondrial standpoint and am curious to see the outcomes from that view when looking at my labs. The point is actually to see if I do the right things, a 14 day fast may (may not) be very difficult and the expected shift in biomarkers.I then plan to do the same 14 day fast with a different set of parameters...not doing as many things correctly to see how big of a difference that might make.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-04T18:00:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/14-day-fasting-experiment.17476/post-188197>

I did a fast for 12 days and it was equivocal. IF worked way better. I do think the healthier you are the easier and better a fast as you are trying would be.

### Pebbles — 2016-02-04T20:07:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/14-day-fasting-experiment.17476/post-188212>

Its now the 11th day - feel no hunger, just the smells are driving me crazy for that 20 minutes while .... still cooking for MDH)
Not so much natural light here - I am at 45L, so days start to get a bit longer, today down started at about 16.30. With SoCal light will be much easier.
Overall, despite weak light season, I am OK.
Started with a Mg cleaning. Shaded 7kg out of the 19kg plus I got last year in 2,5 month right after floxx. My tongue is clear and pink.
Better mood, no signs of depression at all!
In 10 days will have a big hormones lab, so wondering to continue or not? How will this influence my labs?

p.s. The doc got an Epi-paleo RX last yr in Nov - she was very happy to see something else than the 165gr carbs/day for IR protocol , so hopefully she gone thru it and also she muddled on the blog and forum as well)

p.s.2 - My 73yo mom is doing it to)

### Scompy — 2016-02-06T01:57:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/14-day-fasting-experiment.17476/post-188385>

My personal findings/biohacks:

For the last few months, I've been doing two shorter fasts per day (2-meals): Breakfast and Dinner only. That gives me about a 9-10 hour for my first fast between breakfast and dinner, and a 14ish hour fast from dinner to the next breakfast. Lots of water around lunchtime to help with any hunger pangs. On some days if I'm still hungry around noon due to a smaller breakfast, I'll only do a tiny bit from a protein source (like a fist-full). No lethargy anymore in the evening, stablized insulin levels, mental and emotional evenness overall. I have a large protein breakfast around 8am, then eat one last time around 5-6pm with a higher fats, lower protein meal. Works great with exercise timing, because 3-4x a week, I get a workout in before the dinner meal.

I used to do IF from the 9am-2pm window for a good year in 2013, but found it to be a major fail because of often waking up in the 4am window due to a lack of glucose if I pushed the fast past 16-18 hours in a sleep cycle. Also, that longer window wouldn't be compatable with trying to exercise in the afternoon.

### Barry — 2016-02-09T16:54:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/14-day-fasting-experiment.17476/post-188615>

Jason

I get your point but what do you want to achieve ?

why 14 ?

Hunger strikers , without electrons, UV, etc.... have survived up to 66 days without food.

maybe if you fasted for this long, and stayed healthy, and didnt die... then it would be something.

But going for 14, when you are already healthy, to look at some labs.... to prove or show what ?

Even seriously ill people, only need to do 3 day fasts, and it has been shown to regenerate the entire immune system

I've done a lot of fasting myself... but the context and the purpose needs to be clear

otherwise it can backfire... or at the least... be pointless.... just my 2 cents

For the past few months, I fast for 10 hours between breakfast and dinner and then fast again for 12 hours between dinner and the next morning's breakfast, I guess I could count that longer than a 44-day fast if I add all of my times together now lol. From my experiences too, longer than a few days is just not healthy. It was part of the reason why I ran marathons in the past for the error-in-my-thinking that I was benefitting from the running, when it was actually the UV being outside! What is an interesting takeaway to me is that I've found it's much harder to stay away from refined sugars than run a marathon or fast for 5 days! Seriously, eating ketotic in the winter takes a great amount of effort and fortitude with no recognition. I mention recognition because society rewards people for doing 10ks, half-marathons and iron-mans. Exercising is often made easier psychologically due to the numbing with an adrenline rush. This is why I also believe a marathon is easy-beans compared to getting your sugars down to about 15-20 grams daily, and it's exactly the sugar that spikes insulin that sets the body up for fat-storage if that's the goal to fast--to lose bodyfat. Fasting is probably harder than marathons for many people too.

I have to ask myself, "Isn't the better path doing what is the healthiest for the human body? Knowing what doesn't make us die as faster?" I wish I could take back the wear and tear the marathons did to my body, and the really long fasts the were total fails because they invoked more gluconeogenesis, and right after the fasts, promoted the behavior that it's okay to 'splurge more on junk food' because of the notion "I had been internally cleansed." It's utter hogwash. Anyone that finishes their dinner at 5pm and eats again at 7am gets a 14 hour fast daily for a shit-ton of cleansing. All of the past studies point to the optimal fasting window in the 16 - 22 hour range for HGH and leptin/insulin baselining. Beyond 24 hours, most studies show problematic things starting to happen. This is the message I'm saying. If somehow you're able to mitigate the damage caused by those activities, good-on-ya. Just throwing out my past experiences that those that over-train or over-anything that's not the natural human condition often (not always) have a shorter life expectancy. It's probably natural to go several days without food, no doubt, but I think the lines crosses into suffering, inflammation and gluconeogenesis to eat up our systems at a certain point.

### cmegan — 2016-02-11T20:01:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/14-day-fasting-experiment.17476/post-188789>

Scompy I totally agree with you...I have a history of fasting (veggie juices, water) but I felt that when I followed the protocol that you are doing I never felt better in my life and my liver function was so good! When I first did the LR reset 4 years ago I fell into a pattern of eating at the same times as you, about 7 or 8 in the morning and then around 5 at night. I remember being absolutely exhausted after breakfast for the first 4 days and had to nap (I was off from work) but then after that I had more energy than I had ever felt in years, no brain fog, etc. I look at both time periods between both meals as mini fasts. I have fallen off of this wagon since the holidays but I just started back on it today!

### prAna303 — 2016-02-11T20:57:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/14-day-fasting-experiment.17476/post-188792>

For me a fast would have its place in the coldest month...

Did it by accident in january, or it simply did happen.

Long trip into the wild. Four days planned with some company, the good thing was and i think this is vital, cold and snow.

The temp was about -13C down to -31C.

One meter of fresh pow.

Living outdoors 24 hours each day.

Going long distance each day, snowshoes.

I had no sense of hunger AT ALL. Did drink melted snow, five to eight litres each day.

In my own way of thinking, a fast should not be done in summer, to much natural food to chew up on and build some fat layers for the winter.

### Scompy — 2016-02-11T20:59:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/14-day-fasting-experiment.17476/post-188794>

I find too that the hardest part of this journey is being truthful with ourselves. It's much easier to be truthful with the rest of society. Great to hear you're getting back on the wagon

Lotta insulin-wave-riding in my past, bad redox, leptin issues, obesity, etc. It's important for me to start every day with a good chunk of protein and usually seafood of some type. Dinner is a reverse process to increase the fats and lower the proteins. When I find I'm still a bit hungry at lunch, I'll munch on something small but not a full meal. Most days I don't want any lunch at all, some days, a little snack is fine. I'm not totally "must obey these rules" but instead, "listen to my body" once I've experimented with an eating pattern for a few weeks to make a proper analysis of the pattern.

### cmegan — 2016-02-11T21:38:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/14-day-fasting-experiment.17476/post-188796>

In my own way of thinking, a fast should not be done in summer, to much natural food to chew up on and build some fat layers for the winter.

I agree with this, fasting is more natural for us to do in winter since food would have been more scarce and we were more keto adapted

### Pebbles — 2016-02-15T15:26:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/14-day-fasting-experiment.17476/post-189082>

Finishd after 14 days the 0 cal fast. After almost one week returning to normal = keto, 1/day meal which many call it IF, my labs look like
\- Cortisol ( serum, noon) 203nmol/l - 7,96 yg/dl
\-Insulin 7,83 mIU/L - 54,26 pmol/L
\-Estr 267,64 pg/L - 982,5 pmol/L
\-Prog 1,45 ng/mL - 4,6 nmol/L
DHEA-S- 9,8 umol/L - 362,96 yg/dl
Test ttl 2,03 nmol/L - 58,5 ng/dl
SHBG 106,5 nmol/l - 11,97 mcg/ml
Free test index 1,9%
\-TSH 3,46
FT4- 18,75
FT3- 4,31
BUN/Creatinine 8
ACTH and rT3 will be ready 3 weeks later only.

SHBG increased, as usual during or after fasting, and DHEA increased as well, rest of labs were not significantly influenced.
Pg/E ratio sucks, like before - perimeno knocks on the door.

From Doc- this comment was given to someone else. however I wonder if these applies to anyone with high SHBG:
"If your SHB is high and estrogen levels normal then you can localize your electron steal syndrome to either the plasma or the liver. Based upon your other labs you appear to have a mixed pattern of both. Your DHEA level tells us that the IL6 in your CSF is likely higher than you want it to be and this could directly be affecting your liver via the vagus nerve. This picture you have painted here is one that is mixed. But make no mistake you have a significant loss of electrons occurring to ionize SHBG to lower its charge. This depletes your plasma of electrons and the SHBG rises because it can not do its job with a lower level of electrons present within the blood. This is concerning because it also means you might be at risk for clotting or DVT 's with excessive airplane travel. The water issue must be a player here. I think you need to move to abetter source like Spring water or RO water. I also think you could help yourself with significantly adding ubiquinol not ubiquinone to your regimen. Resveratrol of polyphenols from organic blueberries would also help."

### mobeus — 2016-05-20T06:21:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/14-day-fasting-experiment.17476/post-195119>

@[Jason Prall](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17130/) - What did you learn? Curious to hear your results

# Thread: Need to help my dad!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-to-help-my-dad.17477/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Allanah — 2016-02-04T05:53:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-to-help-my-dad.17477/post-188145>

Hello again everyone!
Thank you for all your help with my previous questions! Now I have a few concerning my dad! He was/has been very skeptical about everything I'm doing, but hasn't refused to let me, so I can't really complain. However he recently inquired about my Leptin Reset and how he wouldn't be able to do it, so I really want some tips on how he can do the Leptin Reset. The first problem is that he works shift works, so he is the opposite of optimal on that, but I would like to help him as much as possible.
He wakes up at either 4:45 AM or PM depending on his shift. He eats and goes to work. He eats lunch at around 2-4 PM or AM and the works until around 7:30, when he goes home and eats, then goes to bed around 9:00 or 9:30.
He understands that he can't eat so soon before bed, but he needs a lunch that he can take in his lunch box that won't smash or go bad. He currently takes things like hard-boiled eggs but that's not going to work if he only eats 2 meals a day. Any suggestions about foods or tips he can use? If I can give him some tips so that he thinks it's doable, I think he may be able to take control of his health and get on the road to optimal. I think if he has success losing weight with the Leptin Rx he may see how all of this works together and take more steps to improve his health. Thanks to anyone who answers in advance!

### caroline — 2016-02-04T09:52:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/need-to-help-my-dad.17477/post-188152>

What about taking cans of salmon or sardines? And a separate salad loaded with vegetables? Roast pork? Roast beef?

He could take a thermos with bullet proof coffee maybe?

Make his lunch his dinner......

# Thread: Free water deficit - U.M conversion
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/free-water-deficit-u-m-conversion.17484/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Pebbles — 2016-02-04T20:19:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/free-water-deficit-u-m-conversion.17484/post-188213>

I am lost.... - in EU, so have to convert Na u.m

Formula:
mg/dL = (mmol/L \* gmw ) / 10
Where
gmw = Gram Molecular Weight
Example:
Convert 200 mmol/l of Sodium to mg/dL . The gram molecular weight of Sodium is 22.99 g/mol.
mg/dL = ( 200 \* 22.99 ) / 10
\= 20 \* 22.99
mg/dL = 459.8

My serum Na is 139mmol/L - lab range is 135-190
Converting it to mg/dl gives me 319 mg/dl - which is almost double vs the 140 optimal.

With this number, I am getting to +61L free water.
Using this calculator [http://www.medcalc.com/freewater.html](http://www.medcalc.com/freewater.html)

I am doing something wrong, or if not, where I am screwed up?
Thanks!

### Martin — 2016-02-06T15:16:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-how-you-found-jacks-blog.17486/post-188403>

Got really sick seven years ago and crashed, started researching various causes all the while wondering what a "natural" healthy human would look like. I figured an ancient human with no diet books and gym memberships would be interesting to study and wondered what their labs would look like and how mine would compare. I knew that I could find an answer in my bloodwork and symptoms instead of being all in my head. I became intrigued with "cave man diets" and went all Weston Price in my habits. Developed extremity pain and posted a question about "peripheral neuropathy" on my local Weston Price community forum and MamaGrok said I needed to go check out Dr Kruse's blog post on just that. "Here's my guy.", I said to myself. Liked his style and the way his work spanned many science fields and tied things together through observations of Nature.

### nonchalant — 2016-02-06T17:56:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-how-you-found-jacks-blog.17486/post-188411>

> [endless said:](/goto/post?id=188348)
>
> First thing I saw was the TED talk. My sister sent me a link to it and then I searched the blog, back in early 2012.

Too bad that talk was "lost".
I found Jack while following the tweets Dr. Eades received. Jack kept sending links to his blogs and after reading 3 or 4 of them, I started looking around his website and was hooked.

### Samy Colddigger — 2016-10-29T08:06:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/share-how-you-found-jacks-blog.17486/post-203418>

Studied a lot of science (biology, chem, stats) as well the history of science in my undergrad in Halifax, N.S. Originally from Northern Canada, I had always sensed there was something special about the energy where I grew up (magnetism and lots of cold haha). So I had intellectual and intuitive nose for the material when I came across Jack's site. I only found about him from looking at a Naturopathic Medical school website and seeing him as a guest speaker. I had also been learning a lot about EMF from other sources and when I started reading Jack's blogs..... ie Quantum Prometheus and Is Technology and Achilles heel?.... I was hooked! My life has change a lot since those first few eye opening but terribly draining two weeks of reading on the computer for hours on end . But It has been worth it. I am now lucky to say I live in a very sparsely populated area and I feel much better. No wifi or cell service. We have wood heat and propane stove. We turn our breakers off at night. We drink from a spring down the road whenever it is not frozen. We eat mostly seafood, and we never get sick anymore. Not even a cold or low energy or muscle pain. I have an outside job and I ground get lots of UV light all day. I try not wear my glasses whenever I have to be outside of the truck. Feel so much better than any job I have had working under blue light.

Woke up this morning to Northern lights at 6:45 in the morning. Horizontal bursts green across the sky like that I have never witnessed before. Might they be real? Or just illusions of microwaved world? Not the long, oscillating tails of lights that I am used to!

Thank you so much Dr. Kruse for all that you do and have done. You have brought so much light to my life. So much understanding and clarity. Thank from every cell in my body, every buzzing or perhaps slightly redox-shifted mitchochondria in my body. I can only hope to meet you person some time!

Sam

As a result, this alters the amplitude and oscillation of the standing wave made by mitochondria in the EZ cell water around the mitochondria. This cell water touches every part of the cell via the Tensegrity system of the cytoarchitecture. This signal is also transmitted to other cells in the tissue by pressure solitons and by the amount of ELF-UV release from adjacent cells. The alterations of that standing waves made by mitochondria oscillations inside every cell, is fully capable of moving things suspended within the cell. This makes it an ideal signal. That wave is critical to life. Listen in this link at the 55:00 part of this talk.

What does this wave form do to EZ water inside and around cells based upon Archimedes’ principle?

Water is lost in the MINOS if the mitochondria becomes able to swell too much. The cell dehydrates on a relative basis. This is a fundamental law of physics, tied directly to fluid dynamics. When water is lost in a cell, it changes its ability to electrically and chemically couple cycles of light, water, and magnetism. Gilbert Ling showed us this 65 years ago. We ignored it then, and remain clueless about its significance today.

When a cell loses its ability to generate a standing wave in mitochondria; this creates an even bigger problem for life because free radical generation is altered and tunneling speeds slows down. This increases the % heteroplasmy in a tissue and energy levels drop within cells of this tissue. This happens in all diseases. The generation of a normal standing wave in a cell is the key way a cell is able to tune’s itself to the native electromagnetic vibrations of sunlight and oscillations of the magnetic field in our environment. This is why cells don’t grow in culture as they do in tissues. The stretch on a cell on glass is never the same as it is inside a tissue because of these physical relationships. It is also why dehydration is a major clue to a clinician that coupled cycles, may now be uncoupled. Uncoupling cycles in biology, uncouple energy from light in the EZ, which act to cause circadian light cycles to uncouple from growth and metabolism cycles controlled by the central retinal pathways in the eye. This uncouples how biochemistry can act coherently at deeper levels from the retina in the brian where the leptin receptor resides in the hypothalamus. Moreover, this divorces the clinicians knowledge from how nature's wave forms are acting incoherently within his patients body when they are in blue light or have laptop in the lap. This is why your doctor struggles treating chronic diseases today. It is not because they do not care, its because they don't know physics well. You can bet when sleep is uncoupled from ocular melatonin, sulfated Vitamin D3, cortisol, and DHEA cycles, your metabolism and cell cycles are also altered, if you look for this evidence. No one does, and know one knows why we should look because biology does not understand how 3 physical forces can bring life’s spark to fruition in a cell.

What else happens to the sea within us when we uncouple? Water losses imply density alterations in the EZ and this causes more electrical and mechanical energy loss. Energy is lost at every step. As they change in unison, something bad happens in the brain. The optics of water changes in the hypothalamus because of physical laws. When optics change, proteins can no longer function as they were designed by evolution with the sun or magnetic field. When proteins do not function well, their redox potential is altered, their size and shape change and this is when ubiquitin marks a protein to be replaced. When this occurs, this is the signal DNA responds too in a cell, to begin expression to make new protein. This is how epigenetics works. This is why ubiquination rates are increased in all cases when % heteroplasmy is also raised in mitochondria; this is where homeostasis is unbalanced from any environmental changes in any way. What is the collateral damage of these effects? It alters the size, shape, of mitochondria and this alters % heteroplasmy. As % heteroplasmy rises the muscle and fat mass are affected first. Humans store our stem cells in fat and muscle tissue. Neurosurgeons see these changes in MRI’s images all the time. We see it, but few really know what these changes mean bio-physically. They are signs people are actively dying because they are losing energy to the environment Humans see well, but they don’t observe well. We begin to see fat where muscle should be and fat where bone should be. We see low T and low GH levels. Why? The environment is altering Archimedes principle in us!!! Fat and muscle have different density and buoyancy to mimic the thermodynamic changes in these tissues to reflect energy loss or gain. This is why fat replacement of muscle or bone on an MRI or DEXA, or white matter changes within the brain, is a key finding of uncoupling of light from metabolism. The altered water flows are seen as abnormal proton signals in the white matter, bone, and in muscles; Morevoer, every one of them is tied to Archimedes principle is occurring in the person to the astute clinician.

# Thread: My current situation. Advice?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-current-situation-advice.17488/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Karl K — 2016-02-05T00:52:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-current-situation-advice.17488/post-188256>

I've lived in Dallas, TX my whole life. Recently quit my last job which I was very excited to be a part of (it was a restaurant that adhered to Western Price's nutrition protocols but had 6 wi fi routers connected to wireless tablets that we were required to wear on our bodies). I was having shooting nerve pain on the left side of my head and feeling dizzy. I toughed it out for a little over a week because I needed the money real bad and after bringing the issue of radiation up to management a few times I saw that they wouldnt do anything as they had invested too much money already so I left. I have $20 to my name, some stuff I could sell but I am having wifi headaches just from using my laptop (havent gotten an ethernet adapter for my macbook yet) and want to get to a healthier location. There are restaurants in the area that I could go back to for work a while and I remember getting the same wi fi headaches there on occasion probably due to dirty electricity/the nearby power lines/the crowds of people with cell phones on. My energy has been crap for years, need 10 hours of sleep to feel decent and can hardly get up before noon or I feel wiped out. Thus it has been difficult for me to maintain my health and make the money I need to for managing my financial situation which continues to spiral out of control. My Mom and Step Dad live in Woodland Park CO. There are only 5 cell towers in a 4 mile radius from their house whereas I have 74 around my apartment. Moving to Colorado seems like the most feasible thing to do right now. This will minimize EMF and artificial light exposure however it is much further North and I thought the idea was to stay South for more sun exposure? Thanks for any insights and feedback.

### Karl K — 2016-02-05T00:53:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-current-situation-advice.17488/post-188257>

. If you were to believe the statistics though I should be writing you this from the after life but I can assure I am alive and kicking

Moving to Colorado seems like the most feasible thing to do right now. This will minimize EMF and artificial light exposure however it is much further North and I thought the idea was to stay South for more sun exposure? Thanks for any insights and feedback.

There are either 200 or 400 cell phone towers around me - the closest one is 1/2 mile away - it seems far away enough because people have to go outside of my house to make a call... also, there is no 4G here - yet - down the street, people are trainwrecks - especially from the stuff in their house - I think mitigating the house is a huge move - if you are this susecptible to EMF, give it up on the restaurant thing... **Subforum:** Adrenal Rx and Leaky Gut Rx

### Nick12345 — 2016-02-06T21:54:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaky-gut-uv-diarrhea-candida-whats-up.17499/post-188435>

My wife has had leaky gut for many years. She was diagnosed with a few food allergies about 6 months ago, so she has ceased eating those foods. We traveled to Florida one week ago to get some UV. Within 48 hours of arriving, she began having diarrhea that has now been ongoing for 4 days. She also had one stool that had white string-cheese looking pieces in there (candida perhaps?). Is this a normal detox-type experience? Or is it more likely she ate something bad that has coincided with the travel timing?

### Aerose91 — 2016-05-05T01:23:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaky-gut-uv-diarrhea-candida-whats-up.17499/post-194190>

I can't speak for your wife particularly but i can give you mine and many others' experiences.

Im pretty seriously ill and felt best in the southwest- AZ, CA, NV. I went to the florida panhandle in late winter to get more UV and didn't do well there. The humidity, mugginess and mold in the air took a real toll and I'm still not even close to recovering, now several weeks later.

Many others in the adrenal fatigue, lyme and CFS community get worse in the southeast, the air quality there is terrible. In fact, 2 of the top doctors in the country are there and even recommend their patients move west.

Im not saying your wife has these ailments, just that sick people tend to get sicker in Florida. You could also look into herbs for parasites (black walnut, wormwood, clove) which could be what you guys were seeing.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-05-05T02:31:15+0300

### Jack Kruse — 2016-05-05T02:45:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaky-gut-uv-diarrhea-candida-whats-up.17499/post-194194>

Leptin resistance = too many protons = too much inflammation = leaky gut.

Vitamin D levels link to the solar environment = latitude and altitude and population density. Do not forget it.

IBS = Candida is a classic leaky gut disease........so let us look here first.

Studies like this worry me. Why? The are details about supplementation of Vitamin D3 without any context here. You must get it from the photonic power of the sun to avoid surface effects in the eye, skin, GUT, and lung. I have found that over supplementation with oral vitamin D3 usually leads to a very rare eye dysfunction. The IBS improves for the first few months but never fully reverses with oral D3. To fully reverse it you need endogenous Vitamin D3 production from sunlight to get the maximum reversal. If you listened to my Nov 2011 podcast on [JoanneUnleashed.com](http://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=http%3A%2F%2FJoanneUnleashed.com%2F&h=YAQFh3EIJAQGG_1w4f_5gLPBRYw53E-EH-PdsOZ5jc0-pdQ&enc=AZNaHSS9N3HMd5KIE6SSpH5yC_SD_nyx9iYSOOP6RqEyW66nX9oZqIA8TZSCf9jK5Xswp3FGQsSYGIx_4FeHwKFwsQ5vU9SxJWbXKPoOYnXnSDKESMI0dZMY5Kyfj0tLR5qW09EcY3ZiEaMVDydRTNjPK-JO38OLRIIjbsJ_NG9bsKRJoOtsIakXzxOYgbhxKjKNE8vQgbGWNLfCfgTXUWQp&s=1) you might have wondered why I read in low light and red light levels during my own recovery and reversal. I was checking myself for signs that I was pushing my Vitamin D3 status too high with pills initially. I began my reset in Vitamin D winter in Nashville. When a person over supplements Oral D3, individuals can become unable to distinguish images in low levels of illumination. Vitamin A deficiency affects vision by inhibiting the production of rhodopsin, the eye pigment responsible for sensing low light situations. The supplement is a chemical signal of a photochemical process in the skin. Calcitriol and rhodopsin tied in a feed back loop with one another because of light. D3 = UVB LIGHT, and Vitamin A = photoperiod of light in a 24 hour day. The longer the photoperiod, generally the higher D3 level should be. This linkage remains because of the link with latitude, altitude, population density, and photoperiod. Population density is the NEW METRIC everyone forgets to add in. The more populated and area the more nnEMF present and the more vibrations in your microenvironment to knock electrons from cells. Less electrons = LESS LIGHT ASSIMILATION for a cell even if you LIVE in a good UV site based upon latitude and altitude. This is why California and Southern Florida are not ideal choices today in my opinion. Now lets look back to the world before technology. The choice was EASIER. then The closer one gets to the equator the longer light remains even in winter. The higher you are (above 5000 ft) the better off you are. This drives higher D3 levels in a cell. What happens in the eye and gut? Rhodopsin is found in the human retina and is composed of retinol, which is an active form of vitamin A, and opsin a protein made by the retina. Its production is linked to D3 production in the skin. Basically, if you “over do” the use of Vitamin D3, you will not make enough rhodopsin because of a lack of feedback control (from a relative Vitamin A deficiency) and you will suffer from night blindness. This lowers ocular melatonin levels which is needed to regenerate cones during daylight. If you live far from the equator, at sea level, or in a populated area, and it is winter and you over supplement D3 you lose the ability to make ocular melatonin and you cannot regenerate locally in the GUT. Read Time 9 for details there.

I pick this up in patients who notice night time visual driving issues or in people who find they must use bright blue light at night to see or read. Many times these people have early onset AMD. Most people won't read in low light levels these days because of tech gadgets, like I do to assess themselves for this issue. But you can use this simple test too bio hack your own abilities. If they do notice it, Their PCP's usually send them to the optometrist or ophthalmologist, but most are cured once they tweak their diets to add more carotenoids or begin to use artificial sources of UV light at night. Both of these things can be bio hacked to add back some feedback control of the vitamin A cycle using light or foods with photosensitizers in them.

My real concern: They also suffer from a more serious subclinical problem. **They can never reverse their leaky gut due to chronic inflammation from a subclinical infection.** Often they see a short improvement with the pill but it lasts 6-8 weeks. The reason for both of these clinical features is that they likely have a co morbid lack of Vitamin A in the diet (especially in winter) to offset the raised supplemental Vitamin D3. This rarely happens in subtropical areas but happens more frequently at latitudes above Atlanta in winter in the US. Each cell in our body has 2 vitamin A receptors for every Vitamin D receptor. We also have VDR's in our gut. So as our vitamin D level rises because we take the supplement orally we give our gut VDR's a falsely elevated signal. This changes optical signaling in the GALT and its T cells. The cells sense the D3 chemical signal but the tissues do not have the photon energy or momentum. and therefore there is not enough UV photon power within the tissues. As a result of feedback loops our endogenous Vitamin A source is used up even quicker and an imbalance results. We need to make sure we are getting enough of the fat soluble Vitamin A too as Vitamin D3 goes up from pills. rarely does this get spoken about in people outside the subtropics in autumn or winter. These two vitamins are metabolically coupled but one is made from UVB light and the other is made by the photoperiod of the day. This is probably the most common reason people have persistent bloating and cannot clear H Pylori or Candida infections from their guts when they are not getting a full spectrum sun signal to support the D3 chemical. I've seen this develop in people when they transition to a meat laden paleo template while supplementing Vitamin D3. It is rare in an epi-paleo Rx because seafood and mushrooms have a lot flesh that contain enough of the photonic power embedded in the food stuff. We are designed to make D3 from sunlight and not a pill. So if you have IBS be aware of this unusual possibility of you live further from the equator. [http://www.medicalnewstoday.com/articles/304361.php…](http://www.medicalnewstoday.com/articles/304361.php?utm_campaign=trueAnthem%3A+Trending+Content&utm_content=5678245f04d30149b0c26741&utm_medium=trueAnthem&utm_source=facebook)

**Fundamentally, Leptin Resistance (LR) is loss of UV light from cells. When light is lost it favors a Warburg style metabolism on a chronic basis. This is why intermittent fasting is a better choice because it is not a chronic stimulus as fasting for weeks is.**
Why does the Leptin Rx tell you to hold off on exercise in the beginning stages? LR is a defect in excessive light release from cells due to their “relative mitonuclear mismatch” = increase % heteroplasmy rates . Exercise causes you to lose more light from your cells in this situation and heavily favors a Warburg metabolism longer term. This further lowers your redox potential and can hurt you. LR is results when any respiratory protein deficit causes improper signaling to the nuclear DNA. LR is the signal the hypothalamus gets when the proper redox shift is not made in reference to supply and demand between the mitochondria and nucleus. What results instead, is an incompatibility between the mitochondrial respiratory protein functional capacity and your nuclear DNA. This situation causes mitochondria to move away from your nucleus, distance wise, when researchers look at the relationship via electron microscopy. This effect is magnified in species (humans) who use oxygen as their terminal electron acceptor in their mitochondria. Oxygen has a very strong affinity for electrons, hence why it is able to provide a pull for electrons on our inner mitochondrial membrane. When the respiratory proteins are not properly aligned, not even the valence shell desire for those electrons can be met fast enough. This is why pseudohypoxia exists in these states. Humans, birds, and bats are a species who has a very high aerobic capacity because they have a large mitochondria capacity. They all use oxygen as their terminal electron acceptor. This is why David Sinclair’s paper, mentioned above, on pseudohypoxia, is incredibly important for you to understand completely, on your road to optimal. For a human to survive this situation they would need to retain the ability to alter how electrons flow in their mitochondria to optimize function to their current environment. Why is the Exercise Rx important for you to understand now? Because how the electrons are created in mitochondria is also a variable few consider. I got the insight long ago when I found the melanocortin pathways connects to and from the eye and brain. Laser surgery taught me about free radicals and LR.

When the respiratory proteins do a poor job of this, low NAD+ and SIRT 1 ratio’s are the result in cells. How does ketosis fit into this scenario? If you read my book, The Epi-Paleo Rx, you will see that ketosis is a “small critical part" of reversing most diseases. If you don’t understand my blogs, you will think I have just contradicted myself in my book. I have not. Ketosis can change the flow of electrons on the inner mitochondrial membrane, and alter free radical signaling, but it has zero effect on the electron spin or state, moreover it has no effect on size of the ECT protein components. Ironically where ketosis works of us, is by narrowing the distance between the respiratory proteins in the mitochondrial electron transport that ultimately determines the speed of flow of electrons. That is 100% tied to the environment your MITOCHONDRIA SENSE via the MINOS water around the respiratory proteins.
Ketosis only augments the flow of electrons. If the electrons are in the singlet state is this a good thing? Nope. It means you build a body faster than falls apart sooner. Ketosis is an accelerator pedal of electrons only. Wellness is not based solely on electrons speed to oxygen. You cannot recapitulate the quantum coherence just by quickly TUNNELING electrons !!!!! It will sustain life until you get every detail correct. This is why I you always here me say, you can never reverse a disease if you do not alter the environment you got ill within. The environment determines the state the electrons are in and your diet choices control the flow from cytochrome 1 to oxygen. Wellness is not linear it is non linear and far from equilibrium. Ketosis is only a tool in reversals to buy you some time to fix your light environment.

Fake light causes your mitochondria ECT to slow because the respiratory proteins enlarge to slow electron tunneling while also creating singlet state electrons….…..when ECT slows, you up regulate carbohydrate metabolism by way of AMPK pathways. All this occurs just from the change in frequency of light. The brain can not tell sunlight from blue light, only your mitochondria can because it is what gives electrons its spin. Moreover, alternative and ancestral clinicians are unaware of this. You no longer can afford to be.

In defense of the southwest- the large majority of it is unpopulated and far, far more sparse than anywhere in the east. Considering the person isn't drinking tap water and is buying spring or RO water while living in or near a small town out there, its beautiful. Phoenix or LA would be obviously bad choices.
The clean, arid air of the desert, or on the central Cali coast is a mecca for people who are mold and toxin sensitive. I spent a period in Big Sur as well as Jenner and felt great on the coast but instantly got sick when going into the pine forest. Im talking seconds. This wouldnt clear up until i returned to the coast or desert.
It was the same everywhere in the FL panhandle. The damage from mold and humidity outweighs the benefits of increased O2 for some of us, regardless of how much sun there is. Every single M.E. or adrenal fatigue person ive met has the same experience.

@[Nick12345](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/18626/) not trying to hijack your thread, i know very little of this probably pertains to your wife.

# Thread: Jumping In - Gut issues, Hyperthyroidism, Anxiety, Sleep Issues

### Coreena — 2016-02-07T00:53:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jumping-in-gut-issues-hyperthyroidism-anxiety-sleep-issues.17501/post-188447>

Hello to All,

I'm SO new to JK Forums, but decided to go ahead and start my journal. I'm not even done reading the book...

My stats & history:
Age: 34
Ht/Wt: 5'3", 120 lbs

Current Health Issues: Sensitive to Gluten, possible leaky gut (need to get some labs done), Slight hyperthyroidism with nodules. This is at the forefront of my mind right now because I saw the doc last week and the nodules have gotten bigger. Albeit no one could look at my thyroid and notice the difference, but it's never good when nodules on your thyroid get bigger. Am getting labs done this month to see the blood levels. I've never had to take medicine for it and I'm determined to FIX THIS NOW. Especially since AI stuff seems to run in my family...
I can get stressed/overwhelmed easily and I'm a perfectionist, so I can really make my own head explode. Which can cause sleep issues. Sleep issues aren't very bad at all, but really, none of my "issues" are. My goal is to stop the minor problems from EVER becoming major problems. I want to feel great for as many years as I possibly can.

Diet: Strict paleo for the last 10 months. Did GAPs for 5 months before cutting out dairy (except for ghee and a bit of heavy cream). Had a month (Dec) where I basically binged on peanut butter cups...don't ask me why... and I quickly saw the ill effects. So that is all out of my system. Getting back to what works. Strict paleo, and this time around, more seafood, cutting out nut butter, heavy cream and the little bit of honey in my coffee every morning. Just keeping ghee and seeing how I feel. Might even cut out the coffee altogether during leptin RX, and replace with KeVita. Again....have to finish the book! Have been eating big breakfast, no snacking after dinner for past week.

Have starting hacking environment too - wired ethernet, landline, unplugging things, blue blockers, amber bulbs, more sunshine, more water, etc.

Glad to be here....

### nonchalant — 2016-02-07T01:46:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jumping-in-gut-issues-hyperthyroidism-anxiety-sleep-issues.17501/post-188449>

Welcome Coreena. I had an enlarged thyroid a couple years ago. I took Armour thyroid for about a year, but now I'm off of it and my thyroid is good (both in the way it feels and in my labs). I think sunshine was what turned it around, but it could be a combination of Jack's protocols.

Your stress might also ease up, with improved health. Mine sure did. I used to overreact to minor problems. In fact, that is one of the symptoms that I watch for, because it is an early warning of environmental issues for me. My ability to tolerate temperature swings is another. Next time you want to binge on p.b. cups, you need to see what is wrong around you. December wasn't because you were weak or bad.

### caroline — 2016-02-07T12:16:32+0200

### Rubicon — 2016-02-07T02:17:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/creating-a-custom-title-on-the-forum.17502/post-188451>

I noticed Yew had a clever title under his profile nickname and dug through the settings till I found how to customize mine. Just go to **[Personal Details](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?account/personal-details)** and modify the **Custom Title** field

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

# Thread: Tell me what I can do better (psoriasis sufferer)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tell-me-what-i-can-do-better-psoriasis-sufferer.17503/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lawrencep93 — 2016-02-07T02:44:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tell-me-what-i-can-do-better-psoriasis-sufferer.17503/post-188455>

So what I have been trying to do this year:

-   Trying to get sunlight with minimal clothing before 10am
-   Use red lights in my room when I am inside, Flux and twighlight on my PC and Android
-   Eating a keotgenic diet, minimum 1 dozen oysters per week + seafood like raw tuna
-   Sleeping 8 hours
-   Using cold where possible, mainly sitting in my room cooled and cold showers
-   Fast and eat dinner post work out mainly for appetite control and ease of lifestyle

So basically I live in Melbourne Australia.
I have Psoriasis which has gotten better the past few months (it is summer here=more sun)
Also short sighted, which I find is at it's worse at night or in places that are lit with artificial light, but my vision is much better in sunlight.
Have lost over 66lb but still wanting to lose a further 25lb
Thinking of doing UVB therapy over winter as the UV from the sun has greatly helped my psoriasis over winter and a friend of mine with the same condition has seen amazing results, considering lack of sun here in winter.

I have been reading Jacks blogs and I really like how he recently spoke about psoriasis, quite a few things go over my head, but I am constantly trying to learn more and more to try and keep up with the vast knowledge provided

### Eilee — 2016-02-07T21:44:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/problems-with-magnetico.17505/post-188483>

I've used my new Magnetico for the past 4 nights (20 G) and each night I am waking before sunrise and unable to get back to sleep. Instead of feeling refreshed, as others are reporting, I am feeling extremely depleted throughout the day. It has been building so that today I feel like a total wreck. In general, I have sleep problems and have always taken sleeping aids (Please, no judgements). My hope was that the Magnetico would help me ween from them. Also, it has always been the case that whenever I wake too early, I feel like crap. And it is no different with the Magnetico.

Wondering if anyone has experienced this? I'm really nervous that perhaps this won't work for me, but I really don't want to give up on it. I can't go on waking up this early however without it taking a toll on me. Would love to know if anyone has any thoughts as to what might be going on.

### nonchalant — 2016-02-07T23:07:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/problems-with-magnetico.17505/post-188488>

That does sound concerning, Eilee. Do you awaken when the sky is still dark? How much earlier than dawn?
When we got our magnetico, DH started falling asleep very quickly, like within 5 minutes, which was very unusual for him. Lucky thing. It wasn't like that for me, however. My sleep is very slowly improving. Sometimes I fall asleep quickly; sometimes it still takes a while. But currently I wake up about 30 minutes before dawn.

### Eilee — 2016-02-07T23:35:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/problems-with-magnetico.17505/post-188490>

Also, there is a detoxification aspect to magnetico use: [https://magneticosleep.com/about-magnetism/detoxification/](https://magneticosleep.com/about-magnetism/detoxification/)
It may be advisable to use DMSA when first using the Magnetico if you have a heavy metal
toxicity

### Jamie Ward — 2016-02-07T21:45:07+0200

Hey guys,

So, I live in the UK currently and have never felt like it was home (it is the only country I have lived in). I have always had that feeling I belong in another part of the world. Finding this community confirmed my suspicions that the UK is not an optimal choice for me.

I am currently looking at Nicaragua as a place to move to. It ticks a lot of boxes for me from a health perspective and also offers some enticing incentives for expat investors. I am a businessmen and when I saw all the tax breaks it got my dripping with excitement.

I just booked to go for 2 weeks in April and if I like it I will go back at the end of the year for a few months to see what opportunities I can dig up.

Does anyone have any experience with Nicaragua and could offer me some advice?

Much appreciated!

# Thread: Jamie's Journal: UK based, weightlifting & hormone imbalances
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jamies-journal-uk-based-weightlifting-hormone-imbalances.17513/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 24
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jamie Ward — 2016-02-08T15:48:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jamies-journal-uk-based-weightlifting-hormone-imbalances.17513/post-188563>

Hi guys,

So I have decided to finally start documenting my journey. Thanks to everyone's help and support so far, here and on the FB group

To give some background...

About 18 months ago I was about 2.5 stone overweight and living a very unhealthy life. I started hitting the gym hard and started watching my calories, eating a mainly paleo diet. The weight poured off and I eventually got a set of abs.

However, with this has come a cascade of hormone issues. My testosterone levels fell to non existent levels and I was feeling terrible. I also became quite an irritable person! My liver and kidney levels also went through the roof... I have never quite found out what was causing this, however I have some retests this week where I haven't been training for 2 weeks and have been of creatine for a month...

I have posted before I have very cold hands and feet, hinting at bad circulation. Jack gave me some insights and suggested that where I am training is likely adding to the problem. Since he said that, I have actually stopped weightlifting in the gym due to the bright artificial fluorescent lights.

I also had some salivary cortisol and DHEA tests recently that hinted at early stage adrenal fatigue. This is another reason I have stopped training again, to focus on my recovery and care less about abs!

**Context**

I am a property developer in the UK who spends a fair amount of time in office and the car driving to sites.
I was going to the gym about 5-6 days a week and lifting intensely under artificial lighting
I have started trying to get outside for barefoot walks along the beach when I can (2-3 times a week)
I look at the sun when it comes out. I get up at 6.30 AM everyday
I have switched to mainly seafood since January and now I have huge amounts every day. I have notified a significant benefit from this.
I have removed all blue light from my flat, replacing with some UVA bulbs
I wear day time blue blockers and then Uvex in the evening
F.Lux on all computers and set to max in the day
Hit up low pressure sunbeds a few times a week
Just starting some red light/infrared therapy with UFOs
Drink a ton of water
Electric and WiFi turned off at night
CT 3-4 times a week in the tub

**Goals**
Get my hormones rebalanced
Find a form of exercise I enjoy and is healthy
Move abroad, possibly to Nicaragua at the end of the year

All my labs are attached!

Thanks

### Jamie Ward — 2016-02-09T15:09:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jamies-journal-uk-based-weightlifting-hormone-imbalances.17513/post-188606>

Hi guys,

I have been re-listening to January's webinar and JK referred to Jim Al-Khalli and his BBC documentaries. Sadly, these aren't on their website anymore but I found them on youtube...

[https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLk4aZhY2J7MN8ukOGomB0nfZEdzCeRg-d](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLk4aZhY2J7MN8ukOGomB0nfZEdzCeRg-d)

I hope this helps anyone!

### Jamie Ward — 2016-02-09T15:12:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jamies-journal-uk-based-weightlifting-hormone-imbalances.17513/post-188607>

I forgot to mention in my first post I took a course in TM (meditation) about 6 weeks ago. I had been measuring my HRV via Heartmath Emwave Pro.

My ANS was incredibly imbalanced, with a Parasympathetic Nervous System to Sympathetic Nervous System ration of about 12:1! I am now down to 2.4:1 ... significant improvement!

I was interested to hear JK refer to the fact Heartmath don't fully understand the technology they created. I wonder if anyone could expand on that?

Thanks!

### Joe Gavin — 2016-02-09T15:51:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jamies-journal-uk-based-weightlifting-hormone-imbalances.17513/post-188609>

Great shit Jamie. Walking the walk. Check out this link [https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/humming-brains-help-us-learn-rapidly.11112/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/humming-brains-help-us-learn-rapidly.11112/)

### Jamie Ward — 2016-02-09T19:39:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jamies-journal-uk-based-weightlifting-hormone-imbalances.17513/post-188635>

Cheers Jo dude!

A few things to add with lighting... so I have managed to remove the majority of blue light from my life, even in my office. However, office is still my biggest risk from a nnEMF standpoint.

As I am based in the UK, I have added some UVA CFLs around my apartment and have one on my desk at work. At work I also have just started with a solar Glo bulb, although not convinced I like this so I might remove. I also got a sperti lamp to help me raise my vit D levels.

I have also just added in red LED UFO and Near Infrared LED, similar to a UFO.

I'm also going sunbeds for c. 10 mins, 3 times a week at the moment.

Having read Pollack's book I am convinced my poor circulation is down to lack of EZ in the cells and lack of UV for the charge of the engine. Time will tell.

I am drinking abou 6-7 litres a day of Buxton water and Pelligrino. I have carbon filter berkey filter but I have just stopped with that to see if bottled mineral water leaves me feeling more hydrated.

### Arclite — 2016-02-10T19:00:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jamies-journal-uk-based-weightlifting-hormone-imbalances.17513/post-188691>

Sounds to me like overtraining. I once ascribed to the more is better philosophy, and routinely would put in 5-6, hour+ _intense_ sessions per week. Now that I have drastically reducd my training volume ( 2-3 days, only when I _feel_ like it, when I _want_ to) I have improved the totals on all my major lifts. When I most fervently sought after strength than did it most treacherously evade me. I improved but relative to the expenditure, these were embarrasingly modest improvments. In the last 3 weeks though, since adopting this regimine, my deadlift has increased by 5%. That may seem miniscule to the uninitiated but considering the poundage involved and the degree of muscular control and activation required to execute a succesful deadlift, it is a substantial amount and not one that falls within typical day to day variations, to which a seasoned lifter is less prone. I've gotten stronger and by doing _far_ less. Not only am I hoisting more weight, I am doing it with relative ease, my perecieved rate of exertion at 405 is dramatically less than it was at 385 just a few weeks ago. No longer do I need to psyche myself up, I calmy grip the bar and elevate the weight. I wouldn't worry so much about the fake light in your gym. A half hour or less a couple times a week is all you need. Focus on heavy compounds, keep the rep range low, don't train to failure, and I guarantee that T will shoot right up to where it needs to be.

### Jamie Ward — 2016-02-11T13:06:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jamies-journal-uk-based-weightlifting-hormone-imbalances.17513/post-188763>

Completely agree... classic overtraining. I am just taking some time off for now to help heal hormones a bit before going back into it. When I do, I am just going to train 3 times a week and not kill myself... pretty much following what you said above to be honest.

Ratio on my HRV was down to 1.3:1 today so everything is starting to come back to homeostasis. Running some cortisol labs again on the weekend

### Mystic Rose60 — 2016-02-11T15:59:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jamies-journal-uk-based-weightlifting-hormone-imbalances.17513/post-188765>

Anytime you train under artificial lighting in the gym, you will make singlet o2, vs the triplet state that you will make when you train outdoors. It all adds up and remember, light only flows to the tissue that has a lot of the triplet state free radicals. If triplet is missing=NAD LOW=Oxygen as well. I know that Jack has said before, that the only time you should go into the gym, is to say hello and goodbye. It is something to be concerned about! I resigned my gum membership and purchased my own equipment and train outdoors now. I'm not one to use the word "can't" very often and this will be one of the few times that I do use it. But here goes....You can't half ass your way to optimal guys! Just caring and sharing with you!

### Jamie Ward — 2016-02-11T18:32:33+0200

It all adds up and remember, light only flows to the tissue that has a lot of the triplet state free radicals. If triplet is missing=NAD LOW=Oxygen as well. I know that Jack has said before, that the only time you should go into the gym, is to say hello and goodbye. It is something to be concerned about! I resigned my gum membership and purchased my own equipment and train outdoors now. I'm not one to use the word "can't" very often and this will be one of the few times that I do use it. But here goes....You can't half ass your way to optimal guys! Just caring and sharing with you!

I agree with singlet vs triplet etc. However, my o2, judging by my haemoglobin is sky high so I don't think oxygen is my problem. Training outdoors in the UK sucks!

That light is used to power up electrons and this electrons have to get assigned a spin in mitochondria. So light acts as the currency in the compound pharmacy in our pituitary every day to make things we need from light. This is a staggering level of power and control. So when you open up any biochemistry book and realize that biochemistry only uses 100,000 substrate in reactions you realize light can control it. When you factor in that the photoelectric effect acts instantaneously, with no time delay, then you begin to see how 100,000 biochemical reactions can occur per second using light frequencies from the visible spectrum easily.

People tell us all the time to block the sun with sunscreens and glass. Nonsense. We have built in natural sunscreens for UV light. Vitamin C is one way in how we modulate light assimilation and transport at surfaces.....This is why equatorial fruits have vitamin c in them. It is also why taking too much vitamin C is bad.........it block the sun even more. What else can happens to SHBG because of an altered copper metabolism linked to Vitamin C? When it rises your body has no light contained in its system and % heteroplasmy is rising.............What to do? MOVE to the sun because modern life is lowering it yield every day around you.

A rising SHBG is a beacon of a light problem for the person with the condition of poor light assimilation.............It affects ceruloplasmin and the carotenoids cycles in the skin and without dopamine in the eye, you make no hormones and the ones you do make are released incorrectly.
DNA breakdown on the surface layers of the skin is the another ingenious way to do it using non linear optics.........and then their is the carotenoid system on surfaces as another fail safe for UV light assimilation. This is why vegetarians do OK around the equator and no where else...........veggies have them. You dont need veggies outside the tropics. It worsens UV assimilation. Terry Wahls fail right there folks. This is how a half truth gets you killed or sicker. This redness of the skin is the signal from the sun you need to pay attention too because it means you have reached your dose of UV for the day. If you eat too many veggies or too much fruit outside the tropics it is akin to using chemical sunscreens, so that, you also lose that signal increasing the chance you will burn and fry the system.
