# Thread: Leaving the environment you are adapted to
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaving-the-environment-you-are-adapted-to.17515/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Queen of Elves — 2016-02-09T09:48:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaving-the-environment-you-are-adapted-to.17515/post-188595>

Hello guys,

I'm new to the forum. Last year I found the blog and read Dr. Kruse's book.

I am 42 years old, former vegan (strictly for 10 years ) and dealing with some small issues (hypothyroidism/hashi?, infertility, myopia, blackouts, anxiety disorder...).

I am in week 6 of the Leptin Rx, lost a bit weight, dropped my FBG and lost nearly all cravings
At week 4 I started with face dunks for 9 days, went on with ice and compression shirt/shorts for 4 days and tolerated it for 60 min from the beginning. So I put ice directly on skin yesterday and tolerated it for 60 min without problems. I will do this a few times more and soon be in the tub.

I am living in Germany where the sun rises at the moment at 7:44 and sets at 17:30 PM and I am trying to get as much time outside as possible, which is not easy with 9 hours in an office in front of the screen (I will change that soon).

I saw people writing about perfect places, zip-codes, holiday destinations and wonder if it is always beneficial to leave your not optimal environment (with regard to light spectrum and nnEMF), even for a short period of time? Even if you are perfectly adapted to that place, say that you respect circadian cycles, live in nature, are cold and grounded? Couldn't it result in stress for your body, to stop its well known rhythm, get on a plane and plump a few hours later into a place with a totally different climate? I suppose it would at least take some time to cope with the new zip-code...

If it actually is to prefer to get some good sunlight at a distant place, no matter what season is in your current place, no matter how well you are adapted and your ancestors lived here for a long time, does that mean, you can never get optimal in a place that is not the place where the species evolved? Would people from Greenland benefit from a 1 week trip to Mexico?

Just trying to figure out if I will move or adapt and meanwhile should take every opportunity to get to places with more sunlight.

### caroline — 2016-02-09T11:02:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaving-the-environment-you-are-adapted-to.17515/post-188596>

Hi queen and welcome.....
I get what you are asking .....but - can/do you live in nature?
You say you are in front of a screen for 9 hours a day and you know this isn't optimal.
What is your home environment like?
Can you opt out of all that? And live in nature? And be cold and grounded? And respect circadian cycles?
This is everyone's problem ..... to navigate all this in our own
Little piece of the planet.
There are a few people here that live in Northern Europe and manage very well ...

### Lahelada — 2016-02-09T13:03:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaving-the-environment-you-are-adapted-to.17515/post-188599>

> [Queen of Elves said:](/goto/post?id=188595)
>
> Hello guys,
>
> I'm new to the forum. Last year I found the blog and read Dr. Kruse's book.
>
> I am 42 years old, former vegan (strictly for 10 years ) and dealing with some small issues (hypothyroidism/hashi?, infertility, myopia, blackouts, anxiety disorder...).
>
>
>
> I am living in Germany where the sun rises at the moment at 7:44 and sets at 17:30 PM and I am trying to get as much time outside as possible, which is not easy with 9 hours in an office in front of the screen (I will change that soon).
>
> ......
>
> If it actually is to prefer to get some good sunlight at a distant place, no matter what season is in your current place, no matter how well you are adapted and your ancestors lived here for a long time, does that mean, you can never get optimal in a place that is not the place where the species evolved? Would people from Greenland benefit from a 1 week trip to Mexico?
>
> Just trying to figure out if I will move or adapt and meanwhile should take every opportunity to get to places with more sunlight.

Welcome ! I will stick my neck out here and say it is always beneficial to move. A one week holiday is great but if your dopamine levels are in the tank on arriving back you will have a slump as your body will rebel for depriving it again. So just like apres ski you will need to have an apres UV plan in place, tanning beds,black lights etc.

Moving : moving does not necessarily mean moving to a Caribbean beach and taking up spearfishing. Moving may mean moving out of a densely populated area to a place with one family houses where there are not that many WiFi systems looking in on you.
Especially important if you are living on the 10 th floor of a high rise building.

From your comment that you are 9 hrs in front of a screen I assume that someone else is the owner of your time. If you have the means it would be a good move to shed those shackles.

Moving from Germany: I would ( in fact I did but not for Krusian reasons) If you are in the North I would consider moving south as a first step. Have a look at the UV map of Germany. Then look at the French Riviera. How is your French?

Further flung places : explore this once you have soaked up some UV in other places.

Great strides on the Lepton reset. Congrats ! One observation do you get sun or being winter time outside? UV A is present year round.

### Queen of Elves — 2016-02-09T15:37:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaving-the-environment-you-are-adapted-to.17515/post-188608>

Thank you for the kind welcome and things to consider, caroline and Lahelada!

To throw in more details: I am in South Germany, 70 kilometers to the south of Frankfurt. My working place is 30 kilometers from Frankfurt in a city with 150.000 residents.

I live in small village and will be moving to a quite smaller one in 1-2 month. This new place isn't even a compact village but single farms left and right from the street. My future home is an old farmhouse from 1786 with much ground around it, where I will be keeping my own cows, sheep, pigs, chickens, and bees
There is even a small creek with crystal clear, cold water, which I can use to fill my CT tub. The house has rooms without central heating, two weeks ago, there were frost pattern inside on the windows!

The only thing I am concerned about is a pole for the telephone connection of my house and the neighbor's, there is no underground supply yet, but anyway I'm not sure what is worse.
I am trying to convince my boyfriend to not use WiFi anymore, but I think this will be not easy for him.
And getting fresh seafood is a problem here, but not impossible... (mentioned that I'm iodine deficient and can't get my levels up?)

The next step will be changing my working hours, because they are flexible, but here comes my next question: **What is more important, getting AM sun or darkness after sunset?** In the summer it's no problem, but in the winter sun is rising at 8:30 AM an setting at 16:30, so that I can't get my whole working day in between. By starting work later, after sunrise, I would have to eat dinner at the office (if I even eat dinner anymore next year) working till 7 PM in artificial light and being at home at 8 PM.

In the long term I plan to do my job part-time besides working on my farm. I don't need much stuff anymore, just enough money to explore some new places for the next chapter.

Lahelada: Unfortunately I don't speak French, but that sound good. Although I'm totally drawn to the Atlantic, Portugal perhaps, because I want to surf! Okay, I'm 42 and didn't even try it already, but funnily enough I am craving it so much....

### Lahelada — 2016-02-09T16:22:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaving-the-environment-you-are-adapted-to.17515/post-188612>

It sounds lovely where you are moving to. I grew up in Mannheim so probably know the area you are moving to.
An old-fashioned telephone pole I would not worry about all that much.
Portugal would be a great place. Worth pursuing if it calls you that strongly.

My gut tells me that am light would be important for you as this is what sets up your hormone cascade for the day. Arriving home at 8 pm to a place like you describe is a vast improvement to where you are now. Think about Blue blockers,installing f lux on all your computers,handhelds and what not. You will also have to look into the hot topic of the moment - supplemental light (red/uva and maybe IR)

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-09T16:51:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaving-the-environment-you-are-adapted-to.17515/post-188613>

### jenaf — 2016-02-09T17:58:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaving-the-environment-you-are-adapted-to.17515/post-188625>

Welcome!

I also changed my work hours to get morning sun, then I use the blueblocker glasses to protect my eyes in the dark evenings here in Canada (similar hrs of light in the winter). For me it works very well and I think reversing it would hinder me.

### nonchalant — 2016-02-09T18:44:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaving-the-environment-you-are-adapted-to.17515/post-188628>

Welcome Queen! I think early morning sunshine is most important, because you can do things to mitigate light at night, as Lahelada and jenaf (and Jack) said. No one can duplicate sunshine with artificial lighting or even through a window. The effect of that sunshine on the hormones that are made in early morning is so important.

### Cpt.Tired — 2016-02-10T06:15:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leaving-the-environment-you-are-adapted-to.17515/post-188675>

> Welcome!
>
> I also changed my work hours to get morning sun, then I use the blueblocker glasses to protect my eyes in the dark evenings here in Canada (similar hrs of light in the winter). For me it works very well and I think reversing it would hinder me.

I am considering doing the same.
I could take the afternoon shift at my work (2:30pm - 10pm) so I can get good sun in the AM.
I could even do ocean CT at Belcarra!
I just don't like ending my day with work. I am really starting to hate the indoor 8 hour work day now that I know how terrible it is for us (blue light, wifi, pollution and a lunch room with five microwaves). But maybe I won't mind as much if I get an improved redox and a rockin' vitamin D level.

I just wish that the neighbour's dog would stop barking at me every time I step in the backyard!!

### Queen of Elves — 2016-02-10T08:50:12+0200

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=188612)

> An old-fashioned telephone pole I would not worry about all that much.

Arriving home at 8 pm to a place like you describe is a vast improvement to where you are now. Think about Blue blockers,installing f lux on all your computers,handhelds and what not. You will also have to look into the hot topic of the moment - supplemental light (red/uva and maybe IR)

Lahelada: How funny! I know Mannheim, I grew up near Darmstadt. I'm glad about your thoughts about the telephone pole and my working hours. Seems absolutely doable now! I already have orange goggles to wear at home after sunset and a red headlamp for reading (my boyfriend makes fun of me, but I will stand it..). I want to get glasses which I can wear at work. ZEISS manufactures glasses with a blue protection covering in the range of 380 to 455 nm that are clear - would be perfect to wear in public. I still can't make a decision whether to get distance lenses with blue filter or just with the blue filter. I'm shortsighted and trying to leave glasses off, whenever possible. But since BlueProtect covering is costly, I consider getting glasses to wear at my working place (no distance lenses needed) and when driving at night (distance lenses absolutely needed). Besides blocking the eyes from natural sunlight, is it harmful to wear distance lenses or rather is wearing distance lenses inside a room or car harmful to the eyes?

@jenaf&nonchalant: Thank your for your advice with morning sun, that encourages me to change my routine!
Jenaf, may I ask you, when you go to bed at winter times? Around the shortest days in December I tried to get up with sunrise at 8:30 am. With the recommended 7,5 - 8 hours sleep, I went to bed at 1 am - isn't that too late? And I didn't know what to do in all those dark hours, doing the dishes and cleaning the house with my headlamp felt a bit weird

@[Cpt.Tired](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/12957/): I feel the same with working indoor and will try everything to change that! There is a small chance, that I could do part of my work at Home-Office, perhaps 2-3 days per week. That would mean less nnEMF around me, less stress driving to work and finding a parking place and being able to take more breaks outside or even working outside!

### Allanah — 2016-02-09T20:30:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluoridated-water-better-than-nothing.17517/post-188644>

Good morning everyone! I have a particular situation I need help with today..
I live in South Dakota, USA. (It's freezing out right now here!) Anyway, I live in two different places, one during the week (Monday-Friday) and one during the weekend. At my place during the week I have access to RO water at all times, and I drink up to 2 gallons daily. There is no issue here. However, where I live on Saturday and Sunday I have only access to tap water, which I'm sure is fluoridated.
My question is this: On those two days should I drink the fluoridated water at all? Is it better than no water? Or should I drink only a little, etc.? Thanks again!

### shah78 — 2016-02-09T20:44:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fluoridated-water-better-than-nothing.17517/post-188647>

Biohacking tip #722: taking oral melatonin might be a real bad idea. It could lead to alcoholism of another serious gut issue. Why? Oral melatonin is an inhibitor of retinal dopamine production. If you use it for sleep you might be lowering ocular dopamine and frontal lobe dopamine levels leading to circadian disorders may affect cone functioning. What else? Bio hack the impact of oral melatonin usage on your cone response and the regeneration of your retina using electroretinography (ERG). You might be shocked at the results.

It turns out the reason your retina has more DHA than the brain dose per gram is because of how ocular melatonin regenerates rods and cones. It is the hormone that controls the process.

Oxidative stress is involved in activating photoreceptor death in several retinal degeneration experimental set up.
Growth is necessary in the eye for wellness, but it is always stimulated by damage (ROS) and limited by regeneration. So how does the eye accomplish this?

Docosahexaenoic acid (DHA), the major polyunsaturated fatty acid in the retina. It protects cultured retina photoreceptors from apoptosis induced by oxidative stress and promotes photoreceptor differentiation. It works with dopamine to accomplish this task. So why does the retina have more DHA? It is the one part of the human that has ability to make DHA from EPA. Investigations have shown that eicosapentaenoic acid (EPA) is the booster to making more DHA in a complex photochemical dance using the side chains of tyrosine and UV light. EPA is a metabolic precursor to DHA and it has been shown to have an effect on retinal neurons so that they could activate Δ6 desaturase (FADS2), which introduces the last double bond in DHA biosynthetic pathway to metabolize EPA to DHA. In the retina, because of melatonin and dopamine EPA is able to promote photoreceptor differentiation and rescued photoreceptors from oxidative stress-induced apoptosis from light through its elongation and desaturation to DHA. Experiments have now shown in humans for the first time, that isolated retinal neurons can synthesize DHA in culture.

This increase of DHA is important in running the SCN faster than all other circadian clocks.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetism-and-light-a-uv-ir-story.17523/post-188687>

Cold definitely aids in superconductivity. However, superconductivity at room temperature continues to elude many researchers. Might the missing ingredient be light? Yes it does, but how?

Light effects electrons and electron physics are linked to magnetism. Electron at rest generates electric field. Electrons that are moving without acceleration produces electric and magnetic field. Electrons moving with acceleration produces electromagnetic waves. So electrons in the an atomic lattice like the cell membrane with DHA pi electron cloud are stable.........but when light hits them using the photoelectric effect they begin to move without acceleration (Fermat's Principle)

Air, light and sun baths in the treatment of chronic complaints

by [Monteuuis, A. (Albert), 1861-](https://archive.org/search.php?query=creator%3A%22Monteuuis%2C+A.+%28Albert%29%2C+1861-%22); [Rothwell, F. # Thread: Impact of oral melatonin on the electroretinogram cone response
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impact-of-oral-melatonin-on-the-electroretinogram-cone-response.17531/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Penny — 2016-02-10T23:06:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/impact-of-oral-melatonin-on-the-electroretinogram-cone-response.17531/post-188719>

But what if it is administered at night when you are supposed to make melatonin? The next day, are your cones photoreceptive again? I wonder how long the effect lasts?

I stopped taking it a couple months ago - but if I have trouble sleeping, I wonder if lack of autophagy due to lack of sleep is worse than this cone effect and by taking an exogenous mel, you clean up cells -

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2785757/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2785757/)

**Abstract**
**Background**
In the eye, melatonin plays a role in promoting light sensitivity at night and modulating many aspects of circadian retinal physiology. It is also an inhibitor of retinal dopamine, which is a promoter of day vision through the cone system. Consequently, it is possible that oral melatonin (an inhibitor of retinal dopamine) taken to alleviate circadian disorders may affect cone functioning. Our aim was to assess the impact of melatonin on the cone response of the human retina using electroretinography (ERG).

**Methods**
Twelve healthy participants aged between 18 to 52 years old were submitted to a placebo-controlled, double-blind, crossover, and counterbalanced-order design. The subjects were tested on 2 sessions beginning first with a baseline ERG, followed by the administration of the placebo or melatonin condition and then, 30 min later, a second ERG to test the effect.

**Results**
Following oral melatonin administration, a significant decrease of about 8% of the cone maximal response was observed (mean 6.9 μV ± SEM 2.0; P = 0.0065) along with a prolonged b-wave implicit time of 0.4 ms ± 0.1, 50 minutes after ingestion.

**Conclusion**
Oral melatonin appears to reach the eye through the circulation. When it is administered at a time of day when it is not usually present, melatonin appears to reduce input to retinal cones. We believe that the impact of melatonin on retinal function should be taken into consideration when used without supervision in chronic self-medication for sleep or circadian disorder treatment.

# Thread: On the grueling path to Optimal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-grueling-path-to-optimal.17535/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PathFinder — 2016-02-11T08:59:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-grueling-path-to-optimal.17535/post-188751>

I hope to write journal as my ability to communicate allows. Communication, thinking, decision making and focus are uphill tasks and the key issues along with sleep. Sometime it is even impossible to write a single line

I have great hope from this forum (in fact this is the only forum which discusses and have possible explanation for my illness). Reading the forum/website gives me strong clues about my problem, Problem is lack of roadmap My idea is to put as much info as possible

Background:

53 yrs old male. Since early age worked in chemical industry and shift work that triggered this disease. Problem persists since teens and early 20’s. Slender built weight 130lbs. Gallbladder taken out 12 yrs ago.

Current physical and mental health, status

Insomnia: Sleep fall Ok, not too much effort-However In a typical night when sleep starts at 10:30p then wake up at 1:50a-3:30a and 4:30am to empty bladder. So I sleep 3,4 and 5 hrs depending upon number of times I wake up. It is difficult to go back to sleep at 4:30a, wakeup feeling is like a switch is flipped on. And sleep is shallow for most part. Hands are always cold even before since when this insomnia and adrenal burned out didn’t set in. Muscle injury doesn’t heal at all. I have damaged muscle from weight training for more than year even when I have abandoned it completely. Food is like an 800 lb gorilla to deal with, Current food: I am on low grains diet. Take small amount of rice or wheat twice a week. My staple is black chickpeas with protein (eggs, mutton, beef, chicken and fish). Take some butter fats and food is cooked in industrial seed oil. No processed food and minimum to moderate sugar intake

Past EMF status, and professions

Worked in chemical industry few years and then in heavy duty IT job for many years. Not currently in IT or blue light dominated field except wifi and white light at home. So EMF and lack of sunlight and abundance might be contributor to my illness but not a causative one. Infact it has a strong genetic component. Some at atleast couple of close members in family arrived at same destination (same ballpark) by taking totally different route.

Past meds

Took SSRI 16-20 yrs ago that help in sleep and mood. Because of side effects strongly dislike ‘em. Taking bromazepam 1.5mg almost daily. But I believe bromazepam and other Benzos are mentally paralyzing.

Past labs

4 years back Candida IgG(29.9) Ref +ve >11, Vit D(37) Ref, DHEA-S(328 ug/dl) Ref 80-560: B-12 serum (479.6 pg/ml) Ref 189-883

**What fails sleep**

Wheat and rice both help and disturb sleep. Carbs help in sleep onset but play a key role in disturbing continuous and restoring sleep. It seems food opens the Pandora box as soon as it is ingested.

GFCF diet helps in clearing burden off the mind. Grain free diet is even better for mind. However it fails to improve the sleep and severe weight loss is show stopper.

Fermented food specially sour disturb sleep.

**Whats helps in Sleep**

These things help in sleep not in any particular order. It is not possible to sustain and maintain good sleep.

Herbals:

Herbal concoctions of Ayurvedic help in sleep and mood. However it is very difficult to sustain the benefits for long or attain substantial improvement.

Antifugals help in insomnia. I had a course of difflucan for three months four years back along with power doze of multi Vitamin and my sleep noticeably improved.

saccharomyces boulardii also help in calming mood down and slightly improves sleep.

Probiotics (mixed 12 strains of Bifido and Lacto bacteria) also help in sleep.

Vitamin D/C

4000 iu daily helps in sleep before effects tapering off completely in 10-14 days.

Intense Fasting: 15 hr fasts for a month or longer improved sleep few years back. That improvement quickly tapered off(15-30 days) when switched to regular 3 meals or 2 meals with eating fruits, tea etc in between, These were conditions for fast to be helpful for sleep.

_Must feel ravenously hungry at middle/tail end of the fast. Ie; Not to overeat before start of fast._

_Eat lots, of sweets when breaking fast and ideally workout after so that dinner doesn’t becomes detrimental to sleep. Min 3hrs between dinner and sleep._

_Eating grains/legumes/lentil at dinner also helps. But it doesn’t matter whether you eat meat, rice, chickpeas, wheat bread and fruits. Sleep comes naturally._

Regular fasting also helps. Feel better at the tail end of 8-9hr fasts. Must eat sweet sugary food at the end of dinner. Problem with Fasting and no-grain diet it acts as two edged sword-results in T3 demise and subsequent destruction of testosterone.

HIIT and wheat

Interestingly wheat (sourdough bread) helps in sleep in combination with HIIT or weight resistance exercise and some probiotics etc. However effect of opiode, peptide from gluten and casein adversely affects focus n mood.

**Vitamins**

Multi B+ vitamins trash sleep- B-12 injections/B-12 tabs initially help but soon degrade sleep. However endogenous B-12 produced by e-coli/salmonella bacteria when stomach is upset improves focus, cognition and mood tremendously. B-12 link is my hunch nonetheless very strong one and repeatable.

Problem is of roadmap. As noted there are dozens of routes and interventions which help in sleep once in a while. This also raises hope that “sleep inducing pathways” might be damaged but not severed. But for last few decades I am lost and running in circles. I am positive it is circadian cycle dysfunction and leaky gut with adrenal fatigue but mounting a coordinated defense against it out of my hands. Without knowing the gist of each route and underlying chemistry and holistically addressing issues, this is an exercise in futility.

### caroline — 2016-02-12T01:57:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/on-the-grueling-path-to-optimal.17535/post-188813>

Welcome.....

Do you have Dr. K's book? The Epi- Paleo RX ......It is the first couple of years of his blog ....and much easier to get they!

Do you listen to JK's podcasts ? These are a great way to get fast info and mostly in terms we understand!

If you can manage it .....join as a "gold" member. There is a monthly webinar and a follow up live Q&A where you can ask Jack anything.

> Background:
>
>
> 53 yrs old male. Since early age worked in chemical industry and shift work that triggered this disease. Problem persists since teens and early 20’s. Slender built weight 130lbs. Gallbladder taken out 12 yrs ago.
>

>

>
> Insomnia: Sleep fall Ok, not too much effort-However In a typical night when sleep starts at 10:30p then wake up at 1:50a-3:30a and 4:30am to empty bladder. So I sleep 3,4 and 5 hrs depending upon number of times I wake up. It is difficult to go back to sleep at 4:30a, wakeup feeling is like a switch is flipped on. And sleep is shallow for most part. Hands are always cold even before since when this insomnia and adrenal burned out didn’t set in. Muscle injury doesn’t heal at all. I have damaged muscle from weight training for more than year even when I have abandoned it completely. Food is like an 800 lb gorilla to deal with, Current food: I am on low grains diet. No processed food and minimum to moderate sugar intake

# Thread: The Heart as a Bi-Directional Scalar Field Antennae
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-heart-as-a-bi-directional-scalar-field-antennae.17538/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx

### Psyche — 2016-02-11T17:49:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-heart-as-a-bi-directional-scalar-field-antennae.17538/post-188772>

This amazing paper is dedicated to the memory of Francisco Torrent-Guasp (1931-2005).
[http://www.omicsonline.com/open-acc...field-antenna-2090-8369-1000121.php?aid=60866](http://www.omicsonline.com/open-access/the-heart-as-a-bidirectional-scalar-field-antenna-2090-8369-1000121.php?aid=60866)

> _Conventional thinking is that the brain is the seat of consciousness and memory. Evidence summarized here opens the possibility that as a scalar field antenna the heart might be able to tap into so called “empty” space, which appears to contain structure and energy and information in coherent holographic form \[[30](http://www.omicsonline.com/open-access/the-heart-as-a-bidirectional-scalar-field-antenna-2090-8369-1000121.php?aid=60866#30)\-[32](http://www.omicsonline.com/open-access/the-heart-as-a-bidirectional-scalar-field-antenna-2090-8369-1000121.php?aid=60866#32)\].Many modern thinkers are drifting away from purely mechanistic explanations of how the universe and those within it function, as well as the nature of consciousness_.

> _Further research by McCraty et al. led to extraordinary and compelling evidence that **the heart’s energy field is coupled to a field of information that is not bound by the limits of time and space.** Rigorous experimental studies demonstrated that the heart and brain receive and process information about an event before the event actually happens \[[19](http://www.omicsonline.com/open-access/the-heart-as-a-bidirectional-scalar-field-antenna-2090-8369-1000121.php?aid=60866#19),[20](http://www.omicsonline.com/open-access/the-heart-as-a-bidirectional-scalar-field-antenna-2090-8369-1000121.php?aid=60866#20)\]. Moreover, the physiological data showed that the heart appears to receive “intuitive” information a few seconds before the brain. The researchers suggested that the heart is directly coupled to an ambient subtle energetic field of information that surrounds the body, which, in turn, is entangled and interacts with the multiplicity of energy fields in which the body is embedded, presumably including fields present in the quantum vacuum_.

### Psyche — 2016-02-11T17:53:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-heart-as-a-bi-directional-scalar-field-antennae.17538/post-188773>

[http://www.ecocardionet.com/entrevista.pdf](http://www.ecocardionet.com/entrevista.pdf)

The paradigmatic change in ventricular anatomy and function prompts research in every field of cardiology. In the words of Dr. James L. Cox, ‘‘It is such a dramatic change from the understanding of the heart’s physiology that we can almost go back and start repeating the experiments of the last 50 years with this new knowledge and see how we interpret these experiments now."

# Thread: Traveling Tips.. India
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/traveling-tips-india.17539/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Allanah — 2016-02-11T20:49:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/traveling-tips-india.17539/post-188791>

Hello! I need some advice! I am a 15-year old girl currently doing the leptin reset among other things. I may have the opportunity to travel to India this summer through an exchange program, so my question(s) relate to food. I have been doing some research and they eat a lot of rice, legumes, nightshades, etc. They don't tend to eat much beef or pork which is fine and in some places they eat seafood. When they make dishes (a sweet one for example) they use things like molasses, etc., not sugar. They also eat a lot of fresh yogurt. When on this trip, if I am able to go, should I try to follow every rule I can? Or since it is not the same processed garbage we have here is it okay? Should I do something in-between? Is the yogurt actually healthy in this case? Or is it likely made from bad milk too? Ultimately I'll make my own choice but any input is greatly appreciated!

### Lahelada — 2016-02-11T22:21:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/traveling-tips-india.17539/post-188798>

I would live the experience to the full, food included.

Depending where you travel you are very close to the equator so a higher carb diet would not be a circadian mismatch.
Mumbai to Equator 1300 miles
Chennai 905 miles
Delhi 2000 miles

For comparison New Orleans is 2100 miles away,North Dakota 3200 etc
What does this have to do with anything?
The closer you are to the equator the more carbs your body can handle but step off the gas on fats and brake proteins a little. The yogurt should be fine as due to the tropical conditions the cattle breeds are different and the casein issue goes away. Just bear in mind normal tropical hygiene rules.
HOWEVER,if you get little time outside all bets regarding carb restriction are on again. In order to live like the locals you must spend a lot of time outdoors if you can. I personally would avoid the sweets.They are very very sweet and I think the info about Molasses is now outdated but I could be wrong.
Have you ever stuck a hot wet spoon into sugar and the sugar congealed on it ? That is the sweetness level you can expect. Indian ice cream I would eat on the rare occasion.

### Just Breathe — 2016-02-11T23:19:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/traveling-tips-india.17539/post-188806>

Do your best to enjoy the food. There is nothing like traveling to a place and experiencing it fully. I feel like the US has the most compromised food system with GMO's and other poisons in it. Whenever I travel elsewhere, even if I'm breaking the rules, I feel fine!
Having said that, I worry about gut health more than the food when I travel. I take emergen-c packets and put a few drops of biocidin in my daily emergen-c glass and I'm good to go. Enjoy your time out there.

### Allanah — 2016-02-13T06:09:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/traveling-tips-india.17539/post-188931>

Thanks to both of you! Those were all the same things I thought but I'm glad to know I wasn't wrong. Thank you!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ideal-job.17541/post-188810>

### thomas — 2016-02-12T13:52:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ideal-job.17541/post-188842>

For me if we're talking about a "job" as in something you do just for the financial motivation, it's the one that can get ENOUGH money in as little time/effort/mental-space as possible. There are a lot of ways to do that. personaly I just sell online infoproducts.. read the 4 hour workweek then APPLY(what 99% of readers won't do).
For me money is just a problem to solve, allowing you to concentrate on living your life.(doing things you actually want to do/exciting, going to places, meeting people/having a social life/family/friends, learning cool ideas/skills, improving your health etc...).
Ideally yeah you make money doing things you want to do and that fulfill you, and that's integrated in your lifestyle. For me personally it's just simpler to deal with the problem of money separatly, allowing me to focus on the other stuff.

### Karl K — 2016-02-12T13:55:36+0200

Almost everything outside is better, but often worse paid.

The best job is foraging food from our environment because it's plentiful, and everything has been provided for us to live perfectly, perfect water, air, environment... Oh it's 2016, maybe a few thousand years too late. Bedroom box to car box to office box to supermarket box to bedroom box.

In lieu of getting a job that fits, trying to work park time and getting outside at other times is probably a good idea.

# Thread: Light Magnets and Water???
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-magnets-and-water.17542/>
**Subforum:** Redox Rx
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Sean Shick — 2016-02-12T02:27:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-magnets-and-water.17542/post-188817>

Im a farmer, and I do my best to follow Jack's blog, but frankly I do feel like I get lost in the weeds. Lets face it, this stuff is complicated. Jack's recent push on light and the wearable device got me thinking about if there was some kind of application for this tech in Ag. So I proceeded to search out the idea a bit and found quite a few products out there that utilize magnets, and a few that incorporate light in some manner. However, there seems to be very little explanation/proof of these devices working. Im wondering if this lack of information/evidence stems from a general lack of study in this area, or if it just not applicable.

I don't really have a firm enough grasp on all these topics yet to actually come up with some kind of premise of mode of action.

So I guess what Im asking is...Would applying a magnetic field to water yield some kind of beneficial results for Ag? How about light? UV?

### Dia — 2016-02-12T02:43:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-magnets-and-water.17542/post-188818>

I don't know if there is more here on Ag, but take a look at the thread below. Don't know how much help it will be for you

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...ism-results-are-astounding.13964/#post-161760](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/using-jk-ideas-in-agriculture-i-e-h20-magnetism-results-are-astounding.13964/#post-161760)

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-12T03:15:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-magnets-and-water.17542/post-188823>

Ag is all about light water and magnetism........
Cold definitely aids in superconductivity. However, superconductivity at room temperature continues to elude many researchers. Might the missing ingredient be light? Yes it does, but how?

Light effects electrons and electron physics are linked to magnetism. Electron at rest generates electric field. Electrons that are moving without acceleration produces electric and magnetic field. Electrons moving with acceleration produces electromagnetic waves. So electrons in the an atomic lattice like the cell membrane with DHA pi electron cloud are stable.........but when light hits them using the photoelectric effect they begin to move without acceleration (Fermat's Principle)

[http://www.mpg.de/9949877/superconductivity-fullerenes-light-induced](http://www.mpg.de/9949877/superconductivity-fullerenes-light-induced)

So if you read this........how could mammals have taken advantage of this effect? DHA diets.......from the marine chain. Next subject to be posted here today will begin to deconstruct the paleo solution as a meme built by bad data.

### Linz — 2016-02-12T16:38:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-magnets-and-water.17542/post-188855>

Read Hidden Nature: The startling insights of Victor Schauberger. Fascinating book with lots of information about water behaviour and soil mineral movements in nature and how to use and enhance. Very easy to read too.
Lots of practical advice in biodynamic agriculture manuals as well - and everything here helps to interpret what is really happening.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-12T17:47:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-magnets-and-water.17542/post-188866>

Viktor Schauberger found in water what modern day physicists are finding out about graphene. What does this finding imply? That low energy and high energy physics operate differently. The low end is quantum based but the high end is relativity based. That is all carry on.

### nonchalant — 2016-02-12T20:09:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/light-magnets-and-water.17542/post-188873>

Water in an underground reservoir or a deep pond would pick up magnetism from the earth. Animals would do best when in constant contact with the earth (and sun).

# Thread: EMF Analog Signal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-analog-signal.17544/>

Hey all,
We moved recently, worked with Michael Neuert on fixing this house with the correct wiring.

Our Magentic readings at the old place were between 1.50 and 3. Now they are .06. PERFECT. We have cutoff switches to cut power to the room at night, all seems good.

Our EMF reading great. No cell phones, no WIFI, etc. However, the house of course has steel wrapped wiring, so the North side of the home all rooms (on 2 acres) as I get close to that wall will pick up a signal, EMF about .015 uW/cm2. I was told to not hold the tester and place it on some wood or cardboard and check, reading was then fine. He said this is an Analog signal. Not Digital. He said this can happen.

However, my wife is experiencing extreme joint pain (worst she has ever had) at night. Now she has major join issues, they are literally all stuck, long story there.

Can the analog signal still cause issues. Moving to the new home amazingly her migraines were cut in half within a week of leaving that electrical nightmare of a home. I am just worried that analog signal may be hurting her. Is there a way anyone knows to reduce it? Lots of harmonizer products out there, anyone with experience here?

Thanks all...
Kevin

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/zika-virus-has-a-lesson-for-us-are-you-listening.17545/post-189235>

A group of Argentine physicians claim that the sudden microcephaly outbreak in Brazil was not caused by the Zika virus, but by a larvicide injected into the country's water supplies.

[http://www.techtimes.com/articles/1...-of-brazils-microcephaly-outbreak-doctors.htm](http://www.techtimes.com/articles/133548/20160214/monsanto-larvicide-not-zika-virus-true-cause-of-brazils-microcephaly-outbreak-doctors.htm)

> Pregnant women all over the world have been advised to take caution. The Zika virus infection has been linked to newborn babies with the birth defect microcephaly. This is a congenital condition in which babies are born with unusually tiny heads.
>
> The notion, however, has recently been challenged by a group of Argentine physicians. The group suspects that the Zika virus is not to blame for the rise in microcephaly cases, but that a toxic larvicide introduced into Brazil's water supplies may be the real culprit.
>
> **Not A Coincidence?**
>
> According to the Physicians in Crop-Sprayed Towns (PCST), a chemical larvicide that produces malformations in mosquitoes was injected into Brazil's water supplies in 2014 in order to stop the development of mosquito larvae in drinking water tanks.
>
> The chemical, which is known as Pyriproxyfen, was used in a massive government-run program tasked to control the mosquito population in the country. Pyriproxyfen is a larvicide manufactured by Sumitomo Chemical, a company [associated](http://www.sumitomo-chem.co.jp/english/newsreleases/docs/20141209e.pdf) \[PDF\] with Monsanto. However, PCST has referred to Sumitomo as a subsidiary of Monsanto.
>
> "Malformations detected in thousands of children from pregnant women living in areas where the Brazilian state added pyriproxyfen to drinking water is not a coincidence," the PCST [wrote](http://www.reduas.com.ar/wp-content/uploads/downloads/2016/02/Informe-Zika-de-Reduas_TRAD.pdf) \[pdf\] in the report.

### Scompy — 2016-02-12T20:34:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandoras-box-of-vanity.17547/post-188875>

Okay, I'm going to put this out here. I've recently been confronting society's **_value of vanity_** (f-ing VANITY) trumping health on Facebook this week! This pisses me off to no end because how could anyone choose vanity over health, because it's so incredibly obvious having better health promotes a better-looking person! This is often lost on the masses in 2016. I lament. Vanity, selfies and the like have become a sad truth about the sheeple we must live among.

Our beloved dermitology profession has promoted the idea that **wrinkles** are caused by sunlight exposure: the myth of the idea that we will turn into leathery-prunes if sunlight is shed on own skin. Plus, the sunburn **cancer myth**. I would argue these two pieces of conventional wisdom synergies are at the root of it all.

On FB, I've been arguing points with them about the grand picture of their overall health, knowing their redox, and understanding the mechanisms involved with promoting the preservation and destruction of collagen, their melatonin relating to re-zipping collagen and pointing out what happens when a person has a lack of fats in their diet. **Time #8 really helps to provide ammunition.**

I say use their vanity against them!

1\. I want to find people that have **avoided sun** for decades that are now wrinkled-prunes!
2\. I want to find people that have **embraced the sun** and look visually amazing at a later age.

Having the reverse images shown in comparison has got to be a good tactic to change society back into a realm of reason about sunlight...lack of sunlight leads to more wrinkles and more cancer.

The saddest part of my FB conversations is that evidence appears totally lost on people that have a warped belief about sunlight. Lack of dopamine in their lives, no doubt!

### Scompy — 2016-02-12T22:08:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandoras-box-of-vanity.17547/post-188883>

_Time 8 hit me over the head with this:

"What does full spectrum sunlight destroy naturally? UVA and B light naturally lowers adrenalin (the stress hormone of the sympathetic nervous system) while its photons re-zip collagen that cortisol release causes at 4AM that wakes us up by allowing water flows to occur between glial cells and neurons via the aquaporin 4 gates in the brain. AM sunrise light has no UVA or UVB present initially. It has blue, green, and red present. It has more blue than any other part of the spectrum and this is stimulatory to waking us up. Naturally, UVA light shows up later in the AM, depending upon your location in latitude, and this frequency of UVA light acts to begin to re-zip the collagen in our skin and eyes. That initial blue light stimulus from the sun’s rise is used to unwind our collagen to increase water flows to stretch the interspaces in neurons to wake our body and mind up at dawn from sleep. The aquaporin 4 gates are what are destroyed in Multiple sclerosis, which is another autoimmune condition.

When we are missing UV and/or IR light for any reason, these photoelectric and photochemical are not made. When full spectrum sunlight is absent in someone who is chronically stressed for any reason, sleep cannot be induced because the ocular melatonin cycle requires that these two frequencies of light be present to stimulate the regeneration processes in the eye during daytime. If you think about your childhood, when you spent the day at the park of the beach, you might remember how easy it was to fall asleep and get a sunburn. The reason is simple, sunlight induces sleep because the regeneration pathways that use melanopsin need daylight to regenerate. When you did fall asleep, the redness of your skin did not come from the thermal burn, but it was from the increased blood flow due to the release of nitric oxide that acted to bring the arterioles of the dermis layers to the surface. This is a photochemical change induced by sunlight to allow the skin to absorb the UVA and UVB light at the surface. UVA and UVB light does not penetrate deep. TO absorb the UV light we need the circulatory system to come from the dermis and engorged the arterioles with RBC’s. The RBC’s are filled with hemoglobin and porphyrins that absorb both UV and IR frequencies. The sunburn is really an absorption of too much thermal IR energy. Deep sunburns can result from several factors: excessive sun, or thin skin, thick skin, or a poor adaptation to seasonal light due to chronic use of UV blocking makeup, clothing, or sunblock use in strong light cycles."_

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-13T01:40:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandoras-box-of-vanity.17547/post-188908>

WHY MUST YOU GET THIS?

The near infrared portion of natural sunlight is clearly beneficial for the skin and has been exhaustively worked out in the literature. No reads it.
[https://saunaspace.com/near-infrared-saunas-best-thing-for-your-skin/](https://saunaspace.com/near-infrared-saunas-best-thing-for-your-skin/)
The sheer number of Low Level Light Therapy (LLLT) studies (all non-ablative, i.e. low-power) is staggering if you look in pub med. Interestingly, almost all these studies are using near infrared light (~ 600-1000 nm wavelengths) to heal the skin and the body.
This article posted today shows how near infrared light, the photobiologically active portion of the sunlight, positively impacts skin health and skin conditions. [https://saunaspace.com/near-infrared-saunas-best-thing-for-your-skin/](https://saunaspace.com/near-infrared-saunas-best-thing-for-your-skin/) So why did I post this here this AM. You need to understand how light and its momentum or lack of momentum works in your body. Where light hits our surfaces has many effects. These effects work by a sliding scale that is capable of going up and down based upon the redox of cells where light hits the surface. So if your redox is poor light cannot and won't do what you expect. That light either pushes or pulls cell membranes loaded with DHA. Can light push things like cell membranes? Could this result in phonons and solitons that get transmuted by the tensegrity system in a cell? You bet your ass it does. It has long been known that light carries both energy and momentum, but no one seems to know how momentum and energy get turned into quantum information for the cell. The momentum of light has not been so easy to describe for physics. New experimental results now suggest that the answer depends on whether or not the light can put fluid into motion: Since all cell membranes are fluid in humans this begins to make sense. If light can move a cell membrane, light slows and its momentum decreases and it exerts a pushing force on a cell membrane; This is how Vitamin D3 is made with the interaction of UVB light. Note that if the skin redox is poor you won't be able to make any D3 even in full spectrum sunlight. This should be an eye opener to people. It should help MD's realize why some people like the obese need whopping doses of D3 to get their level north. It is also why some people will need K2 with their D3 to get success. The darker one's skin is also is another factor in how the momentum of light works. Dark skinned people need more not less sun. Same is true with the obese and this with skin conditions like psoriasis. Clearly Vitamin D3 needs a lot of momentum to be made and this is why the most powerful frequency of light is the only frequency capable of making it. What happens if you have no UV light? Say you live indoors under an LED? What if you live in the UK? Light cannot move the cell membranes as well and lights momentum increases more and speeds up into a tissue and it exerts a pulling force on the lattice around it. This is similar to what a bullet tract does when you get shot by a gun. No bueno. If you live with a poor redox disease or in a poor quantum yield area you need sunlight and sauna. Consider a move and a change of behavior.

Here is the link to the physics research. [http://phys.org/news/2015-06-physicists-pressure.html](http://phys.org/news/2015-06-physicists-pressure.html)

### Valerie — 2016-02-14T17:41:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandoras-box-of-vanity.17547/post-189014>

**Peroxidized cholesterol-induced matrix metalloproteinase-9 activation and its suppression by dietary β-carotene in photoaging of hairless mouse skin**

**Abstract**
The activation of matrix metalloproteinase (MMP)-9 leading to the formation of wrinkle and sagging of skin is an essential step in the skin photoaging on exposure to ultraviolet A (UVA). This study attempted to elucidate the role of peroxidized cholesterol including cholesterol hydroperoxides (Chol-OOHs), primary products of lipid peroxidation in biomembranes, in MMP-9 activation and the effect of dietary β-carotene in MMP-9 activation. Hairless mice were subjected to periodic UVA irradiation for 8 weeks. The amount of peroxidized cholesterol detected as total hydroxycholesterol in the skin was increased significantly by the exposure. The activity and protein level of MMP-9 were elevated with wrinkling and sagging formation. MMP-9 activity was also enhanced by the intracutaneous injection of Chol-OOHs into the mouse skin. Adding β-carotene to the diet of the mice during the period of irradiation suppressed the activity and expression of MMP-9 as well as the wrinkling and sagging formation. The amount of cholesterol 5α-hydroperoxide, a singlet molecular oxygen oxygenation-specific peroxidized cholesterol, was significantly lowered by the addition of β-carotene to the diet. These results strongly suggest that Chol-OOHs formed on exposure to UVA contribute to the expression of MMP-9, resulting in photoaging. Dietary β-carotene prevents the expression of MMP-9, at least partly, by inhibiting photodynamic action involved in the formation of Chol-OOHs.

### Janet Shold — 2018-02-05T21:23:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pandoras-box-of-vanity.17547/post-234185>

I have an anecdote. My father spent hours and hours all summer in the sun while golfing. For half the year he sported very brown skin on his face and arms. Tee times were, more often than not, early in the morning or later in the afternoon - meaning big red light times that also included some uv as the games were four hours long.

He always had a deep “golfers” tan, and had no wrinkles. Even though his last years were spent indoors, he died at the age of 90 and didn’t even have crows feet. He didn’t have three legs on his stool, as his diet had no seafood (he thought he was allergic), but the sunlight must have tremendously added to his health. His diet did him in, though, as he wound up with diabetes type two and dementia.

I wish I knew then what I know now! All these old people in long-term care facilities rarely feel the sunlight and never have bare feet on the earth. The lighting is bad, and the food is mediocre fare using the traditional western food pyramid. I feel so ashamed that my own father spent his last days like this!

Viktor Schauberger found in water what modern day physicists are finding out about graphene. What does this finding really imply to humans who observe nature well? It means that low energy systems like cells and high energy systems like CERN use physics very differently. The low end is quantum based, but the high end is relativity based. What does this imply?

Is relativity the physics of large and QED the physics of small?

Energy and mass are essentially the same things.........they are forms of matter.

What controls the state of energy and mass? The third part of the equation of C^2 isn't it according to mass equivalence?

Einstein gave us the conversion factor for high and low ends of mass and energy. Both appear to act using different forces on the low and high end of their states and the light associated with them determines how things act.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-13T05:47:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bio-physics-2.17549/post-188926>

What is missing in biology and medicine today is a modern unified quantum theory on how disease and aging merge and form % of heteroplasmy in mitochondria. This determines how the charged particles in mitochondria and all life can work in the state, or environments we allow. Light and cold change the states of matter,specifically mass and energy, in ways we still are ignorant of. You would do well to understand the strongest force in our universe, the electromagnetic force, is the basis of the inflection point where aging and disease meet in my opinion. The basis of this merger mimics what physics does in the standard model of physics. There are four fundamental forces in physics. In biology, I submit, there are four cornerstone forces which act with quantum mechanisms to affect the protein backbone that is coded for by our DNA and RNA to alter our expression to meet our environmental pressures. This makes understanding the complexity of biology a lot easier, in my view. Cold definitely aids superconductivity. However, superconductivity at room temperature where we live, continues to elude many researchers. Might the missing ingredient be light? Yes it does, but how? Light effects electrons and electron physics are linked to magnetism. Electron at rest (realivistically speaking) generates electric field. Electrons that are moving without acceleration produces electric and magnetic fields. This happens in many places in cells. Electrons moving with acceleration produces electromagnetic waves. This happens in mitochondria to change the percentage of heteroplasmy. So electrons in the an atomic lattice like the cell membrane with DHA's pi electron cloud are relatively stable, but leave a trace of their interactions in water networks inside all of our cells. This is what Viktor Schauberger tripped over in his study of water in vortexes..........but when light hits electrons in these different states that electromagnetic force carriers can bring to bear on them using the photoelectric effect they begin to move without acceleration (Fermat's Principle). This is when we create a photonic Maxwell Demon capable of something we label life.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-13T05:48:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bio-physics-2.17549/post-188927>

How electrons work from food with water in our cells seems to offend the “common sense of biology.” I find this concept to be a very good thing because the the principles of relativity, quantum mechanics and quantum vortices in black holes also offend biology. This is the way nature works in us, and across the whole universe. Fundamentally, water is a chameleon. It is globally elastic and locally fragile because it can act in many different forms it can assume chemically and electrically. Water is very much a molecular mirror for light's collisions with our surfaces and it can form a massive semiconductor in our brain with the action of DHA. Water can organize itself differently when it is adjacent to different material, allowing it to be the perfect chemical for interactions with the electromagnetic force. These interactions allow life to exist as a photonic Maxwell demon. Any protein/lipid/molecule in a cell that catches electrons or protons can and should be considered a Maxwell demon. Most of life's Maxwell demons also have the innate ability to capture some part of light's frequencies from the sun. For example, tyrosine captures UV light to make dopamine. Cysteine quenches the amount of photonic power in this interaction to keep the circuits from over or under heating. Heat is important to life. Mitochondria release heat for a deep reason. It is tied to electrons. In order to observe the hydrodynamic system at play in cells, we need to develop a precise way to measure how well electrons in the system carry heat. This is what aquaphotomics does using NIR spectroscopy. Materials conduct heat in two ways: through vibrations in the atomic structure or lattice; and carried by the electrons themselves. Biology needs to find a clever way to ignore the heat transfer from the lattice of the cell membrane and focus only on how much heat is carried by the electrons from foods and from the photoelectric effect. Water researchers are doing just that.
Water is a molecular mirror for the noise that light creates with electrons photoelectrically. Why do you think I introduced you to aquaphotomics in the Time series? At finite temperature, like our body temps, the electrons move about randomly but the higher the temperature (more protons = fever= leptin resistance), the "noisier" the electrons become. This means their vibrations make waves inside cells we can measure. By measuring the temperature of the electrons to three decimal points, researchers are now able to precisely measure the thermal conductivity of the electrons. In this way, you should begin to think of water like the ultimate molecular electric adapter for life. Converting thermal energy into electric currents and vice versa is notoriously hard with ordinary materials in life. It is ridiculously easy when water and DHA meet light. Water’s behavior is a function of its microenvironment. An MRI image alone, is proof of this concept. Water looks different in different tissues of the human body, yet, it still remains H20 at a biochemical level of observation. This biochemical scale is not good enough to explain how life organizes thermodynamically or on a quantum level because mass and energy react differently at the high and low end of both states of matter. Few remember that Einstein taught us that energy and mass are one and the same thing. The light environment they are found in is what determines the physics at play. To understand how life makes use of water, you need a smaller scale: the quantum scale. That is what I teach my members.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-13T06:05:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bio-physics-2.17549/post-188930>

The molecular story of life has over 300 major “actors” that talk the language of intercellular communication. Some of these “actors” only speak one language. Most play the “hero” or the “villain,” but not both. No single “actor” can fit into all of the acting roles required for intercellular communication, but there are some actors who could play multiple roles. Biology must begin to realize that the same molecular keys can open the doors to heaven and the gates of hell depending upon the context these keys are generated within. the basic elements are light water and magnetism but the environment contains forces that control the tension on energy and mass contained within light water and magnetic flux.

Aging and neolithic disease generation are clearly linked biologically, chemically and in a quantized fashion by the percentage of heteroplasmy created in mitochondria. It is here where electrons, photons, and protons all come together in the smallest spaces to interact to exchange energy and mass in a way a cell can use it to make life.

Correcting the physical chemistry of your inner ocean inside your cells is like a healthy aquarium; it will go a long way towards elevating mood and dropping depression by altering your dopamine level. The reduction/oxidation potential, is a measure of total photoelectric charge levels in ponds, aquariums and US!

Final thought: Schrödinger’s famous statement about life, he said, “It is by avoiding the rapid decay into the inert state of ‘equilibrium’ that an organism appears so enigmatic. What an organism feeds upon is negative entropy.” When I first read it, I was young and very interested in science, but I did not understand what he really meant. But there was a footnote in the book that explained it better. In that footnote, he said that by using the term “negative entropy,” he meant the cell somehow harnesses free energy. He clearly did not know how it happened, but he knew it did happen.

I had also read Albert Szent-Györgi’s famous speech about semiconduction to the Budapest Academy in 1941 by that time, and I immediately linked those two concepts. Today it is now well known and accepted that the ultimate source of all our energy and negative entropy is the electromagnetic radiation of the sun. When a photon interacts with a material particle on our globe, it lifts one electron from an electron pair to a higher level. This is how the photoelectric effect works and why Einstein got a Noble Prize for discovering it. How biology uses this effect in us is just beginning to be understood. That affect is now well known in plant chloroplast for photosynthesis. Today we have a model for that interaction in the RPE of the eye of every human reading this post.

It is my contention that no big changes in biology or medicine will be possible until we begin to understand how the human cell catches electrons, photons, and protons like chloroplasts do. This is where the grand theory of how a cell works must be re-written by nature’s laws and not the theories founded by the people who run biology and medicine today. This is when quantum cell theory replaces modern cell theory. How we energize and animate life is where this should begin. So to motivate you to elevate this stairway of knowledge. Think deeply about light water and magnetism and not about what you learned long ago in school.

# Thread: Journal on a path to change the world..

### Allanah — 2016-02-13T07:08:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-on-a-path-to-change-the-world.17550/post-188933>

I'm Allanah Rolph. 15 years old, 5'3" and 160 lbs. (until I started the Leptin reset and gained 10 lbs, probably because I started to actually eat until I was full) I have been overweight since I was young and started taking steroids for my asthma. For the last 1-2 years I have been getting increasingly interested in health, but it wasn't until the last 8 months or so that I discovered the Paleo diet because I became allergic to the whey and casein in milk after my first of two knee surgeries (total ACL and meniscus reconstruction after skiing accident and second one to fix the scar tissue). At my highest weight, after the first surgery which kept me bedridden and on crutches for 2 months I was 5'3" and around 210-215 lbs. After I discovered my new dairy allergy and continued to be overweight even after joining the cross country team I decided to focus on overall health instead of weight and calories. I have lost around 50 lbs to date, but hit a plateau a while after starting the Paleo lifestyle. Not only that, but I was constantly struggling with always wanting food, no matter how "full" I was. My brain always wanted me to eat. So I started looking online and at first I thought I had "food addiction" and I even went to the length of ordering books on the subject, but then I came across the hormone "Leptin." I started researching that and found my way to this blog. The first day I did my BAB with 50 grams of protein, all my cravings were gone. It seemed like a miracle. I ended up having to stop the reset because of uncontrollable circumstances but I started again on January 1 and am eagerly awaiting results. I have hit many roadblocks along the way, such as having a very small selection of seafood (canned salmon and tuna, frozen salmon) but through looking and taking with my parents I've found more (canned clams, mackerel, and oysters one time). Every day is a journey and I'm just doing my best. The biggest problem is that I'm part of a big family (I'm the second oldest out of 6 kids) and my brother was diagnosed with leukemia over 4 years ago so ever since it's been more or less a battle to pay the bills. This makes it hard for my parents to justify paying for the things they think are expensive (coconut oil) but they're starting to understand more every day. I also realized that if I want to live a better life I'm going to have to make sacrifices too, so I pay for some things myself ( blue light blockers, blue light screen filters, skin thermometer for CT, books, etc.) and since I have a job until track season hopefully I'll have everything I need by then. Some other variables out of my control are the amount of nnEMFs since we all have phones, Wi-Fi, etc. But I'm going to start grounding to try to protect myself a little. I will be starting cold thermogenesis once my thermometer comes so I'm excited for that. My life is very busy right now because we live in two different houses and have a 5 day school and work week which I'm not used to so I have a limited time to write and research, but it's what I love so I try to make as much time as I can. I am currently trying to find a way to completely get rid of fluoride from my environment, starting with finding a natural toothpaste. Another limitation is that where I live during the week is 2 hours from the nearest city with actual stores which is good for EMFs but bad for finding natural foods and products. But I'm making it work. Most of my family is very skeptical of most things I do but my brother is starting see the light, I believe that soon he'll understand more about why I do what I do and why I don't eat bananas when there's two feet of snow outside ) he's an athlete though so he does care about his health a little. I want to get some labs done but I don't know if my parents will let me or how much they cost, let alone how to read them so for now I haven't asked yet. I think that once my family starts seeing results from Leptin Rx they will start to believe in this idea more. I hope they do because I don't want to outlive them because I'm the only one who listened to Jack.

I call this the journal on ts path change the world because that's what I want to do when I grow up. I feel privileged to be in the position that I'm in because I am learning all these things at a young age, before I've made any big life decisions. What I mean by this is that although I may not understand everything on the blog right now, I will someday and I can choose to help people. I don't know what I want to go to college for because obviously medical school isn't going to be much help but if I get a degree in some kind of science maybe that's what it would take to get people to listen, maybe I'll just be the moderator for people who will never understand Jack's blog, but no matter what I do I know it's going to help people like my grandpa who passed from cancer even though he was a "healthy" guy. He ate fruits and veggies, went on walks every day, and was a doctor himself. He loved in Canada though so he was constantly bombarded by nnEMFs and had huge environmental mismatches from eating bananas in winter, but he had no idea. He thought he was healthy. This is why I want to educate people. I just first have to educate myself.

### Scompy — 2016-02-13T08:09:22+0200

Great post above! Amazing wisdom and perception too about this journey were are all on. For us that are parents, we are actively teaching our children about the 3-legged stool: sun, water and electromagnetism. So there should be many more in the future that also have acquired the knowledge of quantum bio. My son is pretty funny... for a seven-year old, he went to sleep tonight telling me a make-believe story about **melatonin muffins** and what happened to a guy that ate them...knocking him out for 3 years after the first bite. My son actively blue-blocks every night on his own!

Thanks for sharing your story! It was a great inspiration to read. Perspective is everything. Most of us are here because of various ailments that have plagued us in the past or at present. I see you've already stumbled onto that key when you said you might not understand now, but will in the future... it's can be a lot to absorb over many years, and the science itself often shifts and changes too. Making it a point on a continual basis, the connections, processes and information eventually sucks itself into the brain. I love moments when the teacher becomes the student and the student is the teacher.

### Allanah — 2016-02-20T03:14:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-on-a-path-to-change-the-world.17550/post-189420>

Update #1: Scores Galore!
Hey guys! (I cannot explain how much I am loving this blog/forum, it's my one time I can talk about all my "crazy" beliefs without criticism)
My life is the busiest it has been in a while, so it's hard for me to even check my own threads, let alone continue to learn more, but I'm doing my best. Even so, I got sick a few days ago (I thought it was strep, I'm not sure- but I told mom I'm not going to the doctor and taking antibiotics so I wasn't tested, score one for natural health!) so I'm finally getting to update!
I finally received my first sets of blue-blockers! My brother bought a pair too, he's finally understanding the effects of artificial light (score #2!) I have been wearing them the whole time I've been inside with blue light, and it amazes me the differences I already notice!
Although I haven't been feeling the best, I decided to go on a barefoot walk outside today, since it was nice (50 degrees F... score #3) and though it became painful near the end (out driveway is long and rocky) it was a good experience. I'm somewhat ashamed to say that this was only my second barefoot adventure this year as the snow has been at least 6 inches and my parents aren't exactly supportive of all of this, but I did walk barefoot home in the snow once.. that was fun. Anyway, my brother and I walked the 1/4 mile down and back from out house to our mail box to pick up score #4, my blue light filtering screen protectors for my iPod. Although I didn't get him to walk barefoot because he doesn't yet believe "all that electron garbage" he _did_ take his shirt off to get as much vitamin D as possible, so I'll take what I can get.
Another score is that my family (especially my dad) are starting to see things more in this light, last weekend my dad took me grocery shopping and we picked up.. wait for it.. seafood! We went to Walmart (not the best, I know, but the best we can do for now.. especially where we live!) and picked up salmon, calamari, a seafood mix, canned sardines and oysters in water, and onions, SINCE THEY'RE ALWAYS IN SEASON. (I just learned that and got very excited.  Sorry if I'm writing or sound like a child, but I kind of am, and this stuff makes me excited, so that's the result.) AND not only did _I_ get sone of the things I've been wanting, but we picked up healthier options for the family, too. Instead of noodles, we got beans. Instead of cereal, we got eggs. We also got coconut flour, milk, and oil, and RAW almonds. (those have been very hard for us to find, but our Sam's just started carrying them) Although obviously not optimal yet, but I'm glad my family is on it's way. Or at least less suboptimal.
Also, this week, thanks in part to Caroline and in part to the world wide web, I have stopped using toxic fluoridated toothpaste and instead use a coconut oil, baking soda, cocoa powder, and mint extract concoction that's wayyy better. (still have to get over the super salty-like baking soda taste, but it's still better than chemicals and fluoride )
Although not completely related to this blog, but also good news for me, my hair has finally gone through the transition phase in the "no-poo" journey and now I can wash with water only (score like #10) AND I got a boar bristle brush to distribute the natural oils through my hair.. so in short, Amazon has made my week pretty great.
Arriving next week, hopefully Monday, will be my IR Skin Thermometer so I can FINALLY start CT safely, without my parents being concerned, hopefully. (I probably won't tell them unless they ask anyway..)
We may even start ordering products in (my dad seems to like the idea) so it's not so stressful when we're short on time and I can actually get what I want without the fuss. We also found a small natural/organic co-op where I found coconut aminos, actual cacao powder, cacao nibs, Bragg's organic, raw, unfiltered apple cider vinegar, and many other great things. (grass-fed bison for me to buy someday!)
PLUS I almost forgot to metion, through thriftbooks.com (I HIGHLY recommend that website) I got my copy of The Body Electric, Electromagnetism and Life, and Electromagnetic Fields. (The total for all those books was less than or around $15, AND shipping is free on orders over $10, so that's hard to beat.) Anyway, I'm on my way to understanding life even better.
Overall, it's just been a great week overall on my journey to optimal, and no one really understands these things except the people on here, so since I had time I decided to share it with you guys. Not that it's actually inspirational, but I was getting frusterated in my position until all these things rolled together, and reflecting on this week has even made me more hopeful for the future, so maybe I'll look back when I'm discouraged and see this and keep going forward.
Stay on the path to optimal guys!

You're starting to realise that your health is screwed, your families health is screwed, all the people around you have terrible health. You get in to fitness, paleo, movement etc, and you come across this guy Jack Kruse calling out all your gurus, challenging all the dogma you had believed to be truth.

You read his blog, don't understand what's going on but sense something special nonetheless, you read the quilt, you intend to read every blog post, you actually listen to every podcast, read his tweets, watch his videos, come to the forum.

He's made you realise how important the three legged stool is. He's helped you realise how important your environment is, he's helped you connect with the sun, he's helped you source out the best available water because you now know how critical it is.

Then independently to Dr Kruse you realise it's all under attack, he woke you up, he showed you what you what was really important. He helped you get of your arse and stop taking all the ingredients for life for granted.

A message that must spread to all. Make it your purpose to inspire and open the eyes of those around you as well as Jack has done for you.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-14T01:12:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jack-kruse-is-a-gateway-drug.17552/post-188995>

Awareness, alone doesn't enlist change. Stop joining groups for support of diseases. All they do is institutionalize you and wallet biopsy you to death with promises of funding for research but they never do. Why? Because without their harvesting of your wallet CONSISTENTLY they would never be allowed to exist. You all have known this; you have to understand why you must change your environment. Then wellness can visit you. Just reconnect with nature and not any awareness group. It is OK to share with them..........but that is where you need to stop.

Learn about the sun, and your skin really works using Fermat’s principle of least time. Modern biology and medicine has a lot yet to learn about the photo-electric effect and the biology at surfaces. Wellness is an enlightenment is intimacy with light. The Fermat rabbit hole for light is quite fascinating for scientists and a black hole for biology because they do no understand light. Our tissues are set up to handle lights full spectrum situation, but our modern choices dramatically alter this natural biologic set up. Full spectrum light decides which is the shortest time based upon our tissues crystalline lattice what path to take. Will light go extreme one with more distance or the shorter one? Light alone chooses that path based upon the environment it faces. But you wonder what allows light to do what does? How does light find out how to do this? Does it smell the nearby paths, and check them against each other? The answer is, yes, it does, in a way. That is the feature which is, of course, not known in geometrical optics, and which is involved in the idea of wavelength; the wavelength tells us approximately how far away the light must “smell” the path in order to check the path light chooses.
