# Thread: The Times They Are a-Changin'
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-times-they-are-a-changin.17558/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 71
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Queen of Elves — 2016-02-15T17:01:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-times-they-are-a-changin.17558/post-189095>

Hello guys,

I already posted in the Meet&Greet and CT Forum but now I'm starting a journal because I need to have the things clear for me, which I need to work with. I have to pay attention because I always act on impulse...
It is obviously my dopamine is low and in the past I tried to compensate with food. I didn't recognize it, because I thought it was healthy food, so I was just caring about my body. I ended up with 10 years being a vegan, without fingernails, hair falling out and a big nodule in my thyroid, that was visible from the outside. After failing to remove the liquid in it, I got recommended to have half of my thyroid removed. I saw another MD, who put me on a T4/T3 compound which was way to high so I had heart palpitations and went hyperthyroid. Hashi was diagnosed (perhaps the antibodies occurred because of the overdose?), but not anymore..

I questioned my vegan approach and began to read more about nutrition and came to Weston Price and Paleo. Read many books, went to Cross Fit - and gained weight. Fingernails and hair grew again but my labs didn't improve.

After visiting 2 more MDs with different therapies I am now taking 10 mcg T3 and 100 mcg T4 while my TSH is suppressed. I don't like my thyroid to be shut down, the only thing that's positive is that the nodule disappeared.

Here are my labs:

Creatinine 0,58 g/l (0,6-1,8)
TSH 0,02 (0,4-4,2)
fT3 2,44 pg/ml (2,3-4,2)
fT4 1,01 ng/dl (0,99-1,62)
Thyreoperoxidase (MAK/TPO) 28,3 kU/l (<60)
Thyreoglobulin-AK (TAK) < 15 U/ml (< 60)
TSH-Receptor-AK (TRAK) 0,5 lU/l (< 1,8)

I'm iodine deficient although I eat fish everyday. I got iodide prescribed (first 100 mcg, then 200 mcg), but the labs didn't show any increase.

I'm also taking Vit. D, which is now sufficient, and bioidentical progesterone cream because of a worse prog/estrogen ratio.

I'm in week 7 of the Leptin Rx an began Face Dunks and putting ice on my belly and thighs which was no problem with compression clothes but gave me bruises without clothes, so I slowed the procedure down.

My next goal is to get off thyroid meds but I couldn't figure out already if it would be better so go on with meds while doing Leptin Rx, CT, limiting blue light after sunset, limiting nnEMF and getting more AM sunlight or reducing dose while optimizing the rest? MD told me, I will gain more, when reducing the dose. I'm clueless.

After losing a few lbs weight loss is currently stalling, I suppose because of increasing the iodide...

I'm sorry I have to stop for the moment, will add some more information soon!

Rather than using progesterone consider going one step higher,
consider supplementing with pregnenolone.

(To prevent pregnenolone steal make sure that you took care of having good DHEAs level, as posted above.

Using pregnenolone you should be able to take care of your progesterone levels in first half of your menstrual cycle (1.4ng/mL),
Test blood in 2-4 day of cycle.
You may possibly need more pregnenolone in second half.
Test progesterone in days 19-21.

For my self, (I am 76yo man) I had a good results with
pregnenolone Micronized Lipid Matrix from Nutricology, 150mg tabs, scored.
But at first I had to use large doses, latter smaller.

### Queen of Elves — 2016-02-24T16:49:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-times-they-are-a-changin.17558/post-189701>

Lahelada, I hope I won't need to supplement with iodine, but I'm a bit concerned about my daily seafood intake because I am still surrounded by too much blue light and nnEMF at work and after sunset. I listened to a podcast with Dr. Kruse and Jason Prall, he was saying, mercury in seafood is a problem in such an environment. Not sure how I will go on, because I think I need much iodine to support my thyroid.

The last few days I was trying to get my light routine changed - which interferes with my whole daily schedule...

It's clear now for me, the Leptin Rx ist about light, not food. The first 7 weeks I focused mainly on food and meal timing. This brought some changes, but no signs of leptin sensitivity.

Now this week I began to get up with sunset and have my BAB outside in the light. Sleeping so long isn't easy for me, I wake up at 5 AM (my usual time) and have to go to the bathroom. I use only the red light from my head lamp and hope that is okay. Then I return to bed and sleep until 7 AM. It's the third day I'm doing this, so perhaps my body will adjust in a while and I hope to sleep through until 7 AM.

Indeed, sleeping in a completely dark room brought a huge benefit. Before, I had to get up several times a night to go to the bathroom...

I am planning to adjust my waking time to the sunrise, even when I finished the Leptin reset (this is week 8 ).

It pisses me off, that I still have some bruises from the spot icing I did weeks ago! I feel the need to be cold! By the way, I always did. I took and take every opportunity to jump in ice cold water, no matter what - pool, ocean, lake. The most amazing situation was in the Austrian mountains in a very small, crystal clear, sky-blue tarn where I felt I was the first person ever swimming there. The second amazing situation was in November at the Baltic sea, swimming in 8° C water while it was snowing outside...

I put ice on my body (with compression clothes on) a few times more with no harm but I think I need to be in water to make progress. In my current home I have no bathtub so I ordered a blow-up pool.

**I read Time 9 - BOOM - I need UVA light!**

I'm trying now to halve my T4 meds while getting more UVA light.

UVA appears later in the morning...damn. So far I thought it will be okay to get some light after sunrise to trigger the pathways through eyes and skin. Therefore I get up with sunrise, spend some time outside (30 min), drive to work, find a parking place and walk another 30 minutes to my working place. Then I go outside in my break at noon for 30 min again. So I get 1 hour between 7-9 and half an hour at noon. By the way this week was the first opportunity to get some Vit D in my latitude, according to dminder! So in the past I started working at 6 AM, now I start around 9 AM. But I am not satisfied with the light yield, because although being three hours later at work it brings me just one hour AM light, but not UVA...

I could do another break for half an hour at 11 AM. But I think that you need to sense all the different light frequencies through the day, they are all important, also afternoon and dusk light and even if you go out at a time and get the natural sunlight, when you return to your artificially lit office, despite of your blue blockers, you will confuse the whole system again . It feels like on an off, again and again. I don't feel able to biohack my working environment with my current state of knowledge, because I can't prioritize the single steps I could take, so I am lost in uncertainty if what I'm doing is helping or harming me. Actually, I feel like living in a terrarium and it makes me angry to look out of the window, seeing the sun and the blue sky while being captured inside. **This is not how I want to live. I always knew that, but now I know why.**

I will read more and try to use artificial light in the right spectrum for different times of the day, that matches the current natural light spectrum outside. I even think about using a tanning bed in the seasons without sufficient sunlight for my thyroid which means overcoming some kind of inner resistance and this has to be prepared properly, because I still want my body to sense wintertime and doing some kind of hibernation

### Queen of Elves — 2016-02-29T10:45:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-times-they-are-a-changin.17558/post-189966>

I am recognizing that light an grounding really make a big difference. On Saturday I spend several hours barefoot outside in the sunshine and wasn't getting hungry at noon as usual. The same yesterday with my short CT and being barefoot outside: I wasn't hungry till dinner.

Today I woke up too late and had AM light only for a short time while eating my breakfast plus sky was cloudy. There will be no sun all along this week, so I decided to try a tanning salon.

The only thing that is disappointing me is my weight, which went up last week
This is my soft spot. As long as the weight goes down, I am confident that I am doing it right. Then it goes up without any reason and I want to mess up everything.
My insecurity is caused by my thyroid condition. I read Time 9 and understood, how the thyroid gets driven by light and I try to adjust my daily routine to light cycles but since I am taking thyroid meds I am concerned, that the external hormones might inhibit the natural pathways. "If you are designed to make it, don't take it." So I halved my dose of T4 along the last week, and gained 1,5 kg! I think I have to give the readjustment more time but the environmental conditions aren't optimal yet (sun and temperature), so I am afraid of making everything worse by cutting my meds. I will do it more slowly now and the more I reduce the dose, the more light I will add - even by using a tanning bed. To offset the increased nnEMF from that I will go on with CTing.

But it is still a little frustrating because my boyfriend loses one kg every week by calorie restriction and cheating every weekend

### Queen of Elves — 2016-03-11T16:20:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-times-they-are-a-changin.17558/post-190657>

The last week was very stressful and I wasn't able to keep my routine of morning CT and AM light due to a business trip to Berlin.
Already the train ride was very uncomfortable, so many people, so many cellphones, fake light...it was overwhelming me, as if I was more sensitive to those energies. Until now, it didn't bother me so much, I feel much more vulnerable now since I am perceiving the negative impact. I actually felt hurt by the people and the energy around me.

I fell back into old behavior of stuffing me with sweets and coffee and watching TV in the hotel room
I felt absolutely helpless, like something took control over me.

But I noticed something interesting, my body seemed to handle all that crap different, I didn't get sleepy like in the past, instead I got warm and felt awake

On the way home I felt like an alien among all the travelers, watching them from a distance.

However, I'm trying to get back on track again. My next challenge will be a business trip to Munich in April...

Today I had to leave out breakfast because of a blood test, but tomorrow I will yoke my meal times to circadian cycles again.

### Queen of Elves — 2016-03-16T12:42:36+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-times-they-are-a-changin.17558/post-190957>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=190939)
>
> Stop with food. Focus on light. Why? Light will shrink respiratory proteins as a plus but to keep them shrunk to do well LONG TERM you need AM UV and IR too. Reduction in heteroplasmy first is critical to get right before ketosis to drive ECT because you can shorten telomeres and build new mitochondria. Mitochondrial DNA is controlled by melatonin cycles so this is why being cold dark and boring is key in the beginning. The cooler the surface the more UV you absorb and the more heat your mitochondria release to condense the respiratory proteins to control local geometry. This is controlled on a macroscopic level by dopamine and melatonin cycles. Time 9 re read it.......carefully

Since reading Time 9 it is clear to me that mitochondria have to be fixed before using ketosis as a tool. But I was still wondering since I practiced a ketogenic nutrition for several months, why I had such a great energy all the time. It seemed to be the proof, that ketosis works for me. On the other side, I was still hungry despite of the BAB and I was getting used to eating more an more (protein and fat, mostly seafood) and I wasn't losing weight. Now that I reduced my doses of T3 and T4 I'm sure, that energy came from the exogenous thyroid hormones and therefore isn't a reliable factor for me until I completely dropped the meds.

**So I will be even more focusing on reduction of heteroplasmy**

-   I am doing a cold bath outside every morning in 5° C water for 20-30 min and will work that up to an hour as temperatures go up.
-   The few times the sun was out the last days, I exposed my whole body to the morning light.
-   I am grounding every morning and will increase this too.
-   On weekends I am taking every opportunity to be barefoot in the sun over the whole day.
-   I always wear blueblockers at night

What I'm not sure about is how to eat to "relieve" mitochondria. Just add some more carbs to avoid ketosis?

### Queen of Elves — 2016-04-21T17:18:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-times-they-are-a-changin.17558/post-193164>

I'm still here
Have been busy with my job, travel and working on my new house and could not read/learn as much as I wanted, and also wasn't able to do all things 100% right but at least I kept my "light routine" every single day and limited online-time. The weather here pisses me off, but I try to get as much time outdoor as possible, even if it is raining or cold.

Things I did

I get up every day with sunrise
First thing I do is going outside, barefoot with little clothes on
Eat my breakfast outside, 30 minutes after waking up, still barefoot
Jump in my plastic pool for 10-15 minutes (9° Celsius) and then sunbathing (or mostly light bathing these days) naked for as long as possible - on working days at least 15 minutes
Driving to work with open windows to get more light
Taking off my jacket while waiting for the street car
Getting my blue blockers on while in the street car or in places with fake light, wearing long sleeves
I arrive at work around 9 o'clock , which means I get natural light from waking up around 6.30 to 8.50 AM
I have my lunch break outside
I take another break to get some UVB
I use nicotine gum
I tried MB but my urine went green
I skipped Vit. D supplements
I reduced my thyroid meds further
I try do be barefoot and with as much skin exposed, but that's mostly on weekends
I bought shoes with leather soles
I stopped wearing high heels
I eat seafood nearly every day
Sleep in complete darkness

Things I noticed

I am less hungry
I don't need my BAB anymore
My sweat smells sometimes
I developed muscles on my upper arms although I didn't exercise
My legs look and feel firmer, but I didn't do anything
Hair loss is starting again, I think due to stopping Vit. D and reducing thyroid meds - that's tough for me!
Tinnitus gets worse when I am at my working place
There is a WLAN hot spot every 50 m in the environment I am working in

Things I will do

Adding more AM UV light between 9-11 (not sure how I could do that) - driving to work with my old cabrio = 40 min more AM UV
Reading more books, less online - Finished: Life on the Edge, Richard Feynman - QED, The Monk who sold his Ferrari, Better Body better Brain
In two weeks I will be moving to a new place (my farmhouse, which will be hardwired)
Trying to do home office, working part-time or getting a new job - Got a "Bootstick" for working at home, just for testing, but okay.
Learn Portuguese

By the way I increased T3 and T4 a little but I am not happy about it. I wished, I could get it working by just improving my behavior and environment. Perhaps I need to be more patient, but when it comes to my hair I can't  So I will try to stop the hair loss while increasing time outside.

### JanSz — 2016-05-24T01:32:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-times-they-are-a-changin.17558/post-195233>

\---
hair loss------>>> investigate estrogens
sleep problems-----> investigate progesterone

\--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
You said:
My ancestors came from Austria - far away from any source of DHA,

DHA is super important.
We need very little DHA.
Large amounts of DHA may be hurtful if not directly then because it oxidizes super easily.
Those small amounts are relatively easy available or self converted.

Personally I prefer to do blood test so I know where I stand with my fatty acids.

\--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Queen of Elves — 2016-07-29T17:49:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-times-they-are-a-changin.17558/post-199110>

Bad news for me: I was diagnosed with chronic lyme and adrenal fatigue.
The doctor even measured my mitochondrial capacity, which was also poor, and oxidative cell stress, which - surprise - is high. Oh man, I am despaired!
I thought I was on the right way but now I know I have to take a step back and fix those issues. He says it's possible and will probably also heal hashi.
He says in the next weeks I need to avoid heavy meals, especially protein and start fasting to relieve the liver. Then adding back vegetable soups, greens and so on while taking antibiotics for several weeks, then we will see how it is working. I should further avoid everything that stresses my immune system - too much sun , too cold water , too much work

I learned that it's not about the food, but in this case I think it makes sense to leave out protein because my body can't handle it well.

Surprisingly the result of my vitamin d test was different than usual. The usual result was "vitamin d is too low", now the ratio between the storage form an active form (hope, I remember that correctly) was measured and is way too high, which means, the stored vit. d is much too high but the active form is used up too fast because of inflammatory processes and therefore shows low results.

This doctor doesn't take part in the current vit. d hype and doesn't recommend supplementation. He is practicing "biologic medicine" with some unusual (at least in my experience) therapies which work at the cell level and he is developing his own treatments based on his experience and the main thing for me to trust him is that he engages me to be mindful with my body, to listen, to be aware what is going on inside me and to notice the changes. He said to me that we have lost so much of our potential and are captured in our dull routine. I never talked to a physician in that way and I think it is the right time I met him, now that I have learned so much about the cell metabolism thanks to Dr. Kruse. One year earlier, I wouldn't have been able to either understand the difference in the treatment or recognize the essentials of his advice.

I have got my report on diagnostic findings and the treatment plan:

Diagnoses:
Chronic lyme disease
Chronic chlamydia infection - cant't believe that
Blocked vitamin D-receptor because of chronically active infectious diseases
Inflammation of the intestinal mucosa, elevated ALPHA-1-antitrypsin, "exsudative enterophatie" (sorry, can't translate this one)
Indication for leaky gut
Distinct mitochondrial disorder with cellular compensation mechanisms, which point to a longer persisting condition

Treatment:

Intravenous oxygen
Biophoton therapy according to Prof. Fritz Popp
Ozone therapy
"BowTech"
vegetable antibiotics: Cranberry, garlic, walnut,...
Bowel cleanse
slightly exercising/sweating

Protein and carbs (besides vegetables) should be avoided

If there is no improvement after 3-4 weeks, antibiotics need to be taken for several weeks in different combinations.

The complete treatment will take several months...

I'm pretty shocked, because I didn't expect so much damage.
Especially the mitochondrial disorder bothers me, as I did so much (I thought) to help my mitochondria for example with CT, sunlight, grounding and avoiding nnEMF. Seems that didn't work very well. But it's good to know now, that I don't need to fill me up with DHA/electrons because my mitochondria aren't able to use the energy and therefore I am gaining weight. But why did it start when I began taking thyroid medication?

Anyway I hope that after my body gets cleared of all those toxins, I can start again with better results. Wish me luck!

And I **do** follow JK's recommendations about light for more than a half year now, which means,I get up with sunrise every day, eat breakfast, stay outside in the AM light, grounded, and then drive to work mostly with the cabrio, so I get another 45 minutes of AM/UVA light.

-   At work I am wearing blue blockers in front of the screen from 2 PM and have my breaks outside.
-   In the evening I'm also wearing blue-blockers when I have to use fake light and go to bed not later than 11 PM.
-   I finish dinner at 7 PM (around June 21. sometimes at 8 PM)
-   I fall asleep in less than 5 minutes, have no light around my sleeping room and mostly don't wake up at night - only sometimes when my boyfriend needs to go to the bathroom.

Nothing that I did in the last six months brought any improvement. What I am doing is not enough but I don't know what else I could do in my current situation. Being locked in my office the whole day, working at a computer is my main problem.

# Thread: House Sitting In Florida

### Danny — 2016-02-15T19:05:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/house-sitting-in-florida.17560/post-189103>

I would like to plan some vacations this year and was wondering if there was any interest in using my home as a vacation spot for someone on the forum while I am vacationing.

I live in NW FL b/t St. Augustine and Daytona.

I live in 3/2 single story house in a rural area, surrounded by pines w/ ample sunlight, about 25 min from Flagler beach (best spot on the east coast IMO), and about 1 hr from half a dozen springs.

Fresh fish shop 15 min away,
no smart meter,
10 G magnetico on king

Let me know if interested even if your curiosity is piqued just a little.

# Thread: Speak to me of aquaglyceroporins!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/speak-to-me-of-aquaglyceroporins.17561/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hansen Kenimer — 2016-02-16T00:16:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/speak-to-me-of-aquaglyceroporins.17561/post-189136>

I've been playing around with glycerin in my coffee, off and on, and now I'm suddenly intrigued by aquaglyceroporins. I know Dr. Kruse speaks often of aquaporins, but how about aquaglyceroporins? Has there been any discussion of these? I can't find anything when I search the forums.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-16T01:00:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/speak-to-me-of-aquaglyceroporins.17561/post-189142>

They are a form of aquaprorins.......if you search AQA 4 you'll find a lot. Aquaglyceroporins help change the size and shape of water molecules to alter they way light can be used in a cell when it releases ELF-UV light. Aquaglyceroporins are pieces of matter with mass that effects energy. Energy is neither created nor destroyed. It just changes shape. Energy and mass are both one and the same thing..........the field around them dictate their state and conversion.

### Hansen Kenimer — 2016-02-16T01:42:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/speak-to-me-of-aquaglyceroporins.17561/post-189144>

So the fact that they can diffuse glycerol in addition to water is obviously significant. Why this is important is what I'm trying to figure out. I've read they have some impact on gene expression, too.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-16T01:44:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/speak-to-me-of-aquaglyceroporins.17561/post-189145>

Read Pollack's book. Anything that changes the coherent domains in water determines the size of the EZ = size of the battery = the amount of water that can be stored.

### Hansen Kenimer — 2016-02-16T02:58:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/speak-to-me-of-aquaglyceroporins.17561/post-189150>

I guess what I find intriguing is that there are not just aquaporins, but these interesting aquaglyceroporins...meaning, I think, that in these, glycerol plays as large a role as water. There is a type of aquaglyceroporin exclusive to the heart, and glycerol is the energy substrate. Makes me think about glycerol's ability to diffuse through these aquaglyceroporins...perhaps why nitroglycerine works so quickly?

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-16T03:31:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/speak-to-me-of-aquaglyceroporins.17561/post-189152>

aquaporins = aquaglyceroporins as A is equivalent a.

### Hansen Kenimer — 2016-02-16T16:50:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/speak-to-me-of-aquaglyceroporins.17561/post-189176>

That just doesn't make sense to me, or they'd all be one or the other, right?

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-16T19:10:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/speak-to-me-of-aquaglyceroporins.17561/post-189186>

Are you human? Is you mom? A = a is the same analogy.......same family that slightly different functions

### Hansen Kenimer — 2016-02-16T19:24:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/speak-to-me-of-aquaglyceroporins.17561/post-189187>

Yes. But just like all moms are human but not all humans are moms, all aquaglyceroporins are aquaporins, but not all aquaporins are aquaglyceroporins. Lol. Just wondering why.

Maybe I'm just more curious why glycerol diffuses like water through these aquaglyceroporins. Seems glycerol might play a larger role than we know, especially because they are found places other than the liver, like the brain and the heart.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-16T19:41:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/speak-to-me-of-aquaglyceroporins.17561/post-189189>

glycerol is the back bone of all lipids.

# Thread: Life's Chameleon's:  Water is a shape shifter.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lifes-chameleons-water-is-a-shape-shifter.17562/>
**Subforum:** Redox Rx
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-16T02:14:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lifes-chameleons-water-is-a-shape-shifter.17562/post-189148>

Why are microwaves fundamentally bad for us and food?

microwave vibrates molecules and they lose electrons........

So this brings up a point: does boiling water on a cooktop cause any change in the molecules. And what about food in the microwave? Does the same concept apply due to water content in the food?

Sure it changes them and not for the better........

heating and microwaving cause two different effects and both change the shape of water's coherent domains in their hydrogen bonding networks. This is the work of Martin Chaplin, Phillipa Wiggins, Emilio Del Guidice, and Gerald Pollack all rolled into one. This affects the amount of light they can absorbs because size and shape of coherent domain are proportionally related to EZ size. That relationship exists because of mass equivalence equation of Einstein.

Coherent domains in water's hydrogen bonding network are pieces of matter with specific masses that effects energy and it conversion. Energy is neither created nor destroyed. It just changes shape. Energy and mass are both one and the same thing..........the field around them dictate their state and conversion = mass equivalence of Einstein.

Coherent domains in water = instantaneous changes in size and shape of water molecules to incident light , magnetic flux, or nnEMF or blue light.

Coherent water = EZ. Liquid water is therefore a two-fluid system in a cell. It is bulk when it is not touching hydrophilic proteins and not in light. This is similar in analogy with superfluid helium in cold or heat. Bulk water consists of a coherent phase which is about 40 percent of total volume at room temperature and an incoherent phase 60%. In the coherent phase only, the water molecules oscillate between two electronic configurations in phase with a resonating EMF. In nature that should be the sun or the Schumann depending upon the presence or absence of light. The common frequency of the EMF and the electronic oscillation of the coherent phase being 0.26 eV; whereas the energy difference of the two electronic configuration of the coherent phase is 12.06 eV, which gives the wavelength of 1 000 A (100 nm) of the coherence domain. The remaining 60 percent incoherent phase is extracted by thermal fluctuations from the coherent phase. So EMF's can really mess with water composition and show how big a chameleon water really is. The two phases have widely different dielectric constants: Bulk water is 78. Coherent water is WAY higher. This is why electrons and photons from the sun make water supercharged. The coherent phase is 160, due to the high polarizability of the coherently aligned water molecules that are oscillating in concert; while the dielectric constant of the incoherent state is about 15. 160 +15 = 175. Divide by two and you approximate 78 which is what you see published.........on bulk water. Nobody breaks it down like this because no one see water as I do.........it is life's version of a superconductive superfluid on Earth like helium is in the sun. Coherent water = EZ = magnetohydrodynamic fluid of immense ability to make quantum computing possible. The externally applied electric fields are only felt in the non-coherent phase because sunlight can affect the 40% coherent phase to alter the 60 % depending upon the environments our choices make. This is why the DC electric current is operational in wakefulness. Just when you think you know water............you dont. Not only is water stranger than we think, it is stranger than we can think about it. I personally think nature's imagination with respect to water's abilities Is so much greater than our own could ever hope to be, nature is never going to let us relax because of how she shape shifts water to create life's magic.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-27T02:54:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lifes-chameleons-water-is-a-shape-shifter.17562/post-193634>

[https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/can-...-jack-kruse?trk=hp-feed-article-title-comment](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/can-microwaves-turn-good-food-gmo-jack-kruse?trk=hp-feed-article-title-comment)

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-27T03:27:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lifes-chameleons-water-is-a-shape-shifter.17562/post-193641>

April 2016 webinar left something out for those of you who heard it: There is a fifth phase of water...........and it is tied to scale.

Water becomes a quantum dot when it is confined. Water, a MOLECULE, not an atom is capable of "quantum tunneling" itself. Hence, why all living things will never be found devoid of water. [http://www.gizmag.com/fourth-state-of-water/42999/](http://www.gizmag.com/fourth-state-of-water/42999/)

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-27T04:03:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lifes-chameleons-water-is-a-shape-shifter.17562/post-193649>

In a quantum dot, there are both negatively charged and positively-charged particles that are missing electrons. The attraction between the electron and hole creates a quantum state with a very strong light-matter interaction and a quick release of light. Guess what helps this interaction? Cold causes confinement and then CT + light = potent combo in the smallest spaces in your mitochondria where water does some serious magic.[http://phys.org/news/2016-04-superfast-source-artificial-atom.html](http://phys.org/news/2016-04-superfast-source-artificial-atom.html)

# Thread: Baby Steps
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 294
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jenelle — 2016-02-16T21:35:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189196>

Hello all ~

I am new around here, and would like to start this journal as a place to document this new chapter of my journey.

I have been hanging around the forum for a few days now, and I have read many things on the site, so far. Jack's book is on its way.

I realize there is so much to learn. I will probably ask a lot of questions "out loud" here ~ but I am not always expecting answers. I am not a baby, and I do not expect to be spoon-fed... but I will be taking baby steps, as time and life allows.

Sometimes, we just have to put things down somewhere to get them out of our minds and take a good look at them from a new angle. If anyone has anything constructive to add ~ I love the conversation and support.

Thank you all for being here and sharing your journeys. I am not on facebook or any social media, so this will be my #1 place to share what is going on with me ... and also the only place where I see what is going on with everyone else.

You'll probably see me here a lot, as I tend to "dive in" to a new area of interest pretty deeply (read: obsessively. haha) So I'll just say in advance: Nice to meet you!

### fitness@home — 2016-02-16T23:08:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189205>

Welcome Jenelle. Pretty name!

### Jenelle — 2016-02-17T00:02:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189208>

Thank you so much!

\----

Okay, so:

Baby Step #1 is that yesterday, I made myself get out in the snow and drive to the tanning place. I have known for years now, that this helps me ... to go a few times per week, in the winter months. **Knowing** and **doing** are two different things, as we all know. But thanks to the urging of a friend who understands the importance ~ I went. And as I already knew, it was a HUGE boost to my mood, energy, and mental clarity for the rest of the day. Just 3 minutes in a stand-up bed. Transforming.

February is still my least favorite month, and there is still the task of making myself go a few times each week. I know that this is just a baby step, but it was an important one for me.

### Jenelle — 2016-02-17T02:19:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189217>

Baby Step #2 was coming here, creating an account, and actually starting a journal.

I have been keeping _somewhat_ of a journal/thread going on another site, which is focused solely on nutrition. I have been a member there for a couple of years now. Over time... I have really started to see the flaws with their program, and I have found it harder and harder to be there. While at the same time, being there more and more. I know... that may sound odd. But I love to help people ~ and I have been able to speak to a handful of folks there who feel SO much better with a more ketogenic approach. Unfortunately, they not only do not encourage a ketogenic approach there ~ they actively discourage it for most people. That has caused some unnecessary stress in my life. It has brought me joy, to be able to help... in the few cases where I have REALLY been able to help. I have changed some lives, no doubt. But a lot of my energy has gone into this place... and I often feel that it is not appreciated.

My first venture into keto eating came after reading Jimmy Moore's book. In October 2014 I did about 30 days. Another 30 days in January 2015, which was a bit different because I did it "Whole 30" style, which is pretty strict Paleo. No dairy, no sweeteners, no dessert-like creations.

As of right now, I've been eating LCHF/keto for a solid 3 months. It **really** feels like the appropriate winter diet for me. I am still researching and debating whether I need to do the Leptin Reset or not. I do not struggle with cravings in any way... but there are a couple of things I am still consuming which I feel like probably need to go (umm, again): All nuts and seeds, and all dark chocolate ~ no matter what the percentage (yes, I can happily eat 100% baking chocolate).

I feel like I drink too much coffee, but I am clearly addicted to it. I quit cold-turkey for about 10 days recently, replaced it with green tea and black tea... that was fine. Decided one day I missed coffee, and went right back to my old ways: 16 oz. 2x per day.

I also really enjoy having a drink (or two) with my husband when we get a chance. This is silver tequila with plain sparkling water. I am not ready to cut this out completely. I may get to that point... but I am not there yet.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-17T02:57:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189219>

Procaffeination = a toxic light environment = loss of electron flow in ECT. Read the end of the Ubi 4 blog. Right there.

### Robert Haas — 2016-02-17T03:31:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189220>

Welcome to the "light" side, friend.

### Jenelle — 2016-02-17T06:24:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189230>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=189219)
>
> Procaffeination = a toxic light environment = loss of electron flow in ECT. Read the end of the Ubi 4 blog. Right there.

I read the whole thing. Thanks!

I actually had to look up "procaffeination". Go ahead and laugh. Dictionary.com had no listing... But Urban Dictionary DID!

Then I found this nice mug:

[](http://smg.photobucket.com/user/wja/media/Screen%20Shot%202016-02-16%20at%2011.09.49%20PM.png.html)

...I imagine the answer to me getting off the caffeine again... is going to be LIGHT.

So is everyone here caffeine-free?

### Jenelle — 2016-02-17T06:26:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189231>

> [Robert Haas said:](/goto/post?id=189220)
>
> Welcome to the "light" side, friend.

Why, thank you, sir.  So nice to be here!

### Robert Haas — 2016-02-17T14:50:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189238>

I'm at 1 cup in the morning. Oddly I never drank coffee my entire life until a few years ago. Yeah I got funny looks over that one too, when I said "I don't drink it". Now I do most days out of habit I guess because I don't feel I need it.
So...if 2 makes a procaffinator does one make you an amature-caffinator? LOL.

### Jenelle — 2016-02-17T18:34:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189242>

> [Robert Haas said:](/goto/post?id=189238)
>
> Now I do most days out of habit I guess because I don't feel I need it.

This is ~sort of~ me, too. When I first began drinking regular coffee again a few months ago (everyone: because we got a puppy and my whole world went crazy  ) ... I _needed_ it. I was freaking _tired._ Now ~ the habit has stayed, whether I need it or not.

I won't lie to myself or anyone here and say that I am not addicted to caffeine, however ~ because I know I am. Green tea gave me a little boost, but not as much as coffee ~ and black tea was strangely stimulating to me ("shaky") a couple of times in a way that coffee _isn't._ Decaf really has no appeal to me right now. I am drawn to super dark chocolate, as well... and these are all things that stimulate "the cats": catecholamines: dopamine, norepinephrine, and adrenaline. I'm **aware** of it... it's just a matter of how to back myself back out of it, now.

### Jenelle — 2016-02-17T18:41:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189243>

Baby Step #3 today: Over 50g of protein with breakfast, and yes ~ as soon as I could get it cooked, after waking. I would like to continue this daily.

This one was pretty easy. I eat an entire can of Wild Planet tuna every morning for breakfast anyway. I subbed out one of my egg yolks for another entire egg, and added a couple pieces of bacon.

tuna 32.5
eggs 12
bacon 6
yolk
kale
coconut oil
homemade mayo

Edited to say: Wow, I completely forgot about the Great Lakes collagen in my coffee. Adding that in now: 12.

So really... I have been getting 50 grams of protein with breakfast every day ~ for a couple of weeks now (since I upped my tuna from 1/2 can to the full can).
\----
62.5

### Jenelle — 2016-02-17T19:03:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189245>

Baby Step #4 last night: We shut off the power strips to both TVs/Xbox/Wii U/U-Verse/etc. last night before we went to bed. I think this should be done every night ~ and can't hurt. The question is: Do we need to actually unplug the power strips from the wall each night?

### Inger — 2016-02-17T19:16:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189248>

Hi Jenelle

I think the thing with coffee is, the more you need it the less you should drink.
If you feel awake and great without coffee it want hurt you too much to drink a little good quality one... just my thoughts

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-17T19:43:40+0200

coffee = a person who is light stressed and has no proper melatonin recycling so they use caffeine to increase ECT speeds to do what missing melatonin can do. Time 9 breadcrumb.

Baby Step #5 REALLY needs to be ordering some blue blocker glasses today from Amazon... but I am having analysis paralysis. Big time. I have looked at these many times over the past ~however long~ and I have never made a purchase.

### Jenelle — 2016-02-18T00:32:21+0200

(I know, _groan..._) I was actually looking for "orange reading lights". DUH. I need to just get them some orange glasses, too!

Winter Brain. This is my winter brain. I promise it gets better!

### Jenelle — 2016-02-18T07:33:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189289>

Question for anyone willing to answer:

If we are using artificial light in the winter-time (like going to the tanning bed or using a Sperti, etc)... Does this make our bodies think it is more like summer, and we crave or "need" more carbs?

### Jenelle — 2016-02-18T08:15:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189292>

**"If our cells make it…….you’re not designed to take it." -- Time 9.**

The only supplements I've been taking are D3 & K2. I've started to realize this may just be dumb.

D3 -- why? Because you hear about it all the time... everywhere... for everything... because so many are deficient... and because I never get out in the sun during the winter. And honestly? Not all that much in the summer, either. Better answer: Get some good light on a consistent basis. Like, DAILY.

K2 -- why? Because I've read so many places that we need this when taking high doses of D3.

### Robert Haas — 2016-02-18T13:15:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189299>

> [Jenelle said:](/goto/post?id=189289)
>
> Question for anyone willing to answer:
>
> If we are using artificial light in the winter-time (like going to the tanning bed or using a Sperti, etc)... Does this make our bodies think it is more like summer, and we crave or "need" more carbs?

I think this has more to do with hours in the daylight. short day/long night = winter. Long day/short night = summer.
Imagine how confused our bodies must be with long days of artificial light (no uv) with short nights 365?

### Josh Rosenthal — 2016-02-18T16:23:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189309>

Keep pushing your needle towards discomfort and enjoy the transformation!

### Jenelle — 2016-02-18T18:26:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189322>

> [Robert Haas said:](/goto/post?id=189299)
>
> I think this has more to do with hours in the daylight. short day/long night = winter. Long day/short night = summer.
> Imagine how confused our bodies must be with long days of artificial light (no uv) with short nights 365?

Rob... my body is confused... no doubt about that!

I really want to keep making baby steps toward feeling better... but it can be hard when you're not even sure what the next step should be.

I've been LCHF/keto for 3 straight months... Then I come here and start reading some of Jack's stuff, and I see that he seems to be pretty down on Jimmy Moore and fellow "gurus"... One post, in particular, really hung me up last night, and I read through it over and over again, trying to decide how it applied to ME. Please read the first post here:

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...ck-results-nicotine-and-mb.17445/#post-187523](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/2015-heteroplasmy-bio-hack-results-nicotine-and-mb.17445/#post-187523)

All I know is: My brain was fried after reading so many things like this last night, and we ordered Mexican food. I ate chips and salsa for the first time in 3 months. I even ate the fried _shell_ of my taco salad... which I _never_ do. Am I going off the rails on the crazy train?

Haha. Nope. I feel too good on keto to make this a nightly thing. ...Been there, done that.

Interesting observations, though:

1) Instead of feeling brain fog and sluggishness (which is what this meal would have done to me in the past) ~ I got up off the couch and walked on the treadmill while we finished our movie. Mentally, I felt good.

2) I had ZERO desire to eat anything in the evening after that... because I was uncomfortably **stuffed**... which is a feeling I have not had for the past 3 months.

I most definitely have been doing the "blue-light-and-eating-at-night-thing" that you are warning me about. Whether it's nuts and chocolate, or just a handful of olives ~ and/or tequila and sparkling water... I am rarely done eating after dinner.

Now, part of that ~of course~ is that I stay up too late. Work in progress!

### Jenelle — 2016-02-18T19:41:04+0200

I have never had oysters in my life... and, quite honestly, have probably had an aversion to most seafood... because I was NOT raised with it. It's out of my comfort zone. Anyone want to help change that?

Do I want to eat the whole 8 oz can? Is that overkill? ...I honestly have no idea.

I opened the can, stared those dudes down, picked them up and inspected them all around... Realized I may have a textural issue with them, and could totally see myself gagging.

Decided to blend them in the blender with a random can of Amy's cream of mushroom soup I had in the cabinet.

Yes. I drank my oysters. Room temp ~ with cream of mushroom soup. And I don't even like mushrooms.

There has to be a better way.

And no... I did not drink the entire concoction. I think my husband will warm up the rest of it later, eat it as a SOUP, and enjoy it!

### Jenelle — 2016-02-19T17:30:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189390>

Just looked back through this journal ~ and I can say that I **am** keeping up with all of my baby steps, so far.

Baby Step #8: This morning, as soon as I got my BAB finished, I _went outside_ in the 53º wind and sat and tried to eat it. I faced the SUN, and I took off my glasses.

My hair kept blowing in my face, and my food was getting cold at an alarming rate (haha) ... so I only stayed out there for a few minutes ~ but that was a few minutes I have NOT done any other morning this winter!

It's actually a pretty BIG step for me to commit to this each morning. But I am going to do it.

On the subject of glasses ~ I've had them since first grade. I got contacts when I was 11 years old. My prescription is pretty bad: Legally blind, according to my optometrist: at -6.75 and -7.00.

I have seen folks posting here about correcting their vision, and I am intrigued. I have never understood WHY mine was so bad to begin with.

On a related note: Over the weekend, as I was reading about a woman here who was using ice packs on her eyes, it sparked something in my brain. My left eyelid had been a little swollen and painful for a few days. I had stopped wearing my contacts. I figured I had some sort of infection and would eventually need antibiotic eye drops. (I haven't had anything like this for many years, but that was the solution back then.)

Anyway ~ I thought, what the heck? Can it hurt? So I got a bunch of ice cubes in a snack-sized bag, and I started laying it across my eyes. The first time, it was just a few seconds and I had to take it off. My eyes watered like crazy and I thought, _"Hey, this is good... maybe it will help whatever this is drain out of there."_

...Well, I really think it did! I did this over and over again, as many times as I could stand it. It was really soothing in a weird sort of way. And by later that night, I realized it was feeling much better and I went ahead and put my contacts back in. No trip to the eye doctor required.  Thank you, forum!

### Jenelle — 2016-02-20T19:17:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189461>

When you realize that you have a million things to do, but you're so overwhelmed and pissed off about it that you don't want to do **any** of them... so you sit down on the couch and get lost in the internet, instead........... Low dopamine, or just, _life?_

### Jenelle — 2016-02-21T04:47:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189499>

Listening to Jack's podcasts is giving me very mixed emotions. On one hand, I am so happy to learn... and everything makes sense and I start making connections in my brain... I feel enlightened... And I also start thinking about possibilities...

And then reality sinks in, and I feel very trapped by my current circumstances. Very limited in my choices. So in some ways, learning these things is making me even more depressed than I was already feeling.

I will keep working on baby steps and doing what I can, when I can. But I may have to stop seeking out podcasts ... until my general outlook on life is feeling a little more optimistic.

Doesn't this make perfect sense?

I remember feeling a little angry the first few weeks. Not over this information, but the flat out lies that I believed in for so long, about food, supplements and exercise. Once I got over that initial feeling, I made the quantum leap 100% and began to feel very empowered with everything I have and am learning. Learning and implementing has turned my health and life around.

You're here and for a reason Jenelle. Go at your own pace and enjoy the journey and eventually you will get there.

### Jenelle — 2016-02-21T17:14:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189525>

Thank you, Rob and Mystic. I appreciate it!

I did get outside quite a bit yesterday ~ the weather was so nice. We took the dog for a walk around the neighborhood, and I spent some time outside cleaning up his messes -- not a fun job, but I did it in a _tank top._ Woo hoo!

I had oysters with my eggs & kale this morning, instead of tuna... Guess what? FINE! I didn't hate it at all. You put enough homemade mayo on just about anything... it's good.

I am going to try really hard to focus on the positives, and I may even start writing things down in an actual journal, to help with that. I don't need to dump all of my negativity on my husband, friends, or the rest of the innocent folks on the internet. I'd love to share positive things and spread _positive_ energy.

I'm going to start now by closing up this laptop, taking the rest of my coffee outside, and sitting down in the sun to **write**.

Hope you're all having a great weekend!

### Jenelle — 2016-02-22T18:20:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189579>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=189254)
>
> coffee = a person who is light stressed and has no proper melatonin recycling so they use caffeine to increase ECT speeds to do what missing melatonin can do. Time 9 breadcrumb.

Do you recommend for people to quit caffeine ~ 100% cold-turkey?

This is how I quit smoking almost 3 years ago. It really was the only way. I used various supplements, I subconsciously turned to sugar, I thought I would use exercise, but instead I STOPPED for awhile...

I did feel better once I got back to lifting, and I haven't turned back to cigarettes for ~whatever~ they were doing for me. But it has been a journey, for sure. Reading about nicotine here is a bit of a trigger for me... and for the first time in a long time, I smelled cigarette smoke in a parking lot yesterday and it actually stirred some things up that I'd rather not have stirred.

I have quit coffee many times, but I don't know that I have ever been 100% stimulant-free. Even through pregnancies... there was always either some sort of coffee, tea, chocolate, sugar. I would like to get to the place where I am not dependent on them ~ but when I try to remove one, I crave the others. I know you know exactly what I mean.

I'm just not sure what is the right way out of it.

Maybe just Baby Steps.

The time for my "morning" right now ~ once I get my breakfast, coffee, and get out there ~ is probably close to 10:00 am. ...I clearly have a few more baby steps to complete in this area.

I have no idea what I am getting at what time of day ... I just know that I haven't been getting any light this winter ~ except all the **wrong** kinds, apparently.

Today I tried to eat / read my book / and face the sun ... and yeah, the book had to be so close to my face that I probably wasn't getting a whole lot accomplished. Lol. ...But I was _outside,_ Rob!

### Jenelle — 2016-02-23T03:41:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189600>

There is a general idea here that you can improve your eyesight by no longer wearing these things. Multiple people reporting that they no longer need them. In order to do that, you've got to go without them. Hence, the "throw out your contacts and don't wear your glasses!" enthusiasm.

In my particular case, that is not really an option. So ~ I'm just doing what I can. I thought it was funny that I tried it this morning, and had to hold the book THAT close to my face... (I wished there was someone around to take a funny picture). So I just wanted to share that, so it is understood how blind I really am. I don't think the blind reading is something I will be doing on a regular basis.

### Sue-UK — 2016-02-23T10:44:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189623>

I've been watching a series about the brain .... From the episode that featured dopamine it said that there is a dopamine burst when something turns out better than expected which tells other parts of the brain to increase the value of that option. So a "high" from caffeine or alcohol might produce a drive to continue or repeat the experience ..... If the circuitry for "I want it now" is competing with the "think about the longer term consequences" circuitry, it can be tough. Apparently willpower is not something we exercise, it's energy we use up ... And it's difficult to run on empty.

The dot I've connected for myself is that on a full tank, (electrons) and getting my dopamine hit from things like light and cold, means that self medicating to try and raise dopamine with things like coffee, alcohol and chocolate just doesn't interest me much. Their value has dropped. The dopamine hit from light and cold, or things like a social interaction with someone that goes well has a much higher value, and is more energy efficient because there is no conflict between the "I want it now" and "think about the longer term consequences" circuits of the brain, so no energy loss to willpower. Well that's the theory ...

> [Jenelle said:](/goto/post?id=189581)
>
> One thing I have noticed since we started discussing it here: I will _want more_ coffee or tea when I do _not_ feel like I need it, in any way. I have to stop and talk myself out of it ... that some is good ... but more is NOT better. It is very similar to what sometimes happens with alcohol... I feel good, I've got a buzz or a high going on... and I just. want. more.

### Jenelle — 2016-02-23T16:39:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189653>

So, just a quick update for today, because I am _feeling really good_ and I've got stuff to do!

Yesterday:

~ I had oysters in my eggs for breakfast
~ a can of sardines in my salad for lunch
~ went to the tanning place for 4 min of UV
~ drank all of my coffee earlier in the day
~ got 5.39 miles of easy walking, according to my fitbit

(Let's discuss fitbits LATER, please! I am _not_ ready to ditch it.)

~ did not feel the call of tequila at bedtime
~ in bed, lights out before midnight
~ slept 8 hours, 24 minutes

Woke up today and had oysters in my eggs again. (Edited to say: I was outside with my breakfast by 9:03 today = improvement!)

Will be on the search today for any canned oysters in my area which say "no BPA" on the can. Discovered the ones I had say nothing about it... and they are from China. I don't know if that's super bad, but I'm guessing it's not good. I know fresh would be best... but ~ Baby Steps. Looked all over Costco for them, and didn't see an oyster in sight?

I am still working on Jack's book, fitting a few minutes in wherever I can. Yesterday, they told me I had to wait for 16 minutes at the tanning place ~ and I could have just kicked my own ass so much for not bringing it with me! I also just got "Blink" from the library... which I believe I have seen him recommend here. My Amazon cart and my library list is filling up again... and as my friend Rob knows, **this** is the real me! She's returning! She's here!

_Thank you all._ I mean it ~ I am thankful for each and every one of you who are here each day and sharing your stories. Things had grown stagnant with me, lately. I have been stuck in a rut. Spinning my wheels. Too focused on just the food part of things, but still not getting it right! I needed a new angle, for sure.

Anyway ~ I hope you all have a great day. I'm off to lift some heavy things!

I'll leave you with this nugget I dug up yesterday from Jack:

"Open your mind to change...........
You don't invoke moderation in a transformation. You can't be neutral on a moving train with a destination."

### Jenelle — 2016-02-24T07:25:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189694>

Baby Step #9 is that I have been drinking A LOT more water. I haven't even read about what is the ~right~ sort of water yet... But I am just more thirsty / more aware of it lately? Because of increased sunlight/tanning? Because of increased fish? I don't know... I also heard Jack on a podcast talk about how MUCH water he drinks. Anyway ~ I am definitely drinking more water than I had been previously.

My oyster search today did NOT go well. It appears, at our regular grocery store, my options are:
1) canned, from China, with no mention of BPA on the can
2) in a plastic tub, which clearly says "farmed", and no mention of BPA

Ugh. I left the store with no oysters whatsoever. I am not sure what is best to do here.

But I had my remaining oysters for breakfast ~ sardines for lunch ~ and salmon for dinner. This was super energizing for me -- like being buzzed up on coffee, but BETTER.

### Robert Haas — 2016-02-24T18:45:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189707>

> [Jenelle said:](/goto/post?id=189694)
>
> 2) in a plastic tub, which clearly says "farmed", and no mention of BPA

There's nothing to be afraid of since most domestic oysters are "farmed". Where I live they have many aquiculture areas in local bays that are set aside to raise oysters. They are in their native habitat in protected waters tested to be safe from disease. It's rare you'll find "wild caught" oysters since most have died off due to overharvesting and vibro (bacteria).

Also most are GMO to be disease resistant but again, nothing to be afraid there either.

[https://www.chesapeakebay.net/issues/issue/oysters](https://www.chesapeakebay.net/issues/issue/oysters)

### Sue-UK — 2016-02-24T19:04:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189709>

> [Jenelle said:](/goto/post?id=189693)
>
> I have read that one. I see that they are talking about doing it incrementally... But I don't have "reading glasses". I just have glasses and contacts ~ with the same prescription: -6.75 in one eye, -7.00 in the other. More than _twice as bad_ as what that person was dealing with. I am intrigued... but I can't say I am very hopeful on this one.

I'm still dealing with losing the vision in my right eye last September. I didn't need glasses before for reading (my eyesight wasn't perfect but my eyes sort of compensated for each other), but that changed overnight. As a short term measure I used a large magnifying glass to read normal size print, and printed out the blogs in print large enough to read without glasses for my left eye. I bought a pair of reading glasses at the airport so that I didn't have to use a magnifying glass reading a menu in a restaurant on holiday. But I sort of think that I would use a telescope to bring the stars and planets closer to view, but I could also just gaze at the stars ......so I try and read without glasses, but perch them right on the end of my nose to read if I need to, so that I still have natural light falling on my eyes. I have regained some sight back, using DHA, light water magnetism and eyeball CT, and I know I am helping protect and improve my left eye. (My greatest fear was losing sight in that eye too). I have better vision in both eyes outside on a bright clear day, with my bare feet on the grass. With that, my right eye can now see the blog page, and the black print, but I can't read it yet. But I will get there .....

### Jenelle — 2016-02-25T00:16:07+0200

I really think I have decided to purchase a Sperti. My tanning membership is done toward the end of May ... And besides that, I feel like having a unit at home is sort of crucial. For myself and maybe the whole family.

Today I woke up and it was raining. And I know ~ I have known for a long time ~ that I am very sensitive to this. I feel \*like a whole new person\* on sunny days. ### Penny — 2016-02-26T06:08:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189799>

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/melanin.17273/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/melanin.17273/)

This is an excellent talk showing you that light is the key to the eye clock mechanism and contains the answer to your question. They miss completely why the SCN is affected by temperature. They also miss why blind people are truly at a disadvantage. Without light dopamine levels are never optimized in the eye or brain. They do mention how the blind gut clocks become uncoupled from the SCN but never go into the detail I do about how this causes metabolic syndrome. It turns out the light is the stimulus humans use to proton tunnel, and proton tunneling controls the tyrosinase enzyme that makes ALL pigmented proteins in the body like dopamine, serotonin and melanin. Anything that brings protons or electrons closer in geometry lowers the energy barrier for them to tunnel. As a result this allows UV light to interact with tyrosine in the eye to create dopamine. Dopamine is linked with health and aging and eye fitness. The higher the endogenous dopamine level is the mor planar DHA becomes to act like a wire filled with electrons. This UV light stimulus also stimulates production of things like folate and B12 that make neuronal firing higher. Folate and B12 are made of amino residues that also need UV light to form properly. In the aging eye dopamine levels drop. In illness dopamine levels drop. Dopamine is fundamental in melanin synthesis in the RPE. Without melanin UV light can not be made photoelectrically. Taking exogenous dopamine or its analogues always leads to problems because the exogenous pathways are not specifically quantized to light interactions with the RPE in the retina. This is why our cousins, the chimps sleep 12 hours a day and humans only need 8 hours a day. It is a function of the dopamine levels being made in the eye tissues that generalize to the frontal eye fields and frontal lobes along the central retinal pathways. Chimps do not have well developed frontal lobes like humans do. The higher your dopamine levels are in the eye and brain the less sleep you need to regenerate. Chimps need more sleep than we do. This also links to their metabolic pathways because the central retinal pathways control both via the organization of the retina. This make the stimulus to UV light critical. Dopamine is made from tyrosine and tyrosine absorbs UV light 260-289 nm. Tyrosinase enzyme works via proton tunneling. This is how the gears of the eye clock fundamentally tick and this is what this BBC program is missing. The quantum details of light and temperature. This is what [www.jackkruse](http://www.jackkruse) give people. Details. [http://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/b06rzd44#play](http://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.bbc.co.uk%2Fprogrammes%2Fb06rzd44%23play&h=CAQEB_achAQGhCh43OFLxh4soGJxHP1n_f1MfCMXe-dvtUA&enc=AZMD4JW1HctaMFD_V8XtpLcm8piNpuBaSV9p0bnoV75RpWmy1XLv4yshEv8Bo2Bw71msHqILZYkuWbDq4TDWIvIAa15uT_QTxA-wybDLyRNg-1ZhCr1QM9uJVTReuMOttF3w9U5YcIo4lNuduOg_ilOmkRf29-jwTW6Dbj68VigCaQAvCVm2V5cZEPBKZgcm2ObiTKWBdZ8BsKE7BcLk7g2Y&s=1)

Expose your eyes to the morning sun without anything on them - eat seafood beforehand - face dunk in cold water - oysters contain zinc which is great for your eyes - they also feed the gut flora - although I think that works better when raw - gut flora make neurotransmitters:

[http://www.theatlantic.com/health/archive/2015/06/gut-bacteria-on-the-brain/395918/](http://www.theatlantic.com/health/archive/2015/06/gut-bacteria-on-the-brain/395918/)
Almonds are great for feeding the gut flora...

Here is some great info on melanin and your eyes :
[http://beforeitsnews.com/free-energy/2011/05/could-human-photosynthesis-power-the-future-609065.html](http://beforeitsnews.com/free-energy/2011/05/could-human-photosynthesis-power-the-future-609065.html)

nicotine followed by sunlight might be a good move for you although not if you quit smoking probably - you could do the tyrosine/methylene blue hack:
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...lasmy-bio-hack-results-nicotine-and-mb.17445/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/2015-heteroplasmy-bio-hack-results-nicotine-and-mb.17445/)

### Jenelle — 2016-02-26T06:52:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189801>

Wow, Penny ~ **thanks** for all of that info! I will definitely check out these links and respond more later. I truly appreciate the time you took to post here.

There is so much great information coming to me right now... Everyone, please know that I DO go back and re-read this thread when I have more time, or when I am in a different mood ~ to try to reflect, make sure I haven't missed anything, and absorb things better. If I don't respond right away ~ I _will_ respond eventually.

Cpt ~ If moving were an option for us, I would sooooo GO. I know, I know... I saw someone post here: _"Can't means won't"_ ~ and that is powerful, and true. So I am not going to say that we _can't._ For now I will just say that it does not look likely in the near future.

\-----

We had kind of a screwy day today, and I did not get to eat as much as I should have, nor have as much DHA going in. By the time I was getting the kids to bed tonight, I was feeling it. Just ~ _pissed._

MOLD has been discovered along one wall of our bedroom carpet... the dog has brought it to our attention with his digging over the past couple of months. Well.... guess who is feeling better and was finally ready to tackle it today?

[](http://smg.photobucket.com/user/wja/media/IMG_0867.jpg.html)

LOL. Yup... **me**.

I worked so hard today... I tore out the carpet and the padding in that area, I meticulously scraped the concrete slab to remove all traces of the foam padding that was stuck to it, tore out the rotted tack strip, the moldy trim... vacuumed like a madwoman, trying to suck all of this stuff up and get it OUT of our house.

I don't even know where I am going with this... The plumber is coming early tomorrow to fix what is ~probably~ a slow leak of some sort going on in the wall...

I think my point of posting this is to say that I am feeling _really good_ ~ capable and strong again. Confident and ready to tackle things.

I'd like to thank my friend Rob for helping me get to this point. Because... a couple of weeks ago, I was feeling pretty down and defeated, honestly. Just NOT like myself. Fish and sunlight... sunlight and fish... this is what I was missing. I already had the keto diet down pat ~ but I needed _more._

### Starfish Prime — 2016-02-27T01:47:42+0200

>
> Jenelle, if you are no longer light stressed, and are recycling melatonin properly, _you might find that your need for caffeine etc just disappears ...... No need for cold turkey ..... No willpower required ...... My experience with sugar and caffeine ... Light water magnetism_ .....

I can vouch for this-I was a faithful coffee drinker for years...and got the light...not right, but better-and coffee just doesn't sound good anymore. I forgot to drink it one day-and the next, and the next, and next thing you know it was a month and no headaches or anything! Sugar was kind of the same way-it's just not appetizing.

More DHA and more light = more dopamine!<--From the tone of your post it sounds like you're moving the right direction!

I sympathize with the contacts-I wore RGP's for years (-6.00 here..with serious astigamtism!) and my vanity cried big alligator tears at the thought of losing them. But I want the A more (and besides, my hair has gotten curlier doing this and curls>contacts for me). Take the specs off in the sun every chance you get  I walk to the office/car without them-granted, it's a quiet street, but I can make out the sidewalk-mostly

I am a bit north of you-and I broke down and ordered a Sperti on Wednesday-should be here monday. I'll keep you posted on how it works!

How is your home for wifi/nnEMF other than the shall-not-be-mentioned-fitbit?

It can get overwhelming...but it's worth it! Glad you got the mold issue ID'd-that's a nasty problem!

### Jenelle — 2016-02-27T07:54:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189857>

> [Starfish Prime said:](/goto/post?id=189846)
>
> But I want the A more (and besides, my hair has gotten curlier doing this and curls>contacts for me).

Hey Laurel ~ thanks for stopping in!

^ I have read this part again and again, and I feel dumb, but... can you explain it further? I'm not sure what you mean by any of it.

Even though no one answered, I think what I'm picking up on is that folks are ordering the Sperti Vitamin D unit. I have not informed my husband that I've made up my mind about this yet ~ but I think I have. It is unrealistic for me to get to the tanning bed every day -- and we do NOT have sunshine here every day. I do understand that we get a certain level of exposure, rain or shine -- and I also know that I'm not going to go outside and sit in the rain or snow. It's just not happening. But if I have the Sperti here at home ~ there is NO excuse. Like, none.

I am not finished reading Jack's book yet, so I really haven't gotten to the details about nnEMF, CT, or what sort of water he recommends. These are things I can see like tiny specks on the horizon right now... I know they are coming, I just haven't gotten there, yet.

We have wifi at our house... and the kids do use the internet for a lot of their curriculum/learning. It's not realistic for us to have it shut off during the day -- but I HAVE been killing the power to it and all of the other TV/electronics every night since I started posting here.

Yeah, the mold issue... Found out today that it is not the plumbing at all, but water getting through tiny, hairline cracks in the grout between the shower tiles... running down through the wall and into our bedroom.  So unfortunately, he was not able to fix it today. We have to decide if we want to have the tile/grout re-done (again) or if we want to replace it with a fiberglass shower insert and be done with it. I am glad I got all of the carpet, padding, tack strip, etc out of that area ~ and glad that he did NOT see any funky nastiness going on _inside_ our walls. I can live with half carpet, half concrete slab in my bedroom for awhile... I canNOT live with mold festering in there.

\---------

I got to go to lunch with a friend today, and we talked about the trips they have taken to Mexico. She directed me to a website where they have booked their package deals for their past couple of trips.

I really want to go now.

This last time, they stayed at an "eco resort" in the Cancun area (Playa del Carmen) ... animals, and jungle ~ and ALL-INCLUSIVE......... We have never done anything like that. I will officially be daydreaming about it until it finally happens.

[http://www.sandos.com/hotel-sandos-caracol-in-playa-del-carmen.htm](http://www.sandos.com/hotel-sandos-caracol-in-playa-del-carmen.htm)

I need to go back and get a new Rx. I'm already legal to drive with my first pair of computer glasses and my vision is way too sharp in them.

### Starfish Prime — 2016-02-27T17:07:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189873>

I meant that I'm not inclined to settle for average or just ok when excellence is on the table. I want to thrive and I want my boys to thrive-not just survive being part of the flock of lemmings falling off the cliff. And as far as the curls bit-it's silly....all my life I've envied the girls with the clouds of curls and the more I stick with this the curlier my hair gets. I'd rather wear glasses and have curls than wear contacts and have lank, anemic waves. So  it is.

I think the reason no one really specifically answered re the Sperti is that context is THE key-what's right for he may be the exact opposite of what you need to find optimal. Everyone needs the basics-sun, water, nnEMF reduction, but how much and how critical it is are very individual.

And not to overwhelm you, but nnEMF isn't looming on the horizon, it's the 800 lb gorilla in the corner hiding under a lampshade. Does it have to be wifi that the kids use or could you simply hardware it and kill the wireless router?vFood for thought

### Jenelle — 2016-02-27T17:41:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189874>

> [Starfish Prime said:](/goto/post?id=189873)
>
> And not to overwhelm you, but nnEMF isn't looming on the horizon, it's the 800 lb gorilla in the corner hiding under a lampshade. Does it have to be wifi that the kids use or could you simply hardware it and kill the wireless router?vFood for thought

No ~ I gotcha ~ I do realize that we are living in it every day. And have been for years.

But there are only so many hours in the day, and I can only make so many of these baby steps at a time. The word "overwhelm" is accurate in this case, if I let myself go there...

I have considered going back to cable. We are not happy with the wi-fi service we have, nor the bill. We just talked about it a little bit last night, in fact.

The idea of our laptops having to be plugged into a wall for an internet connection is just so ... well, depressing, to be honest. I **love** being able to carry this thing to any room of the house and have that internet access at our fingertips. My kids aren't in school ~ so the whole world is their classroom. If I don't know the answer to something, or we want to know more about something -- we look it up. That happens anywhere, everywhere... _all the time.
_
I am not making excuses. I do understand that it's a problem. It is a "speck on the horizon" for me right now because I haven't learned enough about it yet to make an educated decision. I need to really understand it, and be able to explain it to the other members of my family if we are going to make a huge and very inconvenient change.

When I see people here talking about smart meters -- where do you get such a thing?

And is there an internet link for how much nnEMF there is in certain places? I have seen Jack talk about some cities being worse than others, etc... but where do you find this information?

### Lahelada — 2016-02-28T02:09:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17568/post-189884>

I hope this helps .

I have considered going back to cable. We are not happy with the wi-fi service we have, nor the bill. You could wire the internet to more than just one point in the house. It is better that you do not carry the laptop on you though so having it rest on a surface with your body away from it is a good thing. It is also better if the laptop is not plugged in the mains when you are using it . Plug it in for charging only and leave it akone when it is charging

When I see people here talking about smart meters -- where do you get such a thing? A smartmeter is what you DO NOT want. It is the electricity meter that sends info back to the electricity company via wifi automatically every so often ,very often,,,, You are probably confusing it with the EMF trimeter people are using to find out what is happening in their environment

And is there an internet link for how much nnEMF there is in certain places? I have seen Jack talk about some cities being worse than others, etc... but where do you find this information? Yes there is but not an easy read one . It is a picture that you have to piece together\[/. antennasearch.com and opensignal.com are good sites but they only give you half the story

How are you doing with blue light mitigation ?

The biggest thing to worry about is your cordless phone (land line) with DECT technology. (Most are DECT these days) They are the very WORST for nnEMF! They pulse 24/7.
When my meter came in the mail last year and I turned it on I went "holy shit! Where is THAT signal coming from?"
It was the cordless phone base unit (s).

Best option- Get a corded land line

Next best - Get an older style 600Mhz cordless phone

Next best - Trim the antennas off of the DECT phone base
