Saichi are you not concerned about the prevailing wind pushing the worst of the radiation over the Americas? Some say the worst of it is going away from Japan and all ending up all over the Americas but north and north of South America. Like revenge for the two nukes dropped.

I wonder how the harm from Fukushima radiation compares to the harm from other things?

### Saichi — 2019-06-11T08:42:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-in-order-of-optimality-help.17581/post-272428>

> [Solidsilverteeth said:](/goto/post?id=272400)
>
> Saichi are you not concerned about the prevailing wind pushing the worst of the radiation over the Americas? Some say the worst of it is going away from Japan and all ending up all over the Americas but north and north of South America. Like revenge for the two nukes dropped.
>
> I wonder how the harm from Fukushima radiation compares to the harm from other things?

The aerial fallout was almost entirely in the first few months after the accident before they could begin recooling the freshly spent storage pools and melt-through reactor cores. Yes the radiation went all over the states but it was mostly a concern for the northwestern states, I mentioned the precautions I took which I believe were sufficient. After the first few months all the rads have been flowing directly into the ocean. Before a year or two after March 2011 we used to see families of seals on the beach during every coastal afternoon walk. Since about 2013/14 we've seen none on the sand bars, literally none. I followed the independent news reporting the die-off of seals all along the western coast so it came as no surprise to me. I have to say I did see my first seal in years little over a week ago when I was doing CT in the ocean around 7am, about 15 meters from me had the head up out of the water staring at me like an alien. I really cherish any moments with wildlife now.

On a slight tangent we used to have a swarm of non-migrating birds in our backyard every morning chirping up a frenzy every day of the year for almost a decade. We noticed in hindsight about the time PG&E installed the 5G cabling in our neighborhood couple years ago the birds all disappeared. We used to get annoyed about not being able to get back to sleep in the early mornings, before we knew of circadian natural law, but now it's eerily quiet. Only semi-resident birds we have left is a bluejay and one woodpecker which is nothing compared to the amazing chorus we used to have. I have friends who've also noticed the disappearance of songbirds from their yards.

The sorrow can be overwhelming at times.

### mrc — 2019-09-21T23:24:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seafood-in-order-of-optimality-help.17581/post-278508>

**[Dr. Jack Kruse](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/?__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARA2IHE4mJPX8yPUhv2Lhwq1esp-VY96aq0hrJ1nZJThlQHAM2XUJWbVI96MLKEiMnhBTgqduGuV1JE85BeswSg1duXDrm9vbXNN7tdgmhV4LEMj_Bf5bc1kUrs6lPez6msRJ8I8ccorp20S_-Y8adUvqCHT9GvvWQJ86szGLSvHRNYnl4s86SOxSbl4NgsBy656KfEHbEktFeqI5qMvH8p6rOk7NzeAY_LfcRTf28iz1XH4HJumaY0jkLdihc__I3dirKTewDmlZPzWng_2eENmmIG0ASEdj7p616g0znBldmAbwgyTXKIJ7cwVG55BKvrWHQFR-FlrqHyZNEwZjUpJEw&__xts__%5B1%5D=68.ARBsubVfnnv7e0sFS3huePa5o_NarZN5wKxfo16PBIrSeAP9sZIHg348yav_UuPtrKCwCZAQS3NWPMRiKZQxUVYVtEVwJOv_gSRPEtUQzPNnq2HEaWiVMHCCP5ZE4CZvHNc3fazFrpZmm3zHYHOpLT1RNsb-a_HlcDruJzbJ8O2lHNxb-ysvTozrqrtTD4trjJOVUs8buPW2GfExgbO0aKccXORymelnt16ySPQNeaRPIia9D88ONfsWCmU_x95PKM2-dQcqVMP9van7q2WMs19B7DMJx4JyaVh0LJrhX13IX9i6snTT7aYrYWcicJnz5geBeZg3fR9TTg9J5BUsrgmyzg&__tn__=kC-R&eid=ARDzjn7beyI_GLm0jDibwN6jSV8ncM5G8dfjiOeDdfggoAhigyDvLnyJUa078rDbvCLzUe_eFmvWIAd8&hc_ref=ARR0NxL7ljsvtlT1lO8pUcyH11ioAGTkIxMi1_a4VKfNF4oSSKsQPJj4A4KwBTl56wo&fref=nf)** [1 March 2016](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/1191766344221051?__xts__%5B0%5D=68.ARBsubVfnnv7e0sFS3huePa5o_NarZN5wKxfo16PBIrSeAP9sZIHg348yav_UuPtrKCwCZAQS3NWPMRiKZQxUVYVtEVwJOv_gSRPEtUQzPNnq2HEaWiVMHCCP5ZE4CZvHNc3fazFrpZmm3zHYHOpLT1RNsb-a_HlcDruJzbJ8O2lHNxb-ysvTozrqrtTD4trjJOVUs8buPW2GfExgbO0aKccXORymelnt16ySPQNeaRPIia9D88ONfsWCmU_x95PKM2-dQcqVMP9van7q2WMs19B7DMJx4JyaVh0LJrhX13IX9i6snTT7aYrYWcicJnz5geBeZg3fR9TTg9J5BUsrgmyzg&__tn__=-R)
The topic of radiation in seafood is overrated and misunderstood (few know about hormesis and transcription factors like PARP1 and Ku70). Sharing this for people who say, "I'm not sure about eating seafood due to Fukushima."
[https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/1191766344221051](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/1191766344221051)

"Science evolves even if your thinking remains stagnant. We must expand our grip of science…………or risk falling from the cliff.
I tell this to my patients and all the alternative functional medicine doctors who keep telling their patients seafood is bad.
It is really bad advice based on outdated thinking.

Radiation has been condemned by the entire scientific community as being a “dangerous bar”.
This is surprising, however, since there is strong scientific evidence for the molecular mechanisms of how transient “radiation drinks” could promote health.
I tell my patient's seafood radiation has many surprising benefits. Today we know that low doses of radiation stimulate DNA repair through the activation of four transcription factors, PARP-1, PARP2, ATM, and Ku70.
In response to low dose, sublethal radiation (or xenohormetic compounds). PARP-1 and PARP-2 activate two DNA repair pathways called “Single strand break repair” (SSBR) and one called “Base Excision Repair” (BER).
Both of these repair single-stranded DNA breaks. In response to low dose, sublethal radiation (or xenohormetic compounds), Ku-70 activates the DNA repair pathway called “Non-homologous end joining” (NHEJ).
[https://scholar.google.com/scholar?...HN68hO6ZS3W9Rxrh790od9\_cMqMeBwE-9dNElXxR3Ar78](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=radiation%20DNA%20repair%20parp1%20parp2&btnG=&hl=en&as_sdt=0%2C22&as_vis=1&fbclid=IwAR2RH0-rfHOLTSHN68hO6ZS3W9Rxrh790od9_cMqMeBwE-9dNElXxR3Ar78)

Hello everyone

I need some suggestions regarding fats and seasons. I had an brain tumour removed last summer, and they took away everything. I'm doing a raw ketogenic fish diet, with cold showers, cold river baths, walking in shorts outside every morning and try to harvest much full spec light and sun. The feeling is amazing, I have more and more energy, the sky is the limit?? Its filling me up with electrons and energy.

I'm interested in Johanna Budwig and her great knowledge about electron rich diet and her protocol. So if someone have some experience with this AND JK protocols, CONTACT ME!!

It's winter here in Norway and I try to follow season, so stopped coconutoil, olive.(maybe too early for following seasons like this??) so I'm using mostly raw animal fats with some siberian pine nut oil. But today I start using the Budwig mixture of quark, flaxoil.

My questions is should I stick to season, or start consuming raw coconutoil, olive oil, MCT oil to get better healing?

I'm considering starting using nicotin gum to boost mitochondira function, but don't know yet. Hopefully someone here can give me more insight of why this can help me in this situation in healing my brain for cancer cells.

Have a good weekend everyone !

### rlee314 — 2016-02-21T02:38:35+0200

so...i'm 7 months post birth and exclusively breastfeeding my baby.

in light of this article i've noticed something in regards to my pregnancy and the weight loss post birth. during pregnancy i gained more in my hips, thighs and butt than my previous pregnancy. i was epi paleo prior to and all throughout this pregnancy. previous pregnancy i wasn't-but still ate a lot of fish.
both times post birth i lose weight rapidly. getting back to prepregnancy weight quickly--and i do not 'work' at doing this. i'm still epi paleo post birth and i eat a lot of fat. BAB etc. however--i am losing a lot of weight and size from my hips, thighs and butt!! i'm not sure i can eat more fish-i eat it 2-3xday.
i'm not a hipy or big butt girl to begin with...
anyhow...any ideas?

### rlee314 — 2016-02-24T22:32:23+0200

after breakfast we walk at the beach barefoot most days.
we do beach days 1-3x/week.
go to parks etc.
i stopped wearing my contacts several months ago.

it's just weird. my fat friends w/big asses who don't eat nearly as much dha as i do nor do they get as much light as i do...they wear sunscreen and sunglasses etc...but, they have big asses and hips and thighs. and i have lost a ton of weight in these areas-not trying-and eat at minimum seafood 2x/day and have done that since 3 months prior to conception and 90% of pregnancy.

maybe she's just sucking all the DHA from me? i don't know!

# Thread: optimal 1st foods for baby
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-1st-foods-for-baby.17587/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### rlee314 — 2016-02-21T02:41:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-1st-foods-for-baby.17587/post-189489>

hi all. me again.
i'm 7 months post birth and considering introducing solids to my baby. with my 1st child the first food was coconut oil followed by avocado followed by egg yolk and squash.
we live in hawai'i so fruits are abundant.
she is getting her first 2 middle bottom teeth.
i have given her tastes of poi from my plate--poi is a traditional 1st food for hawai'ian babies. it's fermented taro.
[http://www.huffingtonpost.com/2013/11/28/poi-baby-food\_n\_4353758.html](http://www.huffingtonpost.com/2013/11/28/poi-baby-food_n_4353758.html)

any ideas or suggestions for 1st foods?
thank you!

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-21T02:48:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-1st-foods-for-baby.17587/post-189491>

Why would you do anything different for them? what you eat they should eat........they need mostly fat for brain growth.

### rlee314 — 2016-02-24T22:25:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-1st-foods-for-baby.17587/post-189724>

thank you. i don't know why i was getting so hung up on it? i reread some blogs about how they/we don't really need to masticate the food so well.

she's liking salmon and the poi so far. and coconut oil. i make pretty fatty milk so she's still getting a lot of that.

# Thread: Dopamine and exercise

### Cpt.Tired — 2016-02-21T04:15:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dopamine-and-exercise.17588/post-189494>

Exercise is probably one of the most well known boosters of dopamine but is there a specific type of exercise that is optimal? (aerobic or anaerobic)
I know that multi-joint exercises like squats with heavy weights can boost hormone production which requires dopamine to regulate.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-21T04:24:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dopamine-and-exercise.17588/post-189495>

Exercise works best outdoors when UV light is present to build dopamine and melatonin at the same time........in this way when both coupled cycles are built properly the % heteroplasmy in mitochondria is lowered. You make better engines to transform energy properly.

Exercise has nothing to do with anything else. It lowers heteroplasmy to optimize you. If you exercise indoors you defeat the purpose and in many ways make things worse. That is buried into Ubi 24, Time 6 and Time 9.

>
> Exercise has nothing to do with anything else. It lowers heteroplasmy to optimize you. If you exercise indoors you defeat the purpose and in many ways make things worse. That is buried into Ubi 24, Time 6 and Time 9.

So Jack, based on my context, would I benefit even more if I were to train outdoors more than twice a week to help lower my heteroplasmy and build more dopamine and melatonin? My concern is, overdoing it, even outdoors. After coming so far, I don't want to go backwards again, but I do enjoy my outdoor training sessions.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-21T16:09:44+0200

My concern is, overdoing it, even outdoors. After coming so far, I don't want to go backwards again, but I do enjoy my outdoor training sessions.

I am also concerned about overdoing it, but it makes sense that exercise outdoors is safer simply because of the many ways that it improves our redox (sun, grounding, quality air etc.)
But I still wonder about what type of exercises gives us the most dopamine. If I were to guess, it would have to do with the amount of intensity.
Dopamine creates motivation. It takes a lot of motivation to exercise at high intensity. So pushing yourself hard probably creates the most dopamine.

# Thread: Night workers and ketosis?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-workers-and-ketosis.17590/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nomad — 2016-02-21T13:26:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-workers-and-ketosis.17590/post-189510>

I _**think**_ I remember seeing a YouTube video where Jack was talking about the detrimental affects of working night shifts, and I think he said that although the only real solution is to change jobs, a strict ketogenic diet can help combat the negative affects of working at night.

I have looked for some kind of reference to this theory but not managed to find it anywhere, which makes me think I may be getting my wires crossed. Can anyone confirm this either way?

And, does anyone else work night shifts here? If so, what do you do to combat the negative affects?

Unfortunately for me, night shifts are just a part of my career, no 9 to 5 in my line of work! I would eventually like to change career, but its going to take some time before thats a possibility.

I'm currently ketogenic and doing CT, and quite pleased with the improvements they has given in the past 5 months or so. But the lack of UV, grounding and messed up cardiac rhythm etc means I'm still missing out on huge potential to make further improvement. When I have the funds to do so, I will look at red light therapy, magnetic pillows, Quantlet, water filter/spring water source etc. Also planning to move somewhere with concrete floors and less non-native EMF (I currently live in an apartment block = too much wifi, and I guess concrete floors would provide a form of grounding if floors are tiled?) and I'm in the process of negotiating with my wife to stop using WiFi at home!

To my knowledge I don't have any _specific_ health problems, but I know I'm a long way from being at optimal health which I guess will inevitably lead to specific problems sooner or later. I just feel like I have little drive, its always a battle to wake up, I need more sleep than most, have little interest in social activities.. kind of feel a bit brain dead at times! CT & keto seems to have improved all of those issues somewhat.
But despite being quite fit, I sometimes get out of breath from everyday low-intensity activities, my arms will often go numb just from holding holding a book or phone which I guess points to poor circulation, and I have an annoyingly continuos tight or tense sensation on the right side of my body.. These have been niggling at me for some years now, and have yet to see any improvement from keto/CT.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-21T16:12:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-workers-and-ketosis.17590/post-189520>

It si not a theory.......shift work is very dangerous and it is well accepted in chorine biology. Read the data before making a judgement.

### Nomad — 2016-02-21T16:28:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-workers-and-ketosis.17590/post-189521>

Sorry, just to clarify.. I know that shift work is dangerous to health, I wasn't sure if I was correct in saying that although the only cure is to stop working at night, a ketogenic diet can help combat the side affects of it?

I thought I heard you say this in a video I once saw, but I maybe be getting mixed up with something else.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-21T20:40:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-workers-and-ketosis.17590/post-189538>

that is the Rx. Time 9 is specific why

### thomas — 2016-02-28T21:34:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-workers-and-ketosis.17590/post-189937>

Shit work LOL. If some entrepreneur proposed to cut your arm for money would you do it???

### Karl K — 2016-03-05T04:22:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-workers-and-ketosis.17590/post-190209>

Hey Nomad, I work night shifts on occasion. Ketogenic diet has been absolutely critical for my health and energy. Has helped incredibly. Sweet potatoes, bacon, eggs and coconut oil are my religion.

### Nomad — 2016-03-05T12:00:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-workers-and-ketosis.17590/post-190226>

> [Karl K said:](/goto/post?id=190209)
>
> Hey Nomad, I work night shifts on occasion. Ketogenic diet has been absolutely critical for my health and energy. Has helped incredibly. Sweet potatoes, bacon, eggs and coconut oil are my religion.

Yeah, it seems to be doing the job for me, but do you stay ketogenic throughout all seasons or just in winter to keep in line with the epi-paleo lifestyle?

Aren't those Sweet potatoes going to knock you out of ketosis?

### Karl K — 2016-03-05T18:46:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/night-workers-and-ketosis.17590/post-190238>

Sweet potatoes actually have a lower glycemic index (and are generally more nutritious) than regular potatoes as I understand. I don't see any reason to not try and stay ketogenic all year round given that I am highly carb resistant and have very sensitive blood sugar.

I'm having a hard time imagining a society in which there are no firefighter or police on call at night because it upsets their sleep cycles. I know guys that have been working night shifts for 30-40 years and have more energy than I do. Live and be as healthy as you can, but also don't take what you read here as the absolute truth. Nobody lives forever. Do what makes your life worth living, especially if it allows you to contribute to others meaningfully.

### JanSz — 2016-03-05T19:30:53+0200

only 15-20.000 years ago we were living with

no day, no night, constant light

relatively to what is available now,
no Circadian Rhythm.

when in waiting mode

to change your lighting.

similar grid of lights at home.
Total darkness during sleep time.

# Thread: Greetings from Northern Arizona!

**Posts:** 12

### Bigknife7 — 2016-02-21T20:23:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-northern-arizona.17591/post-189533>

Hi Everyone and Dr. Kruse ~
My name is Molly and I've been watching videos, reading blog posts for several days now, and now I'm reading the forums. Thanks everyone for all of the great discussions!
I've started my morning sun exposure and my protein laden breakfast + more DHA (sardines), and I am working on decreasing the EMF/Blue light exposure. I haven't started CT yet. I don't have any current chronic health issues other than I get easily fatigued. I rarely have trouble falling asleep, but I usually wake up a couple of times during the night.
I live in a rural community, however it's compacted and "connected" enough to have cell towers and Wifi, with neighbors close enough to have their Wifi signals show up on my "list".
I live in northern Arizona, but at 6,800 ft. elevation. My question is, what role does my elevation play with my recommended sun exposure (time of day, duration, etc.). Also, being a 49 year old female and at a higher elevation, how does that affect my sensitivity to EMF/Blue light? I am mixed race Native American with a light olive skin tone.
Thanks for your insights and expertise!

### jackware — 2016-03-08T08:56:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greetings-from-northern-arizona.17591/post-190390>

Lived many years in Flagstaff and enjoyed the clean tasting tap water (believe it comes out of Lake Mary), cool and clean mountain air, and of course the altitude. It was before I knew about all this stuff on the blogs, and I was younger then (high school and college), but my health was always great when I lived there. Great cardio condition and I would always be out wearing shorts all the time, even in those cool evenings and morning, and the summertime breezes to go with that intense sun, man I'm missing it right now, writing about it!

### jackware — 2016-03-08T08:57:14+0200

# Thread: Toby's Journal to Optimal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tobys-journal-to-optimal.17593/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Toby King — 2016-02-22T14:36:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tobys-journal-to-optimal.17593/post-189565>

Hey everyone, so I decided to just get started on my optimal journal, here is some background from an earlier post:

\----------------------------------------------------------------------

I am 16, and have had many issues with health for quite a while. After living in Southeast Asia for four years, I developed many problems with adrenal fatigue, nausea and headaches every day, and took around a year off of school. I started working with a naturopath and changing my diet/lifestyle, I have been strict paleo for 12 months, but even though I have had some success I still am struggling with quite a few things:

-   Adrenal fatigue - Cortisol levels of Morning 6.0, Midday 4.2, Afternoon 1.4, Night 0.3
-   Low Testosterone - 284 pmol/L
-   Estradiol at 72 pmol/L
-   Estrone 6.0 pg/ml
-   Lack of energy
-   Still a higher body fat than I would like

After scouring many of Dr. Kruse's blogs (Thank you so much by the way!) I have been working intently on changing my environment. I have been avoiding all blue light after sunset for the past few months. E.g. Blublockers/F.lux/Red LED strips throughout the house. Have been on the Leptin reset starting my day with the BAB for around a week now, quite a change from the Bulletproof coffee/intermittent fasting of the past year.

I have been experimenting with CT for more than 6 months, but am starting to have issues with worrying parents and my naturopath is very concerned that it is damaging to my adrenals. I love CT, but my parents seem to think that it has attributed to my low cortisol. I would love some advice for convincing them that it is a power for healing.

I have been following the Epi-paleo diet for a while now, unfortunately I can't stand the oysters... Any time I try to eat them I retch  Luckily here in Australia we have found a source of Canadian wild caught salmon that I consume as much of as possible.

After reading the Adrenal Rx I am starting my day with 5-20 minutes of sunshine for my eye-clock, and try to get as much sun throughout the day as I can.

\----------------------------------------------------------------------

Now onto current day:

I am in week four of my final year of school, and I think one of my biggest challenges is dealing with the large amounts of emf that come from every single class room having its own wifi unit, and every single teenager with their own phone on, combined with the horrible fluorescent lighting that fills every room. After reading Jack's post about back to school headaches as an epidemic I think I am starting to see why my cortisol and testosterone are tanked.

To try to combat all mentioned above:

\- I have hard wired my entire house with ethernet, so that we have no wifi on at any time, when I am not at school.

\- Every BAB I have 5-6 pastured organic DHA rich eggs I source from our friends with chickens

\- I am consuming as much of my electrons as seafood as possible, we have access luckily to both organic farmed salmon, and wild caught canadian salmon frozen and shipped here to Aus. Unfortunatly oysters make me retch, and sardines aren't much better, and I am doing mainly salmon, and tuna at the moment.

\- I have been working on light as much as possible right now, exposing my retina and skin to it every morning, and afternoon, before and after school, and on the weekends I try to get as much sun as I can without any intense burns. In this respect I can feel that my body is starting to deal with sunlight better, as it takes a lot more time than it used to for me to get burnt, even in this strong UV Aussie sun.

\- We have a cafe which I can utilise to purchase san pellagrino wholesale, and I try to drink at least two 1.5L bottles of san pellagrino a day

\- I just finally made the decision to buy a freezer to get more serious with CT, I think it has helped me, even with my low adrenal status.

\- My light environment at night is as red as I can make it, I have about three physical filters on my computer screen, plus flux, so there is nothing getting out of that.

\- Much to the chagrin of my classmates and teachers I have taken to wearing either gunnars, or sunglasses, in class to try to combat against the fluorescent lighting.

\- I guess it just took a little bit of time but my hunger cravings have easily passed, and I am finding it much easier to have an early fat/protein dinner at least 4 hours before bed.

\- I have stopped pretty much all supplementation, although I am considering getting another IV vitamin C 15grams, as I had it a couple of months ago, and felt a huge burst of energy. I would love to see the comments and opinions on this from all from the forum.

\- I signed up as a gold member, and have been making my way through the webinars, attempting to use as much of the information as I can

\- I have a SIBO test ordered, and should arrive soon, as I would like to see if that has cleared up yet, as I had a positive result about 10 months ago, and dealt with it herbally and with oregano oil and the like.

\- One of the biggest wins for me is if I can get into nature. I am blessed to live on a 65 acre property with waterfalls, creeks, big beautiful trees, and lots of area to explore with my dog. Any time I spend there is time I cherish.

Another issue that I am still dealing with is higher than preferable body fat, and I am hoping that the leptin rx with ct protocol combination and some biweekly 5-10minute HIIT weight training will work to better that part.

Thank you awesome people!

Toby

# Thread: Greeting from Rochester, NY!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greeting-from-rochester-ny.17597/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kingers — 2016-02-23T15:25:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greeting-from-rochester-ny.17597/post-189637>

Hi everyone,

My wife and I live in upstate NY, specifically in the Rochester area. I've been a health coach for a number of years and am fascinated with the information presented by Dr. Kruse.

Over the past few weeks, I've started my first n=1 experiment. At night I have started to use candles to light the house and use orange tinted glasses to block out any of the other blue lights from devices/appliances/etc. In addition, I've been turning off the circuits to our bed room as much as I can. We were sleeping with our bed right against the wall, with sockets directly near our heads. After just a few days, I was and still am dreaming substantially more every single night. I'm taking this as a good sign. Time to do some reading on blue light, melatonin, EMF, and sleep.

Glad to be part of the crew!

Sincerely,
Ken

### Kingers — 2016-02-23T15:34:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greeting-from-rochester-ny.17597/post-189638>

I should add that I work as a software engineer so I'm particularly interested in hacking that part of the environment. We're planning on converting our current wifi set up (which is turned off when not in use) to a hardwired Ethernet set up for our home.

### caroline — 2016-02-23T22:49:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/greeting-from-rochester-ny.17597/post-189676>

Welcome Kingers......

# Thread: Finding purpose in life
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-purpose-in-life.17602/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 27
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### roebby — 2016-02-24T15:53:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-purpose-in-life.17602/post-189699>

Hi everyone

This is going to be a very long story about my life and the way i did to find out what i really want in life. This is a long story because i want you to have details so you can give proper advice.

I am struggling for a couple of years to find my purpose in life.
The many things i do in life doesn't give me satisfaction.

You can say it started around 2011...

I was in a relation and we just started to live together. There was no romance anymore and the relationship became dreadful and i ended it. Luckily enough i was welcome again at my parents.

This relation broke because of the fact that i was always horny and wanted sex all the time. that was the only think i liked in life, just make love and eat. For the rest of the day i was just tired and feeling disappointed and wanting more sex and more food.

After that moment my "search" began. I felt depressed and didn't know what i wanted out of life.I questioned the work i was doing so i changed workplaces up to 4 times a year. Allways the same problems, fight with the boss, wrong colleauges and stuff like that. And i can say for myself that i'm a really good guy, always try to help people, motivated when working for a boss, if i get money then i need to do my best.

But i never found a satisfying workplace.

All this brought up the idea that i needed more sex in my life, because that would satisfy me more, that was all i really enjoyed except eating. So i started to follow courses on how to seduce woman. I did this for almost 2 years. I went on bootcamps and even travelled thru europe for this. After some period of time, there was a little bit of succes, i could seduce some woman, (still not the hot ones) and take them to my home and have sex with them.

I thought my life was getting more and more complete. BUT like it was going with everything, i was not satisfied, i felt empty. It went even worse, after a good half year with this lifestyle, my libido was getting less and less and i didn't even enjoyed sex anymore. My erection went away and i was not horny at all.

That moment i was really hopeless, the only thing in life i loved, wasn't giving me satisfaction anymore. I had no idea what to do. The last "decent" relation i had ended after 4 months, i was not ready to stay with one woman. She took care of me like my mother but i was nog satisfied.

She gave me some advice to go see a woman in The Netherlands, that woman reads your energy and can help you figuring out things. So i did that and that is still happening for 2 years, every month i get one consulation. These consultation are helpful and sometimes very emotional, but i still can't find what i want in life.

I feel empty, no energy, i don't know what i like...

Around July 2013 my best friend and i had a crazy idea of starting an own business, We talked alot about being self employeed around that time.

So we did it, we started a company and i have to say, it was a succes. We where working hard, the money was coming and after 6 months we hired our first staff.

In September 2015, we had like 8 people working for us and all we had to do was managing.

But like it always went.... the first of september in 2015 i told my friend i wanted to leave the company and go on a search to findout what i really want (AGAIN). I was feeling bored again, no energy, lifeless, ...

So 1.09.2015 i sold my shares and planned to find out what i wanted in life.

In november 2015 i travelled to Thailand and Bali, those countries allured me a long time.
I visited the TAO garden and did alot of tao practices and healing workshops.

After a detox of 7 days, my path was clear and something was telling me that i needed to go back to Belgium before xmas. So i did and i was just back in time for the xmas meal with the family.

So now after a few months from this travels i still have the same feeling as in that relationship where everything went wrong.

I don't know what i want, i even don't know what i like. If you gave me all the money in the world, i don't know what to do....

My questions:

\- The fact that i don't know what i want, is that a sign that my health is not okay, hormones, ...?
\- Why is my libido gone?
\- Why do i feel so empty?

Apologize for the long story and my bad english.

I hope i will find some relieving answers on this forum and i allready thank everybody who wants to give advice.

Kind Regards

Robby

Passion follows focus.......and focus is what you dont have.......focus comes from one thing. High dopamine with high melatonin levels.

### Karl K — 2016-03-05T05:48:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-purpose-in-life.17602/post-190218>

Hi Roebby, dietary and lifestyle guidelines are all good and I take them very seriously too. As far as inner peace and mental clarity you are talking about your relationship to your thoughts and yourself. For this there is nothing I could recommend more than Byron Katie's "work" or self inquiry. I experienced years of anxiety, depression and searching myself. What I found from doing the inquiry meditation is that our unquestioned attachment to our own thinking can create a very dissatisfying life. Self inquiry is about noticing what you are thinking and projecting on to other people, noticing how your react to your own judgements etc. and turning it around... in other words living the advice that you give to others and also noticing that you are whatever you judge others to be. I hope this gives you some clarity. She has a school in Germany and in California and of course her website contains everything you need for free. Just google byron katie. Best of luck.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-03-21T05:43:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-purpose-in-life.17602/post-191237>

Passion and satisfaction go hand in hand Robbie, and without them, any happiness is only temporary, because there's nothing to make it last. You must care deeply about yourself to finally tap passion. Passion lies in all of us slowly burning. This fire, however must be contained otherwise few stop to warm themselves at it. Those transients help raise our dopamine Robbie. You goal in connecting with nature is to share that heat and stop allowing the passers-by only see a wisp of smoke. Bury your dead ends Robbie and tap love to find what turns your burn into a blaze. Hope is just a dulled passion for the possible yet to come. Tap that hope by doing Robbie until you find it. Once the rain starts falling in our life it's hard to tell it to stop because of your expectations. Caution is always the path to mediocrity Robbie and I have a sense that might be why you have not found your spark. Stop riding the middle ground and reach beyond by doing things you dont think you can. Gliding on the winds of life, creates a passionless mediocrity......and that is all most people think they can achieve from middle ground.

### roebby — 2016-04-06T10:28:29+0300

This morning i did a few bloodtests on my own, i measured my ketones, my glucose level en cholesterol panel. This scared me...

Glucose: 3.89 mmol/l (this is normally i think because i don't eat carbs)
Ketones: 2.7 mmol/l ( this looks normal to)

Chol: 7.86 mmol/l = 303 mg/dl
HDL: 2.12 mmol/l = 81.9 mg/dl
LDL: 5.18 mmol / l = 200 mg/dl (this scares me, way to high, i think)
TRI: 0.87 mmol/l = 77 mg/dl

Hi guys

I'm struggling with a situation here and i would like to have some feedback about wath to do.

For the last couple of weeks i'm struggling with my diet. Normally i follow a ketogenic diet, very strict, no carbs at all. I also try to eat 1 meal a day, around 5pm.

But lately i'm getiing tired of that, really lethargic. and after 3 or 4 days of doing this diet plan i faal back and eat lots and lots of carbs, espacially ice cream, cookies, rice, bread...

I don't want to put to much importance on my diet but this lethargic feeling is not helpful, it stops me from undertake things in life en become very lazy.

What i do feel, i pratice some tantric techniques, is that when i didn't ate for about 20 hours, i feel the energy better and i feel grateful that my next meal is comming. so it's very strange, most of the time lethargic feeling and sometimes i feel very good and not lethargic.

Does anyone has some experience with this?

Kind Regards

### Jack Kruse — 2016-06-24T16:07:52+0300

### Jack Kruse — 2016-06-24T16:10:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/finding-purpose-in-life.17602/post-197180>

Learn to deepen your keel. When the winds of life blow you'll remain resolute in your journey even when you have to tack backward a bit from your target. This is the messiness of life; we all carry multitudes, so we must at times drift with the shifts beneath us. We are complicated creatures, and ultimately, the balance comes from understanding that sometimes you are like a boat and other time you actually are the water below. Life wants us to be more water like. Flowing, flexible, soft and chaotic. Subtly powerful yet open. Wild and serene. Able to accept all changes, yet still led by the pull of steady tides we face. Why do we need to be more like water and less like the boat? Water is the vagina for sunlight's penis. It receives and memorizes the information in the sun's light. Your retina accepts that message and signals the SCN via a water network in the RPE which then send to to the local mitochondria which all change their oscillations to react to the message of your local quantum yield. So if your eye is seeing strong summer time sun your mitochondria are expecting electrons with strong photonic energy built in and you are not giving them this with a ketosis bias. When you create this mismatch you face more wind and go against the tides in your life. Factoid of the day: Limiting beliefs comes from an environment that gives our brain a low quantum yield of information. Low quantum yield means not enough sunlight. 'The sun is the most sophisticated information delivery service in existence'.........and you all need its messages to your eye and into your brain to teach you fundamentally what to do in just about any situation. When you listen to the messages your life becomes simple. You become like the hippo and don't have to be told what to eat of do because you already know. So the key is to do what you did Folks, step out into the light and bring as many with you as you can and watch how things improve without doing much at all.

# Thread: I'm.. So.. HUNGRY!!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-so-hungry.17603/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Allanah — 2016-02-25T02:10:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-so-hungry.17603/post-189731>

hi guys!
I am writing because I have a question.. I started the leptin Reset on January 1st and that's been going well, I know eat 2 meals per day, but I've been noticing that when I do eat, I never seem to get full! It's not like I eat a small salmon fillet or two eggs and am still hungry either. I can eat a salmon fillet, can of oysters, and 8 (yes, 8!) eggs and still feel like there's plenty of room. Today I ate a whole bag of a frozen seafood mixture ( minus the imitation crab), two eggs, a salmon fillet, two mushrooms, some almonds, and I will be having some coconut oil, and this is only supper! (and I STILL feel like I could eat a huge steak or a pound of seafood.. I'm a 15 year old girl, and I've been overweight all my life, but I've never noticed until now jjust how much I am capable Of eating.. And it's not a huge apatite, just a big stomach! (or so it feels..)

So here's my question. Do I eat "normal" sized meals like I'm used to, or eat until I'm full?

Also, any idea why this may be?

Thanks in advance!

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-25T02:41:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-so-hungry.17603/post-189732>

Hungry = disconnected from the sun and the magnetic field........why? Because you get free electrons from both. What does food give us? All foods are broken to free electrons for ECT. So if your hungry you have to make up for what youre not getting with food.

### Jenelle — 2016-02-25T03:37:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-so-hungry.17603/post-189734>

Allanah, how is your sleep, also?

Of course this is a sign that something(s) is out of balance.

Have you felt this way all along? If not ~ think about what is different now?

Also know that it is very common for women to need more food around that time of the month. Like, lots more. But no ~ it shouldn't be feeling that way all the time.

### Scompy — 2016-02-25T08:53:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-so-hungry.17603/post-189736>

> [Allanah said:](/goto/post?id=189731)
>
> hi guys!
> I am writing because I have a question.. I started the leptin Reset on January 1st and that's been going well, I know eat 2 meals per day, but I've been noticing that when I do eat, I never seem to get full! It's not like I eat a small salmon fillet or two eggs and am still hungry either. I can eat a salmon fillet, can of oysters, and 8 (yes, 8!) eggs and still feel like there's plenty of room. Today I ate a whole bag of a frozen seafood mixture ( minus the imitation crab), two eggs, a salmon fillet, two mushrooms, some almonds, and I will be having some coconut oil, and this is only supper! (and I STILL feel like I could eat a huge steak or a pound of seafood.. I'm a 15 year old girl, and I've been overweight all my life, but I've never noticed until now jjust how much I am capable Of eating.. And it's not a huge apatite, just a big stomach! (or so it feels..)
>
> So here's my question. Do I eat "normal" sized meals like I'm used to, or eat until I'm full?
>
> Also, any idea why this may be?
>
> Thanks in advance!

I've struggled with RAVENOUS, impossible hunger in the past--all solved now. My issues often had to do with being at work in massive nnEMF, then coming home and eating a lot of protein later in the evening. Jack is 100% correct with the lack of light and nnEMF. When I'm in an nnEMF area, I get very 'munchy', which makes a lot of sense because it depletes so many electrons. I find when I eat in an nnEMF environment, I can get into a loop of hunger too. (For this reason, I generally don't like to eat at all when I'm in nnEMF, period and this is why I have such a small lunch these days due to exposure at work.) My body really wants electron-filled water. Compensating with more higher-quality water throughout the day is a big step. It turned out for me that what I consumed (or didn't consume) in the morning and in the day made all of the difference by dinner. In thinking of my cell pH in the past, I was driving pH lower (acidic) throughout the day with the nnEMF and by dinner and then slamming on more acid (protein) would send me into super-hunger-mode.

Solutions: I found when I crank up my protein and fluids earlier in the day, I can deal easier with my appetite at dinner. I want to make sure to keep the protein under control down below 20 grams at night by shifting my fats much higher, but still eliminate the carbs. So having even 3 eggs pushes the protein grams up to 18g, and usually adding anything more can trigger appetite for me at least--ESPECIALLY when I hadn't consumed a lot of protein earlier in the day. This is what works for me: Breakfast is high protein, moderate fat, low-ish carb. Lunch is moderate fat, and lower carb and protein. But I typically keep my lunch down in overall size portion--maybe enough to tide me over, and have a lot of fluids. Dinner is high fat, low protein and lowest carb.

I should note that about 2-3 days a week, I might actually skip lunch altogether. I often do 50 - 80g of protein at breakfast, and typically do around 15g of protein at dinner (and I keep it to no more than 20g 'cause it can invoke more of a hunger reponse at night). Note that this time of year, I'm also eating ketotic, so my daily sugars are around 20-30 grams a day, and I get around half of that amount at breakfast, usually in the form of asparagus, garlic and/or grilled onion and sauces with pork, fish, eggs or chicken typically.

Avocado and MCTs can help to also curb appetite. Also, my best solution is making a large iced-drink and suck on the ice right with and after a meal. Within 5 to 10 minutes, any generated hunger usually subsides (like 98% of the time). The key takeaway is that **_I have to picture myself what I'll feel like 10 minutes later_**. **HUGE**: I feel a hunger pang, so instead of reaching for something else that can make the appetite even worse, I need to reach for the ice-water or an iced drink or even a tea like lemon balm to tide me over for that next 10 minutes.

This allows me to have larger breakfasts and dinners, but I still have to be very careful about the protein with dinner because it easily can stimulate hunger like carbs do. The #1 thing I've had to keep in mind is that glutamate can also stimulate a lof of hunger in me (and many people report this as well) and some studies have also shown insulin release with glutamate-only and no glucose present. However, this monitoring of glutamate is a bandaid to the root problem of a lack of UV light and also being in an nnEMF environment. I find glutamate specifically (not glutamic acid) affects me most in the winter when I don't get as much UV.

So this time of year in particular (winter), tomato sauces, mushrooms, peanuts, and cheese can invoke more hunger about 10-20 minutes later because they are all generally high in glutamate and I'm not very well UV assimilated yet (though I'm working on it well this week!). Parmesian, cheddar and high-protein, aged cheese in particular can intensify the hunger for this reason. Also, anything high-heat cooked will convert glutamic acid into glutamate...including all meats and fish. Beef jerky is the worst for me. Parmesean, sundried tomato and dried peas are equivalent to eating straight MSG. The best (and only) cheese-choice for me is grassfed feta because it's low protein and little glutamate, but I take care not to eat too much in one sitting.

I find there's a synergy with glucose and glutamate invoking an insulin response and therefore triggering appetite and driving a person to eat more to resolve excess insulin levels in the blood. That's the catch-22... eat more to compensate in the short term (often with some more carb/glucose), then an hour later, feel hunger again beacuse of unresolved insulin, etc. So it's best not to trigger it in the first place.  Also, I personally stay away from all nuts, even if they look like they've got a good profile because of carb content and lectins, and I've also found they can also do a number on my gut. Thought you may want some details based on what other people have discovered along life's way.

### Inger — 2016-02-25T09:39:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-so-hungry.17603/post-189741>

I can relate to this! I can eat HUGE amounts of food too......... I can digest it all so easily I am surprised myself huh.
I would just eat but the great stuff only, and then try to avoid nnEMF and be a lot outside as said above, it will naturally lower needs for food.
But I do eat when I feel like I need it. And I do not gain any weight from it even if crazy amounts. I just get warm, huh! The boyfriend cant understand at all how I can eat so much fat and do not get fat...lol
Just make sure to not eat late. If your last meal is before 5 PM you will feel way better, at least that is my experience. I sleep so sweetly then.

I watched the video with Wallace and the mithocondria.. I could not understand much but one thing I do got! He said that people way north have this mutation in their mithocondria that have them eat more and produce heat.... and that is totally me. Maybe you have that too?

### Lahelada — 2016-02-25T12:21:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-so-hungry.17603/post-189747>

@[Scompy](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/17718/) great post ! I really wanted to like it twice. You posted on Facebook about nnEMF what it looks like and does etc. I wonder if you could paste it somewhere on this site. It would be great if it was Jack Kruse searchable.

Allananah,sorry about the off topic in your journal. I would only add that adding butter maybe a good thing as you are still in winter. I also wonder if you are getting still hung up a little in the lo fat propaganda around us and unconsciously prefer protein over fat.

### Jenelle — 2016-02-26T07:07:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-so-hungry.17603/post-189802>

Another thought is that the leucine in eggs actually does invoke an insulin response. If you are eating EIGHT eggs in a single meal... this may be cause for concern.

Try limiting yourself to however many eggs you can hold in your hand (for most women, this is 3-4). Add sources of pure protein (meat) and fat (like coconut oil) until you reach satiety.

I am just throwing out ideas here. Scompy's post was great, and really helpful.

### Scompy — 2016-02-26T07:54:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/im-so-hungry.17603/post-189803>

> [Jenelle said:](/goto/post?id=189802)
>
> Another thought is that the leucine in eggs actually does invoke an insulin response. If you are eating EIGHT eggs in a single meal... this may be cause for concern.
>
> Try limiting yourself to however many eggs you can hold in your hand (for most women, this is 3-4). Add sources of pure protein (meat) and fat (like coconut oil) until you reach satiety.
>
> I am just throwing out ideas here. Scompy's post was great, and really helpful.

Yes! We all win when we use **un**conventional wisdom. Conventional wisdom is dead to me now. Do what works and leave what doesn't work in the past and take pride in past failures I say!

When I was failing due to a higher nnEMF and had a large dinner with 4+ eggs, it would definitely invoke an insulin response in me. So keeping to just 2-3 eggs was the bandaid until I could mitigate the nnEMF and get more sunlight. I also want to add here that **_food timing_** is very important I have found in practice. As a general rule of thumb, I've been eating all of my lower pH foods earlier in the day, and then minimizing them later in the day. I do this to complement the cortisol spike in the morning, but for also condensing at night.

Here is a section from the Time 2 blog:
"Magnetic fields are produced by the motion of electrical charges. A fundamental property of magnetic fields is that they exert forces on moving electrical charges. Magnetism is best visualized by putting a magnet under a paper and throwing iron filings on the paper. You can see the field the magnet generates by looking at the pattern of the iron filings. If you throw away the filings, and add new iron filings the exact same pattern will emerge showing you that the magnet, and not the atoms of iron, determine the shape of the field of action. **_This action is very similar to what the human body undergoes every night and day cycle. Its molecules are disturbed by daylight constantly, and are condensed back to their form at night._** Sunlight alters, disturbs, and uncondenses magnetic fields. Sometime atoms in us are lost and we have to replace them from our environment. We get a fresh supply of atoms from water, food, and from light. But thanks to the controlling forces within our cells, namely our mitochondrial electric and magnetic fields, new molecules and cells are rebuilt as before and arrange themselves in the same pattern as old atoms were."

When I analyze my previous behaviors and especially when I **_used_** to take supplements, it's so darn obvious that people sabotauge themselves in their thinking that they are doing the 'right thing' by throwing down some Vitamin supplements right before going to sleep. Vitamin B6 and B12 uncondense ... can make you more awake, alert, excited. There's a lot of B-vitamins in meats and egg. This is why there is great advice above in Inger's post too about an early dinner and 5pm. Not only do you get a bit more of a daily fast before the next breakfast, it helps baseline insulin and leptin long enough at night that the body may want to burn additional fat, and doesn't compete with circadian timing of condensing tissues. It's the same reason that if I have any caffeine whatsoever, it's always in the morning since it synergizes with uncondensing. The over-desire or addiction to caffeine is a bigger sign that dopamine is amiss...again, the lack of morning sunlight.

It was shocking awhile back when I learned we do most of our fat-burning while we sleep, and we exhale 83% of our fat via the lungs when it breaks down to CO2 and water. This is not the conventional wisdom society tells us. Most people eat a 20g sugar powerbar on one side of a workout, then another 20g powerbar on the other side and somehow believe the fat is being shredded. No, during the bulk of when I went from 240 pounds down to 170, it was my breath that was toxic in the morning to others. The moment I would eat anything for breakfast, the breath fumes would go away instantly. The keto-breath was a profound signal that ketones were being released through the lungs. Typically, it would kick in after 14 to 15 hours between the past night's dinner and breakfast. I am glad my family didn't kick me out of the house during that phase a few years back.

# Thread: Here we go!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-we-go.17609/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### J-O-H-N — 2016-02-27T06:15:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-we-go.17609/post-189852>

I stumbled on the blog about 4 years ago, but, after reading a few posts and feeling overwhelmed, didn't stay around. A little over a month ago, I happened upon the blog again as I was doing research on UV. This time around the information immediately clicked with me and I have been reading up with just about every free moment I have. Anyway, I decided to go ahead and start a thread for my journal as I finally got around to ordering a couple of labs.

A little background... For the most part, I had little health problems until a military tour to Iraq 2004-2005. While deployed I started experiencing uncontrollable anxiety and muscle tension and pain. Looking back there were probably a number of factors that lead to a decline in health... being forced to wear sunglasses, cover all exposed skin, working long night shifts, excessive blue light, high levels of nnEMF from radios (both in the vehicles we patrolled in and at our base camp). Once I came home I truly realized things weren't right. I couldn't sleep. I couldn't relax even if I tried. I started developing allergies and sensitivities to everything. My stomach began to hurt all the time. I couldn't remember anything. The list goes on and on... When I first came home and started seeking care I stayed mostly within the VA system, except for chiropractic and massage care. I tried every prescription under the sun (ha ha!) for depression, anxiety, pain, muscle tension, etc and spent about 7 years in group and individual counseling before I got tired of trying. Towards the end I worked with some non-VA "functional" doctors, but could never get any results. They all had some different sort of supplement they thought I should take and none of them helped... at all. I tried all sorts of crazy stuff. I even did a couple sessions with a shaman. After suffering for over years I finally came to the realization that mainstream and alternative medicine weren't going to help and, more or less, gave up. I stopped seeking outside help.

Now, here I am, 11 years down the line and suffering more than ever. My sleep is the worst it's ever been. I am in a constant state of fight or flight. My stomach aches all day and night and I have constant bloating. The skin on my scalp is extremely dry and flaky. I almost constantly have goosebumps--they seem to be triggered mostly by any sensation on the skin (contact, change in temperature, etc). I have crazy muscle tension all the time and cannot gain weight if I tried (and, oh, have I tried!). I also have lots of circulation problems. Each night my eyes and throat get so dry it's almost painful.

After several weeks of morning sun, just walking and looking in the direction of the sun, I noticed something shifted--for the first time I started to feel a connection with my children (currently 1 and 4). This for me was enough of an incentive to keep going. Since then I have continued the morning walks and have added in as many sun breaks as I can manage during the day (and as many barefoot as I can). I have also been eating nearly strictly seafood with fat and a few leafy green vegetables for the past month. For the first couple weeks I continued to take some supplements, but have since stopped. The progress has been slow, but I do think there is progress being made. Each day I am still in pain and a nervous, tense mess all day, but I hoping if I can get my hormones in check and keep up with the UV exposure (and cold currently) I might slowly be able to dig myself out of this hole.

Great progress! Remember that you didn't arrive in this condition overnite ... so it will take time to reclaim your health.

### Queen of Elves — 2016-03-02T15:29:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-alexander-wunsch-vimeo-and-homepage-site-good-stuff.17610/post-190077>

It was a great appointment with so much information, I couldn't fall asleep last night and still have to write some points down, not to miss any detail.
It was like talking to a friend who shares the same interests like me and so the time flew by and we had to stop, but I will visit him definitely again to clear some more details.
Introduced him to the work of Jack Kruse, which he wasn't familiar with but he said he noticed some visitors of his vimeo site came from jackkruse.com
Asked him to read Time 9 and he seemed interested, so I hope we can talk about it next time

We talked also about eyes/regeneration of the eyes and how they get harmed by monitors. He said the industry is already reacting to the topic of blue light, so for example the new
iOS will be delivered with "Night Shift" which will determine when it’s sunset in your location and shift the colors in the display like f.lux [http://www.apple.com/ios/preview/](http://www.apple.com/ios/preview/)
But the blue light isn't the only problem, it's also the flickering of the screen because of alternating current (if I remember that correctly) which you don't even recognize but it stresses your eyes and brain. He actually let me listen to light, natural light and artificial light, by using a device that made those amplitudes audible. Fascinating!

He showed me how hot blue light can get on the skin and what this means to eyes, staring for hours into such light and being surrounded by it...
We talked about melatonin and how much we need darkness, that 8 hours are not enough for our eyes to regenerate and that you have to assume, that if you are seeing colors after sunset, there is no regeneration possible, that the light hours ideally need to correlate to the dark hours, ...

We talked about facing no winter anymore, vitamin d, calcium - and he gave me green goggles to stimulate the pituitary if I have no time to walk in the forest
He also recommended that I try a therapy developed by a german MD who practices alternative medicine which sounds a bit weird , I will report more about that soon in my journal...

That's it so far, I'm looking towards my next appointment!

But the blue light isn't the only problem, it's also the flickering of the screen because of alternating current (if I remember that correctly) which you don't even recognize but it stresses your eyes and brain."

# Thread: Brain development and light is a mitochondrial heteroplasmy story.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-27T16:46:29+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-development-and-light-is-a-mitochondrial-heteroplasmy-story.17612/post-189872>

Take a good look at the picture. [http://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/art...ad-gigantic-eyes-bulbous-couldn-t-closed.html](http://www.dailymail.co.uk/news/article-3412269/Baby-girl-born-India-half-head-gigantic-eyes-bulbous-couldn-t-closed.html)

It might disturb you, but there is a lesson here about light. When the environment steals energy from the brain's development the eyes expand thermodynamically due to the light loss to try to capture more to build the brain below. In cases with anencephaly it cannot. This is not a food story at all. Most simple minded people will blame that because that is all they understand. This picture encapsulates [www.JackKruse.com.](http://www.jackkruse.com/) Energy dynamics are directly linked to epigenetic expression of the nuclear genome, but mitochondria in this child's brain must provide the currency of energy from light to do so. In this case, it cannot happen. Why? ELF-UV release is what determines differential epigenetic expression. It should appear to anyone reading the series of blogs on quantum engineering that human evolution could not have proceeded very far without finding a consistent solution to these energy and nutritional constraints placed upon our species by our brain. These have been massive area of foci in my blog. When I began the blog 5 years ago I jumped straight to mitochondria dynamics and I was just going to give people the details of % heteroplasmy and how it relates to disease generation. Disease = light loss from a cell. To remain well you need to retain energy, specifically light. There is also a lot of evidence now accumulated that in our world today filled with blue light and nnEMF our species is suffering suboptimal neural development. This is especially true in humans living on a diet that does not provide these substrates for ketosis to drive NAD+ higher and oxygen levels higher within the brain and gut. Energy dynamics of the mitochondria is directly linked the electric charge and electron tunneling speeds and proportional to epigenetic expression in that young developing brain.

There is also a lot of data over the last fifty years that an “altered field” exists now in our ionosphere that causes the same physiologic changes and symptoms common to many of these diseases as well as laid out by Andrew Marino, Robert Becker, Noro Volkow, David Sinclair, Doug Wallace, Jodi Nunnari, and Russ Addy. The field refers to the local environment that cells and the organism find themselves in and alters the way mitochondria can generate energy from electrons and protons. The field constantly changes as we move through the environment, and these changes have dramatic effects on mitochondria energy production and transformation. As one Angstrom increases between the respiratory proteins, there is a ten fold slowing of ECT speeds. This also results in massive ELF-UV light loss and the redox couple of cytochrome one collapses at NADH/NAD+. This lowers superoxide burst and relegates the mitochondria to an existence of poor energy transformation from light, magnetism, and food. That is where humans are in 2016. Expect more stories of neurodegeneration and abnormal brain development. That is what technology and fake light have in store for us.

### Denise Moore — 2016-03-03T18:26:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brain-development-and-light-is-a-mitochondrial-heteroplasmy-story.17612/post-190140>

When I flood him with healthy fats, DHA, natural light and purple light, he functions quite well. Right now we have an issue with the computer. He is in kindergarten and they already have their own laptops to do work in the class and the teacher uses "computer time" as a reward. She encouraged me to use "computer time" as a reward at home too. He gets a total of an hour of screen time a day. Getting him to put it away when his time is up is torture for him and I. I told the teacher no more and she said he "has to be on the computer because in third grade they do all of the testing and if he can't use the computer he will fail." There is a "Prometheus Screen" in the classroom, no more chalk board, the lighting, wifi... no wonder these kids are all crazy. He wears a weighted vest to school, blue glasses are next. I'm having a hard time finding them in a small enough size. Then, there's the food. This school serves 2 meals a day, all carb based. They think a yogurt, raisins and milk is a healthy meal. Thankfully, the staff is great and he is learning both book smarts and how to be around people. He wants to be an astronaut, builder, doctor, police officer.

# Thread: Going to sleep early
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-to-sleep-early.17615/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### thomas — 2016-02-28T16:21:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/going-to-sleep-early.17615/post-189918>

Something I wonder...
Why If I go to bed (relatively) late, I am destroyed the next day..
If I go early,(that's around 8pm..) then yeah, I can have the best sleep I have in my life, it almost feels like a superpower, But go 2 hours later and the next day it's like I am sick or something. At the same time others seem to have no problem going late everyday and loving life. I kinda did that in the past but I don't think I slept very well..but I wasn't destroyed either. Is it all due to using tons of stimulation and artificial light at night and avoiding the outdoors during the day?

You see, I love nightlife, it's great. And I would absolutely love being able to go out at night and have fun. But I absolutely hate being tired, and not having my general feeling of wellbeing during the day.
Is there a way to actually go out in the evening/night and somehow be fine the next day? Or is it just a forever choice of .. go out have fun and hate the next day.. or go early to bed and enjoy the next day.

Ran into lots of people cruising down the snowy shores and it was all smiles and love being passed along by everybody. My friend and I, Hershey, are really enjoying what Mother Nature is throwing at us!

I, first and foremost, would like to thank Dr. Kruse for providing this platform to myself and all the like minded individuals who share their stories on their path to optimal health and, ultimately, happiness. This cornucopia of health information has been a blessing in my life, and I cannot express in words how grateful I am that I have found this wonderful group of individuals to help me along my journey. I will share as many of my own experiences I can to do my part in giving back to this community, and hopefully be able to affect someone else's life in the same positive manner mine has been.

Peace and love from Canada.

Sam

# Thread: COLD EXPOSURE STUDY
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-exposure-study.17617/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Q. C. — 2016-02-29T03:34:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-exposure-study.17617/post-189958>

COLD EXPOSURE STUDY
I have been doing Cold Thermogenesis baths on a fairly regular basis for almost a year now. I don’t plan on changing this. I like the way it feels and I think there is a lot of scientific support for the health benefits. On the other hand there are a few scientific studies that are troubling. I present one here:

“Cold Exposure Promotes Atherosclerotic Plaque Growth and Instability via UCP1-Dependent Lipolysis”
The study can be found at [http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3701322/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3701322/)
Here is a [review](http://www.science20.com/quotsciencequotstruck_bookworm/cold_activation_brown_fat_can_be_heart_attack_hazard-115795) of this information...
This study was mainly conducted on mice. To summarize in layman’s terms, mice were genetically modified to have double copies of a ApoE -/- gene degrading their ability to clear cholesterol from their systems. These mice developed markedly accelerated Atherosclerotic Plaque Growth.

The study then went on to recruit five human individuals who had high levels of plasma LDL. After 2-day (2 hr/day) exposure to 16°C plasma levels of LDL cholesterol were measured. Consistent with mouse models, plasma levels of LDL cholesterol were significantly increased as compared with the levels prior to cold exposure. Also plasma levels of adiponectin were also significantly reduced, this was also seen in the mouse models.

This study leads me to believe that anyone that has impaired ability to clear cholesterol may be in danger if attempting Cold Thermogenesis.

I would like to hear some comments on this. I also would like to know why the study states adiponectin levels were significantly reduced after cold exposure. My understanding is that adiponectin is increased when people are exposed to cold.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-02-29T05:08:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-exposure-study.17617/post-189961>

mice dont equal humans and they were studied under blue light and they are nocturnal.

End of study.

### Q. C. — 2016-02-29T06:23:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-exposure-study.17617/post-189962>

I can not argue the fact that mice are different then humans and we must be cautious to draw conclusions from mice studies. And the point that mice are nocturnal is well taken. However; this study also used control mice. Presumably the controls were also exposed to blue light. The control mice did not have the same poor results so this indicates to me the blue light may not have been a significant factor. (This is not to dispute the other harmful effects that have been associated with blue light). Thank you J.K. for your comment!

Sun between 8 and 10 qm and again between 3 and 5 pm, sand = earth for grounding Sea for CT . I believe you said you are in near Melbourne ? If you can do the beach between those hours that would be highly beneficial as you will have all the necessary frequencies , the grounding and the surface cooling effect to optimise UV storage. Stay away from artificial lights at the moment until you understand the whys and wherefores.
You may want to read these to start you off [https://www.jackkruse.com/tensegrity-8-communication-breakdown-gut/](https://www.jackkruse.com/tensegrity-8-communication-breakdown-gut/)

Did you rethink your conditions of existence at the time or did you treat the lovely critters just as you would a supplement to be consumed. ### TerrierMom — 2016-02-29T23:49:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-my-life-back.17619/post-190001>

No melatonin then you are killing it with blue light at the wrong times of day. If you expose yourself to blue light at night your body can't make melatonin. If you don't expose your retina to the sun in the morning, you also won't get the right signals to start the hormones going so you can make pineal melatonin that night. Get your blue light from sunlight not phone screens and LEDs. You can't out-eat a crappy environment. Or out-train one. Or out-supplement one. You have to get the light right. I am seeing improvement since getting my morning sun (which Jack told me **3 years ago** in a consult and I only just NOW took seriously!) and also eliminating blue light from my life as much as possible. Blue blockers at night, less screen time/tv in general overall, orange screens over the phone all day long, f.lux on the monitor (can also do on an Android phone). Within an hour after sunset I am in amber or red light. Now bright lights at night literally offend my senses....I treat it the way a vampire would treat the sun!! Avoid at peril of serious harm!!

I replaced bulbs in my home with incandescent ones because at least they have the beneficial IR light and not as huge a blue spike. So..... eat lots of DHA so that when you DO get in the sun, you can turn sunlight into DC electric current .... be well hydrated (no fluoridated water) at the cellular level so you can have a battery to charge.... avoid blue light.... consider Cold Thermogenesis to make yourself a superconductor of energy!! Food only took me so far in my journey, now I am focusing in on light..... Lahelada has great advice up there. I am just chiming in because, I got great advice too, 3 years ago, but wasn't sharp enough to realize how important the LIGHT part was. I kind of dismissed it.... I do not want you to make my mistake too!! DHA SUNLIGHT WATER AND AVOID BLUE LIGHT.  And listen to whatever Jack tells you, if he takes the time to say something, do your damndest to understand it and follow it, because he doesn't say things for no reason. (you may not understand the reason... just do what he recommends and you will understand one day...)

### lilly antic — 2016-03-02T19:07:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/getting-my-life-back.17619/post-190088>

We are striving for **full spectrum sun light** during the day, and **blue light free** in the night (to keep it short).

The normal modern bulbs ( fluorescent, LED) have frequency peaks in the blue range without any UV( which is the super important), so not good at all, neither for day and especially not at night. Incandescent less harmful because better spectrum.
Reptile lights are full spectrum with UV-B, not just red frequencies.
But this is not a substitution for real sun light.
Like Laheleda said try to get as much of the real thing as you can get. Especially in the morning to set your eye clock ,re-zip your collagen, stop hormone release ,lower blood pressure, close water gates(AQP 4) in the brain etc...
For the night get yourself blue blocking glasses. They will block any blue from whatever source around-be that bad lights, tv, computer, phone etc.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-03-02T05:55:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-georgia-desperate-for-wellness-knowledge.17622/post-190067>
