Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Since you been gone.......start where we are now.......just read the time series...........it is the current one. It has hundreds of hyper links.

Here is a snippet of where we are now: [http://www.cnn.com/2016/03/01/health/gray-hair-gene/index.html](http://www.cnn.com/2016/03/01/health/gray-hair-gene/index.html) **Consider this analogy made in Time 9 blog:** Melatonin and melanin are biogenic amines linked to light's local effect. Let us expand this idea to the laws of physics to see how biology follows those rules. Melatonin recycling changes local symmetry therefore it affects the space in tissues and the timing mechanisms in cells. Symmetry is often thought of globally, but it turns out Einstein's general relativity, put local symmetry front and center in physics; it appears melatonin and melanin control local symmetry of light collection for cells too. It is a proxy for how effective we collect light locally within cells. Einstein's idea was unorthodox in physics, but it appears the melatonin cycle uses his idea locally in cells. These things are both related to how light can flow in cells and tissues locally. Physics tells us energy and momentum tells space and time how to curve. As light falls to us it gains energy and momentum, so in this way it has a weight or inertia to it. That weight and inertia affect our surfaces to make things like Vitamin D3 as a chemical signal. Gravitational lensing is tied to energy flows by weight and momentum changes in light. This story linked the IRF4 gene to gray hair by interfering with production of melanin. What did they forget? Local control of symmetry: The gene is not important in this dance. The environment that turns it on is what is important. Light is critical in that environmental switch on or off. Losing the local symmetry of melatonin cycles from poor environmental light signals is the key to why IRF4 turns hair grey. Nature's disruption for cells is dependable, because we cannot control her waves so evolution built cells designed to capture her waves to make sense of them. Cells give humanity a truly unlimited trajectory because they are velcro for waves. That direction, however, can move forward or backward in time. The natural waves allowed into Earth’s ecosystem that we should surf, push man to greater heights, and those made by man set us back and slow and often reverse evolution. Cells are designed to be a repository of collectible wisdom from nature’s energy and information. That energy and data come to cells as wave forms. Given our cellular design to capture and collected and decipher waves, no human being itself should be considered impaired innately, instead there are environmental short comings that cause the impairment. Thus, it is incumbent on the on the clinician to recommend treatment of the environment their patient is in. People react to an inferior environment, way before their genome is altered. That is what the science of epigenetics and ubiquitin rates are telegraphing us, but the modern paradigm is not listening. You must. Your hair greys as % heteroplasmy rises in mitochondria........changing your diet doesn't change it.......changing your environment is what changes your mitochondria which controls how food electrons are handled. Food is just data about sunlight’s travels around our planet seasonally to add light energy to excite electrons. Don't try to let food or exercise be the missing key in your life, or light will stop being your unmatched lock, that doesn't let you merge on the path to optimal. All foods break down to electrons so a diet is non descriptive variable but this is counterintuitive to modern beliefs. Environments control heteroplasmy rates in mitochondria, not foodstuffs. Electrons are all the same in the cosmos. Electrons all have exactly the same properties everywhere in the universe for a reason. This is how energy is conserved. Electrons are conserved because they allow minimum excitations of a filling fluid of the cosmos. Electrons are nature's way of building things from interchangeable parts that are fundamentally different because of the energy they contain; electrons fill the world with a fluid plasma that affects the way space and time operate to create new things in matter and in life. Environments conserve energy by conserving time and momentum..........using electrons as its substrate. How electrons are handle varies in mitochondria by size scale energy. That is why carbohydrate electron's can bother LCHF guru's, but don't bother others folks with low heteroplasmy states in mitochondria. Environments control heteroplasmy rates, not foods. Foods provide a steady source of electrons environments dictate what mitochondria can do with their energy. That is how how energy flows in life.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bluelight-on-the-skin-during-nighttime.17623/post-190038>

Hey guys. Jack mentioned blue light has an effect even on the skin at night.
But I cant find any study that is supporting that.
He mentioned one study, in which they shined light on the backside of the knee. But all studies I found said, there is no effect on melatonin.

Can you help me out?

### Lahelada — 2016-03-01T23:15:02+0200

### lilreddgirl — 2016-03-01T23:24:45+0200

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bluelight-on-the-skin-during-nighttime.17623/post-190070>

Yeah I googled that yesterday with knee melatonin. But all studies I found said, that there is no effect from shining light on the skin. Thats why I am asking here.

No we have to discuss what happened in the first and in the other studies.

### lilreddgirl — 2016-03-02T04:35:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-laundry-detergent-that-actually-cleans.17625/post-190062>

Does anyone use organic laundry detergent? What brands actually clean!?

The ones I had didn't do a good job... I seem to have to go back to using Tide for the clothes that are stained...

### Lahelada — 2016-03-02T12:57:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-laundry-detergent-that-actually-cleans.17625/post-190072>

Have you tried hydrogen peroxide as a stain remover ? Bicarb is a good allround clothes detergent.

### lilreddgirl — 2016-03-03T02:33:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-laundry-detergent-that-actually-cleans.17625/post-190113>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=190072)
>
> Have you tried hydrogen peroxide as a stain remover ? Bicarb is a good allround clothes detergent.

Thanks Lahleda, you're using Hydrogen Peroxide as stain remover? Do you just pour some to the stain and let sit, or rub it in?

It doesn't cause a fade spot?

### Lahelada — 2016-03-03T02:51:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-laundry-detergent-that-actually-cleans.17625/post-190114>

I do a staged attack
bicarb
bicarb and vinegar
hydrogen peroxide

You will need to know your stains . H,Peroxide can leave a stain .If your stain is too old it may leave a discolouration . Bllod leaves a green stain if you do not catch it quickly enough. I have used it neat ( the weak solution) directly on the fabric ,never used cream of tartar as suugested as in the link below. A large percentage of "Vanish " is Hy Perox..#
[http://www.geeksonhome.com/how\_7405276\_use-peroxide-laundry-stains.html](http://www.geeksonhome.com/how_7405276_use-peroxide-laundry-stains.html)

### lilreddgirl — 2016-03-03T04:14:48+0200

I just bought this LOL! it seems quite a few reviewers are impressed with it more so than other products...

It's a GOAT MILK STICK! and then you rub it out with the WOOL part of the stick  ! Apparently it works... just had to try it

### Starfish Prime — 2016-04-03T19:45:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-laundry-detergent-that-actually-cleans.17625/post-191967>

I'm dying to know how your goat milk stick works!

Have you tried soap nuts? Eucalan is another lanolin-based cleanser/wash for wool/delicates that is mild enough to use on animals sans rinsing (which doesn't make a lot of sense...why would you leave dirty water/cleanser on that you're trying to clean? But perhaps for those areas a thorough rinse is difficult or sick/elderly critters.....anyway, I digress)

I have several woolen baby wraps (highly, highly recommend them! Life saver for fussy babes!) that are quite valuable. Eucalan is quite effective at removing poo and milk stains with the soak&ignore method of washing woolies (dump it in a bucket of cleanser&water, ignore for an hour or so, flip it, repeat, drain, lay out to dry)

### lilreddgirl — 2016-04-04T01:22:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-laundry-detergent-that-actually-cleans.17625/post-191982>

> [Starfish Prime said:](/goto/post?id=191967)
>
> I'm dying to know how your goat milk stick works!
>
> Have you tried soap nuts? Eucalan is another lanolin-based cleanser/wash for wool/delicates that is mild enough to use on animals sans rinsing (which doesn't make a lot of sense...why would you leave dirty water/cleanser on that you're trying to clean? But perhaps for those areas a thorough rinse is difficult or sick/elderly critters.....anyway, I digress)
>
> I have several woolen baby wraps (highly, highly recommend them! Life saver for fussy babes!) that are quite valuable. Eucalan is quite effective at removing poo and milk stains with the soak&ignore method of washing woolies (dump it in a bucket of cleanser&water, ignore for an hour or so, flip it, repeat, drain, lay out to dry)

Wow Eucalan sounds great if you can just leave the wraps in there and ignore!

The Goat stick works amazingly well! No wonder it was getting all the rave reviews. I was even able to get out a 6 month old blood stain by applying and blotting with Goat milk stick and then washing by hand (in cold water of course for blood stain). That even impressed my mom.

It has lye as a listed ingredient. But it seems very mild to my hand and smell.

### Starfish Prime — 2016-04-04T02:39:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/organic-laundry-detergent-that-actually-cleans.17625/post-191983>

Lye is used to saponify the fat; it's very common in soap making and it is consumed in the process, much like baking soda + vinegar in the proper ratio uses up all of both. (Maybe you knew that, idk)

I'm a big fan of Eucalan...esp for lingerie and the like... But I'll definitely be ordering one of those goat milk sticks (the name cracks me up...bc apparently I'm easily amused!) for grass stains and whatnot for summer! Glad you mentioned it and even more glad it actually works

I have a bulletproof coffee at about 9:00am and I wont have anything else to eat until dinner at 5:30 - 6pm
I get ready for work at 6am and leave the house at 7am and work till 5pm.
I take my dog for a walk at 6pm sometimes for an hour sometimes 1/2 hour. I try and eat before I go for a walk - in bed at about 10:30pm... I do have blue blocker glasses and have installed flux on my macbook. I am adopting the bulletproof diet - and eat seafood every day - barramundi fish, seafood marinara and today some oysters. Water is little hard for me - I do drink tea with stevia and I have a glass of sole (crystal salt water) first thing in the am. I have salts lamps through out the whole house and at night I put it off in my bedroom , but have it on first thing in the am then goes on all day till I go to bed. I work close to 9hrs a day so time in sun is limited. I do go to the beach and am there at about 7:30am on the weekends and stay there for about 1 hour - with my dog. I work as an accountant.

# Thread: Online meetup
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/online-meetup.17628/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Rubicon — 2016-03-03T06:46:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/online-meetup.17628/post-190122>

I was thinking it might be a lot of fun to have a meetup for members of this community to chat about the latest blog, share biohacks, and socialize. Unfortunately, it's not practical to regularly do so in person, but why not some kind of group video conference online? To that end, I propose the following:

An online meetup at a time that will work for as many people as possible. Lets say, 3:00PM Eastern Time this Saturday. Via [WorldTimeBuddy](http://www.worldtimebuddy.com/?qm=1&lid=2147714,2643743,5128581,5368361&h=5368361&date=2016-3-5&sln=12-13), here's when 3:00PM Eastern is at various locations around the world:

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

If you're in Sydney, meeting time will be 7:00AM Sunday morning. London is 8:00 PM Saturday evening, LA is 12:00 noon on Saturday, etc.

Where to meetup? There are lots of possibilities, but I did a bit of research and found tinychat.com is an option that's very easy to use, has reasonably good features, and it's free. It's basically like Skype's group chat, but no need to have an account or login.

Ok, so on Saturday at 3:00 Eastern, click here to enter a specially created chat room and join the meetup: [**http://tinychat.com/OptimalHealth**](http://tinychat.com/OptimalHealth)

Hope to see you there

### lilreddgirl — 2016-03-03T06:52:48+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/online-meetup.17628/post-190123>

ok, keep telling us though!

### Rubicon — 2016-03-04T22:38:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/online-meetup.17628/post-190200>

Ok, so in roughly 23 hours from now, if you want to hangout online go to **[http://tinychat.com/OptimalHealth](http://tinychat.com/OptimalHealth)**

When you click that link you'll be able to instantly join the chat where you can just hang out and listen-in, type in the group chat, or stream your audio/video. Hope to see you there

### Rubicon — 2016-03-18T01:25:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/online-meetup.17628/post-191108>

Is there any interest in having another meetup this weekend? At roughly the same time as the previous Saturday (but now with daylight savings), the meeting time in various timezones could be:

1:00PM in LA
4:00PM in New York
8:00PM in London
7:00AM in Sydney (Sunday)
etc

Come share progress on your latest biohacks, chat about the latest blog/podcast, ask or help answer each others' questions, or just chill out.

The video chat room at [**http://tinychat.com/OptimalHealth**](http://tinychat.com/OptimalHealth) is always open and anyone can join it without registering. Skype group chat or Google Hangouts are some alternatives if there's interest in that instead.

### Lahelada — 2016-03-18T04:08:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/online-meetup.17628/post-191117>

How long are you online for ? I might be able to drop in a bit later.

GREAT idea by the way !

### Rubicon — 2016-03-18T05:05:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/online-meetup.17628/post-191121>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=191117)
>
> How long are you online for ?

I had my gallbladder removed in 2013 and have been struggling with food and nutrition my entire life. I was recently diagnosed with "leaky gut" from a neurotransmitter and hormone test. I've battled depression and anxiety since my mid 20's. I had PCOS and difficult pregnancies resulting in c sections.

I am a health detective advocating for wellness for myself, my truck driver husband, my 5 year old autistic son and my 3 year old son. I am a stay at home mom and run this show mostly solo.

### healthy2015 — 2016-03-03T16:37:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solar-glow-lizard-lights-question.17630/post-190139>

Hi
I wonder if anyone can help?
My friend and her partner have started using the Solar glow lizard lights and they have noticed a massive decline in his sex drive but when he had bloods taken his T, vit D and Oestradiol had all increased?
Any ideas as to why?
Thanks

### healthy2015 — 2016-03-04T17:26:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solar-glow-lizard-lights-question.17630/post-190186>

Thanks

### Penny — 2016-03-05T04:47:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solar-glow-lizard-lights-question.17630/post-190214>

That is bizarre - a low estrogen would cause low sex drive - even in a man - so here is my wildest guess - the lamp is emitting mercury - this screws up zinc which screws up libido - has his balance gone to crap? That's a definite tell...
[http://www.drkaslow.com/html/mercury-s\_influence.html](http://www.drkaslow.com/html/mercury-s_influence.html)
I would have him eat many oysters or take zinc and see if there is an improvement - but that's not going to bind the mercury - he could eat cilantro and chase it with selenium, but you need to do some research on how to get rid of mercury - no tfun - definitely stop the lamp

Also, if his sense of taste/smell are horrible, that is low zinc - you can also use this stuff called "zinc status" to test your levels of zinc - it's a taste test -

I also think Dr. K mentioned that if you are already mercury toxic, then these lights would be a bad idea - amalgams, being first born (you inherit mom's metals) and living in Seattle or Pittsburgh where there were coal plants or where the coal dust blow over from China...

### Penny — 2016-03-06T03:05:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solar-glow-lizard-lights-question.17630/post-190255>

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/mercury-testing.17261/#post-190246](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/mercury-testing.17261/#post-190246)

### healthy2015 — 2016-03-13T12:21:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/solar-glow-lizard-lights-question.17630/post-190745>

Penny - thank you so much for the reply and my apologies for not thanking you sooner

### Rubicon — 2016-03-04T17:53:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frequency-of-skin-cancer-for-farm-workers.17632/post-190187>

Did those farmers spend a lot of time behind glass windows? My understanding is that glass filters UVB and most UVA, resulting in non-native EMF exposure. If our skin is optimized to absorb natural spectrum, non-natural spectrum may not be optimal and even cause things to go wrong. This may be part of the reason that [evidence shows slightly higher melanoma in truck drivers on the drivers side](http://healthland.time.com/2011/06/21/study-driving-may-contribute-to-left-side-skin-cancers/).

### Penny — 2016-03-05T04:28:50+0200

### Starfish Prime — 2016-03-25T21:09:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frequency-of-skin-cancer-for-farm-workers.17632/post-191517>

Inflammation is the key, I'd say. Stress across the board, excessive nnEMF, and circadian disruption.

Bigger factors by far, I'd guess, than any potential chem exposure, as most applicators have enclosed cabs and are done on zero-wind (or as close as can be found around here) days.

High nnEMF from Bluetooth/CB/cell phones/satcom navigation(ensures accurate application w/ little overlap to minimize chem usage)
\-wifi inside a glass/metal cab plus
\-excess light behind glass (predawn (=no AM light) to as late at night as dewpoints will allow for as many days in a row as it takes to get done)
\-low DHA and
\-a healthy dose of intermittent chronic exhaustion trying to get seeding in during your window before the government says it's too late (regardless of reality) and you lose your crop insurance (that you still pay for).

Couple that with lunch out of a cooler every day but Sunday, water and not enough of it from a plastic jug, coffee and maybe a quick breakfast of cereal/whatever at 4am or before, depending on what time you rolled in last night and what the weather forecast is calling for, a quick supper when you roll in at 11:30 every night for all spring and all harvest and all fall work (so a good 4/5 months of the year), worrying that "I know wheat isn't a healthy crop but seriously,what else am I'm going to grow that I can market around here/fit the climate/fill the rotation needs/still make the land/equipment/tax payments?", financial stress with huge land payments and bankers who may or may not be reasonable, marketing stress, is that notill drill going to need replaced this year ($180K or more) or can it be rebuilt? (with the welder which means more transition metals/nnEMF inside a metal shop), crop health-stripe rust, footrot, branbugs in the bin, and all that for crops that, right now, are selling for less than the cost of production.

Side note: NoTill (historically very chemical-dependant) is a lot more prevalent out here than in the Midwest and the important of crop diversity (aka weeds) is finally getting out. (By here I mean Camas Prairies of ID and the Palouse). [https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Palouse#Farming](https://en.m.wikipedia.org/wiki/Palouse#Farming) I grew up in a farming family, and have been working with farmers/ranchers on a professional basis since 2007. Cover crops (one potential solution for minimal application) are a tough sell due to the current seed cost and tremendous gamble in this area of getting enough stand before winter to make them worthwhile.

Good farmers/applicators are extraordinarily cautious/conservative about chemical application. There are those who are not. but there are good and bad in every occupation.

### Linz — 2016-03-26T13:01:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frequency-of-skin-cancer-for-farm-workers.17632/post-191540>

Laurel, that's a great summary and they face the same pressures here but I feel a bit differently about the problems they face. Often the working day adjusts to begin at sunrise and many will eat that prepared lunch outside and spend far more time outside than other professions - daylight offers protection from poorer food choices and as much of the food as possible (not least because of the economic pressures and availability of growing space) is home grown/produced and seasonal. Those with early morning livestock duties often return home for a proper protein/fat filled breakfast every morning here. One of my biggest worries about moving to the city from a farm was the feeling that I was losing my sense of season not being able to see things growing around me.

Precautions around chemical use have improved but those chemicals are extremely concentrated and I would choose chemical-free above all else having seen the effects of chronic exposure all too close. Very prevalent in my 'smell memory' is one of the old commonly used farm chemicals - even though as children we were kept well away. Wind-free and tractor cabs provide minimal protection. I suspect many destroy ability to respond to circadian signals. Nitrogen run-0ff into local water supplies = another problem in agricultural areas.

### Starfish Prime — 2016-03-29T01:03:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/frequency-of-skin-cancer-for-farm-workers.17632/post-191672>

Welllll, one can feel however one likes about something by that doesn't change its intrinsic nature.

Chemicals aren't applied for fun, they're applied because there literally is no other solution for the issue...And-nitrogen runoff should be placed at the hands of lawn care/CAFOs long before you point fingers at agriculture. The financial penalties for doing that are insane. Somewhere in the neighborhood of WTF. Actually- They probably the same CAFOs now too, I'm not in that business but I know that water/waste mgmt is huge importance for most producers.

I don't disagree that they (chemicals) may have effects beyond the obvious-the shikimate pathway and its effects of humans are no longer up for debate, and you are dead right that there may be other pathways affected we're unaware of.

But even organic farming uses chemicals. Some of the nastier ones used are approved for organic use. It isn't realistic to expect to grow a particular crop on 100-300 acres and not need some form of chemical unless you're willing to see thistle seed,and other more noxious foreign material (like smut, live bran bugs, etc) in your grains. Anyone who thinks otherwise hasn't farmed outside the office. The realities of marketing are such that even if it WERE possible, finding a market that was a) priced appropriately (never met a farmer in it for charity,nthey like to eat too!) b) capable of segregation from conventionally-produced grain at all steps from farm to end-user and c)located a reasonable distance from production is asking a lot and a possibility for only a select few. It's what I do for a living -bringing grain produced in the northern mountain west to market. I move a lot of it too. I see these issues and have these conversations every day.

These guys gross maybe $3.00 a bushel, maybe less, after they pay freight to get it to the coast....that's gross to the farmer,l.
From which they pay the seed bill, fuel bill, land payments, taxes, equipment expense, wages, etc. before they get their wage, depending on how they're set up business-wise. Cost of production right now, around here, is about $5.20. That's for #1 wheat. Low test weight, protein out of spec (depends on variety, affected by plant stress), high foreign material, high dockage (and a bazillion other factors) all reduce the price. If there was a way to not use chemicals, it would be done. Lol kinda like people grumbling about antibiotics in cattle/hogs. When a 10-dose bottle is well over a $1000 to buy, do you really think it'll be dosed out randomly or unnecessarily? Some folks act like its treated like smarties or pez candies.

I lost my father at a young age to a rather unusual form of cancer...Ill always wonder what exposure he had that affected it, if any. But it's an inherent occupational risk just like wifi and fluorescent light exposure is for me.

# Thread: ~~ Like two pine cones kissing noses ~~
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/like-two-pine-cones-kissing-noses.17639/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2

### mobeus — 2016-03-05T10:30:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/like-two-pine-cones-kissing-noses.17639/post-190221>

High Friends

I'm delighted to be here - it's an honor to connect with so many on a similar path. Happy to contribute a little, learn from everyone, and just generally sharing my experience with bio-hacking.

My name is Morgan. I'm 37, living on an island in the salish sea, and i've always been in "good" health. My definition of "good" has been evolving since 2004. But has taken a quantum leap in the last few months. I've spent several years vagabonding around asia/india/europe/iceland/south america. Then i restored a wooden sailboat and cruised for 5 years. Now i'm founding a company to build biologic homes out of cold-fired crystalline ceramics. So many life style changes.

I'm 5' 10" and my weight has fluctuated from about 185 to 155. But mostly between 155-165. I have not eaten processed foods since 2005, i've experimented with a raw diet, spent many months eating only fresh fish (tuna and salmon, still quivering) and rice. In retrospect, the last 6 months may have been my first "bio-hack". I have been eating a lot of potatoes, pork/steak/chicken/bacon, tons of cheese, tortilla chips, sour cream, not enough living foods. I've gained 15 lbs in 6 months. Such is the price of being a penniless entrepreneur 'boot strapping' for two years.

Thankfully I just came across Jack and some pieces fell together - information i have been longing for.

Health Goals for Next 3 months:

1) More wild salmon, more coconut oil, local/organic salads, the more vitality the better...sprouting.
2) Grounded for at least 14 hours/day.
3) Almost no blue light - except i have to look at this screen! How bad is that?
4) High Intensity Strength Training - Doug McGuff: Body by Science.
5) Lots of dancing and running barefoot in the sunshine fields.
6) Singing, stoned, naked, and exalted in the cold rain!
7) Integrating a unified fractal field theory into life experience - Dan Winter.
8) Integrating principles of feng shui into home/work/routine.
9) Practicing non-accumulation to stay in trust and in flow.

Technically i am a full on "newby".

All of the above is in progress. I use a land line and wired internet. Cell rarely. Living on acreage on island with no wifi in range. No bluetooth.

I drink untreated water from a well on my land, and will be experimenting with structuring the water using phase conjugate EM (devices i will build), Flannagan's "crystal water", Sedlemayrs "divinia water", Dan Winter's "Super Imploder", and probably some ORMUS.

With oceans of jOy and light waves of DHA,

Morgan

Looking forward to the next installments of the story.

# Thread: D Moore Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-moore-journal.17640/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 29
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Denise Moore — 2016-03-05T17:18:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-moore-journal.17640/post-190234>

The best place to start is from the beginning...
My childhood was filled with the outdoors, camping and boating.
I was on birth control from 15 to 29.Whenever I went off, painful ovarian cysts showed up. I had panic attacks at age 27 and was diagnosed with depression and general anxiety with panic attacks, put on Xanax. I didn't like that so went through different SSRIs until I liked Paxil. I also went to a therapist.
I got married and got pregnant immediately after stopping birth control in 2009. I was already weaning off the Paxil, my therapist and I decided I was "better" so I went off completely at 8 weeks pregnant. I gained a ton of weight and ended the pregnancy with Preeclmapsia and had an emergency c section July 14, 2010. I asked the OB to induce me so I could try to do the birth vaginally and she agreed. After 36 hours of terrible medication and no movement on the birth I opted for the surgery. During the pregnancy I went to physical therapy for severe knee pain, I had been taking Motrin nightly to sleep. I was probably about 140 lbs at that point and I'm only 5 feet tall.
I had severe pain after the healing of the c section from my belly button down to my knees. Doctors did ultrasound, CT scan, MRI and laproscopic surgery January 2011 only to say I was crazy and find nothing wrong. My PCP put me on narcotics, norco during the day and percocet at night for the pain. I went to an Atkins diet to lose the baby weight. It was very difficult because I lived on carbs my entire life. But, my body responded very well and I lost a ton of weight, was down to 120 lbs.
I got pregnant August 2011, weaned myself off of pain medication and miscarried in September. By this time I was doing yoga and pilates and my hip pain was more manageable. But, I still had my prescription and took "as needed." I got pregnant again and had a scheduled c section and tubal ligation October 2012. Pregnancy was normal, my OB did not do VBAC. I was fine with it. I actually went into labor this time so I'm glad I got to experience my water breaking and contractions. After the I stopped breastfeeding and my periods came back the cysts came back. My periods were so heavy I could not leave the house for the first 2 days. I tried BC pills and severe depression came back immediately. So, I stopped that and turned back to pain meds and not leaving the house.
In March of 2013 I started seeing a ND. I was complaining of extreme low energy, brain fog, pain was back, depression, weepy... pretty much everything that happens with being a mom of two young boys. He found I have the MTHFR gene mutation and put me on 15 mg MTHF. I saw results within a few weeks and felt better than I had in a long, long time. I was no longer taking pain medication for the pain. I decided to do Atkins again to lose the baby weight and this time it wouldn't budge. In May I felt like I was drunk all the time, severe brain fog and dizziness. He treated me for a candida overgrowth and put me on anti-fungal medication.
July 4th, 2013 I went to the hospital with severe pain in my left side. Hospital ran some tests and asked me to come back the next morning for a HIDA scan. The scan showed my gallbladder was working fine they sent me home with pain medication and told me to check with my PCP on Monday. That night I woke up at 1 AM in the most severe pain I have ever experienced. I went to the hospital and after testing they diagnosed me pancreatitis and said my lipase levels were extremely high. After a night of medication my lipase levels were down and my pain was intense I wanted to be put out of my misery. The surgeon came in and suggested surgery, he suspected gallbladder even though the information from the tests said it was fine. He found my gallbladder perforated and it took 7 liters of saline to wash the bile from my body. My entire body was toxic. They put a PIC line in. I was released abruptly after total days in the hospital.
Everything I ate was a problem except for pasta and bread. I was back on pain medication. At least I didn't have to run to the toilet every time I ate from the narcotics. I sought out a new GYN and she diagnosed me with endometriosis. This led me to a medical cannibas prescription in hopes of ditching the narcotics. I went back into depression and came across a neurotransmitter test. The test results said my dopamine, serotonin, glutamate, norepinephrin, and histamine were all extremely high and my GABA was extremely low. My cortisol was backwards. I was officially diagnosed with leaky gut. They suggested a Paleo diet, cut grains completely, exercise and some supplements. I was shocked at how the pain I had been experiencing for years went away when I cut grains and sugar from my diet. And pissed at the Drs and how much money I spent when all I needed was to not eat certain foods. This is when I decided I wanted to research and see what things were caused by poor eating. This led me to Ayurveda, herbs, healing energies and all sorts of natural vitalizing schools of thought. A search of Quantum led me to the Facebook group and this forum. I am devouring this information daily, reading, podcasts, and now webinars (happy birthday to me.)
I am currently practicing Science of Mind. I do a 15 min silent meditation 5 time a weeks. I watch the sunrise every morning. I spend my days outdoors in the sunlight without glasses or sunscreen, which is the easy part because I love the sun. I wear blue blocker glasses at night. I drink well water, we live in the country in an almond orchard, but I am ALWAYS thirsty and my skin looks terrible. I eat seafood daily and just learned how to shuck my own oysters, now I need to find a good source to buy from. I take 15 mg MTHF, 100o mg tumeric, pancreatin 10, DHA, CoQ10, lithium orotate 5 mg 3 times a day, GABA 2 times a day, Cortisolv 2 times a day. I have dabbled with 2 mg of nicotine in the lozenge form. I have medical cannibas and only use that on rare occasions. I am frustrated with food. My gut is pissed off. I'm tired of the narcotic addition. I'm thirsty. I'm angry with a low frustration tolerance. I have very low libido. Often overwhelmed. Low energy and motivation. I am down to 116 lbs.
My 5 year old son has Sensory Processing Disorder and we are awaiting an evaluation to see if he is Autistic, I don't need the eval to tell me he is. I have increased his fat intake and introduced purple light and it has helped exponentially.
I have so many breadcrumbs and feel I need to wipe my slate clean and start all over. I would like to find a practioner to help me with any laps. I have a feeling me gut is not absorbing the nutrients it needs and I need to know where to start to get my body functioning optimal.
I am extremely grateful for this community. The knowledge and the time spent here by everyone is priceless. I look forward to this journey.
Thank you!!

### Denise Moore — 2016-03-08T16:27:03+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-moore-journal.17640/post-190403>

I've started the leptin reset and where I am looking forward to the benefits, I am not enjoying it now. It's challenging fore to eat 30 min within rising. And 50 mg of protein... I haven't been able to eat that much food. I have been eating 3 times a day which is a good improvement and no snacking.
I had been waking, showering, meditation, watch the sunrise, 1 cup of coffee and lots of water until lunch. Now, I get up and make breakfast. The change has thrown off my boy's sleeping schedule too. I don't like then awake at 5 AM. This will all fall into place. Permanent change requires finesse.
I wake up at midnight in pain. My knees and legs ache. Remeniscant of RLS. My shoulders ache and my arms and hands are asleep. I toss and turn, get up and do some yoga stretching and wind up taking a pain pill. The thought of staying awake to work it out and giving up the hours of sleep frightens me. Speaking of sleep, I've been exhausted at 7:30. Get to bed at 8. I've been listening to the earlier webinars and rereading the associated blog posts. Factor x rounded out the information in the book nicely.
I'm in the middle of reading CPC 4 and listening to the Jet Lag RX, flying in 2 weeks.
Can kids (3 & 5) take or do they "need" CoQ10?

### Denise Moore — 2016-03-12T17:53:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-moore-journal.17640/post-190714>

I've been tolerating fat a lot better. I've been taking Hugh dose vitamin K and taking digestive enzymes. I'm thinking my pain in my body is candida die off. I did fall off the wagon and had 2 days of eating SAD, boy that was a mistake. I cannot compromise my health and well being for the sake of anyone else.
I must be a pillar to show others in my family eating good can and must be done. Lead by example. And own the decision to treat my body like a Ferrari. If I won't treat myself like a queen, no one else will either.
I listened to the CT webinar. I'm very interested in adding this element, the benefits are amazing.
I'm a recovering perfectionist and need to realize these changes are permanent and take time to implement. If it were easy, then everyone would be doing it. The rewards are personal and for me to reap.
For now, food is my concern. The light part is easy.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-03-12T19:52:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-moore-journal.17640/post-190718>

your concern will never lead you to light.......which is where optimal is buried. That idea is born due to low dopamine.

### Denise Moore — 2016-03-15T18:54:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-moore-journal.17640/post-190894>

Does an increase in Progesterone cause breast tenderness? This is something that has been increasing since eating fat and being outside topless. I notice it more about a day after having some significant AM sun on the girls.
I am completely in love with butter coffee.
I have mandated blue blockers on everyone after the sun goes down and staying outside until sunset. My boys have been sleeping better.
Looking forward to spending the day in my garden.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-03-15T19:24:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-moore-journal.17640/post-190897>

sensitivity returns when you begin to attune the sensory thalamic relays to sun light.

### Denise Moore — 2016-03-17T04:54:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-moore-journal.17640/post-191042>

Ok. I'm getting it. Circadian cycles are the focus. It's more important to get light, the right kind of light. It is more important to be on point of when you put something in your body than what you put in your body.

I am so effing thirsty!! I drink 20 oz of well water every 90 min. Totally about 160 oz a day. I stop drinking around 8 PM to avoid having to get up all night long. Right now my mouth has a sort of metallic taste and my tongue feels dry and almost itchy. Any suggestions are welcome.

On another note, I started a parent support group for parents of kids with special needs. I find myself looking at these moms, every single one was obese, packaged snacks, noses in their phones... The ones that don't want to do anything about it.

### Denise Moore — 2016-04-16T01:42:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-moore-journal.17640/post-192797>

According to my contact lenses manufacturer, the contacts I wear do not have UV protection. I have stopped wearing them and sunglasses, I only wear my contacts on "special occasions" (purely for vanity.) I was just wondering if anyone had any thoughts as to if there are "safe" contact lenses to wear.

### Denise Moore — 2016-04-20T05:17:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-moore-journal.17640/post-193051>

Today I met with a gastroenterologist. He diagnosed me with colorrhetic diarrhea and prescribed cholestyramine, 1 teaspoon, 3 times a day. He order a stool study to test for parasites and other things and a CBC. I had an order for a food allergy test so I decided to get that done too. I go back in 6 weeks. The surgeon who removed my gallbladder prescribed this cholestyramine shortly after the surgery and I did not like it. From there, my ND said to take Ox Bile. That didn't really work and when pain flared up, I'd takey pain meds and that would take care of the diarrhea.
He is concerned with high dose vitamin k2, says it can be toxic and has nothing to do with the gallbladder. I wish to be more versed in things I learn. To be able to pull out data and quotes off the top of my head.
I knew going in, I would be lucky to find the kind of Dr I'm looking for. At least insurance will pay for the tests and I can go from there.
I just want to be able to tolerate fats so I can stay away from carbs.

### Jenelle — 2016-04-20T05:55:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-moore-journal.17640/post-193052>

> [Denise Moore said:](/goto/post?id=192797)
>
> According to my contact lenses manufacturer, the contacts I wear do not have UV protection. I have stopped wearing them and sunglasses, I only wear my contacts on "special occasions" (purely for vanity.) I was just wondering if anyone had any thoughts as to if there are "safe" contact lenses to wear.

It is my understanding that I have been told to throw away my contacts because they DO block UV light, and we don't want that.

If you actually have a brand that _does not_ block UV light... then I assume that would be a "safe" option. I was not aware that these existed.

I just looked up my brand to be sure, and it says "Class I – Blocks at least 96% UV-A and 99% UV-B radiation" and "One of the highest UV protection available in any contact lens". YAY.

### Denise Moore — 2016-06-28T05:33:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-moore-journal.17640/post-197398>

Bah! Frustrated. Do you ever feel like you are completely nuts? Seriously, I wish we had those body scanners like on Star Trek that could tell us exactly what is wrong and how to fix it. Then, there'd be some nnEMF bullshit to deal with. My body is not cooperating like I want it too. By the time I sit down to write in my journal, I'm not sure where to start or what to write, so I don't write anything at all.
I suppose I can start with wins, been wearing blue blockers, I even have the kids wearing them and a few friends. I use amber lighting at night. I even got amber lighting for our vacation home and the husband prefers that light. We aren't typically together at night, he's out driving a truck across CA. I have blue light filters on my computer and phone. I have wired my internet and turn off all power when items are not in use. I limit all use of artificial light.
I watch the sunrise, barefoot, with minimal clothes, in gratitude every day. I am outside in the morning sun with my tan through suit and no shoes. No sunglasses. Ditched the contacts. Only wear my glasses on the tip of my nose if necessary. I am outside as much as possible. I drive with my sunroof open.
I eat first thing after I watch the sunrise. High protein, high fat. I eat again around 2. I follow an epi-paleo template for food. I do love oysters and raw tuna. Thankfully, our local Sprouts has a nice seafood selection at great prices.
I got my well tested, there is nothing "bad" in it, but I don't know what is in it. How can I find out what minerals are in it, ph, etc? I drink at least 120 oz of water a day. My chronic thirst has died down a bit. The GI suggested I was thirsty from chronic diarrhea. I drink a liter of pedialtye a week. It would be good to figure out how to get the electrolytes without the sugar.
I take cold showers and use ice packs.
We were on the lake for Memorial Weekend. No sunscreen and no sunburn. We drank plenty of water and had a ton of seafood. We had a nice sun callus already too. I was actually surprised that our skin all fared so well.
Now for the frustrating part. I feel like I'm drunk, spacey, equilibrium is off. I run into things, drop things, take corners too sharp. Major brain fog. Even as I type this, I'm having a hard time remembering how to spell words. My gut is pissed off. I have a new pain in my tailbone / rectal pressure. I started 1 drop of methelyne blue in a glass of water, per day, last week, it helps the brain fog, spaceyness for about 3 hours. I suppose I could play around with another dose in the afternoon. Does that cause constipation? I have had issues with chronic diarrhea for three years, since I lost my gallbladder, now, I actually have solid stool. It is not an issue to go, it is just actually a solid turd. That hasn't happened in years. So, I have the pressure in my rectum, gas, horrifically smelly gas, like something died. Earlier today, there was sort of a bursting sensation, sharp pain in my lower right abdomen, knocked me down, ever since then, the gas. I'm bloated. My son was was rough housing and landed on my stomach, I felt like I was stabbed. I have a follow up with the GI tomorrow, I was going to cancel because all my labs came back "normal." With all of this pain, I'm hoping he has an idea... blockage, hernia? I also have mucous coming out of my rectum and some burning / itching. Those things point to candida, right?
On top of all that, my mood is shitty. I am short fused, depressed, and feel hopeless. Depression is a fucking bitch. My mom accused me of going into menopause. I thought, great, so now I need progesterone cream?
I take a shit ton of supplements. I have cut back and I am grateful for that. Every time I go to take something, it is consciously. I don't just take it blindly. That has helped cut out a lot. I do feel like I flip my pancake without lith-oro and kavinace 3 times a day and 15 mg MTHF. Everything else is for my "leaky gut" and chronic pain.
I have decided to focus more on my spirituality and not just on my intellect. Mediation everyday, sometimes twice a day. Spiritual readings. Being present and trusting my intuition. Started a new book, The Presence Process, it's actually quite good.
Ok, I suppose I feel better getting some of the things in black and white. I can only know where I'm going if I know where I was, or something like that.

### Denise Moore — 2016-06-29T02:06:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-moore-journal.17640/post-197476>

Met with the NP and the GI Dr today. I actually liked her. She listened and is concerned, she did not bulldoze me which is a change from the GI Dr. She said, "you're all messed up, have a lot of inflammation and your immune system is shot, but you're trying. I think you need to get to the bottom of what you're dealing with so you know which direction to move in." She did recommend a Colonoscopy and Endoscopy to do biopsies to rule out Celiac and Chron's. She said they will do a culture for yeast and pretty much look for test for everything/anything. I'm not sure how I feel about all of this. The procedure sounds horrible, I will be sedated. I'm not supposed to feel or remember anything. I don't think I'm concerned about disturbing any "good" gut flora, because I don't think I have any. It's scheduled for July 29th and she told me to eat wheat and "bothersome" foods in small amounts everyday so they can get a good picture of what's going on when they're in there. It all sounds rather unpleasant.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-06-29T02:37:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-moore-journal.17640/post-197482>

The issue is simple........look to your environment.

In my view when you have the above perspective about health issues any problem can be solved using the materials in the room around you if you just add your imagination to the mix. I said as much in the June 2016 webinar.

### Denise Moore — 2016-06-29T03:26:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-moore-journal.17640/post-197490>

I do thank you for your response. My environment, working on it. My stress level is through the roof. Is it because I don't feel well or do I not feel well because of stress? There are things I do not know how to change. Those are the things that stress me out the most.
Kind of funny story, in hindsight... I was up to watch the sunrise this AM. My youngest woke up too. My ego chimed in, "5:45 is too early for the children to be awake, I need my quiet, peaceful, grounding, sunrise watching time." My Spirit said, "invite him to watch it with you." My ego said, "no, then he'll be up everyday to watch the sunrise, this is MY time." I then proceeded to start a war. Nobody and peaceful, dopamine getting time this morning.
This kind of shit is ridiculous. My temper, flares uncontrollably, especially around period time, which is due on Friday.
I am in pain, a lot of pain. I wake up at night from pain. I get up out of bed in the morning greeted by pain. I do simple tasks around the house or with my children, pain. I eat, pain and / or stress about what is going into my mouth because is it going to cause pain.
My imagination lives in fear. I do not want it to live there. I do not have the will and decision making power to claw out of this pit on my own. I get easily overwhelmed by the science details, it's over my head. I pick out what I can. I understand the gist. I would be satisfied and remain vigilant and patient if my chronic pain was getting better, but it is getting worse and changing. That scares me too.

### Denise Moore — 2016-06-29T03:36:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-moore-journal.17640/post-197491>

I just read your post on FB: [https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/1296126073785077](https://www.facebook.com/drjackkruse/posts/1296126073785077)
My husband is very conservative and very comfortable, so he thinks and says. He is an owner / operator and has a great contract. Am I to say my health is more important than your career, just trust me on this... we need to move. I feel like as the bread winner, alpha male. Yes, we are in a partnership. He wants to move to the foothills. Schools are terrible there. It's all so complicated. Are they valid fears and excuses? The human body is resilient. There has to be a way to live in whatever environment. I just have to know what I'm dealing with so I know how to hack it. I love where I live.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-06-29T04:55:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/d-moore-journal.17640/post-197495>

So Denise let me ask you this.........who are you good for if youre not good for you. How much are you worth to him if youre in an ICU soon or dead........?

Is your job more important than the people you care about?

An acquaintance merely enjoys your company, a fair-weather companion flatters when all is well, a true friend has your best interests at heart and the pluck to tell you what you need to hear........what should a spouse due when the shit hits the fan with you?

In my way of thinking, we can’t always protect the people we care about, but we always seem to care too much about the people we protect from reality. I'll just leave these thoughts here now.

Ponder, think, reflect. You have to put yourself above everything. Without your health you are nothing.

### JanSz — 2016-06-29T18:34:57+0300

**Location:** Fresno, CA, USA

[http://www.curezone.org/faq/q.asp?a=13,281,2962&q=595](http://www.curezone.org/faq/q.asp?a=13,281,2962&q=595)
**ENDOMETRIOSIS~ Can iodine therapy help?**

It was found that the therapeutic treatment of human patients with an effective amount of elemental iodine (I.sub.2) in a pharmaceutical carrier caused a subsidence of the nodularity of the patient's pelvic peritoneum. An effective dose of elemental iodine (I.sub.2) to cause such a subsidence of nodularity **is about 1 to 20 milligrams per day.** A daily dosage of elemental iodine for the treatment of endometriosis of about 3 milligrams to about 6 milligrams is also useful. The daily dosage can be administered in an aqueous solution containing about 0.30 milligrams of elemental iodine per milliliter of solution."

\=============================

If you want to try that,
**start with 3-4 weeks selenium first.
1/day-->Super Selenium Complex, 200 mcg 100 capsules (lef.org)
**
Continue selenium,
then start (slowly) on Lugol solution.

20 milligrams per day is very small dose.
50mg/day more likely
some even do 100mg/day

48mg/day=0.8cc=full dropper

1 dropperfull/day=48mg/day

### Jack Kruse — 2016-05-15T16:48:30+0300

# Thread: Leptin Reset Drowsiness
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-drowsiness.17643/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### garoha — 2016-03-07T01:53:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-drowsiness.17643/post-190307>

I'm a brand newbie. Just started on the Leptin reset about a week ago. Eating 50 grams of seafood (with greens) first thing in the morning. This is about 6:am for me during the weekdays (to accommodate work schedule) which is way too early for my normal eating patterns. I typically don't eat anything till about 10:00am (which is normally a smoothie as I am working at that time). So, it's very challenging for me to get the 50 grams of seafood down and then I don't feel so good. Very sleepy, foggy headed and sometimes headache. Anyone else have this issue? Will it pass? Any suggestions for boosting energy (without coffee) after this meal?

### Lahelada — 2016-03-07T01:58:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-drowsiness.17643/post-190308>

Garoha,welcome. Are you eating only 50 g of seafood or are you eating 50 g of protein ? You are probably looking at 300 g of seafood to get your 50 g of protein. Also it may be good for you to include some bacon at this stage. Glutathione being the aim of the game there.

How are you doing with going out to see the sun and getting morning sun in general?It may seema detail at the moment but there is a lot of magic in that one.

### garoha — 2016-03-07T02:17:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-drowsiness.17643/post-190310>

> [Lahelada said:](/goto/post?id=190308)
>
> Garoha,welcome. Are you eating only 50 g of seafood or are you eating 50 g of protein ? You are probably looking at 300 g of seafood to get your 50 g of protein. Also it may be good for you to include some bacon at this stage. Glutathione being the aim of the game there.
>
> How are you doing with going out to see the sun and getting morning sun in general?It may seema detail at the moment but there is a lot of magic in that one.

Ah, yes sorry. I am getting 50 grams of protein from the seafood that I am eating. I can't do bacon. I was a vegan before I embarked on this Leptin reset and the seafood was a push for me. I am sticking to more of the shellfish and sustainable varieties, don't feel like I can go beyond that and still sleep at night. Are there other sources of Glutathione out there, or could I do a supplement?

I can do a better job of getting sun on the weekends, but it is difficult to get much sun before work.. I leave at 7:30am when the sun is pretty low and commute into San Francisco where there are lots of tall buildings. I could make it a point to get out of the office for ten minutes and find some sun sometime before 11:00am but I can't get naked  Also, this past week and the week ahead look to be pretty rainy so the pockets of sun are slim. But if I'm supposed to be sort of in hibernation mode with CT and such, is the sun so important for the Leptin reset?

### Lahelada — 2016-03-07T02:22:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-drowsiness.17643/post-190311>

More important than the food... You must go out first thing after waking at least within the first 30 mins after waking and greet the sun, Look about 30 degrees to the east of it. A longer session would be good but whatever you can do. Only do not do zero mins.
Bear in mind it is not the holiday sun effect you are after. SO any sun will do even on cloudy days.

### garoha — 2016-03-07T04:35:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-drowsiness.17643/post-190315>

Thank you Lahelada. I will continue to go out in the early morning sun (even on cloudy days) and hope that my morning energy soon returns. Perhaps my system is still over-whelmed with this load of early morning protein to digest.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-03-07T06:19:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-drowsiness.17643/post-190323>

Light with grounding and the reset........not light plus protein alone is best.......

### Jack Kruse — 2016-03-07T06:25:56+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-reset-drowsiness.17643/post-190326>

All things in the cosmos are controlled by electrons. All foods are broken down to electrons. Chemistry is controlled exclusively by valence electrons, and this includes all known biochemistry in cells. As environmental changes occur with light, this can change electrons from the "identical interchangeable parts" they are to particles with different information embedded in the light frequencies attached to them. We call this excitation. This is how "change without change" occurs EVERYWHERE photoelectrically in the universe using ALL chemicals.

All electrons are exactly the same everywhere in the universe...........so how can different things that made from them, like foods, that look so different to us on farms and in markets? Simple, because electrons can be changed ever so slightly by light frequencies from the sun coming into your eye and skin that "excites" them. Excite = energy increase by light. Light can do this photo-electrically. Those frequencies that excite them should come from the sun and not a fake light in your house, computer or cell phone, or from a farmer's growing lamp. Excitation only come from the light sources that excite them. This is why organic sun grown food exceeds GMO foods in nutrient density. It is a property of the light they contain not the nutrients. GMO foods are grown under and processed with blue light and their electrons are not as highly powered as ones in the sun planted in Earth, so you have to eat more electrons from these foods to get equivalement amount of light for the cell. This is why people who eat processed food really get fat and leptin resistant. **We excite electrons best when we are in the sun grounded to Earth.

It has nothing to do with the electrons fundamentally, it has all to do with the lack of light those electrons have.** Fritz Popp proved this with his photomultipliers in the late 20th century. GMO food has less light contained in them. This unfathomable small change in light frequency confuses the food guru into believing that food, and not light, is our biggest issue. Its a half truth my friend. Poor light confinement in food electrons leads to massive changes on a big scale that our 5 senses are attuned to. This increases our awareness that something is wrong is sensed in our respiratory proteins, and it becomes a signal for us to change our environment. Our 5 senses use many other sensory pathways for waveforms, other than mitochondria to make this information available to our brain in our thalamus. Our thalamus is where our alpha waves found on the EEG begin. They are supposed to fire at 7.83 hertz which matches the Earth natural resonance. That frequency comes a connection with the Earth. This is why grounding with the sun is important. Circadian signals are tied to the alpha wave generation in EEG and people who are LS. Your thalamus, in this way, is a giant tuning fork used by the brain to compare frequencies embedded on your electrons from the environmental source from every mitochondria and every sensory relay in your brain. Your food electrons need to be programmed properly...............

Hey guys!
I have a dilemma. (surprise, surprise
I've been doing the leptin reset for over two months, and II have absolutely no cravings, everything's going well, but I'm pretty sure I should be leptin sensitive by now and I just don't know. I sleep well but I'm not energetic upon waking. I know I need more sleep and I'm trying to get to that point, but I don't know if my leptin is fixed. I've been itching to start with some weights, but if I start before my leptin is fixed will that create a problem? Is it possible that I'm leptin sensitive but still tired?

get UV light during the day, block blue light later in the day. Sleep when it is dark, as dark as possible. This should help making more melotonin which activates leptin and will raise morning cortisol.

So, I got into some hot discussions about chemtrails with the boyfriend, as I told him I saw them spraying here the other day. He searched it and told me it is all conspiracy theories and has nothing to do with the truth. Well... I believe it is true as I do not get why they spray like that. And only when sunny weather. Okay, when clouds one could not see it anyways but when part of the sky is covered with clouds you could still see it.

So please help, anyone know how to be sure about this?
I sure wish it was not true! But it really irritates me that when finally sun is out and clear sky and I want to enjoy the sun fully, they start spraying! Drives me mad! So why do they do it?

### Scompy — 2016-06-09T01:54:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chemtrails-are-they-real.17645/post-196194>

I think it's easy to call 'conspiracy theory.' But wouldn't that be exactly what conventional wisdom wants us to believe that 'everything must be a conspiracy theory?' This method is a great way to control viewpoints and dismiss investigation. The best way to hide something is in plain sight. Any good detective or mystery novel author knows this. Here is the formula: take any natural occurrance that the public becomes acclimated to then use it as a means to an end. Doesn't have to be natural condensation trails produced by airplanes. It could be something like a tree. Everyone gets used to seeing a tree.

[http://waynesword.palomar.edu/images/faketr3b.jpg](http://waynesword.palomar.edu/images/faketr3b.jpg)

Everyone gets used to seeing condensation trails... It's all about finding the details about the natural occurance to know if it's no longer a natural-created event. One would expect to be able to find evidence from the condensation trials by the detection of whatever chemical is there, as well as the loading of the chemicals into planes... who is doing it and for what ends? And what is the **_frequency_** of the delivery of said-chemicals. It would be hard to believe the delivery of chemical agents are frequent thing (like every day), done by private businesses, etc. But aside from chemical evidence collection, when NASA comes out saying to the public they've done geoengineering with lithium, then you have to check the sources to understand why they would reveal such a thing.

I agree that absence of evidence like public disclosure is not evidence of affect. I am just sceptical of absolutes. I have a physics background and was also a USAF pilot for a couple of decades. Jet engine exhaust interacts with the atmosphere. We were always briefed by weather experts before a mission on which altitudes were conducive to atmospheric conditions for creating contrails; we would avoid those to avoid easy visible detection. Not an expert . But, contrails in parallel patterns along a common air route could be what you are looking at. Commercial jetliners don't care about 'conning' so they may well fly at those altitudes--contrails from several airliners flying consecutively along the same route within the conning band of altitudes may look like a nefariuos plot, but may just reflect how air traffic is being routed. But I can't say definitively one way way or the other if chemtrail experimentation is going on or not to study affects on weather or emf affects.

### ElectricUniverse — 2018-08-04T19:31:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chemtrails-are-they-real.17645/post-247490>

I'm old enough to remember what our skies used to look like before chemtrails became so pervasive (I would place that inflection point around late 1990s, give or take). They were really sky blue, with natural looking clouds. If look at old photos and movies that is the case also. But that is not to say there were never any weird clouds and atmospheric phenomena back then, too.

Yes, I think **chemtrails** are _man-made_ and VERY _real_. Their real purpose has never been overtly revealed to public, but a most logical motive is likely geoengineering the climate (to counteract supposed effects of the totally unscientific and unproven theory of man-made CO2 global warming). This is pseudo science and technology gone mad, just like proliferation of EMF electrosmog in today's environment.

Is it an elite conspiracy to kill off large numbers of unwashed humanity slowly with chemtrail constituents falling down to earth to contaminate air, water, and food? If you think about that for a second, consider just how are these people-in-charge perps going to protect themselves and their families from the fallout (and no, I seriously doubt they have exclusive access to a top secret antidote to the pollutants). It is a ludicrous proposition.

A common defense of '_chemtrails are bunk_' camp is that they are really nothing more than engine exhaust (contrails) from jet planes (but today's high-bypass fan engines on today's modern jets exhaust mainly air and little water vapor). So anyway these apologists for our dirty skies claim these weird criss-cross, X, grid, and other streaks in the sky are just contrails on regular airline routes above (and the sky is admittedly covered heavily with airline traffic).

However, they can't explain why on some days, with similar atmospheric conditions, the supposed contrails are gone and the sky is free of streaks. Where did all the airliners go on those days? Did their pilots take the day off?

Anyway, I have noticed recently that chemtrails in my region (in central plains) have disappeared for the longest period I can remember (for weeks). Not a single chemtrail streak or 'cloud'. This is unprecedented. Is it a trend or or are those in charge just regrouping and loading ammo? Time will tell.

**Anyone else out there free of chemtrails scourge for a long time too?**

"China is installing tens of thousands of chambers across the Tibetan Plateau and mountains. These machines will produce very fine silver iodide particles that are then lifted into the atmosphere with upwelling winds. As these particles are dispersed into the atmosphere they act as the nucleating point of condensed water.

In order for water vapor (humidity) in the air to form clouds and eventually rain, it requires a nucleating particle. Typically, this is a tiny particle of dust which en masse produces the clouds we see in the sky. By artificially "seeding" the Tibetan Plateau with silver iodide particles the Chinese government is inducing the formation of clouds where there weren't any before."

Sorry global warming alarmists-- the science and facts simply don't support a man-made planetary heating assumption.

Speaking of cloud nucleation, in a GSM the sun goes quiet, its magnetic fields weaken and go out of phase, and our planet's magnetosphere weakens accordingly. This allows increased cosmic ray flux. These cosmic ray particles serve as cloud nucleation accelerators. End result is more clouds (cooling), more rain and snow (albedo of earth increases).

Jet streams in GSM wander more and become erratic. Resulting movement and collision of air masses will cause weird record cold and even hot weather and unheard of atmospheric compression events (catastrophic flooding, huge-sized hailstorm events, etc.-- we are starting to see all that now).

The big trouble for agriculture (aside from short, unpredictable growing seasons) is that all this precipitation tends to comes all at once and where it happens can not be controlled by man.

GEOENGINEERING 101:

Geoengineering is a form of Electrosmog = Low quantum yield = air pollution = geo-engineering = blocked sunlight = lowered quantum yield = Latitude = higher population density = lowered solar power in all ranges = lowered UV/IRA exposure = low dopamine and melatonin/cortisol/vitamin C = higher melanopsin and leptin dysfunction = higher retinal bonding protein = Earth does not have its full spectrum of light = Tarp over a tree = trees dies no matter if they are watered and have access to CO2 and minerals from the ground = your modern world modeled = mitochondria that cannot make CO2 and/or water and leads to cytosolic cellular dehydration in the cytoplasm surrounding a mitochondria.

### Christina Gagnon — 2019-03-14T16:50:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chemtrails-are-they-real.17645/post-265515>

It is all so heartbreaking - there is so much information out there on chem trails that has convinced me that they are real but I really already knew they were real by my own observation. When I first moved into our home and was gardening the soil was teaming with insects (I even saw a rhinoceros-beetle once) - it's shocking how few insects I see now. Also the trees are dying and so sick-looking. My whole world around me looks sick and the clouds are anything but normal.

### Katie Durham — 2019-03-14T17:26:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chemtrails-are-they-real.17645/post-265518>

Something I noticed a few years ago was that I never ever saw suspect chemtrails when the natural clouds were strictly the puffy white cumulus fair weather type, associated with an unstable air mass. Any hint of cirrus and chemtrails could show up. That had me wondering the extent to which lengthy Xtrails are weather related – in other words, is there something about atmospheric conditions that prevent long Xtrails from forming in unstable air? Has anyone else noticed this, and if so do you have an opinions about it?

EPA regulates jet fuel under the Federal Clean Air Act. Opacity is an issue in certain FCAA programs, like industrial stack emissions. So many thousands of pages of regulations, I never confirmed whether the EPA regulates opacity of jet fuel. But EPA could have blessed various changes in fuel formulations over the years that could have resulted in longer Xtrails from contrails.

Isn’t it also possible that other manmade changes to the atmosphere (nnEMF, whatever) may have changed how quickly contrails disintegrate?

I have never seen these possibilities discussed. I’m not disputing there are chemtrails, just questioning whether all long Xtrails must be chemtrails, which is what I assumed at one time.

A piece of evidence that strongly defends idea that chemtrails are real and actually are aerosol particle injections in stratosphere is a dramatic increase in human heavy metal toxic burdens recently. **Dr Klinghardt** cites informally about lab studies in recent past compared to today that reveal a _striking_ increase in people's aluminum and other toxic metals stored in body tissues. ### Katie Durham — 2019-03-14T18:21:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chemtrails-are-they-real.17645/post-265525>

I'm not disputing whether chemtrails are real. I am questioning why I never see them in unstable skies alongside puffy cumulus clouds and wondering if anyone has an answer to this and what other questions it may raise.

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-03-14T20:35:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chemtrails-are-they-real.17645/post-265535>

> [Katie Durham said:](/goto/post?id=265525)
>
> I'm not disputing whether chemtrails are real. I am questioning why I never see them in unstable skies alongside puffy cumulus clouds and wondering if anyone has an answer to this and what other questions it may raise.

Frankly I haven't noticed a relationship between cumulus cloud skies and presence (or lack of) of chemtrails. I will keep a look out for this. Unless I am mistaken, cumulus are fair weather clouds in stable conditions (thermal convection currents produce them). In unstable atmospheric conditions you are more likely to see cumulonimbus clouds.

Cumulus are among the lowest altitude clouds-- about 2-3,000 feet.

Most of cloud formations I see these days are stratus, stratocumulus, and altocumulus (high altitude). Completely sunny blue sky days (no clouds) are an amazing rarity. Beautiful fair weather cumulus (partly sunny days) seem to be a bit lacking in current climate too (Midwest).

On these frequent cloudy days there could be significant atmospheric spraying above cloud deck but we wouldn't know it.

We are likely to see more cloudy days overall as we progress into Grand Solar Minimum. So enjoy sunny days whenever you can.

### Katie Durham — 2019-03-14T20:58:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chemtrails-are-they-real.17645/post-265538>

Thanks, I may be confusing my clouds. This comes from having been very active at hawk migration lookouts in the northeast, where if hawks are not flying we’re stuck chatting about the weather. The big flights in the fall occur after clearing cold fronts, when sunny skies create strong thermal upcurrents and fair weather clouds. (And assuming a favorable tailwind.) The days that follow are increasingly more populated by cirrus clouds, and hawks do not get lift at those times even if sunny, so we wait for the next passing front. Perhaps stable v. unstable in that context just referred to updrafts. At any rate, I don’t see long trails days when only fair weather clouds are in the sky.

### Dave Key — 2019-04-05T17:28:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chemtrails-are-they-real.17645/post-267399>

1\. Anybody in aviation maintenance (I was) can tell you how turbofan jet engine combustion does not produce vapor trails like the ones you see above. You would have to fly through a waterfall in order to force that much water vapor through a jet engine.
2\. There are usually a couple days per week that you do not see trails from planes. Does that mean all commercial airline traffic stopped for 2 days?
3\. If somebody needs to be convinced of the existence of technology being used to spray our skies they could start by looking at the patents filed for the equipment in use today.
Start with US patent 2010/0043443 filed Feb.25,2010 This equipment is designed with an ultrasound generator combined with a waveguide used to direct ultrasonic waves aimed at the chemtrail plume to avoid trail formation.
In other words they are aiming sound waves at the plumes to dissipate them in order to make them less visible.
4\. Any pilot can tell you that at any altitude or in any weather conditions jet engines do not produce trails.
5\. There are two excellent sources of information on this subject at climateviewer.com and weathermodificationhistory.com
6\. Some people will hold on to their ignorance no matter what so try not to waste too much energy convincing someone that refuses to be convinced.

Thanks for a great checklist of talking points.
