# Thread: Rising From the Ashes
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rising-from-the-ashes.17743/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 42
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Samantha Richards — 2016-03-28T04:33:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rising-from-the-ashes.17743/post-191616>

I discovered Jack almost a year ago on Facebook in the Quantum Health group. I read the pinned post as instructed for new members and lurked, reading the convos, mind being blown everyday. I knew quickly I'd come across something I needed to be paying attention to and so I did, earnestly. What led me to the group in March 2015?

This is my story...

In October of 2014, I was fed up with a lot in my life, the path I was on, the marriage that had fallen to pieces, and I was the only one putting forth any effort to save it. I could no longer pull all the weight to make us both better. I started to realize I could only carry myself to "better". And boy, did I need to get better. I had been, for almost three years, spiraling downward in a dark and fat pit of depression, anger, resentment, and self loathing.

What started my spiral downward was when my alcoholic father lost his job, began drinking all day, everyday, and my mother would call me, desparate for me to come help her, because my father was being physically and verbally abusive. Sometimes he even hit me when I would come to help her. Several times we called the cops. He would spend the night in jail, call home crying promising he wouldn't drink anymore and apologizing. My depressed and confused mother clung to the fact that she'd made vows "through sickness and in health. Your father is sick. I love him. Divorce isn't an option." So the cycle continued. At this time I was 31 years old. Why I even continued spending time with my immediate family is still beyond me. I grew up in a verbally and physically abusive household. Daily yelling, cursing, name calling, and physical violence. Busted lips, black eyes, stitches, punches, bruises, hairline fractures, holes in walls, swollen faces, aching heart from being told you're worthless, a fatass, a loser, a slut, a piece of shit, and being told they wished you were dead.

My father was out of town for work most of my life and my mother was obese, sedentary, and in front of the television my entire life. I always knew I didn't want to be like them. Since they didn't truly raise me, I was raised by the books I read, musicians whom I listened to the most, and movies that I watched to escape the shit that was my home. Education wasn't important to my parents. Who cared if I did my homework? They didn't. So, I didn't either. When classmates in high school were prepping for college, I had no idea what was going on. My parents just wanted me to get a job at the local reataurant and move out as soon as I turned 18. They weren't even concerned with teaching me about bank accounts or getting my driver license. Friends taught me and helped me get my license at age 18 finally. I saved up for my first truck then too. From age 19 up, my friends I spent the most time with were concerned with partying and drinking. Those are the people I was influenced most by. For years. Bar life. Dancing club life. I always felt I wanted a different, more stable, more fulfilling and enriching life. I still searched for it. I still loved reading books. I knew I wanted something different. By age 25, year 2005, I began finding the answers I was searching for. My path was beginning to change. I got a good job at a bank and began working my way up, and was finally beginning to see I was capable and worth something. I met a lot of neat people in banking that I admired. I became involved in the community and became friends local business owners. I began going to church and learned that families could actually have love and support inside them. I knew that I wanted that one day and that if I could have a family, I'd do so much better than what I had growing up.

Fast forward to 2007. My mother had triple bypass surgery. She continued to smoke. Was diabetic. Continued to drink nothing but Dr. Pepper. I tried to get her to go to the park with me and go walking. The pain she felt from her fibromayalgia was too much, she'd say. Every time she'd go from a sitting to a standing position, she'd moan with pain. I hated that she wouldn't do ANYTHING to help herself.

Fast forward to 2012. My mother had several strokes that left her without the use of her left hand or left leg/foot. My parents had lost their house by this time, because of my father losing his job in 2010. They were living in a shitty apartment in the ghetto of Memphis. The place was crawling with roaches. Police tape on the building they lived in because an upstairs neighbor was shot and killed shortly before. I got a call from my mother one day. She was crying. My father had tried to kill himself. She had called an ambulance to come get him. But, she needed me to come help her because she couldn't walk. I went to their apartment and what I found was heartbreaking and infuriating at the same time. There was a lot of my father's blood in the middle of the living room floor from where he'd slit his wrists. There were empty Vodka gallons littering the kitchen and their bedroom. My mother was sitting in her recliner in her own feces and urine, matted hair from it not being brushed in days, bruises from my father punching her. I cried. I called my husband to come help me get her cleaned up. Luckily, my husband was a patient care tech in his college days and knew how to handle her like a champ. I brought her to my apartment. She needed so much to help her. I called work to tell them I needed some time off to help with my family. I ended up resigning from the bank to help and get everything straightened out. We eventually got my mother put in a nursing home because she just didn't want to try to get better. She was okay with my husband and I having to do everything for her, take her to the bathroom, get her pants down for her. wipe her ass after every shit. Take her outside everytime she wanted to smoke a cigarette. She had no will. My father had been living out of their car. He was spending my mother's social security on booze and cheap motel rooms. He'd go visit her in her nursing home and drink his vodka and sleep next to her bed in a chair. One night, the nursing home called me to inform me that he'd been banned from returning and that he'd physically abused my mother. I went to see her. Sure enough, a black eye and bruises on her arms and belly. I was already hating my father. This was the icing on the cake. But then my father called me a few weeks later, crying. He said, "Sam, I hurt your Mama. I don't want to live anymore." I asked him where he was. He said he didn't know. I gave him my address and told him to come to my place. We hung up. I immediately called the police and told them the situation. That he was suicidal and driving drunk. The police showed up a few minutes after he got there. They took him to a crisis center. He was transferred to the Memphis Mental Health Institution where we found out he is schizophrenic and bi polar. I began to feel compassion for him and the hate went away. But, then I began worrying about him, which later I found out was just as unhealthy as hating him. I tried to help him when he was released from the hospital. My husband gifted him an old pick up he rarely used. He lived with us for a while. We helped him get on his feet. He got his own apartment near us. Then he bagan drinking again and got suicidal again and I began losing sleep again. Much longer story short: I have washed my hands of him. I have helped him and worried about him enough. My peace is now more important. I can't help someone that clearly doesn't want to help theirself.

Fast forward to the demise of my marriage in October 2014: Maybe it was the toll of my parents. Maybe there's more to the story. But, between me gaining a lot of weight that got me up to 230 pounds at 5'4" tall, me not being able to get pregnant, and the infidelity, I was fed up. I began Paleo Keto, joined the Facebook group titled as such, and lost weight. I never even knew there were all these facebook groups out there since I wasn't such a big facebooker. Tristan Haggard was in the group and invited me to the QH group where I began learning from Jack. The light story. The dopamine story. It all resonates with me. I'm trying not to become like my parents. The mental health the light story supports calls my name. I want to know better. I want to do better. I want to make better decisions. And for the past year, I've been doing just that.

The names they called me for 30 something years, the abuse. It's over. I'm rising from the ashes, a new woman that has learned this past year how to love myself and what that even means.

Quantum Health is my new path. For life.

Thank you, Jack.

Sam

### Samantha Richards — 2016-04-05T16:56:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rising-from-the-ashes.17743/post-192091>

Sitting out in the morning sunlight, I've been thinking about forgiveness. Why it's so important. Why and how we need to do it. I have let resentment come in and grow much of my life. As resentment has grown in the past, so has my inability to find inner peace.

I have made it a priority to find inspiration to help me forgive because I refuse to keep a poisonous and victim mentality. I now fill my life with people and books that illuminate my path to forgiveness, peace, and growth in this life.

Do I want to live a life filled with anger and bitterness and isolate myself, living in a circle of receiving and giving the poison? Or do I want to be at peace, have joy, love, gratitude, growth and share peace, joy, love, gratitude and growth with others? It is important to always know that I have a choice.

I have replaced the negative energy of self pity and resentment, where I would stuff my face with candy bars, with the positive energy outlets of workouts, meditation, and spirituality. I am still human and have slips occasionally. But I now know what to do. I now know how to change negativity to positivity and release the poisonous anger into love and forgiveness.

I always have a choice.

I moved into an apartment recently that is very close to a transformer. My bedroom happens to be a few feet from it. No Bueno. Still a newby at quantum living and it never occurred to me to look out for something like that. I thought I was doing good by making sure my apartment was on the ground floor.

I've also been craving chocolate a bit more. Last night, instead of going and buying a dark chocolate bar, I decided to make my own chocolate paste with half a scoop of undenatured vanilla whey protein, a tiny bit of water, and a tbsp of cocoa powder. I sweetened it with a bit of organic stevia.

Immediately after eating that, I felt a physical release of pressure in my head and temples. Like an instant pressure release. Katie M tells me cocoa and nicotine have similar effects on the brain. I'm about to search the blogs for any science behind this...

### Samantha Richards — 2016-07-19T18:11:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/rising-from-the-ashes.17743/post-198594>

Recent biohack I had to do on myself. I had to see if all my QH efforts were really the reason for my profound improvement just in the past 8 months alone. I was feeling light years ahead of where I was 8 months ago, better than I ever have in my entire life actually.

Since I'm not a college grad or a scientist, the only way I can see if this science and these claims I read about are real, is if I do biohacks. I have to test these things on myself and go by the results.

So, I thought to NOT do any of the QH protocols for 4 days. For 4 nights and 4 days, I didn't block blue light, I didn't go to the lake for my morning ritual of sunlight and grounding that I do for HOURS everyday with a bag of books that I read a bit from, I didn't turn my phone to airplane mode even at night, I didn't eat seafood, I ate after sunset, I woke in the middle of the night and got on my phone...

I began feeling the effects on the first night, as I didn't sleep well. (I had eaten past 8pm, watched television and didn't block blue light!) The next morning I could see that apathy was setting in as far as me working toward achieving my goals each day and my love of learning and reading books was waning. I actually thought of turning the television on and finding a TV show to watch. Which I did. (And which I haven't had the desire to do in 8 months because the QH path has been too much fun to even consider television) I found a show and realized a couple hours later that I'd just mindlessly munched on food I hadn't really been hungry for and that I'd wasted hours of time I could have actually accomplished something like learning more, or any other number of things. For those 4 mornings I wasn't reading my books. I wasn't making the sunrise a priority to catch. The results? My motivation spiraled downward rapidly. My sense of purpose disappeared. I gained a few pounds. My internal compass seemed broken. I started feeling sad and lonely. I started wanting to throw in the towel on all of my efforts and say FUCK IT ALL. The feelings that led me to Jack in the first place came back with a vengeance and have reminded me exactly WHY I NEED to and love catching the sunrise, reading books while laying in the grass and facing the sun, eating seafood, blocking blue light, drinking tons of spring water, avoiding EMFs, keeping my phone on airplane mode for the most part, staying OUTSIDE as much as possible. I had stopped loving myself. And the darkness quickly crept back in. There is light at the end of this dark biohacking tunnel I put myself in. I will get to that light quickly by riding the three legged stool. Everyday. I need it. And it brings me joy. Dopamine for the win.

Back to creating the life I love, powered by sun, water, and magnetism.

# Thread: Is aerobic capacity a good indicator of mitochondrial health
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-aerobic-capacity-a-good-indicator-of-mitochondrial-health.17745/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Aerose91 — 2016-03-28T20:55:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-aerobic-capacity-a-good-indicator-of-mitochondrial-health.17745/post-191658>

Some people are just "naturally" better endurance athletes than others and i know that probably goes beyond just fast & slow twitch muscle fibers. Since getting M.E. my aerobic capacity hit the floor while anerobic seems relatively unchanged. However, both my brother and i growing up seemed to have really poor aerobic conditioning but fine, if not better than normal, anerobic.

Once i started training heavily i was able to get my aerobic capacity better but never great- and it took more effort for me to get there it seemed. Consequently, it was during a time i was really pushing the cardio hard when i first got sick.
I noticed variances like this in my clients as well.. might this be a reflection of the mitochondrial health you are born with from your mother?

### Jack Kruse — 2016-03-29T03:18:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-aerobic-capacity-a-good-indicator-of-mitochondrial-health.17745/post-191681>

indirect..........but not horrible

Ever tried HeartMath?

When Scott opened up the hatch where all the water and wiring were found he found the are filled with microbial life. See on Earth when our cells and body are connected to Earth's magnetic field and to the sun's rays, it created a sea of EZ water in our cells that surround our mitochondria to shrinks their sizer to control what they can and cannot do. Might the cancer explosion since 1900 be from the human civiliazation creating things that mimic space here on Earth? I will remind you yet again that Tesla and Westinghouse used AC current for the first time in 1893 world's fair in Chicago and pissed off Rockefeller and his kerosene lamps and aggravated JP Morgan and Edison DC current plants.........this is where our modern problems began, in my humble opinion. My members got a webinar on Kawasaki's Disease and electromagnetic sensitivities..........to show these effects in detail. Now you have the third and final nail in the coffin at what is really going on in our blue lit microwaved world. ### Jack Kruse — 2016-08-09T04:02:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/space-cytoplasm-mitochondria-and-kawasakis-disease.17747/post-199447>

Space is the product of dimension and space/time is a synonym for gravity hence it is about scale. Mitochondria par attention to scale. We inherit them from mom. Mitochondria are bacterial in origin. What few people relaize is that the human microbiome is also inherited from mom. Light has massive effects on human development and electrical function. The natural forces in the universe are relative to scale and dimension. The interesting part of nature's equation is that light is not relative. It's speed is constant in space but it only becomes relative when light is captured in a medium. That medium in us is cells and mitochondria and the microbiome are clearly have a cellular base. Nothing is constant everything is relative, but light's speed; and geometry only exists when scale and dimensions exist. This means force can both exists and ceases to exist as the scale of energy changes. Things strong can be made weak, and things weak can be made Herculean. This makes everything we believe today relative to the scale in action. This is why astronauts can develop a disease of aging in a few months in space. Their mitochondria can no longer fat burn and have to default to a Warburg metabolism. This favors NAD+ and pseudohypoxia state. That state, and the nnEMF/blue light also affect the microbiome in astronauts. That change in the microbiome leads to heart disease because our microbiome is like a vagal nerve stimulator on Earth. In space, the microbiome and mitochondria are both altered by the alien environment in several ways. A hippo does not need to know quantum mechanics because he is built to just live it on Eart, but the human astronaut does need to understand it because mitochondria and the microbiome work quantum mechanically and the environment of space and Earth are not equivalent. They offer distinctly different possibilitiies because of geometry changes in the microbiome and mitochondria. Humans have the curious ability to think and fuck things up in all the environments they visit. Their instincts should dominant their thoughts and actions because of the power of their brain...........This is why humans on Earth have to become the hippo. When you do, you won't need to understand the physics, but humans are curious and want to know because of their brain's ability.
Life is really simple, but we insist on making it complicated today. Cells seek simplicity in a connection to the sun and Earth and not a connection to the complexity of technology. Once that happens we lose the ability to employ the massive capabilities of quantum mechanics in a cell. This example is also proven in Subacute combined immunodeficiency. This disease was made prominent in a movie: the boy who lived in a plastic bubble. They usually die by 2 years old but David Vetter was born in 1971 and lived in an artificial environment on Earth. He lived 12 years. To save his life his own sister gave him a bone marrow transplant but the doctors did not know she had a quiescient viral infection of EBV. That EBV virus likely ruined the geometry in his mitochondria and likely ruined the bacteria in his gut from making co factors, vitamins, and energy to sustain themselves and activate his T regulator cells in his gut. His death shocked them because they never knew about quantum biology in 1983. Up until this biopsy he was in great physical and mental health, but soon after the virus altered his bacterial remnants he was altered in quick fashion just like our astronaut in the story. This kid allowed us to see something unique about SCID in a kid allowed to grow.........David had very poor spatial memory, but was hypersensitive to the passage of TIME because he had no way to slow and capture light for his cells, his mitochondria, and he had a very simple microbiome. The light he got was not sunlight either. His brain was built in an non human, non Earth environment, so his brain had no need to find its way around space, it only understood time. This is a function of light constant speed and his mitochondria and microbiome's inability to adapt to this environment. There is a deep lesson here about space/time and the geometry of life.

hi from down under
So I've had radiation and chemo (state 2 breast cancer ) and my oncologist has told me to stay out of the sun. Right now I'm getting my Vit D rose from being in it. Dr Jack any suggestions? I've been sceptical about the whole cancer treatment circus since the beginning; a catalyst for all the research I've been doing for the past 3 years. A global quest for a cancer cure Ty Bollinger, Chris Cures Cancer and Dr Jack Krause. Been able to give all this info to anyone who may need it including my GP.
Also waiting to get my genes tested. Was told by the practitioner that the genes you are born with can be switched on or off. Sometimes the Vit D gene is switched off so no matter how much you expose yourself to Vit D your body will not assimilate it. If you know from testing that this Gene is switched off you can do things to switch it on? Ie Broccoli extract.
Again any thoughts on this ? Always questioning.

[http://www.health-science-spirit.com/borax.htm](http://www.health-science-spirit.com/borax.htm)

I have been having really good luck with alkalizing - I use potassium bicarbonate then go out into the sun - in fact, I put sodium bicarbonate in my coffee and didn't have to pee for 2 hours...

[http://drsircus.com/medicine/sodium-bicarbonate-baking-soda/alkalinity-ph-2](http://drsircus.com/medicine/sodium-bicarbonate-baking-soda/alkalinity-ph-2)

Los Angeles has lots of uranium in its water - I couldn't believe it when I read baking soda will clear it -
[http://drsircus.com/medicine/sodium...bonate-clears-the-kidneys-and-soil-of-uranium](http://drsircus.com/medicine/sodium-bicarbonate-baking-soda/uranium-contaminates-drinking-water-sodium-bicarbonate-clears-the-kidneys-and-soil-of-uranium)

And of course methylene blue might be a good way to go -

My niece has breast cancer and my sister-in-law during her chemo covered her head with ice - it stopped her from losing her hair - ice baths would be fantastic for you as of course a ketogenic diet with seafood -

The first thing though that you should do is clean up your environment... change every lightbulb - lightbulbs have to be non-polarizing like the sun - incandescent lights can be found at Lowes - as well as red party bulbs - measure your environment to see where the EMF is highest - and of course, get your eyes into the sun in the morning - it seems to me if you keep yourself busy fixing up your environment it will lower cortisol which is paramount - Dr. Kruse wrote a "Cancer hits, what's next" blog post that was really good -

Get as much resveretrol down as money can buy... I like Japanese Knotweed - you can get 2 pounds of it on Amazon for $28.00 - I think the dose is 3 tbls/day - this is from Steven Harrod Buhner -

And of course you can read about the light signalling gone awry as per Fritz Popp: cannabis oil anyone?

And of course there's the Italians injecting the tumor with baking soda and curing it...
[http://www.cancertutor.com/simoncini/](http://www.cancertutor.com/simoncini/)

Depth of the cancer is no longer a problem:
[http://www.engadget.com/2015/03/18/light-therapy-treats-deep-cancer/](http://www.engadget.com/2015/03/18/light-therapy-treats-deep-cancer/)
[http://www.nih.gov/news-events/nih-research-matters/targeted-light-therapy-destroys-cancer-cells](http://www.nih.gov/news-events/nih-research-matters/targeted-light-therapy-destroys-cancer-cells)

And here we have breast cancer on ice... :
[http://wreg.com/2015/07/20/doctors-put-breast-cancer-on-ice-to-avoid-surgery/](http://wreg.com/2015/07/20/doctors-put-breast-cancer-on-ice-to-avoid-surgery/)

Are they actually doing this? Aluminum? To reflect the sunlight??? Insane... Sometimes - actually lately usually - I feel like I'm living a nightmare of stupidity around me that I can't awaken from...
[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4717532/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4717532/)

**Assessing the direct occupational and public health impacts of solar radiation management with stratospheric aerosols**

****Abstract**
Geoengineering is the deliberate large-scale manipulation of environmental processes that affects the Earth’s climate, in an attempt to counteract the effects of climate change. Injecting sulfate aerosol precursors and designed nanoparticles into the stratosphere to (i.e., solar radiation management \[SRM\]), has been suggested as one approach to geoengineering. Although much is being done to unravel the scientific and technical challenges around geoengineering, there have been few efforts to characterize the potential human health impacts of geoengineering, particularly with regards to SRM approaches involving stratospheric aerosols. This paper explores this information gap. Using available evidence, we describe the potential direct occupational and public health impacts of exposures to aerosols likely to be used for SRM, including environmental sulfates, black carbon, metallic aluminum, and aluminum oxide aerosols. We speculate on possible health impacts of exposure to one promising SRM material, barium titanate, using knowledge of similar nanomaterials. We also explore current regulatory efforts to minimize exposure to these toxicants. Our analysis suggests that adverse public health impacts may reasonably be expected from SRM via deployment of stratospheric aerosols. Little is known about the toxicity of some likely candidate aerosols, and there is no consensus regarding acceptable levels for public exposure to these materials. There is also little infrastructure in place to evaluate potential public health impacts in the event that stratospheric aerosols are deployed for solar radiation management. We offer several recommendations intended to help characterize the potential occupation and public health impacts of SRM, and suggest that a comprehensive risk assessment effort is needed before this approach to geoengineering receives further consideration.

**

I got a thermometer and put some water in a big trash can to 53 degrees. I was in for 10 min and he temp crept up so I added more ice and brought it back down. I think i stayed in for 15 more min. I began shivering slightly towards the end and when I would move my hands in certain ways i would feel an electric shock through the end of my arm.

Kind of unpleasant. It continued for 5-10 after I got out when I would move my hand in certain ways.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-02T07:29:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/shocks-going-through-my-hands.17761/post-191858>

Ryan I have no idea if you've listened to the April 2016 webinar yet but you must. And really focus on the Time series to get way up to speed.

**So incident light from the sun really acts as the currency of the compound pharmacy in our pituitary every day to make things we need from light to live our life in our local environment**. This makes your hormone panel a “shadow cast” of your local light environment. This is why your “_local zip code_” for light is incredibly important. That is a key point.

So what is the real power of sunlight? The entire spectrum of light has 73 octaves of frequencies. Life only exists in one octave, namely the visible spectrum of light. If you understand factorial math, that means within our single octave of the visible spectrum that retinal cells and skin cells can control many biochemicals. Using numbers to make the case, incident light from the sun provides **8,683,317,618,811,886,495,518,194,401,280,000,000 different frequencies.**

This is a staggering level of power and control that leverages our proteins in cells. So when you open up any biochemistry book and realize that biochemistry only uses 100,000 substrate in reactions every second, you realize light can control every last one of them. The speed at which it occurs also offends the common sense of biochemists, because they fail to remember that the _**photoelectric effect occurs instantaneously with no time delay**_. We just cannot fathom what light is capable of, because we don’t observe what it can do at the small scales in a cell. When you factor in that the photoelectric effect acts instantaneously, with no time delay, then you begin to see how 100,000 biochemical reactions can _easily occur_ per second using light frequencies from the visible spectrum easily.

**Here’s a list of possible intracellular sources of biophotons:**

1\. NADH / NADPH
2\. FAD / FMN (Flavins)
3\. Cytochrome c
4\. Tryptophan
5\. Tyrosine
6\. Phenylalanine & Histidine
7\. Lipid peroxidation products
8\. DNA (during oxidative stress or repair)
9\. Mitochondrial respiratory chain
10\. ATP hydrolysis (indirectly)
11\. Reactive oxygen species (ROS)
12\. Singlet oxygen decay
13\. Microtubules (via speculative quantum or resonance effects)
14\. Chromophores in proteins
15\. Ubiquinone (Coenzyme Q10)
16\. Porphyrins (e.g. heme groups) Chlorophyl
17\. Membrane potential changes (indirect triggers)
18\. Peroxisomal reactions
19\. Excited carbonyl groups
20\. Melanin (especially in pigment cells.)

Carbonyl groups (C=O) are functional groups found in various organic molecules, such as aldehydes, ketones, carboxylic acids, and their derivatives, and they play critical roles in biological processes. However, the concept of "excited" carbonyl groups typically applies to photochemistry or spectroscopy, where a molecule absorbs energy (e.g., light) and enters an electronically excited state.

Carbonyl-Containing Compounds in Humans and Their Purposes

1.  Aldehydes (e.g., Retinal, Glyceraldehyde)
2.  Structure: Contain a carbonyl group (C=O) bonded to at least one hydrogen atom (R-CHO). Purpose: Retinal: A critical aldehyde in the visual cycle, retinal is a component of rhodopsin, the light-sensitive pigment in the retina. When light strikes retinal, it undergoes a conformational change (isomerization), initiating the visual signal transduction pathway that allows humans to perceive light. Glyceraldehyde: An intermediate in glycolysis and gluconeogenesis, glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate contains a carbonyl group and is essential for energy production (ATP generation) and carbohydrate metabolism. Biological Role: Aldehydes participate in energy metabolism and sensory functions (vision).
3.  Ketones (e.g., Acetone, Acetoacetate, Beta-Hydroxybutyrate)
4.  Structure: Contain a carbonyl group bonded to two carbon-containing groups (R-CO-R'). Purpose: Ketone Bodies: During fasting, starvation, or low-carbohydrate diets, the liver produces ketone bodies (acetoacetate, beta-hydroxybutyrate, and acetone) via ketogenesis. These serve as alternative energy sources for tissues like the brain, heart, and muscles when glucose is scarce. They are especially critical during prolonged fasting or in conditions like ketoacidosis. Energy Storage and Transport: Ketones are water-soluble and can cross the blood-brain barrier, providing an efficient energy source during metabolic stress. Biological Role: Ketones support energy homeostasis under conditions of limited glucose availability.
5.  Carboxylic Acids (e.g., Fatty Acids, Amino Acids, Citric Acid)
6.  Structure: Contain a carboxyl group (COOH), which includes a carbonyl group bonded to a hydroxyl group (R-COOH). Purpose:
    -   Fatty Acids: These are components of lipids (e.g., triglycerides, phospholipids) and are stored in adipose tissue. They are oxidized in beta-oxidation to produce energy (ATP) and are critical for cell membrane structure (e.g., phospholipids in cell membranes).
    -   Amino Acids: Many amino acids (e.g., aspartic acid, glutamic acid) contain carboxyl groups. They are building blocks of proteins and play roles in enzyme catalysis, signaling, and pH regulation.
    -   Citric Acid: A tricarboxylic acid in the citric acid cycle (Krebs cycle), citric acid is central to cellular respiration, facilitating the production of ATP by oxidizing acetyl-CoA. It also contributes to the tart taste of citrus fruits.
    -   Biological Role: Carboxylic acids are essential for energy production, structural integrity, and metabolic regulation.
7.  Amides (e.g., Peptide Bonds in Proteins) Structure: Contain a carbonyl group bonded to a nitrogen atom (R-CONH2 or R-CONHR'). Purpose: Peptide Bonds: Amides form the backbone of proteins via peptide bonds between amino acids. Proteins are critical for nearly all biological functions, including enzymatic catalysis, structural support (e.g., collagen), immune response (e.g., antibodies), and signaling (e.g., hormones). Urea: An amide produced in the liver during the urea cycle, urea is a key molecule for excreting excess nitrogen (from amino acid breakdown) via urine, maintaining nitrogen balance. Biological Role: Amides are fundamental to protein structure and nitrogen metabolism.
8.  Esters (e.g., Triglycerides, Phospholipids) Structure: Contain a carbonyl group bonded to an oxygen atom linked to another carbon (R-COOR'). Purpose:
    -   Triglycerides: These are the primary form of fat storage in adipose tissue, providing a high-energy reserve (approximately 40 kJ/g compared to 15.7 kJ/g for carbohydrates). They are broken down into fatty acids and glycerol during lipolysis to supply energy.
    -   Phospholipids: Esters containing carbonyl groups are key components of cell membranes, forming the lipid bilayer that maintains cellular integrity and facilitates signaling.
    -   Biological Role: Esters are critical for energy storage and cellular structure.

**ALL OF THESE THINGS EMIT LIGHT IN YOU. BIOCHEMISTRY MISSES EVERY LAST ONE OF THEM. DO NOT BELIEVE IT? OPEN ANY BIOCHEMISTRY BOOK AND TELL ME IF YOU SEE ANY MENTION OF BIOPHOTONS OR UPE.**

The key finding of the Andechs bunker by Rutger Wever's experiments, was the discovery that human sleep/wake cycles could desynchronize from the circadian rhythm of core body temperature. Stop now and think why they didn't want to stop CT. They sense their mitochondria is shrinking and they are getting better.

This phenomenon, called 'Spontaneous Internal Desynchrony', typically occurred in subjects after a duration of days to weeks. Strikingly, the length of the sleep/wake cycle varied significantly (from 12 h in some subjects to 68 h in others), while the core body temperature rhythm maintained a period close to 25 h. Under these conditions, subjects were typically unaware of the change in their sleep/wake cycle period. The exact biological basis of this desynchronization remains unknown but I believe it is quantized by UPEs.

The light frequencies above can only entangle particles in a therapeutic window and when you put someone in that window they instinctually know they are doing well...........because their % heteroplasmy rates begin to shrink.

Ryan it is time it get up to speed. Let us get to it.

One of the hallmarks of the living system in cells is that they are exquisitely sensitive to specific, weak signals. Most of these signals are electromagnetic. The human eye can detect single photons falling on the retina, which pass information directly to the SCN and all its mitochondria. This retina is where the light-sensitive photoreceptors in cells send out an action potential representing a million-fold amplification of the energy in the photon. This defines what a non-linear stimulus is. Some of you know it by the butterfly effect.

It is not just a property of the central retinal pathways. The entire system is a plasma or syncytium of excitable matter ready to react to the environment's EMF signals. Similarly, a few molecules of pheromones in the air is sufficient to attract male insects to their mates. This sensitivity is characteristic of all parts of the system and is a consequence of the energy stored = related to the AMO physics of organization.

No part of the system has to be pushed or pulled into action nor be subjected to mechanical regulation and control. These signals often direct things in cells into macroscopic actions. I do understand research IS expensive to do, AND peer reviewed is often not a great thing but at least for her to back it with something

Now, I am aware EDTA IV treatments fall under **centralized medicine** (even though centralized medicine does everything to discredit it, even after the TACT I, II, III clinical studies, that make it category A1). At the moment, I am still using chelation for certain conditions, yet I am always reassessing everything I do. For now, it is still on.
As I can teach more patients about melanin and fixing the gut, and as patients start to really apply consistently decentralized tools, and as I get different patients - I would be really curious to see what happens from that point of view. Because, in my experience, EDTA **does not fully fix the problem long term** I am aware of that. It chelates heavy metals and decrease body store, no doubt about it. Many problems do get fixed but some of them (like the cardiac issues) often only for a few years, unless on a maintenance protocol - which never made sense to me...

(note; Riksa- You are correct, oral EDTA pills make no sense, they are not absorbed enough in the bloodstream, and will chelate nutrients from food in the bowels, but not substantially the heavy metals from the body. Liposomal EDTA might be better, same with EDTA suppositories, but I have no experience with those)

_Dr. Love has 18+ years’ experience training or working in the field of natural medicine and organic nutrition. ### caroline — 2016-04-03T04:14:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/april-1-2016.17762/post-191931>

> [Richelle Jones said:](/goto/post?id=191887)
>
> well i will miss your fb posts because i read everything... i am sorry i am not able to read things and post amazing questions but i am just a plodder and I read everything and i am amazed that i can listen to the april webinar and i think i understand what u are talking about but i can't ask any pointed questions...but i get what u are saying and i can apply it to my life because i have listened to every webinar and read every blog numerous times...i would love to help my friends who have degenerative disc disease and menieres...and PD and my family who have HD and AD...i hope that the forum can be helpful for these because i have been a member for many years but i have never been confident to post questions because some people can be vicious and i just want info ....not judgement .. the fb pages are rife with that but i hope here i can just get help. social media is a vicious world but here where u are paying for privilege of being here i hope will be differnent. I have referred friends here but they have been put off by negative attitudes..i aim to educate all i refer but people have to be patient with them so they are not discouraged at the outset

I am sorry that you and your friends find negative attitudes here .......
Everyone here seems to give an extra ordinary amount of time to this forum - even when they should be off their computer.

Three or four years ago there was a huge meltdown and a lot of negativity - but that was mostly directed at Jack. There was a few of us who did take some abuse - but that was only directed at us. People have commonly said that we were being "lambs" led to the slaughter ...and we couldn't think for ourselves.
JK did tell me to stop being so nice ....so I did tell a few people exactly what I thought - but those were very negative people and people like that usually move on pretty quickly.... and that was directed at me.

There are a number of us who have stuck it out with Jack ......... we are the ones turning our life around!

All the long time members are always happy to try and help and answer questions if we can .....but we also have learned what are the most important things to live a long and healthy and happy life ..... and getting off this damn computer is #1!

I apologise if we haven't seemed to be helpful .....but please remember this is Jack's life's work - and we wouldn't presume to put words in his mouth. Maybe I have my head in the sand - but I can't even imagine what negative attitudes you are speaking of???

Also ..... JK's main principle has always been ..... he will put the stuff out there - we have to do the hard yards. He has always said he will not spoon feed this stuff to us - we have to dig in and do the work ....how can it be worthwhile otherwise?

A lot of new people that stop in here ....state their problem and want answers - that isn't what Jackkruse.com is all about. All of us long timers have done the work for ourselves ...our own n=1. That does not translate to anyone else in any way, shape or form!

We all spend huge amounts of time reading and working on our own n=1 ....and with no science background \[in my case\] it is hard.

We are all a work in progress ........JK does give a huge amount of help on a regular basis - and that is all for free. And, as Jack has said, if you want the real "juice" then become a member and help pay some of the freight around here. That is only fair and responsible.

Thanks JK for all of this......

Words can never express what you have done for so many of us.

If I was of child bearing age ..... I would name a son Jack! LOL!

> A lot of new people that stop in here ....state their problem and want answers - that isn't what Jackkruse.com is all about. All of us long timers have done the work for ourselves ...our own n=1. That does not translate to anyone else in any way, shape or form!

>
> Words can never express what you have done for so many of us.

# Thread: new to this
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-this.17763/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### hello — 2016-04-02T07:27:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-this.17763/post-191857>

Hello all!

Just introducing myself. I'm a Sydneysider new to all this. My story in short is that I moved to London winter for a research fellowship and after about 3 months fell dog sick. I had pains in the gut and shortness of breath every day for about a year. A slew of doctors couldn't diagnose me with anything, except one well meaning doc who said that 'for some reason' my lungs were only filling up halfway. I tried loads of prescription meds and also tried every natural supplement and several different diets but still felt like I was locked in a chamber with no oxygen in it. it was a pretty nasty experience.

Anyway I come back to Australia........ and one day read about vitamin D and its connection with respiratory disease. decided to sit in the midday sun for 2 hours and see what happened. I ended up sitting in a park and ended up grounding too (knowing literally nothing about magnetism, light, or grounding at the time). Long story short - the three days after were the first days in over a year that I'd been able to fill my lungs more than halfway.

Right now I have no trouble breathing anymore, so long as I ground for an hour a day or get a half hour of sunlight. I'm SO GLAD this forum exists because if I hadn't found it I'd be stuck with literally no idea what the hell was going on. I haven't been able to bring myself to tell anyone that sitting in the sun in a park is the best health intervention I ever made because I know they'd look at me like I was nuts or was faking my symptoms. looking forward to reading everything there is to read on here. Thanks Dr Jack for making this forum.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-02T07:32:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-to-this.17763/post-191859>

welcome. You can never get well in the same environment you got ill within........begin there. It won't be easy but anything in life worth while i worth fighting for.

# Thread: diet, genes, and epigenetics
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diet-genes-and-epigenetics.17768/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### hello — 2016-04-03T14:13:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diet-genes-and-epigenetics.17768/post-191956>

Hey all,

I wanted to see if people had any thoughts on this. I've read a lot of Dr. Kelley's work on optimal diets -- one thing with his approach is that he didn't prescribe One Correct Diet for everyone. He initially prescribed raw vegan to all his patients, but later ended up making his wife very sick by getting her to eat raw vegan too. She got sicker and sicker, then one day he wondered whether for some set of reasons, she was constituted such that she needed a lot of meat. The story goes he fed her a whole heap of raw liver and she went from being on death's door to making a full recovery. From there he developed a questionnaire he gave his patients, asking in detail about how they feel after eating different sorts of foods, what they crave, and some other bits and pieces, to divide people into a number of different 'eating types' (ranging from eating raw fruit and veggies only, all the way to eating fatty red meat three times a day).

I've been thinking a lot about whether this approach is a good one ---- and on a personal level I find working out what to eat complicated ----- I've read so many studies that appear to show positive correlations between raw vegan diets and not getting cancer, for instance (ie. The China study)..... but on the other hand I don't feel very good when I have raw vegan. I could just eat vegetables for half the day, but if I left myself have a steak, or even a hamburger, I feel my brain come back almost immediately. So I've been oscillating between trying to be vegan (which I'm terrible at doing because I feel my brain falling apart when I do it) and focusing more on some variety of paleo.

And so this got me thinking about one of Weston Price's observations, namely that he saw lots of healthy groups of people living in different parts of the world, eating different diets, and all lived to old age, had healthy teeth, no degenerative disease, etc. This included both the Inuits eating a lot of seafood, I think some tribes in Africa where they mostly ate grain, and then tribes in the tropics eating mostly fruits and vegetables.

I've been thinking about this in tandem with the idea that I'm sure I read here, which was that whether or not eating a lot of carbs (grain, fruit, etc) is gonna be bad for you depends on things like whether or not you live somewhere cold, and whether or not you are cold adapted (ie are you doing CT).

If this is right, then wouldn't this be essentially saying that it is one's epigenetics (rather than a hereditary connection per se) that control what diet we have to eat? A corollary being that whether or not it is good for you to eat fatty fish three times a day depends not on whether you have Inuit ancestry (i.e. hereditary) but on whether you have turned on the epigenetic switch that makes you cold adapted + where you currently live? And if that were true, then that would mean that a person who had Inuit ancestry and was designed by evolution to eat fatty fish three times a day and you stuck them near the equator or in Cairns, then despite the evolutionary design it wouldn't be ideal for them to be on that diet given that there was now an environmental mismatch between what their genes were born telling them to eat and what they in fact needed to eat based on where they are living. Another way of putting the question would be: how would we know, for instance, that people from the tropics can be cold adapted in the same way that Inuits and Europeans are? Is it possible that say, Filipinos, or Indigenous Australians, can't cold adapt their bodies in the same way because they evolved in hot climates?

I've come to no conclusions on this but am just trying to work out whether what we should eat should be based on ancestry/heredity, or on epigenetics and where we are currently living. if you have any thoughts or suggested reading materials keen on hearing them!

ps sorry for the length of this

### JediMindTricks — 2016-04-03T20:25:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diet-genes-and-epigenetics.17768/post-191968>

It's not complicated! We need water to survive, so we must of evolved close to sources of water, which means that seafood was likely fundamental in both our development and survival. Other than that, we all have the ability to adapt to our environment.

Think of it like this: if all humanity ceased to exist than how would you survive? You'd eat what mother nature provides, which means more carbs in the summer and less in the winter.

Life's so simple.

### lilly antic — 2016-04-03T20:54:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diet-genes-and-epigenetics.17768/post-191970>

The environment you are in is the key. It is not just about the cold, but about the light.
Someone who lives in the tropics could even be OK as vegetarian, as the UV light stimulus is very high and so not so much DHA needed for the photoelectric effect.
The fruit and veggies( carotenoids) also act as a natural sunscreen, blocking UV frequencies in the skin.
The further away from the equator you are, the less UV light you have, and the more DHA in cell membranes you need for the photoelectric effect.
Nature is very wise and provided everything accordingly-there is plenty of fatty fish found in cold water and not so much in the warmer sea around the equator. ( exception is the Gulf of Mexico due to the big crater...)

### hello — 2016-04-09T04:31:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/diet-genes-and-epigenetics.17768/post-192388>

thanks guys!!! yeh you're right on this. It's about matching to our environment right now - genes don't dictate what to eat.

# Thread: Horrible Study Finds a Majority of Chocolates Contain Toxic metals
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/horrible-study-finds-a-majority-of-chocolates-contain-toxic-metals.17770/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Penny — 2016-04-04T22:22:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/horrible-study-finds-a-majority-of-chocolates-contain-toxic-metals.17770/post-192035>

[http://www.grubstreet.com/2016/03/chocolate-toxic-metals.html](http://www.grubstreet.com/2016/03/chocolate-toxic-metals.html)

Another round of bad news for chocoholics: Not only can molten aluminum [not melt](http://www.grubstreet.com/2016/03/chocolate-easter-bunny-resists-molten-aluminum.html) your favorite candy, but the odds [are also pretty high](http://www.asyousow.org/our-work/environmental-health/toxic-enforcement/lead-and-cadmium-in-food/) it contains toxic levels of lead, cadmium, or even both, according to a new study by consumer group As You Sow. The advocacy group tested 50 different popular chocolates for the two heavy metals, and results showed the amount was "above the safe harbor threshold," as defined by California, in 35 of them. They're pretty much all dark chocolates — the long list includes a variety of high-percent-cacao products by Hershey's, Mars, Godiva, Lindt, Ghirardelli, Trader Joe's, and Whole Foods, as well as Cadbury's dark-chocolate eggs and specialty bars by Vosges and Taza.

Altogether, it's 18 manufacturers, and As You Sow says it's petitioning them to add warnings to their labels (futilely, no doubt). It's worth noting the metals aren't being added by the companies; cocoa plants absorb them directly from the soil, though it's typically understood to be in trace amounts and varies depending on where the plant grew. (Milk chocolate gets more of a pass because it straight-up has less cocoa.) Advocacy groups have noted these high levels before, but experts don't exactly agree on what dangers they present. Consumer Labs [advises](https://www.consumerlab.com/answers/Why+is+there+so+much+cadmium,+a+toxin,+in+cocoa+powders+but+not+in+dark+chocolate/cadmium_in_dark_chocolate/) people to limit their intake of chocolate to one serving per day, while As You Sow leaves the amount up to consumers but notes that lead is linked to "a variety of neurological impairments, including learning disabilities, seizures, and a lower IQ," while cadmium can cause "damage to the kidney, liver, and bones, while also impairing neurobehavioral development."

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-05T03:20:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/horrible-study-finds-a-majority-of-chocolates-contain-toxic-metals.17770/post-192047>

If you listen to the April webinar you'd know why this is a non issue.

# Thread: Chronic Diarrhea
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chronic-diarrhea.17771/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### trudyap — 2016-04-05T00:36:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chronic-diarrhea.17771/post-192037>

I've been searching the forum regarding this and haven't found any answers regarding this. My brother and I are both Leptin resistant - he is type II diabetes and is taking 3-4 meds for high blood pressure. His doc now wants him on cholesterol meds but he is resisting. He tried the Leptin rX for 8 days and experienced chronic diarrhea the entire time. I have been following the protocol as well and have experienced some diarrhea but not chronic. He knows he needs this and desperately WANTS to do it but cannot function with the chronic diarrhea. Can anyone help with this?

### Starfish Prime — 2016-04-05T01:11:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chronic-diarrhea.17771/post-192039>

Welcome.  I am not much help on the chronic diarrhea issue, but more context will be needed, I am sure. I wonder if digestive enzymes would be useful but I'll defer to others who are more knowledgeable.

First things first:

What was he/are you eating?
Where do you - and he - live?
nnEMF? WiFi? Cell phones?
Have either of you had cataract surgery with IOL implants?
Bariatric surgery?

In the meantime, get the rest right-
Prioritize getting your light cycles tight...zero artificial light before/after sunrise/set (this is hugely important!).
Uvex glasses - they make fitover version if you wear prescription lenses.
Get outside first thing in the morning-sunrise to 9 or 10 depending on your latitude
Get on the grass on bare feet.
Eat as much cold-water seafood as you can afford.
Maximum UV exposure on as much skin as possible and get it into your naked eyeballs.
Make your night as dark as possible after sunset. No TV/phone/ipad/etc. At bedtime you should not be able to see your hand in front of your face.
No snacking and no food after dark/4-5 hours before bedtime.

It sounds complicated but it really isn't. Eat as much cold-water seafood as you can but only while its light out...no artificial light...as much sun as you can get on as much of your body as you can until you turn pink (don't burn).

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-05T03:18:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chronic-diarrhea.17771/post-192046>

Well that change is actually a good sign because T2D patient all have an altered gut flora.......so getting the runs tells me your gut is trying to change itself likely for the better.

### trudyap — 2016-04-05T04:03:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chronic-diarrhea.17771/post-192052>

Both of us use our cell phone with a headset only - I think he carries his in his pocket - mine is not on my body. We are both in front of the computer a lot. We both live in Wisconsin and neither have had cataract surgery or bariatric surgery. I need to lose 60 pounds and he could stand to lose probably 80-100 pounds.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-05T04:05:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chronic-diarrhea.17771/post-192053>

There is his and your problem

### QuantumRadical — 2016-04-10T17:25:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/chronic-diarrhea.17771/post-192463>

I have psoriasis. I teach Physical Education outside for 2-3 days a week and the other days I'm outside a lot.

# Thread: Cold is the worst thing for me. Why
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-is-the-worst-thing-for-me-why.17774/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 20
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Aerose91 — 2016-04-05T19:27:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-is-the-worst-thing-for-me-why.17774/post-192109>

Before the questions come out- i do everything right. Circadian rythems are great, ketotic, sun in my eyes and as much skin as i cam handle every morning starting at 8, only emf is my phone and it's off most of the time, no blue light at night and grounding 5-8 hours/day. My DHA was off the charts on a fatty acid profile so i eat dha to keep it topped up but have balanced out my fatty acids better. I even eat breakfast in the sand on the beach with the sun on me when its not raining.

It's taken the better part of a year to be able to handle and hour or two of sun without crashing so i recently thought to try a LITTLE cold. First was a light hoodie one night instead of a coat- crashed me badly for almost a month.

Now, nearly 6 months later, since i was trying to get a full 2 hours of sun without a shirt i decided to go in the ocean (61 deg) just to cool off. Not even c/t really but thats all it took. 5 or so minutes and right afterward i was spaced out, motor function decline, muscle weakness and terrible sleep since that night. It has spurred on the worst adrenal crash I've had in about 2 years which has really bummed me out.
I now have to limit my sun exposure to 10-20 minutes/day or i feel the crash getting worse and it stays that way for days.
Just the other day i felt overheated a bit so i just splashed some cool water on my face and neck and i even felt negative effects from that. There are also other signs that my immune system has declined

I'm not unique in this experience in the every person ive spoken to with severe or late stage M.E. has the same problems, I'm just the only one here asking about it.

Sorry for the novel, i just know that these questions will be asked so i want to paint the full picture.

### shah78 — 2016-04-05T19:39:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-is-the-worst-thing-for-me-why.17774/post-192111>

healing is not linear for sure.

### cantweight — 2016-04-06T16:44:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-is-the-worst-thing-for-me-why.17774/post-192175>

Totally talking out my ass here, but these are my thoughts....

I know Jack says cold is not hormetic but I dont think that is the case for everyone when it comes to CT. It seems to me your problem is more with heat than cold....the body's response to cold is to raise its temp.

Between your skewed fatty acid profile and prior mold exposure lipolysis may not be your friend right now.

You could try a hack...when you have exposure to a lot of sun or cold try taking charcoal (4-6 capsules) and see if you notice a lessening of symptoms post exposure......speaking from my own experience, this idea is likely not accepted in the quantum think tank.

### Aerose91 — 2016-04-07T04:14:04+0300

The only thing i can think of is Dr Kruse mentions how cold is energy expenditure and thats a big no-go for ME people. I just hoped i could handle small doses. It seems that the farther someone is into disease the more paradoxial a lot of these things become.

@[cantweight](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1283/) ill try the charcoal, i have some. May be some time before i can get out in the sun again tho, crashes like this can take months.

### freesia — 2016-04-07T04:44:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cold-is-the-worst-thing-for-me-why.17774/post-192208>

FWIW.  ME here since onset at adolescence....1980's. Severe ME since 1998. (CCC and Oxford criteria, bedbound 2007 - 2010 and mostly housebound until 2012.)
I had to acclimatize really slowly. I had to control the temp and the time I was exposed to cold fastidiously or risk crashing badly. I did crash in the beginning and it was horrendous. I feel for you!! I learned from that.
CT takes precious energy expenditure until you've built up more active brown fat. In your condition you _have_ to do this in baby ant steps. Light hoodie for a whole night?? Forget that. Light hoodie for **10 minutes** mate...then when you return fully to baseline - ie feeling as well as you did when you started... and that might be 48 hours or more, do the light hoodie thing for 11 minutes. As you gain temperature regulation fitness you will be able to take bigger steps. (I promise). Until then take it ridiculously slow and stay safe.

When my kid was at his sickest, he could not handle cold or sunshine... a cheap hack that helped him was soaking his feet in epsom salts - I would recommend you spray 1 part epson salts to one part water prior to going out into the sun - also, your potassium levels are probably low - some dha eggs, smoked salmon, epsom salt spray on your skin and a green smoothie (or kelp - it's fantastic) - rhodiola also helped him a lot - in fact, I had a kid here who couldn't get off the couch and after rhodiola, a week later he was walking a mile - I also gave him supps for h pylori - zinc is always low in everyone I've tested also - sense of smell bad? U need zinc...

BTW, I admire your perseverance - the only advice I can truly offer you is \*never\* give up...

I think a drop of MB in a 1 oz dropper bottle and starting with a drop of that would be a great move for you - it's mito biogenesis - which is what you need -

I also have some hacks that might work that are free... meditation and prayer - it doesn't even matter who you pray to - your "prayer voice" will fire at 8 hz... which will help structure your water...

Deep breathing elevates oxygen - which is low in people like you -

> Pseudohypoxia develops because mitochondrial respiratory proteins are stretched out........deep breathing won't help it unless you know how to shrink them. FYI. April 2016 webinar alert.

Well, I was FOS on that one But usually I advocate rhodiola for hypoxia - the stuff is a miracle worker...

[http://www.greenmedinfo.com/blog/biophotons-human-body-emits-communicates-and-made-light](http://www.greenmedinfo.com/blog/biophotons-human-body-emits-communicates-and-made-light)

**Meditation and Herbs Affect Biophoton Output**
Research has found an oxidative stress-mediated difference in biophoton emission among mediators versus non-meditators. Those who meditate regularly tend to have lower ultra-weak photon emission (UPE, biophoton emission), which is believed to result from the lower level of free radical reactions occurring in their bodies. In one clinical study involving practitioners of transcendental meditation (TM) researchers found:

The lowest UPE intensities were observed in two subjects who regularly meditate. Spectral analysis of human UPE has suggested that ultra-weak emission is probably, at least in part, a reflection of free radical reactions in a living system. It has been documented that various physiologic and biochemical shifts follow the long-term practice of meditation and it is inferred that meditation may impact free radical activity.[\[13\]](http://www.greenmedinfo.com/blog/biophotons-human-body-emits-communicates-and-made-light?page=2#_ftn13)

Interestingly, an herb well-known for its use in stress reduction (including inducing measurable declines in cortisol), and associated heightened oxidative stress, has been tested clinically in reducing the level of biophotons emitted in human subjects. Known as **[rhodiola](http://www.greenmedinfo.com/substance/rhodiola-tibetan-ginseng)**, a study published in 2009 in the journal _Phytotherapeutic Research_ found that those who took the herb for 1 week has a significant decrease in photon emission in comparison with the placebo group.[\[14\]](http://www.greenmedinfo.com/blog/biophotons-human-body-emits-communicates-and-made-light?page=2#_ftn14)

### Scompy — 2016-04-07T06:06:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-birth-of-astroturf-in-science-and-medicine.17777/post-192211>

Hiding truth gets society nowhere fast... spinning the wheels continually. I would consider newspaper tactics and media outlets as a huge part of the problem, as they omit information to sell their narrative--usually to promote conventional wisdom. Sadly, it's already a norm to push a narrative over actual observation and truth-seeking. If science jumps on this manipulation-bandwagon full-time, it truly takes an open-mind to absorb enough knowledge from everything to decipher the truth. Also, access to the truth might be a problem in the future too. The credibility of science itself is already walking a fine-line.

# Thread: After years of high blood pressure, stress and anger.....Low dopamine!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/after-years-of-high-blood-pressure-stress-and-anger-low-dopamine.17784/>
**Subforum:** Success Stories
**Posts:** 26
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mystic Rose60 — 2016-04-07T19:27:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/after-years-of-high-blood-pressure-stress-and-anger-low-dopamine.17784/post-192242>

As some of you know, my 82 year old Mother suffered from two TIA'S last fall and I tried to work with her so that she would heal with light, water and magnetism. I purchased uv black lights, the right red lights and a Sperti for her. I also made sure she had Uvex's, only incandescent bulbs for daytime use when needed and also, an electron foot bath to be used with her uv black light. All of this and some of Jack's blogs to read, which she understood, because she is a retired RN. However, we all know that people who are very low on dopamine are procrastinators, amongst having various other disposition and health problems as well. My Mom was fighting the protocol and decided to take her Big Pharma drugs which were prescribed to her for her high blood pressure, etc. I made the wise decision to not engage in battle with her, but, just spend time with her and listen to whatever it was she was interested in talking about during our visits. I remained positive and would talk a lot about my brother's and my progress (which was very noticeable to her) and healing in Nature. All this while I knew she was progressing downwards with her health.

You have to be the change that you want to see!

Her list of drugs that she was taking follows
Lipitor
Cozar
HCTZ
Catapres
Bisoprolol
Carvedolol

The first drug to go was the Lipitor because my Mom said that she could actually feel the life being sucked out of her and knew it was killing her. She had no energy and her cognitive function really began to decline. I gave her one of Jack's articles and she took it to her Dr. Her Dr. was angry with her and told her "If you stop taking the Lipitor, you will die." My Mom fired her and flushed the Lipitor down the toilet Within one week, she started to feel more energy coming back, but was still experiencing her HBP and cold extremities in her lower right leg and foot and arm and hand as well. This is when she made the wise decision to get off from these drugs and ask for my help. She finally realized that her doctors could not help her and she would die if she continued on this path.

Be the change that you want to see folks!

It's been one month since my Mom began her Light, water (Pellegrino) and no fluoride showers and her electron foot baths, religiously . She now swears by this and for the first time in my life, that I can ever remember, this woman, at 82 years old, actually feels happy and as she told me this morning, "The stress just disappeared." she also swears by her purple uv lights and keeps them on and sits by them during the day. By the way, her favorite color has always been purple too. I have helped ween her off from all of her drugs and her blood pressure is normal. She has been on various meds for her HBP for decades, but no more. I actually look forward to our talks now and spending time with her. I'm looking forward to getting her out for some walks in nature here soon and I don't know what else to say here folks!

Just be the change that you want to see. Be the Sphinx, or, the lemur and be kind and patient and genuine and see what happens. Give gratitude and remain positive! By doing this, you have the ability to make a world of difference in the World.

Thanks for reading! I had to share!

### caroline — 2016-04-08T03:08:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/after-years-of-high-blood-pressure-stress-and-anger-low-dopamine.17784/post-192313>

You and your Mom have found so much to be grateful for..... think of how much you have both learned and grown and this wonderful chapter in both of your lives will continue to grow and blossom and flourish.

Do you have a pic of both of you? what a transformation!

please share more.....

### Mystic Rose60 — 2016-04-09T00:15:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/after-years-of-high-blood-pressure-stress-and-anger-low-dopamine.17784/post-192375>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=192313)
>
> You and your Mom have found so much to be grateful for..... think of how much you have both learned and grown and this wonderful chapter in both of your lives will continue to grow and blossom and flourish.
>
> Do you have a pic of both of you? what a transformation!
>
> please share more.....

You're so right here Caroline. I think that our relationships with others are one of the biggest determinants of our happiness, and especially a Mother/Daughter relationship.
I've been keeping a gratitude journal for a while now and suggested to my Mom that she might want to keep a journal too. She thought it was a wonderful idea and so today, I took her shopping for her journal to write in. This is going to help her focus on the positive things in her life and gives her something to look forward to each day.
Mom and I both have greater wisdom and so much appreciation now for the time that we have left and we are going to make the most of it.
You asked me to share more here and I'm choking up a little with some tears when writing this. My Mom never told me that she loves me until about a week ago. I had never said those words to her either, until about a week ago and I didn't just say them, I meant what I said to her....I love my Mom.
I don't have a recent picture of the two of us together to share, but, as soon as I have one, will be sure to come back here and share it with you.
Thanks for your kindness and caring words here Caroline.

Thanks, Inger and Jack too. So much gratitude!

### caroline — 2016-04-09T01:47:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/after-years-of-high-blood-pressure-stress-and-anger-low-dopamine.17784/post-192381>

So well said Rose! when we are fixing ourself we are able to have better relationships with others. Just a shoulder to leans on, some kind words, to really "see" someone, to really listen ....these are all gold.

We have learned about the three legged stool ...... and now we can share so much. I no longer think it is the words we share with others that are the most important ...in fact I think it is kind of presumptuous sometimes to bombard people with all this .

But we can be a beacon of lite and hope and gratitude and kindness ....and we will accomplish so much more!

Do something crazy and wonderful and off the charts fun with your Mum!!! Something that screams "I am alive and not going down without a fight!!

I am going sky diving on my 80th birthday....and then on my 90th and then on my100th!

Unfortunately - I said that out loud and will be held to it! I am gonna walk the harbour bridge this year for my birthday ....even tho I am terrified of heights!

looking forward to pics of you and your Mom!

Does she get up every morning and take a pic of the sunrise?

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-09T19:17:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/after-years-of-high-blood-pressure-stress-and-anger-low-dopamine.17784/post-192419>

Comments I just made in Time 12 to Randall are critical for all people with low dopamine: FYI

### Sean Ryan — 2016-05-19T06:42:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/after-years-of-high-blood-pressure-stress-and-anger-low-dopamine.17784/post-195059>

Rose is there a shopping list for everything you mentioned?

I'm in a similar boat... After my heart attack in October 2015, they've put me on lipitor, cardizem cd, plavix, and aspirin. The plavix and aspirin I will live with until they tell me to go off it, and the lipitor already went in the trash. But I'd like permission or at least enough hard evidence of normal blood pressure to get off the cardizem, which is an awful calcium channel blocker.

My BP is usually around 123-127/70-79 ... Just borderline to where they won't give me the go ahead to go off the drug. But any sustained marginal improvement is going to be enough to break me free.

### Inger — 2016-05-19T12:23:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/after-years-of-high-blood-pressure-stress-and-anger-low-dopamine.17784/post-195063>

have you tried nude tanning to lower blood pressure? To get as much skin as possibly in the sun and grounded simultaneously... and preferably in the morning sun... should do wonders for high blood pressure... I would do it as much as possible!

### caroline — 2016-05-31T03:23:42+0300

### Mystic Rose60 — 2016-05-31T23:06:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/after-years-of-high-blood-pressure-stress-and-anger-low-dopamine.17784/post-195740>

Thanks everyone! My Mom is still doing good and now that it's warmer outdoors and the sun has been shining a lot and the days are longer, my Brother and I have been keeping her active outdoors because nothing beats the real sun and barefeet on the ground. When she's had enough sun, she sits under her favorite tree and even takes her nap there. She keeps here favorite reading material next to her and works on her jigsaw puzzles and listens to her jazz music. I talked to her about eating her meals outdoors and she really liked the idea. So, even though she still dines indoors on occasion, it's no longer by artificial blue light. Just her red LED and UV black lights when needed.

@[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/) , Mom is now rising and gazing at the sunrise first thing. I think you would get a kick out of the way she talks to her lady friends about light. She swears by it now and raves to them that these "special" lights, along with the sun and electrons and her fancy water have healed her. She tells them all, "It all just disappeared."

She hasn't found another Dr. and actually, hasn't looked for one. She said she has no intention of going back. I say, good for her! Let Nature be her healing drug and live life and enjoy whatever she has left! At the rate she's going, who knows! Afterall, it's no longer about the number of years left. it's all about that quality of life and my mom agrees

My Mom fired her and flushed the Lipitor down the toilet Within one week, she started to feel more energy coming back, but was still experiencing her HBP and cold extremities in her lower right leg and foot and arm and hand as well. This is when she made the wise decision to get off from these drugs and ask for my help. She finally realized that her doctors could not help her and she would die if she continued on this path.
>
> Be the change that you want to see folks!
>
> It's been one month since my Mom began her Light, water (Pellegrino) and no fluoride showers and her electron foot baths, religiously . She now swears by this and for the first time in my life, that I can ever remember, this woman, at 82 years old, actually feels happy and as she told me this morning, "The stress just disappeared." she also swears by her purple uv lights and keeps them on and sits by them during the day. By the way, her favorite color has always been purple too. I have helped ween her off from all of her drugs and her blood pressure is normal. She has been on various meds for her HBP for decades, but no more. I actually look forward to our talks now and spending time with her. I'm looking forward to getting her out for some walks in nature here soon and I don't know what else to say here folks!
>
> Just be the change that you want to see. Be the Sphinx, or, the lemur and be kind and patient and genuine and see what happens. Give gratitude and remain positive! By doing this, you have the ability to make a world of difference in the World.
>
> Thanks for reading! I had to share!

That is awesome! I am so happy for your Mom and you.

I have done the electron foot baths a while back and they made me feel so much better.

I am hoping to get the whole program going all at once as I feel this will really make a difference in my brain cognition.

Afterall, it's no longer about the number of years left. it's all about that quality of life and my mom agrees

**High blood pressure and low vit D level go together.
I think it would be a good idea to work on your Mom's vit D.**
Goal Vitamin D (25-Hydroxyvitamin D)(250nmol/L=100.16ng/mL)
1.5 of above is ok

The general function of norepinephrine is to mobilize the brain and body **for action**.
Catapres Clonidine inhibits release of norepinephrine (NE)

I just finished my hack with Red Sperti.
Got my Red Sperti in Sep 2016.
Since I got it I use it every day 16 min/day. (one full timer's turn=16min)
My navel is at the middle of the lamp level. I am very close to lamp, sometime touching it.
I started with heavy duty sun exposure last summer, plus some vit D supplements.
Over fall and specially winter not much sun exposure.
Do not used vit D supplements since I got Red Sperti.
5/17/2016 vit D=130.0 ng/mL(30-100) (winter, low sun 5drops Bio-D-Mulsion Forte)
8/16/2016 vit D=80.3 ng/mL(30-100) (lots of sun, (3-4)drops Bio-D-Mulsion Forte)
2/27/2017 vit D=63.0 ng/mL(30-100) (16min Red Sperti, no supplemental vit D)

I think from now on I will
use sun as best I can
keep using Red Sperti 16min/day
add 4 drops/day of Biotic Research Bio-D-Mulsion Forte, 1drop=2000iu Vit D

\=====================================
