# Thread: Replacing windows with UV transmitting plastic
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/replacing-windows-with-uv-transmitting-plastic.17785/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lilreddgirl — 2016-04-07T23:28:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/replacing-windows-with-uv-transmitting-plastic.17785/post-192276>

I remember someone asking about this but can't find the member's name or initial question. They had a picture of themselves and family including kids as a gravatar.

Well I found these mentioned elsewhere online

**Rohm and Haas' UVT Plexiglass, or American Cyanamid's Acrylite UVT**

These are plastic that can be used to replace glass windows.

A source also suggested to use the thinnest plastic (as they come in varied thicknesses) to further improve UV transmission and also because even thin plastic would be a superior insulator to glass (for temperature reasons). Get ready to bleach your colored furniture with that incoming UV!

# Thread: "Fasting Shrinks ECT and Lowers Heteroplasmy" JK
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-shrinks-ect-and-lowers-heteroplasmy-jk.17787/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Danny — 2016-04-07T23:58:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-shrinks-ect-and-lowers-heteroplasmy-jk.17787/post-192284>

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...-start-a-thread-on-fasting.17779/#post-192248](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/in-which-forum-should-i-start-a-thread-on-fasting.17779/#post-192248)

### Danny — 2016-04-08T00:14:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-shrinks-ect-and-lowers-heteroplasmy-jk.17787/post-192288>

47 hr fast : Started at 130P and Ended at 1230P 2 days later

Ending the first 1/2 day w/o food was my normal for about the last 3 weeks.

2nd day: I decided to skip my one meal and fast. Got a good amount of sunshine this day. Brain was functioning a bit higher and nothing seemed to rock me even though I had, for me what would have been, a stressful work situation later in the day. Got tired early... went to bed at 9P.

3rd Day: Had nausea in the morning. Felt as though was going to vomit. BM relieved feeling a bit. Took 1 charcoal tab, drank water, and got out in sun naked. 2 hours later felt better. A little lack of energy and maybe a tiny bit of muscle soreness. Lower back and upper back/neck usually bother me. On this day the discomfort was almost completely gone. Finished the last day strong after the meal. Over ate a bit, but not feeling it too much.

Ended my fast in a sloppy manner: 2 doz oysters, 5 - 6 oz of ground beef, 1/2 cup of rice w/ salmon roe, approx 1 lb raspberries, banana, 2 dates, and 6 marshmallows (I know that was stupid... but sue me... I was feeling good and it's important to be honest)

### Danny — 2016-04-11T02:31:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-shrinks-ect-and-lowers-heteroplasmy-jk.17787/post-192484>

Finished 2nd 48hr fast: A few notes

This time no nausea at all. I realized during my last fast that I had salt and water around hour # 32. Woke up the next morning w/ salty mucus and nausea. Didn't put 2 + 2 together apparently.

I didn't seem to be counting the hours this time. I just went about my day in a more productive way than normal I might add. I ended my fast around 230P this time. I wanted to be fasted while getting sun exposure around solar noon. My energy felt steadier this time... probably b/c no salt induced nausea My mental functioning is definitely higher when fasting. I am way more productive and better mood.

Ended my fast in a little more cautious manner: 20 oz frozen raspberries, 4-5 oz of rare salmon w/ dill, few raw beet greens, cooked kale and onions, 3-4oz rare burger w/ dill, rosemary, green onions, few slices of bacon, 2 heirloom carrots, few mulberries from my mulberry tree , a little dandelion from the yard, an apple and 1 lb of marshmallows (ha! just kidding about the marshmallows- none this time).

Next, maybe a three day fast. Will see how it goes as the UV index climbs higher here.

### caroline — 2016-04-11T10:06:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-shrinks-ect-and-lowers-heteroplasmy-jk.17787/post-192494>

How do you feel Danny when eating that much after fasting? In my experience with fasting - they tell you to introduce food slowly ....and marshmallows not until the 5th day!

I have fasted for 12 days and loved it .... would have gone longer but for some reason had to end it - but not because of how I felt.

I had so much energy and I may have been able to understand Jack's blogs if I would have had them way back when!!

### Danny — 2016-04-11T15:34:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-shrinks-ect-and-lowers-heteroplasmy-jk.17787/post-192503>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=192494)
>
> How do you feel Danny when eating that much after fasting? In my experience with fasting - they tell you to introduce food slowly ....and marshmallows not until the 5th day!
>
> I have fasted for 12 days and loved it .... would have gone longer but for some reason had to end it - but not because of how I felt.
>
> I had so much energy and I may have been able to understand Jack's blogs if I would have had them way back when!!

I feel totally fine. As a matter of fact my stomach can handle things, like raw greens, that would ordinarily be a little tough. Now, I've also only fasted for 2 days not 12. That's awesome by the way! Don't know much about the veracity of stomach shrinking phenomenon when fasting.

My MO for now is to find out what works best for me... either chipping away at my % HeteroplasmY w/ 2 and 3 day fasts or going for the longer fasts for maybe quicker results. Is one 12 day fast like 50 two day fasts or does this depend on the individual (assuming you are getting UV)? My hunch is telling me that the health benefits of fasting are exponential. The 2nd day builds upon the first, 3rd day builds upon the second, 4th upon the 3rd... and so on in a non-linear fashion. I'm trying to understand all of this HeteroplasmY stuff in my own way. I have so many questions. Fasting while getting doses of the strong Sun (UV 9) is working well for me at the moment.

Do you remember what time of year you did your fast? Were you getting strong UV? Btw on the 2nd night of fasted sleep, my body feels electric! I have these feel good sensations coursing throughout w/ like a whole body armor of goosebumps. Can't wait to do the 3 day.

### Danny — 2016-05-08T17:14:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/fasting-shrinks-ect-and-lowers-heteroplasmy-jk.17787/post-194366>

Hour 42 of fast. Hunger subsided, but been fatigued ever since I woke up today. Sun helped some and ACV helped a little more. Gonna try a little nicotine.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-11T01:48:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-methylene-blue-for-mitochondria.17788/post-192482>

In MB oxidized state, the color of MB is blue due to the fact that the phenothiazinium molecule absorbs visible light strongly in the red region of 600–700 nm and this does intact shrink the geometry between the respiratory proteins to improve electron tunneling speeds and the efficiency of the ATPase. Recall that the ATPase is a quantum light machine that uses red light and water's viscosity. [http://www.nature.com/articles/srep12029](http://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.nature.com%2Farticles%2Fsrep12029&h=wAQGt3IXVAQFgPF053PADmm7SP2_WbZT58SrV9gbuJ1TEbg&enc=AZPKY2jRJLR7nXCmeLpVdl4Q1i2Uj_CAP1Oj8udO88JKRxif-f6D-WmN2sRDFgjH9TkT1orLeDNw5QphMmhQbgiA6cJUrlS6tZwfB2rE2n-PJDJRlsPCXxAzEiQoR1F3jAzAK856UH1bIMS5Djh5WVck_7CPGTVRLgaPjLsmpaYKIIVdIPJpRngK9QmHW8YpVCoV8-2iD7-CVd-o549Uol_y&s=1)When this bio-physical change occurs light in the violet and blue range pass through to give the liquid its classic color. The ring structure of the molecule is the key to this light changing ability. It allows the remainder of the visible spectrum (350–600 nm) to be transmitted. In its reduced form (leucoMB), it becomes a colorless chemical and does not absorb light at all in visible region. LeucoMB is uncharged, lipophilic, and enters cells by diffusion across the plasma membrane where it can be re-oxidized and thus sequestered within the cells for use when the redox potential is altered. Oxidized MB and leucoMB together form a reversible oxidation-reduction system or electron donor-acceptor couple. Methylene blue is a light redox-cycling substrate for various reducing and oxidizing substances. The reducing agents such as NADPH convert MB to leucoMB, which is then oxidized by O2. You might remember how important NADPH is in the PPP (EMF4). Most nnEMF diseases create mitochondria that cannot make superoxide. Without that signal cell function alters, pseudohypoxia develops, NAD+ falls, and ELF-UV is lost. MB absorbs energy directly from a light source and then transfers this energy to molecular oxygen creating singlet oxygen (1O2). This can be used in the cell to replace the inability to make superoxide in the cell. Singlet oxygen is extremely electrophilic and can oxidize directly electron rich double bonds in biological molecules and macromolecules. The antifungal and antibacterial activity of MB can be increased significantly by light activation. Excitation of MB by light can induce singlet and triplet stages of the molecule and transfer the energy through either electrons (Type I mechanism) or energy (Type II mechanism). The Type I mechanism can lead to the formation of hydroxyl radicals and lipid hydroperoxides. The Type II mechanism proceeds through singlet oxygen resulting mainly in breakages of nucleic acids, mostly at the guanosine site (Wainwright, 2000).

medical grade powder: [http://www.universalmedicalinc.com/...eVeJC8BzNsJ0mWlmK654spJYM1y3\_O8DZxoCRkXw\_wcB;](http://www.universalmedicalinc.com/ibi-methylene-blue-25g.html?gclid=CjwKEAjwuuy4BRCvs43g9fX9mz4SJACiYydPR5sLqM5BeVeJC8BzNsJ0mWlmK654spJYM1y3_O8DZxoCRkXw_wcB;)

### Sue-UK — 2016-07-12T14:52:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-methylene-blue-for-mitochondria.17788/post-198254>

I'm using MB in the outside bath. Put a small amount in when I refill it, no blue staining on my skin or tan thru (visitors at the mo). I then add a tiny bit of fresh each day. The weather has been pretty bad, but I am slowly tanning. I often sit in the bath under cloudy skies or in the rain, and when it stops or there's a break in the clouds I go and stand in the sunshine and drip dry off. If it clouds back over I get back in, and wait for the next sun break, and repeat with whatever time I have. My skin condition is improving, less glycation generally, and circulation to the skin is better, but the most obvious improvement is the shrinking of skin tags under my arms. More improvement in a month than I've seen in a year. I'm also on nicotine, so I'm not sure if it's just the MB or the combo, and just how much the cold water helps the MB. The air and tub temps are low for the time of year. If the summer ever really arrives  I will get more careful with it in a high uv index until I'm sure it won't cause me to burn. I think hydration is critical, from what I'm reading up on plant photosynthesis (I'm trying to map the human equivalent), if a tree is short of water, or anticipates a shortage, it closes its stomata in strong uv sunlight and stops photosynthesis. So I think my cool very wet garden isn't being triggered to stop photosynthesis under a hot summer sun, and is photosynthesising at a different pace and thriving. Is drinking lots of water, and being cold and wet, strongly signalling human photosynthesis when I get out of the cold water and into the sun, and MB helping the process?

### Sue-UK — 2016-07-15T15:35:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-methylene-blue-for-mitochondria.17788/post-198397>

> [endless said:](/goto/post?id=198336)
>
> How much are you putting in your bath? I hadn't considered using it topically....

I put the end of a teaspoon into the powder, and then tap it until there's a bit left. Then mix it with a little water and put in a half ounce dropper bottle, then top up the bottle with extra water. When I fill the bath I put 2 dropper fulls in, and then add about 6 drops before each use. I'm not sure how much I'm actually absorbing, but it can't be more than trace amounts, intuitively I think it may be more about signalling, cold, wet surfaces, well hydrated .....and maybe something to do with sweat. I have a book that shows skin photographed under an electron microscope "blue droplets emerge from sweat pores on the skin of the back of the hand". (Is that a start photosynthising signal?)

Yesterday was a beautiful morning, and I added some Epsom salts too. Hopped in one bath, and lay stretched out in it in the sun, when I had warmed that one up I hopped into the metal shorted seated one which was colder, dipped in for a couple of minutes and then stood up to get the sun, repeated several times, I was out for about one and a half hours. I had to go to the city for DD's graduation. I had a disaster with my dress, so had the panic of buying another on the way to the ceremony, in a hall with a thousand people so loads of NNemf, no grounding and only snatches of sunlight throughout the day, I had a glass of bubbly and some not very optimal canapés, meals out not completely optimal either. I coped amazingly, I was stunned at my energy levels all day. It was like my morning bath turbocharged my battery

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-methylene-blue-for-mitochondria.17788/post-215156>

Well, BlueBrainBoost increased their prices since last year, last I checked were out of stock. Wait till this hits the fan:

Want beautiful young looking skin? Look no further than Methylene Blue!

Methylene blue, a traditional mitochondrial-targeting antioxidant, being an electron donor and acceptor, showed a potent ROS scavenging efficacy in cultured human skin fibroblasts derived from healthy donors and from patients with progeria, a genetic premature aging disease. In comparison with other widely used general and mitochondrial-targeting antioxidants, Methylene Blue was found to be more effective in stimulating skin fibroblast proliferation and delaying cellular senescence.

[https://medicalxpress.com/news/2017-05-common-antioxidant-symptoms-aging-human.html](https://medicalxpress.com/news/2017-05-common-antioxidant-symptoms-aging-human.html)

I found an MB eye drops (Collyre Bleu) yesterday, but am very cautious of trying it without knowing a lot more about it. I'm hacking ghee as a sun cream, (and cloud and rain bathing ) and this post from Jack

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=215071)
>
> HOW TO MITOHACK TRAUMA CALL WITH A QUANTLET? What is water fundamentally? We make electricity from water using solar light to liberate free electrons. So if you live in any environment that decreases water production from your mitochondria you are effectively missing a way to generate and send electrons from sunlight to cytochrome 1 in your mitochondria. When this happens NAD+ drops and pseudohypoxia sets in. How can you fix this via a mitohack? You take all your clothes off and get outside in the sun put half your body in warm or cold water and use some methylene blue as a mitochacker would. Why? Methylene blue is how you provide a dehydrated system electrons that water cannot because the redox system in a mitochondria is not working well for a myriad of reasons. Methylene blue preferentially enters neuronal mitochondria after systemic administration, and at low-doses forms an electron cycling redox complex that donates electrons to the mitochondrial electron transport chain. When you add in Low-level near-infrared light applied via your radial artery (Quantlet alert) or transcranially via sunlight you can deliver photons to cortical neurons that are accepted by cytochrome c oxidase, which causes increased cell respiration and cerebral blood flow and ATP without eating a thing. Biohack #989 now in the books. [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4428125/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4428125/)

had me putting a tiny drop of MB in a little bit of ghee and rubbing it on my wrist before using red/NIR light. I figured getting it up to the neurons and my eye through the blood supply was a safer bet without knowing what the eye drops would do, or what else was in them. Will extend the cream round the top of my wrist and report back on the skin.

This may be a stupid question but I was looking at whether the ancients could have used woad as a MB and found _"When Australian toxicologist Elizabeth Gillam was studying bacteria implanted with human DNA, her cultures unexpectedly turned blue. She suspected a mold contamination. But after conferring with Fred Guengerich, a colleague at Vanderbilt University, Gillam realized she'd stumbled onto something wonderful: **The bacteria were producing the indigo molecule as part of their metabolism.** "This is a good lesson for student scientists," says Gillam. "If something looks bizarre, don't discount it. It might be much more interesting than the result you expected."" ([http://ngm.nationalgeographic.com/ngm/0306/resources\_who.html).](http://ngm.nationalgeographic.com/ngm/0306/resources_who.html)
_

### Sue-UK — 2017-06-05T11:37:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-methylene-blue-for-mitochondria.17788/post-215411>

> [lohd2015 said:](/goto/post?id=215405)
>
> Not to mention the PEAK in the IR end as well. Do let us know your experience.

Started with ghee – better skin condition, improved tanning. 1 teaspoon does whole body. Did experiment with 1 glass of from the tap cold water, with a blob of ghee versus a glass of hot from the tap water with a blob of ghee. Left both side by side in UV7. In the cold water the ghee stayed in a lump with a few bits separating out, when I gave up the experiment the ghee hadn’t bleached much. In the hot water steadily losing heat to the cooler surroundings the ghee spread out in a circle on the top of the water, and at the end of the experiment the ghee was completely bleached.

Then I had to make up a MB solution, so did it as usual and got some on a piece of kitchen paper when I was transferring it to the dropper bottle. Usually I would just put it straight in the bin, but this time I took it outside and put it in direct sunlight. Before long it bleached. So then I put the same number of drops on two pieces of kitchen paper, put one in direct sunlight, and the other under a rosemary bush in complete shade. In the same timeframe, the sunlit one bleached, the shaded one kept its colour. I was interested in why the ancients in Britain and Europe painted themselves with woad in a low UV yield environment. Then I found the spectra of Indigotin from woad

[http://wwwchem.uwimona.edu.jm/courses/CHEM2402/Textiles/Dyeing\_Fibres.html](http://wwwchem.uwimona.edu.jm/courses/CHEM2402/Textiles/Dyeing_Fibres.html).

And compared it to MB.

So I wondered if, as well as the red end frequencies, they were using it to amplify UV in a low quantum yield (and it was also used on poultices etc for wounds, had they sussed out its role in preventing infection?)

So then I mixed 3 drops of MB (very dilute solution) with the ghee (almost immediately it started bleaching the carotenoids in the ghee) and applied it as usual. Went outside and sunbathed. Didn’t notice anything particularly. Yesterday morning I did 7 drops (ghee went a very faint blue – not noticeably blue at all when applied to the skin). It was cloudy and cool, and I noticed that my tan seemed lighter, lost the sunkissed glow IYKWIM, as if the cream and/or the cool air was keeping the blood further in. When I came back in (the equivalent of shade or evening?) my skin then started to go redder, like the delayed reaction to a session in a higher UV sun. It was raining the rest of the day so I was out less, but that prompted me to do a Finsen red room session after lunch. (Amazing ….).

I was thinking that the delayed reddening of the skin may be a defence mechanism in a high UV, to prevent overbleaching of the RBCs, or in the cold a defence against heat loss. The stimulus stopping may be the trigger for the blood to go to the surface to collect the harvested photons.

I was hacking MB in the CT tub last summer but this summer I’m hacking it with the ghee, and if it goes well I’ll use it in the winter. But my first thoughts are that the surface skin lipids (or something like ghee if the skin is deficient) is important, as is getting light into the usually higher levels of subcutaneous fat in more northern latitudes. (Winter supply of photons?) Nowhere near connecting or discarding dots though …. But experimenting is fun.

I'll repeat the experiment with MB and either coconut oil or ghee on a glass plate, either in full sunlight or shade, and see if there's a difference.
_"Out of these nonvolatile or fixed oils taken, the SPF value of olive oil and coconut oil was found to be around 8; castor oil, around 6; almond oil, around 5; mustard oil and chaulmoogra oil, around 3; and sesame oil, around 2. Hence it can be concluded that olive oil and coconut oil have the best SPF values, a finding that will be helpful in the selection of fixed oil during the formulation of sunscreens." [https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3140123/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3140123/)_

Its why at a latitude that doesn't grow coconuts or olives I would only ever use coconut oil or olive oil as an after sun lotion, but intuitively even that doesn't feel right.

### Sue-UK — 2017-06-07T15:26:40+0300

@[lohd2015](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/18338/)
We've had a storm here so have so far I've been unable to do the test, but @[Mystic Rose60](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/14425/) has an interesting report in the Methylene Blue thread in the ask Jack section. From the ghee and MB, it hasn't deepened my tan, but I held my energy level and dopamine up despite 3 days of torrential rain and then intermittent rain but storm winds and grey skies. I was scavenging a bit of light at an open doorway for a short while, and sticking my head out the window to at least get the circadian signal to my eyes. But the weather didn't frustrate me like it would normally, I was surprised at how well I felt before I could head out this morning under not so strong winds and intermittent cloud. There's enough to top up.

### Sue-UK — 2017-06-20T11:29:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-methylene-blue-for-mitochondria.17788/post-216593>

I think with ghee MB slows the tanning process, but with our inconsistent weather that might not be easy to prove or disprove. Even though I'm overweight, I'm doing a hack to reduce the amount of BAT between my shoulder blades,  so for the last couple of days I've applied plain ghee, and concentrated getting UV on my back (but only after sunrise to UV coming in preconditioning - I wouldn't do it without that). I took MB orally before getting into the tub yesterday afternoon, and had a few drops in the water. I dunked to wet my back, but otherwise sat turned so that my back was facing the sun directly, water up to my waist and with hands and feet submerged. My back and shoulders haven't burned at all on either day. I'm also hacking different blue blockers/bedroom lighting at night that cut down the yellow and daylight blues and adds the dusk frequencies. Within 2 hours of sleep I'm generating heat, but not in an inflamed, menopausal sweat kind of way ... Night 1 after the longer sun exposure was worse, I had to get up to pee a couple of times (more to get rid of heat than anything else), drink cold water and cool my skin surface. I was able to go straight back to sleep each time. Because I'd slept in a bit later because of the wake ups, I cut back on the sun exposure a bit yesterday (but still got plenty), had the 2 hour into sleep hot spell, but not as intense, and only the once. Today I'll be doing a repeat of yesterday to double check the effect, but will then go back to the MB ghee. But my initial thoughts are, that because I'm overweight, and with an inconsistent UV availability, that its more important to get the light into my fat cells than get a tan.

### Lahelada — 2018-01-15T03:38:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-methylene-blue-for-mitochondria.17788/post-232574>

"Oxygen, on the other hand, binds with hydrogen to make water (H2O). However, oxygen happens to prefer binding to deuterium (heavy hydrogen) over regular sized hydrogen, and it also holds onto to it very tightly once it binds. This allows oxygen to help deplete the deuterium in your tissue by turning it into water and dumping it to your biological fluids like urine and saliva. " This quote is from a letter from Cigna

MB increases the oxygen carrying capacity of hemoglobin.

This from a Russian paper that @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) posted
"It is well known,for example that under the influence of heavy water ???? is inhibited by insulin triggered by glucose output from the pancreatic islets of langerhans tissue and decreases the rate of absorption oxygen mitochondria cells "

How about if I rewrite it like this :
It is well known for example that under the influence of heavy water mitochondria cells are inhibited by glucose output triggered by insulin from the pancreatic islets of langerhans and it decreases the rate of oxygen absorption.

From the paper that @[JMO](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/403/) posted :
"Erlenmeyer, Schoenauer, and Stillmann (4) prepared deuterosuccinic acid and incubated it with succinic dehydrogenase in the presence of methylene blue. They found that the fumaric acid that was formed as well as the unchanged succinic acid contained a higher hydrogen to deuterium ratio than the initial succinic acid, and suggested that hydrogen exchange occurred from the leucomethylene blue."

Glacier water + MB = DDepletINGW ?

Check my thinking please ...

### HopingMom — 2016-04-08T16:13:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-hair-growth.17789/post-192349>

Hello,
Wondering if there is a solid understanding for exactly why we have hair?

My body is in dis-ease, but my head hair is flourishing. I would call it the exact opposite of receeding hair line as the new growth is all along my hair line. I feel like it must be some kind of protection mechanism and it must be pretty important and on-going because it is progressively increasing.
Diet has absolutely no influence either way and it is only my head hair (not body hair).
Any ideas?

### CTforlife — 2016-04-08T16:30:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-hair-growth.17789/post-192353>

Bcus fractal energy dynamics. Hair is on your head for a reason. And it's not on other areas of your body for a reason.

### HopingMom — 2016-04-10T02:50:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-hair-growth.17789/post-192443>

I guess I'm just trying to understand what would make new head hair start growing without provocation:

\-Trying to prevent heat loss from body?
\-Trying to project the head from something external/environmental that is posing a threat?

### Sue-UK — 2016-04-12T19:28:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-hair-growth.17789/post-192583>

> [HopingMom said:](/goto/post?id=192443)
>
> I guess I'm just trying to understand what would make new head hair start growing without provocation:
>
> \-Trying to prevent heat loss from body?
> \-Trying to project the head from something external/environmental that is posing a threat?

I'm 59 this year and for the past few months my hair has grown thicker, to the extent that I've grown my hair to shoulder length and have been having layers cut in and it thinned out a bit with to make it manageable without a hairdryer, tongs etc. For me its a good sign. I was reading Lyall Watson's book "The Dream of Dragons, an exploration and celebration of the mysteries of nature" today, and in one chapter he talks about the theory of humans evolving from an aquatic ape, and why we might have kept our head hair. He discusses how a Dr Djarkovsky pioneered the system of underwater childbirth, and that the Dr's own three month old daughter became so proficient underwater that she was able to hold her breath underwater for more than three minutes. But (aquatic ape or not) one idea he put forward is that an active mother, feeding herself and her offspring on molluscs and crustaceans would need something for her infants to cling onto when they went underwater. Which may be why having developed solar panels, we may have kept our head hair. The hair on her head is a woman's crowning glory .... her plumage almost.  Maybe the increased growth is a sign of improvement, a movement towards the optimal "blueprint" for your hairline.

### fitness@home — 2016-04-12T20:52:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-hair-growth.17789/post-192586>

> I guess I'm just trying to understand what would make new head hair start growing without provocation

Well, I would love some of whatever it is! You described that it was the opposite of a receding hairline. Personally, I feel that it is a GOOD sign that you are growing new hair.

I was blessed with fine, thin hair and would love to have more come in.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-13T21:14:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/new-hair-growth.17789/post-192641>

Losing hair? It is likely related to the light you allow on your skin and not much else. Why? Light control ubiquitin marking of proteins.[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC2724002/…](http://l.facebook.com/l.php?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov%2Fpmc%2Farticles%2FPMC2724002%2F%23%21po%3D5.29412&h=8AQGhJUlKAQF0xtkuQhwPqqD_Uqr7ruq8ZKriPA_3H1oLOg&enc=AZOQyg26meTYJ-e8WalhfxZabw1bsbpeguVPA14bUYuSuSb00bmmeBLu6dMT9eiHE9lWY48HU1xEyC-hkEKwm4P6jxId3u1kz60LLtRciqGQAbZjabd25qSEa6NnLRRBo5ti4sihAfwOlS9U5OhNdZ3wkOkiC_1HGeuikKf6b9tiQpSy76OBJ1TV2GcSdD_fpDmCGXP2VeyMi8c_Lxcw5bB2&s=1) Once you learn about light and its ability to control biology you have to ask yourself some difficult questions. Are willing to walk a trail of the unknown and blaze a trail for yourself? When you follow a crowd you only get as far as they get. Their precepts of truth might become your glass ceiling for optimal health. If you’re willing to walk alone you might find yourself in places no one has ever been before. Be bold with this info; some folks will think you’re mad initially, but it’s more insane than being timid and following a wandering clan with no vision. Ignoring light effects upon your skin is akin to inviting loss of hair and disease to your door step. Do not die while you’re still alive. An elevated ubiquitin rates for protein marking allows that to happen. No one can fathom the light environment is the key to ubiquitin marking of proteins in our body. I wish people would go back and read the first edition of Darwin’s book on evolution; even he realized that the environment had more control than we thought, but could never prove it. He spent the last years of his life trying to solve the mysteries using his “pangene theory”. Our genome is designed to respond to environmental signals, not control them. Conditions of existence in our environment are far more powerful than natural selection. Darwin’s own words are used here verbatim, but modern science has forgotten this lesson he wrote in 1859. All mammals are covered by hair and a loss of hair is a deep sign of a de-evolution due to a lack of sunlight.

Sue-UK - I read that too...isn't that amazing? It creates this mystical feeling picture in my mind when I read it. I always thought your hair was some kind of antenna. ### michellehp — 2016-04-09T01:49:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-im-a-new-orleans-girl.17791/post-192382>

I have been lurking around for about 3 weeks on the website and forum. I bought Jack's book and read it. Iwas born and raised here in NOLA. Besides my years at LSU, I've been back since Katrina. I am a "retired" Big Pharma rep (sold antipsychotics, antidepressants, broad-spectrum antibiotics, diabetes meds, allergy meds and PPI's) - I never had to take any of them (except some allergy stuff occasionally) thank goodness. Anyway I'm here to learn as much as possible. My kiddo (10 yr old son - pregnant thru Hurricane Katrina) has Asperger's and inattentive ADD with a phenomenal IQ- so I'm interested in the ways to help him be more social.
I took the iPad away 1.5 years ago because it changed his behavior and mood instantly. Anyway long story short- happy to be here to learn as much as I can! I can get enough of the knowledge you share!

I always saw huge differences in my son during summer and it had noting to do with no school. The more he swam in the pool, swam in the Gulf, ran around in the sun, the symptoms of his autism would disappear! It was really different when the iPad was out of the picture!

**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2

### lilreddgirl — 2016-04-09T05:48:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-uv-and-ir-are-invisible-what-do-we-see-from-blacklights-and-heat-lamps.17793/post-192393>

If UV and IR are invisible what do we see from blacklights and heat lamps ? There is certainly "light" to be seen...

### thomas — 2016-04-09T21:11:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-uv-and-ir-are-invisible-what-do-we-see-from-blacklights-and-heat-lamps.17793/post-192433>

you see visible light. lamps aren't perfect and there is always unwanted unefficient "light".note.. experiments have shown that some people can see ir up to about 1000nm if i recall.
But yeah it's uneficiencies in the design of lamps basically.

I have listened to quite a few of his webinar (on youtube) and it has completely distracted me from completing my PhD as I want to spend hours reading the blogs etc.

Thank you for asking

Hello Forum!

My name is Peter. Coming from Germany and living south of Frankfurt at the Rhine-river.

After being sick the last 2 weeks I used the time to dive a bit deeper into JK and read the Epi-Paleo Rx. Before that I came across some articles or references in YouTube, which opened up my curiousity, but didn't had real life consequences yet.

Having said that, the consequences are just to about to follow and manifest after reading the Epi-Paleo Rx. Curiosity is high, as the will to change, question my 'lifestyle'-choices and make better choices for my n=1.

I'll just start my optimal journal and give you some more insights about who's writing here.

Looking forward to learn, connect and develop new insights!

# Thread: PeVe - A journals journey
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peve-a-journals-journey.17797/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PeVe — 2016-04-10T17:20:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peve-a-journals-journey.17797/post-192461>

Let’s get the journal started!

As touched on in the introduction field, my name’s Peter, 28 coming from south-west Germany. I’m working in preclinical research with focus on Alzheimer’s disease. Throughout my life I had an interest in life. Being outside the whole day as a kid, having a fishtank (then several… and lot of books about them) studied biology, focused on neurobiology and now doing research in this field.

About a couple of years ago, I started to be also more interested in health. First, mainly in nutrition and sports – the conventional way. I was pretty active quite my whole life, but now it got more focus. About three years ago I switched from Martial Arts to Yoga and came across the Paleo movement and a guy named Paul Chek. This started to shift my perspective quite a lot. It became apparent for me, that my work was having its focus on disease to make people healthy (or to get rid of the symptoms). As balance I started to spent my free-time to focus on ‘what-is-health’ to make people (or better myself) healthy and get to the core.

I would say that this is the point where my n=1 really started. I got rid of grains and sugar (with some relapses), changed my diet, circadian rhythms, movement practice – generally speaking my whole view and acts on life.

Over the time there were a lot of positive changes, some setbacks but I’d say I’m on my way to optimal

After having a quite bad Enteritis, I took the time to read through the Epi-Paleo Rx and had the feeling that quite some missing mosaic-pieces fell into my picture. Within the Chek system, I started to get educated and it appears that with Jacks insights the picture gets now well rounded.

As it has been said: Be the change you want to see in the world - this is my journal about my change.

Now, what are the current action points? First, I want to take about a week time to get to ‘normal’ again. I already spoke to my physician and he is willing to help me with my bloodwork.

The action points (x = already done)

**Work environment**

1.1 As working in the lab, I get a full dose of EMF. The goal is to find ways to reduce them.

1.2 Work starts at 6.30 am. To get more sun I’ll shift my break between 8-11am and go to a park next by to get at least 20 mins of sun

1.3 Get my bicycle in shape again and drive to work (about 20 minutes each direction). Drive without glasses to get more sun.

**Home environment**

2.1 Made my bedroom free of electrical devices. Just one left – dawn simulator as alarm clock. As I don’t need light in the bedroom my circuit breaker for the bedroom is off all the time. (x)

2.2 Get rid of my Wifi – using cable

2.3 Activly track EMF and find ways to change them

2.4 bluelight-management (in progress)

2.5 bluelight-filter glasses

**Behavior/personal adjustments**

3.1 Cold Thermogenesis – getting started

3.2 Get more sun! (in progress)

3.3 Review water consumption (currently about 3 – 3.5 liters daily, but after being outside more often, I have the feeling that I’m thirstier)

3.4 being more barefood outside (currently doing my Tai-Chi practice barefoot in the sun -> win-win-win situation)

3.5 eat more seafood

3.6 establish a bloodwork protocol as baseline and to review changes (normally I go through with my feeling, but Jack touched the little scientist in me who wants to quantify  )

… and more to come.

Regarding the bloodwork. I know this is quite an extensive theme. What would be necessary, what not? Which labs are enough to give a decent baseline? What information would be needed to have a meaningful overview?

The standard bloodwork from my physician would cover these measures:

CRP

ALT

AST

Alkaline phosphatase

Gamma-glutamyl transpeptidase

LDL

HDL

Triglycerides

Serum calcium

Serum sodium

Creatinine

Glucose

LDH

Serum potassium

Total bilirubin

Total cholesterol

Uric acid

Red blood cells

White blood cells

Hemoglobin

Hematocrit

What would make sense to add to that? On my list I got:

hsCRP

DHEA

Omega6:Omega3 ratio

Cortisol (24h)

Melatonin (24h)

Progesterone

PRegnenlenone

Glutathione

Homocysteine

TNF (or TNF-alpha?)

BUN

Total testosterone

Free testosterone

% free testosterone

DHT

LH

FSH

PSA

IGF-1

IGFB3

TSH

Free T3

Free T4

Reverse T3

TPO Ab & TgAb

Ferritin

% saturation

TIBC

Serum Iron

Vitamin D

HbA1c

IL1-beta

IL8

VLDL

Do you have any insights, recommendations, simplifications, objections or remarks? Would be glad to read them!

### caroline — 2016-04-11T10:09:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peve-a-journals-journey.17797/post-192495>

Great to see you here Peter! ...I will come back and read in the morning.

### PeVe — 2016-04-17T17:00:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peve-a-journals-journey.17797/post-192921>

Some additional info to round the picture a bit more.

I am lucky not to have a chronical issure (or at least I'm not aware of any). But there are some recurring ills and also some current issues.

The recurring ill would be dairrhea. In the last 2 years this was the only issue that took me to the doc (about 3 times). I would couple this more with my experimentation with protocols, but that's still something prominent in the overall picture... and it just happend about four weeks ago I had the worst bout lasting over 10 days with loosing about 5kg bodyweight. After a week of recovery I probably got a secondary infection in my throat which again threw me back a bit for the last week. And after trying fish and seeweed in that time I also had a slight 'detox effect'... my guess would be too much seaweed. Sometimes I tend to overdo things instead of slowly increasing the 'dose'. There is always a margin for improvement

Two other issues I experience currently are 'pre'-cramps in my calfs during the night. I didn't have this issue for quite a long time but with my condition over the last weeks it became also really prominent. Hope with a re-filling of my tanks that this will subside again.

The other thing is my sleep quality, with the almost-cramps and the nighty 'I-have-to-go-to-the-toilet' sleep quality declined definetly.

As I had the chance to visit my doctor in the last weeks he agreed to do the bloodwork. I now made a list with test and he will check what he can do here - which tests are available.

Since I had some time I started with the book Better Body - Better Brain from Anja Leitz. Good lecture as startup and building up a base of understanding/connecting the dots.

Also implemented to be outside (without glasses) first thing in the morning - with a tea and a book for about 10 minutes. Further went outside again to get an hour of morning/noon sun while being barefoot every day. Worked quite well even though there was rain or clouds.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-17T17:50:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peve-a-journals-journey.17797/post-192929>

That is a sign of an altered environment and bad micriobiome. So when you eat marine food chain it contain many things which kill a bad microbiome (bacteria) naturally. This is no detox, it is a natural vaccine to clear out the bad to recolonize the good if you continue to exist in a suboptimal environment.

The most important prokaryote (bacteria) to human life is not your gut microbiome, but the one inside of your cells called a mitochondria. Why? Roughly 10% of your entire body weight consists of mitochondria inside your 30 trillion cells. Have you ever wondered why this make up this high a percentage of your biomass? For 4 billion years bacteria function has been modified by viruses to conform and adapt to environmental change on planet earth. Viral DNA make up the large majority of human DNA. Our nuclear DNA takes all its direction from the shape size and speed of quantum tunneling that occurs on a mitochondria's respiratory proteins. Viruses play an integral role in shuffling the deck of our nuclear genes when the environment changes but those changes only occur after they are "sensed" by our mitochondria. Viruses are dominators of particular niche's on Earth. They control local zip codes and as such become the change agent of the environment. Evolutionary biology has generally failed to consider the contribution of viruses to to evolutionary history largely because they do not understand the quantum interplay of how the mitochondria and nuclear genome work. This relationship can best be described as a quantum casino where the dealer only deals with probabilities. The cards are quantum tunneling of protons and electrons through our anisotropic structure. What holds the casino all together is sunlight and EZ water. That maintains the sea within us to gives us the life our choices in the environment we allow to happen. Our mitochondrial respiratory proteins change as our decisions about how we live and where we chose to live changes.

The marine chain has 10^7-10^8 viruses in a liter of sea water. We should be eating filter feeders of the marine chain to get this natural bounty to kill the bacteria that fundamentally hurt our biology and mito-nuclear co-apaptation.

Viral genomes can permanently colonize their hosts genomes, adding data and information to host lineages. Some will cause massive alterations to the host and its effect will be reflected in the ancestors genomes. Most viruses are co-apatative and adaptive to a new environment and now that we are in the ra of genomics, we have definitive proof that viral imprints and remnants are present all over the human genome.. they are present in all domains of life and the past evidence of viral colonization is the baseline of life and not an exception. All living things are built to be susceptible to viral elements for a quantum reason. Change without change is the reason behind this effect. This becomes the casino dealer inside of every cell for environmental changes that occur on this Epi-paleo blue dot.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-19T18:56:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peve-a-journals-journey.17797/post-193032>

Light drives viral amplification, and that amplification drove evolution by amplifying the genome.
Viral genomes can permanently colonize their hosts genomes, adding data and information to host lineages. Some will cause massive alterations to the host and its effect will be reflected in the ancestors genomes. Most viruses are co-apatative and adaptive to a new environment and now that we are in the era of genomics, we have definitive proof that viral imprints and remnants are present all over the human genome.. they are present in all domains of life and the past evidence of viral colonization is the baseline of life and not an exception. All living things are built to be susceptible to viral elements for a quantum reason. Change without change is the reason behind this effect. This becomes the casino dealer inside of every cell for environmental changes that occur on this Epi-paleo blue dot.
The genome is an ‘organ of the cell’, not its dictator.
**Genes don't have free will. Something must have forced them to misbehave: altered light**
The gene does not lead, it follows. What does it follow? The electromagnetic force.
Treatment guidelines are often based on outdated evidence, turning evidence in harm. Do no present harm is what should drive consensus.
Wisdom allows great people to become small, to understand complexity.

When water is heated water under goes a blue shift. When water is cooled water undergoes a red shift. This gives water a doppler effect with respect to temperature to determine flow and direction.
Water molecules can surround the ions in solution and effectively neutralize charges just like the magnetosphere does to sunlight
DNA decreases the hydrogen bonding network in cell water. UV light orders the water molecules by increasing the hydrogen bonds in water.
I’ll never tell you what to do, but I will always remind you of what you already know, that a wild intelligence resides in your DNA.

The key is transforming the environmental energies to something different and new..........We are all made up of broken pieces and out mitochondria are the glue that make life work..........that is heteroplasmy in a nutshell.

Evolution:

It does not mean random

It does not mean to improve

It does not mean morphing during a lifetime

It does not mean abiogenesis

It does not mean the origins of the universe

It does not mean social Darwinism

It does not mean amoral

It does not address theologic claims

It does not mean atheism, communism, racism or any ideology

Evolution means change over time brought about by environmental change

If you deny that things: The universe, change over time, then you deny reality

That theory of biological evolution attempts to explain the facts and connect the data that indicate that life on earth is related through a common descent and has been changing for a long time.

If you think you can do better, become a scientists and show us.

That's why the detox was put in '', really sensed that something was going on. Thank you for putting it in context! Just my timing was a bit ... unfavorable for me. But just came back home with a big bag of fresh fish and different seaweeds.

I see it in my Taiji practise, the more practise the more humbled I become about the complexity of the whole system and how fine you can tune into your body. Quite the same for Science - coming across your work and this site had a big humbling effect. Now there are quite some knots in my brain, but I'm eager to learn, put it into practise - reflect- and learn further. I'm really happy about this journey!

In the last days I catched quite some morning sun and also have been outside for about an hour or more - barefoot on the grass. Really like the feeling and think sometimes I become a lizard - like it! Found a UV-index page that shows that there is almost no UV before 11am here. So plan is to get up and have some sun first thing in the morning and then also getting outside again as UV becomes prominent. From tomorrow on I'll cycle to work (about 25 mins) and also put in my lunchbreak to get out of the lab and into the sun (20-30 min in a nearby park).

Started also with CT. Just measured my tap water temp today which is currently 15°C (59°F), went from 10 mins full emmersion to 15 mins today (with some additional lizarding afterwards). Recognized that my feet and especially hands get very cold in the water and stay that for long time afterwards. So I took my hands out and got some socks on. Works fine and I can read some printed JK articles while in the tub. Will try to make 20 min work tomorrow and then start to include ice to cool the water down step by step while trying to maintain the 20 min (feels like a good timeframe).

Also went to my optician to have a baseline check of my eyesight. Want to incorporate a bit of the techniques from Todd Beckers talk on myopia:

Have -1.5 (left) (was previously -2, but didn't do anything yet, except of taking of the glasses regularly in the last months) and -3 (right). Now I'm curious how this will develop.

My physician is now looking for the prices for the individual bloodwork and depending on my available funds I hope I can get my baseline next week.

### Inger — 2016-04-21T12:12:48+0300

### PeVe — 2016-05-01T20:04:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/peve-a-journals-journey.17797/post-193971>

> [Inger said:](/goto/post?id=193147)
>
> Welcome Peter!  One more German on the board.... nice
> Where in German do you live? I live 1 hour south of Hamburg.....

Thank you for welcoming! I live close to Mannheim, about 1 hour south of Frankfurt.

The last week has been quite interesting. Last weekend I have been in France and there was vegeterian food served. Luckily, I had some cans of fish with me. As I didn't eat much starchy food for a long time the effects had been quite interesting. Pimples came, digestion was out of balance as well as energy levels. Two days back at home - with different food. Everything normal again. So that's a clear sign of what my body needs currently.

Got my bike ready and left the sunglasses in the bag. Got some time off the lab in the morning (about 30 min) and went eihter to a nearby Park or an 'calm' area on my companies property. Barefoot on the grass and towards the sun (even if it was covered by clouds). Also started to enjoy gardening even more now. Hated lawnmoving. Now barefoot, glasses off and in the sun, I really enjoy it!

CT goes on and now I'm on 20 min in 12°C cold water. Get quite shivery afterwards, but after about an hour everything back on normal. Neoprene socks help a lot as my feet were really cold afterwards. Now I'm getting into my rhythm, yay!

Also got some bluelight blocker glasses and really sense that I fall asleep faster. That was quite an issue over the last days/week before that.

Finally, I got a lot thirstier. Usually I would drink about 2.5 liters a day. Now I'm at about 3.5-4 liters a day.

... finished Better Body - Better Brain, read some more blogposts and get my brain still fried... like that too!

# Thread: April webinar and the link a new view of the sun......
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/april-webinar-and-the-link-a-new-view-of-the-sun.17798/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 25

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-10T23:54:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/april-webinar-and-the-link-a-new-view-of-the-sun.17798/post-192480>

Might the cells mitochondria be a reflection of the sun? Is it nature's symmetry at play?

### Sue-UK — 2016-04-11T15:05:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/april-webinar-and-the-link-a-new-view-of-the-sun.17798/post-192500>

I was watching a BBC Horizon program last night about trying to predict solar flares, and I was thinking it reminded me of a mitochondria.

I've also been drawn to the picture in time 14 of the foetus within the tree trunk. It reminded me of Harold Saxton Burr's work whereby he had trees connected to voltmeters, studying how the electric fields of trees change in advance of weather patterns and atmospheric phenomena. Not sure how a tree does it, but its connected to the earth all the time .... So I'm wondering if when we are plugged in, might our mitochondria do the same and maybe adjust voltages - in advance- to suit...?

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-11T15:08:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/april-webinar-and-the-link-a-new-view-of-the-sun.17798/post-192501>

Very wise................

The solar wind and lightening strikes are linked..............on Earth.

### cantweight — 2016-04-11T16:34:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/april-webinar-and-the-link-a-new-view-of-the-sun.17798/post-192508>

> [Sue-UK said:](/goto/post?id=192500)
>
> I was watching a BBC Horizon program last night about trying to predict solar flares, and I was thinking it reminded me of a mitochondria.
>
> I've also been drawn to the picture in time 14 of the foetus within the tree trunk. It reminded me of Harold Saxton Burr's work whereby he had trees connected to voltmeters, studying how the electric fields of trees change in advance of weather patterns and atmospheric phenomena. Not sure how a tree does it, but its connected to the earth all the time .... So I'm wondering if when we are plugged in, might our mitochondria do the same and maybe adjust voltages - in advance- to suit...?

This is so interesting Sue...since moving out to the "country" I've noticed my entire family change in our response to storms, solar flares etc...even our oldest dog...from seizures, heart palps, wrecked sleep etc to an almost energized state. Its not something i truly understand but has been fascinating to watch it unfold.

### nonchalant — 2016-04-11T16:49:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/april-webinar-and-the-link-a-new-view-of-the-sun.17798/post-192509>

Last month I watched a lightning storm in the early morning. The lightning was hidden in the clouds; all that was visible was a smooth light grey sky. Then the lightning would flash somewhere, and I began to notice that with each strike, I could actually see two distinct flashes of light. Then I noticed that the first flash was purple, and the second flash was red. Neat! I tried to watch the fleeting rainbow carefully during the infrequent strikes. Once I thought I caught a flash of green between the purple and red, but maybe it was my imagination... I really can't wait for my next storm!

### Sue-UK — 2016-04-11T22:39:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/april-webinar-and-the-link-a-new-view-of-the-sun.17798/post-192520>

> [cantweight said:](/goto/post?id=192508)
>
> This is so interesting Sue...since moving out to the "country" I've noticed my entire family change in our response to storms, solar flares etc...even our oldest dog...from seizures, heart palps, wrecked sleep etc to an almost energized state. Its not something i truly understand but has been fascinating to watch it unfold.

The book I have is copyrighted 1972, so I did have a vision that with how measuring instruments have advanced, that instead of trying to predict what the sun will do, to protect satellites etc maybe they should ask a tree  a technological tree hug .

I put a glass of water with MB in on the outside table yesterday, I'm watching to see if it's faster to lose its colour now that the uv is around 4, instead of the 1 when I first did it. I went and peered at it today, and I noticed the wind was moving the surface ...information ....... So if the solar wind is an information carrier, (including advance warning) perhaps it transfers the information to the planet via lightning to the weather, for mitochondria (and trees ) to "read" and have time to electrically adapt? If our mitochondria "read" constant nnemf and the wrong light frequencies, and we can't read the real environment, maybe we don't anticipate and electrically adjust in the same way ... Electrical overload?

### Sue-UK — 2016-04-14T15:36:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/april-webinar-and-the-link-a-new-view-of-the-sun.17798/post-192667>

> [nonchalant said:](/goto/post?id=192509)
>
> Last month I watched a lightning storm in the early morning. The lightning was hidden in the clouds; all that was visible was a smooth light grey sky. Then the lightning would flash somewhere, and I began to notice that with each strike, I could actually see two distinct flashes of light. Then I noticed that the first flash was purple, and the second flash was red. Neat! I tried to watch the fleeting rainbow carefully during the infrequent strikes. Once I thought I caught a flash of green between the purple and red, but maybe it was my imagination... I really can't wait for my next storm!

It would be interesting to compare the lightning at different times of day ...Maybe the clouds are important too .... I was reading in Lyall Watson's book "the dreams of dragons, an exploration and celebration of the mysteries of nature". He talks of a Giorgio Piccardi of the institute of physical chemistry in Forence, who was intrigued by the vagaries of chemical reactions, sometimes the work, sometimes they don't. He suspected even simple reactions might be influenced beyond lab control. So for ten years, 3 times a day, he poured bismuth oxychloride into distilled water and recorded the time it took to form a cloudy precipitate. The times varied enormously, but not randomly, there were short term sudden changes he related to the earth magnetic field changes, and long term changes that correlated closely to the frequency of sunspots. My MB experiment isn't likely to produce accurate results ... But my well water has been weather bathing this morning.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-16T03:37:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/april-webinar-and-the-link-a-new-view-of-the-sun.17798/post-192801>

Theory organizes the facts. Theory tells us what ideas to test. Theory is predictive. Supported theory tells us how the world is organized and how change happens as time and the environment changes. All areas of human understanding need theory, for only with theory can we predict what will happen or has happened in cases we have not yet seen. This alerts us to things we do not know now, but that do exist. Theory comes from the creative processes of our mind and its ability to think. Theory works best when it is precise, so we know exactly what observations will support it and what observations will cause us to reject it. Theory is an abstraction, but its point is to understand the real world. There is no better RCT than Mother Nature. The more you know about the real world, the more likely you are to arrive at useful theory. My April 2016 webinar gave you my theory of what life fundamentally is and gave many predictions and testable ideas. Did you know that all of the UV repair enzymes involved in excision and resynthesis of damaged DNA, ligation of broken DNA, and repair of the oxygen radical damage are unique, highly conserved virus- specific genes. Most evolutionary biologist are shocked to find out that most of the known viruses are in fact persistent and inapparent, and not pathogenic or toxic. Does DNA work by quantum entanglement? I think so. [http://arxiv.org/abs/1403.5342](http://arxiv.org/abs/1403.5342)

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-16T03:37:46+0300

Bacteria have been collaborating and competing among themselves for nearly four billion years, and yet only came up with the eukaryotic cell once. It is ironic that viruses have been here longer and can force bacteria to do things it wants and needs it to do. Viruses change bacterial cells and eukaryotic cells massively and they do it quickly. Dead hosts can still make virus and dead virus can also make more virus to change more hosts. This makes viruses unique in biology and it also points out why the "enemy of an enemy is often our friend". I have a deep sense this was part of the equation that formed the mitochondrial nuclear partnership 600 million years ago in our oceans. I was reading lyle Watson's account of his encounter with false killer whales ..... One was dying and close to the shore, being held up by its companions, to prevent it drowning, despite the risk to themselves. If the humans tried to lead the healthy ones to deeper water, they came back. They would even"rescue" humans using snorkels and bring them back to shore, presumably thinking the snorkel noises were a sign of distressed breathing. The humans present did what they could to keep the healthy ones wet and protected from the sun at the ebb tides, but the group would not leave until the sick one died, 3 days later. Something compelled them, but nature took her course...... In humans in many cases we are able to stretch out the inevitable .....and those closest are tied to the energy drain for much longer. It was during such a time that I became intolerant of oysters, and I was kinda hoping that viruses in oysters and the stressed state of my mitochondria explains my changed experience with them. I didn't like oysters but could get them down in smoothies etc, but suddenly they started causing massive problems, first with gut problems (DH on the receiving end ) and for him it was third strike and oysters were out when he found me collapsed on the bathroom floor. The enemy of the enemy in the oysters had stopped being my friend ..... I've been tempted to try them again recently, perhaps a strategic alliance is now possible.

It's as if an organism as a whole responds to its environment ... But there is huge survival value in being able to anticipate it, with enough time to avoid or make use of it. If mitochondria are a sense organ that can sample the future, perhaps they have to be able to operate outside of a normal gravitational field, even momentarily. As a mitochondria swells or if there is higher heteroplasmy, or without a good exclusion zone, perhaps it becomes more affected by gravity, and loses its ability to operate outside of it.

I was reading that there's lots of different types of levitation, magnetic, electrostatic, gas film, acoustic, optical, the Casimir force etc so maybe the mini sun mitochondria has a moment of levitation, say in the pause at the end of an incoming signal. ### Sue-UK — 2016-04-20T16:51:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/april-webinar-and-the-link-a-new-view-of-the-sun.17798/post-193070>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=192501)
>
> The solar wind and lightening strikes are linked..............on Earth.

I love clouds .... Not grey clouds that hang over me like a blanket but ones that scud across the sky or are at altitude ... An outside CT treat. Must have been the sound of the windchimes in the webinar  but I ordered Lyall Watson's book "heaven's breath, a natural history of the wind". In it he describes an experiment done by a grad student from Chicago uni, where he took sterilised water, and put it into an atmosphere of hydrogen, methane and ammonia. He imitated the storms on earth by energising his model with an electrical discharge. At then end of a week it contained organic compounds and a few simple amino acids. More sophisticated tests followed resulting in more complex amino acids and even some nucleic acids. "**Life, or something very close to it, _can_ be created out of non living matter, and spontaneous generation rides again**. All **you need is water, a mix of special gases and a few thunderstorms. Then you can pluck life seeds out of thin air and plant them in a nourishing 'primeval soup', where they could, given time, grow to maturity."**

Then there's solar wind carrying viruses, some bacteria and many spore ....

And.... "**The inability of viruses to hold a tune, their lack of genetic stability, is due largely to their tendency to make mistakes when reproducing themselves. They not only breed faster, but change and mutate more often and with greater facility than any other organism. They have a natural ability to edit the genetic material, to tack loose bits of gene, odd phrases, together to form new compositions. This mechanism is a complex and possibly dangerous one, for which most other organisms have no need. There is really only one natural situation in which genes break often enough to require a full time repair service, and that is exposure to strong ultraviolet light.**

**Thanks to the ozone layer in our atmosphere, most of the Sun's potentially lethal short waves are filtered out before they reach us. So there is normally no risk of exposure to them and no reason for any organism to develop a specific defence against radiation with a wavelength around 2500 angstroms. But that is precisely what viruses do have. And, given their genetic volatility, it is highly unlikely that this one talent should have survived unchanged from a time, perhaps 3000 million years ago, when earth may not have had an ultraviolet screen. Evolution is notably parsimonious and seldom develops structures or abilities without good and recent reason.

The conclusion seems inevitable. These viruses possess efficient ultraviolet repair mechanism because they need them, because they have been recently and strongly exposed to the ravages of ultraviolet light. And there is nowhere that this could happen to them except above the ozone layer, at an altitude of at least thirty kilometres.**

**Somewhere up there, it seems, there is a breeding ground."**

Wonder what viruses bring to the human DNA repair table.

# Thread: Hello from New Delhi -India
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-new-delhi-india.17800/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### pascale ahmad — 2016-04-12T05:17:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-new-delhi-india.17800/post-192533>

Hello! I am a French lady, married to a wonderful Indian man, living in India for many years. I am 25kg overweight, totally unable to lose weight even on the most draconian keto diet. I have very low energy and am insulin resistant and have been suffering from chronic constipation and digestive issues for 30 years (I can't even begin to tell you all the things I have tried over the years to resolve this, colonic irrigation, detox, master cleans, tons probiotic...), I now realise I probably have leaky gut.
I have just completed 10 days of leptin reset diet, after reading Dr Kruze's book. the most amazing thing about it was that after about 3 days I stopped being hungry...even though my diet is not much different (I had already given up grains before) but it seems the timing of meals and the absolutely no snacking makes a huge difference. I do feel incredibly tired though and can't think how I will manage to exercise, I guess I'll cross that bridge when I get to it in a month time perhaps....
What I struggle with the most, is that living in India, the lack of availability of good protein (most people are vegeterians and live mainly on grains and pulses) and particularly fish and sea food. I live in landlocked North India, not on the coast, we only have river fish readily available (which taste strangely "muddy" and I am not sure how safe it is considering how polluted our rivers are). There is not much choice as far as meat goes, only chicken, goat and buffalo meat (beef is banned). I seem to live on eggs (which I am getting sick of!!!) and fresh coconuts.
Getting some sun is also quite a challenge, I do manage at the moment early in the morning but soon in another month the temperature will reach 45c + and for months we will be living indoors with the AC.
Anyway, I shall do my best with what is available here. I want to get well and I want to shed that weight at all cost.

### Jenny S — 2016-04-12T05:27:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-new-delhi-india.17800/post-192534>

Welcome Pascale - can you buy canned fish over there?

### pascale ahmad — 2016-04-12T06:08:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-new-delhi-india.17800/post-192536>

Yes, I can but I am worried about BPA.

### caroline — 2016-04-12T07:32:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-new-delhi-india.17800/post-192538>

Welcome Pascale.....

Some canned fish doesn't have BPA. Just go with what you can get readily.

Can you get outside very early in the morning before it gets too hot? What about grounding?

What about nnEMF in your house? Work?

Please start a journal in the journal section and give Dr. Kruse some health history. Your Mom's health, your grandma's, siblings? Children?
Don't worry about exercise just yet. Can you walk early in the morning? Grounding is very important.

What water are you drinking?

If you can manage it - become a "gold" member and you will have access to Jack's monthly Webinars and monthly live Q&A where you can ask Dr. K. Anything.

Do you listen to his podcasts? They are a fast and easier way to get info.

Do you have some of the books that are a must read?

Like ......John Ott's book .....Health and light

This journey to wellness is a light story .....

### Inger — 2016-04-12T12:04:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-new-delhi-india.17800/post-192545>

Welcome Pascale!

Can you get frozen fish from the ocean?
Maybe goat and buffalo is not too bad  they are both grassfed, no? And yes, canned fish would be better than non
if you get tons of sun, grounded, you will do great on a little less seafood anyways
maybe you can get raw, dried seaweed? You can buy those on the internet

Love how much progress you have already made with those few changes

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-12T14:57:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-new-delhi-india.17800/post-192547>

New Dehli is a lot like a big western city: lots of nnEMF. I'd make friends with an engineer over there and borrow their gauss meter and trimeter and I bet you find your answer. The population density and tech use there is crazy.

### pascale ahmad — 2016-04-12T17:22:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-new-delhi-india.17800/post-192558>

Thank you very much everybody for all you suggestions.
Regarding fish I will investigate further and find out which brands PBA free, frozen is not really a possibility...
We drink reverse osmosis water, we have a device which is attached to the tap and cleans the water. I then store it in the fridge in stainless steel bottles.
At the moment it is still possible to go outside very early in the morning, so far I've just been sitting on the terrace.
By grounding, do you mean walking barefoot in the grass?
Thank you Jack, I don't need to borrow any metre, Delhi has huge EMF due to population density and absolutely no regulations in anything. The air pollution is also one of the highest in the world. We moved to the suburb a few years ago which is a bit better but I still have to go to Delhi for work everyday.
Please recommend books to read and which podcast to listen to to start with.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-12T17:40:10+0300

hi- I'm a real newbie at this but I figured I'd ask anyway. My husband (40 yr old contractor/ recreational tennis player) has a torn hip labrum (injured 10 months ago) that he is having surgery to repair in 2 weeks. I was trying to talk him into a quick trip to Gulf Shores this weekend as I believe it will be beneficial for grounding, water, sunlight and all. We get plenty of sun here in New Orleans and actually spent this whole past weekend outside and in the pool. We eat a good amount of seafood, grass-fed, etc. but was thinking this could give him an edge for surgery. I am also trying to convince him to visualize the whole process as a positive one and draw it, wrote it down. I think he's starting to look at me like he doesn't need "all that".
