### pascale ahmad — 2016-06-04T09:37:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pascales-journal.17812/post-195967>

I really have to get the hang of this journal thing.... I want to keep track and write about my progress but somehow I keep procrastinating.
Low dopamine apparently...

I have now completed 60 days of leptin reset, still have no cravings and am down to two meals per day. I do not feel hungry at lunch time, I have an early dinner at about 5.30pm.
I am however quite bored with my food, eating canned sardine or tuna everyday is getting very tedious but I cannot get sea fish here. I think I'm going to have to find a way to supplement with DHA.

I love my morning routine, I get up at about 5.30, eat my breakfast on the terrace watching sunrise and then walk to the park next door for some more sun and grounding till 7.30.
I watch the last bit of sunset at 6.30 in the evening and I'm in bed by 8.30/9pm
It makes me feel very calm, I have even been able to successfully give up ashwagandha, which I had started taking over a year ago. I was worried that I would go back to my crazy perimenopause split personally but no, so far so good!

I wish I could have more sun exposure during the day to get my vitamin D level up but temperature it is still about 45C at the moment and we have to stay indoors and manage the heat in between power cuts during which the ac doesn't work.
A year ago my D level was only 5ng/ml, my doctor immediately gave me four shots of it during the course of a month. I have no idea was my levels are now, I have been supplementing 5000iu per day for the last two months.

I am now very careful with blue light, I don't switch on the lights at home in the evening any more, except in the kitchen where I wear my blue blockers.
I could not find amber bulbs for the house and candles are not an option with ac bow and ceiling fans so I bought about a dozen Himalayan salt lamps instead. I am hoping that they also helps with EMF which is less than optimal at work and in the house. I can't do much about it for now other than switching off the wifi at night, but we are planning to move house by the end of the year when the weather gets cooler.

I have been reading a lot of blogs lately, quite randomly though, perhaps I should be more systematic and start at the beginning and work chronologically. I am just beginning to understand a few things, although all that science is still a big blur to me. It's not helping that I always hated science and physics at school (that's why I went to art school!) I can't believe it's come back to bite me in the ass!!!!!
Also read Epi Paleo Rx, Mastering Leptin, Brain Grain, Health and Light and currently reading Sex, Lies and Menopause by T.S Wiley, a very fascinating book if you are a woman of 50!!!!

Still no fat loss to report so far and the no progress either on the chronic constipation front, I am still dependent on my Ayurvedic herbs in order to have a bowed movement everyday. I have tried to give them up several times before but I end up not going to the loo at all and then it gets very painful.
I have struggled with this for so long, I know diet is not the answer, eating a lot of fibre (as I have been told by virtually all doctors) does nothing for me other than bloat me up.
Perhaps the answer is to be found in the brain gut series which I am planning to tackle this month.

### pascale ahmad — 2016-06-07T06:13:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pascales-journal.17812/post-196068>

After weeks of procrastination, I finally got round to watching the fat loss for women webinar, which gave me the desired "kick up the ass" to at last attempt CT yesterday evening.
OMG!!!! This was possibly the most horrible thing I have ever done! And that was only head dunking...
I could not keep my head in the ice bowl more than 10 seconds at a time and I was probably counting too fast. I gave up after a few dunks, which was about 5mn but felt like an eternity.
I don't know if it so hard because I attempted to do this when it is really hot here and the temperature difference is that much greater or because I have become very warm adapted from living in a hot climate for so long.
I'm psyching myself up to do it again this evening but only because I am vain and I'll do anything to be thin again!

### pascale ahmad — 2016-06-09T05:24:12+0300

I wish I could get my energy level up. There has been some very tangible improvements in the last two months but I can't seem to shake off the apathy and procrastination.

I have not repeated the head dunking either...
It has to be said that I have never really liked exercising for the sake of it. When I still lived in London I use to walk a lot just going from a to b doing what I had to do, I would choose not to hop on a bus or not take the tube. I can't do that here, 1 because of the heat and 2 because you can't really walk places, there are virtually no pavements, you have to walk on dirty side roads avoiding cows, people or piles of rubbish which is rather stressful.
I have a big park just round a corner from my house which is clean and has a walking track I'm the edge of it and in the past I have used it but purposely walking round and round in circles is so utterly boring.

> .....

> I love my morning routine, I get up at about 5.30, eat my breakfast on the terrace watching sunrise and then walk to the park next door for some more sun and grounding till 7.30.
> I watch the last bit of sunset at 6.30 in the evening and I'm in bed by 8.30/9pm
> It makes me feel very calm, I have even been able to successfully give up ashwagandha, which I had started taking over a year ago. I was worried that I would go back to my crazy perimenopause split personally but no, so far so good!

I'm having a hard time journaling too, feeling at a loss of words. I've wondered if it is low dopamine...

Oh my, I have felt the crazy split personality thing, where did SHE come from? :-( So not the calm easy-going person that I used to be. Well, let me put it this way, I am still a gentle, easy-going kind of person generally speaking but I get really frustrated or angry much easier than before. Dr told me that I'm in menopause per my labs. Really hoping the sunshine and DHA kick in and help calm me down. I'm sure my kids would enjoy me better if I was how I used to be.

You have a lovely morning routine, I'm trying to create my own.

### pascale ahmad — 2016-06-17T11:02:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pascales-journal.17812/post-196764>

Hi Labellavita!
Thanks for your message which is making me write, I had fallen off the journal bandwagon again and yes from everything I've read low dopamine is what would be the cause of inertia and procrastination.
Menopause basically sucks it's like somebody else is living in my body from now on and I really would like to kick her out!!! And you started out with a nice easy going personality, I was always a high strung control freak so imagine what I had become!
Yes the morning/evening sunshine and grounding has make a huge difference to my mood, although in the last few days it has gone out of the window. I have just arrived in Paris for work where there is no sunshine at the moment. It's raining everyday, I am stuck in a hotel with 24h wifi and I can't even open the window. I spend all day in meetings in blue light and eat dinner too late. Anyway just two more days and I will be off to my parents in Burgundy for a whole month holiday in the countryside, where hopefully I can get back on tracks.
Where do you live? Do you have plenty of sunshine?

### caroline — 2016-06-17T12:29:31+0300

### labellavita — 2016-06-17T15:01:26+0300

I spend all day in meetings in blue light and eat dinner too late. Anyway just two more days and I will be off to my parents in Burgundy for a whole month holiday in the countryside, where hopefully I can get back on tracks.
> Where do you live? Do you have plenty of sunshine?

Menopause is really difficult. Haven't had any hot flashes, wondering if that would be easier to deal with than this Dr Jekyll/Mr Hyde inside of my head? Eeek! LOL, I've become more controlling too, my kids (teens) love to joke around but the noise, I really can't stand it and I feel so bad. I have always loved how well they get along, and I still do, just as long as they keep the noise down to a low decibel. Sigh...

A holiday, how I would love a vacation, I think that would help improve my mood, I feel like I need a break. Enjoy yourself! Soak up the sun!

I live in Atlanta, GA in the States. We do receive a lot of sun, I am just really not enjoying the heat or the humidity though. Maybe in time this protocol will help me become more tolerant of both. I have been here forever though and would love to move out of this area to be honest. Initially the desire to move was to get out of the heat, humidity, and traffic, and find a place surrounded by nature on some acreage, but now I'm adding low nnEMF to my list. I'm working on it! ### pascale ahmad — 2016-06-18T09:10:32+0300

I think living in a place with a lot of sunshine is such a plus. I complain about it all the time... but being back home in France (the weather is awful right now, it's been raining all the time and it's cold for the season) made me realise how fortunate I am to be able to go out every single morning on my terrace for my daily sun gazing and the sun is always shines every single day without fail and I can get healing for free!!!

### pascale ahmad — 2016-06-26T18:45:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pascales-journal.17812/post-197303>

After 4 days work in Paris in EMF infested hotels, endless blue light meetings and eating out of circadian timing (albeit a fantastic oysters and sea food platter at La Coupole); I arrived in Burgundy a week ago where I will stay in untill the end of July.
The plan, other than seeing my family, was to get away from the Delhi heat, polution, high population density, EMF and be able to get some sun and have access to plenty of fish and sea food. Although Burgundy is nowhere near the sea, it's nice to be in the modern world with a great food distribution network.
In order to maximise my spending time outdoors, I have been mowing the lawn, weeding out the flower beds, I went mushroom picking in the woods and even took my son fishing in the nearby river. My parents, bewildered by my rather strange behaviour, are wondering who this new person is. It has to be said that, although brought up in the countryside, I was never a country girl at heart; by the time I turn 18 I couldn't run fast enough towards the blue light of the big city and I never looked back. I also asked for the pool not to be heated this year so I could CT, which confirm the suspicion that I had indeed been abducted by aliens and my body possessed by some extra terrestrial entity...
So far, things have not gone exactly according to plan. I'm not getting as much sun as I had anticipated, we are having the wettest spring/summer since 1943, and I did succumb to a few croissants and raspberry macarons. But still, all is not lost, I'm eating loads of fish and fantastic grass fed Charolais beef, spending lots of time outdoors, in between two showers, I breath clean air, sleep like a baby and there very low EMF.
The most difficult part is sticking to my eating plan, there is so much temptation not just because I live abroad and I never get to eat the things I was brought up on but also because drinking a cafe au lait out of a traditional bowl and dunking a croissant takes on another dimension, in it is more than just the food I love. It's my family, my childhood, my culture, my way of belonging and going back to my roots. I don't have to explain why I do what I do, I just fit in effortlessly and I don't want to break that feeling by eating differently to everyone else or at odd timings.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-06-26T19:50:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pascales-journal.17812/post-197313>

good...........use CT when the sun is hiding. Your mitochondria will love you for it.

### pascale ahmad — 2016-08-17T09:54:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/pascales-journal.17812/post-199712>

Ooops I've done it again! I keep telling myself that I will write a regular journal and then I dont...
Anyway, here is a recap of the last two months.
I'm back in India after spending 6 weeks at home in France with my family, who live out in the countryside in the middle of nowhere (literally our nearest neighbour is 4km away and the village 6km).
I have not spent that much time at home since I left 30 years ago and I have now lived abroad longer than I have lived in my own country, so this was an experience in itself. The purpose of this exercise was to put myself in a healthier environment than the one I live in: ie. low population density, low emf, low pollution, access to sea fish and sun exposure and grounding in bearable temperature).
Well, some of it worked out and some didn't...I'm sure that the clean environment did me good, I didn't measure the emf but if the unusual amount of bees in we have on the grounds around the house is an indication, it must be low (in fact its a bit of a problem because they nest in unwanted places like under the roof or inside the shutters) and the nearest cell tower is miles away.
I sunbathed naked everyday in the grass, came back with a hell of a tan, hopefully I managed to store some D, who knows, I guess I have to test.
I even CTed a few times in our pool until the whole family came down on me for not heating the water, no one other than me wanted to swim in 18C water and I couldn't quite transitioned to ice bath, so that was the end of it.
Where things went terribly wrong was my diet; I now realise that family and long lunches are the enemy of optimal health.
My diet demise started with cheese, I loooove cheese! I mean real French cheese that's aged and mouldy and smelly (cause if it don't stink, it ain't cheese!) but that led to eating bread (I know, I know, practically a cardinal sin) which let to eating cakes and before I knew it I was on a rampage.
I became possessed by a "going back to my roots" kind of thing and also started eating frogs legs, snails and sweetbreads (veal thymus gland) and the likes, not bad in themselves but they tended to come with starter and desert over a long lunch or dinner with copious amounts of wine.
Oh yes I was happy for a while, the warm fuzzy glow of family, my childhood home....and then I weighed myself, I had put on 5kg in 6 weeks and my hair has been falling by the handful ever since.
What I do find puzzling though is why are French women, mostly, not fat? They eat wheat at every meal, copious amount of dairy in the form of cheese, are subjected to emf, they don't walking in the grass bare feet, don't CT and don't exercise much either . So why are they not fat?

### Deb — 2016-04-14T17:40:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-carolina.17813/post-192683>

I am new on this forum and just trying to get well, starting on the Leptin Rx. I am an RN and Jack's posts have taught me more than I ever learned in college! Thanks and respect to Dr. K!

I am a former Army Nurse; worked constantly in blue light hospital environment; shot up with so many vaccines I have no idea how many or what they were at this point; I have been exposed to toxins from work and implants. I have been ill for over 25 years and have struggled to find health. I have autoimmunity-Raynauds, sjogrens, episcleritis, vasculitis, and possibly primary biliary cirrhosis. I am tired-fatique is all consuming! I was bedridden but now up about 2-3 hours per day. I have some MTHFR SNPs, homocysteine levels that are high. I guess I begin at the beginning with the Leptin Rx. Any suggestions would be so welcome. I am positive my brain fog is related to all this and therefore I really need step by step so I can assimilate and embrace the information. Thanks for your help!

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-14T20:49:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-north-carolina.17813/post-192705>

Welcome. Nice pic!

Don't survive, thrive. Do life soaring. Our cells read and react to our environment; it shapes us and we are shaped by it.
The six best healers in the world that I know:
1\. Sunlight
2\. Un-fluoridated water
3\. magnetism/grounding to Earth
4\. seafood
5\. Self Confidence
6\. Friends
That is all...........Carry On.
[http://www.nature.com/articles/srep12029](http://www.nature.com/articles/srep12029)

Here is the key that most of the critics miss..........the order of the recipe is critical to the success. Your perception will be thrown off if you do it other ways. I can think of 3 recent members who went of the rails this way. The last 4 series pointed this point this out but few understand the implications. Neil BB and I have been offline talking about it. Your BUN/creat ratio and ketosis must be started before DHA loading. the blogs have been clear that the exclusion zone inside of cells manifests due to proteins within becoming hydrophilic first. How do you do you make things hydrophilic? You slowly add in electrons. The way to add them is step 4 above. The closer you are to the equator means you have more push from your current (DC) and then you may not need as much........unless there is something peculiar about your environment, job, eyes, skin.

# Thread: CT with aquarium chiller
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-with-aquarium-chiller.17814/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### omegajosh — 2016-04-15T09:48:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-with-aquarium-chiller.17814/post-192717>

I have been looking around at the commercial CT baths. They are extortionately priced. Trying to find a cheaper alternative I stumbled across aquarium water chillers. At a 10th of the price does anyone think they might be a decent alternative rigged up to a bath?

### fitness@home — 2016-04-15T13:01:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-with-aquarium-chiller.17814/post-192729>

I use a chiller that keeps my CT tank at a steady 55 degrees. Was lucky enough to find a good one on Craigslist and only paid $150 for it.

There are a couple of others on the forum that have used them too.

### omegajosh — 2016-04-15T13:16:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-with-aquarium-chiller.17814/post-192730>

Great. Can you send me a link for the one you use?
Do you leave it chilled 24/7? I was thinking about just switching it on for an hour before CT to chill down to save electricity/environment.

### fitness@home — 2016-04-15T15:58:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-with-aquarium-chiller.17814/post-192744>

I think it is an Aqua Logic Delta Star. Paperwork is at home, so not positive. We have it set on a timer. It comes on about 2 hours before I get home from work. It only runs 3-4 hours per day. I always turn it off before getting in - don't want any extra nnemf field around me.

My stock tank is mounted in a cabinet that my husband built for it. There are pictures somewhere in my journal (On the Pathways to Methylation, post #650). I keep a foam cover on it when not in use to keep it cool and to also keep out dust, pet fur, etc... Can't stand when the bottom or sides start to feel "slimy". I do put Epsom salts in when we fill it, in the hope it stays clean longer.

Usually drain/replace the water about once per month. In fact, it will be refilled this weekend. We drained it before going on vacation recently.

### QuantumRadical — 2016-04-15T22:40:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-with-aquarium-chiller.17814/post-192786>

I have been using this one for a few years. [https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000TMC2CI/ref=oh\_aui\_search\_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1](https://www.amazon.com/gp/product/B000TMC2CI/ref=oh_aui_search_detailpage?ie=UTF8&psc=1)

Depending on the temp of the water, the desired temp, and the amount of water to be cooled, you can determine rate of cooling. My tub size allows a 6 degree F decrease per hour. Ymmv.

### omegajosh — 2016-04-16T13:57:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-with-aquarium-chiller.17814/post-192825>

6 degree F per hour sounds fine. I am only looking to cool my tap water by a further 10-15 degrees.

### travolttt — 2020-06-23T12:43:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-with-aquarium-chiller.17814/post-290456>

hey, I'm just starting using a chiller, and can't find the right one. Any suggestions? Also, I'm new at this and wanna help other newbies like me. I have been looking for the right decorations for my aquarium for a veery long time and came across this site that helped me a lot. It has all the information about [live rocks](https://arcreef.com/live-rock/live-rock-guide/) beginning with what are they and ending with helping you choose the right ones for you! This site is a true salvation!

# Thread: More muscle with fasting longer?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-muscle-with-fasting-longer.17815/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Fitness
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Butters — 2016-04-15T10:48:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/more-muscle-with-fasting-longer.17815/post-192719>

I recently watched some videos about steroids and GH and about their functions.
And because I now know, that GH is spiking during nighttime after leptin gets into the brain I think about methods to get more GH response.
Now, when we eat not too late in the day and have no light disturbance, would GH rise earlier and last longer in the body? Do we have to go to sleep then early or do we get the effect without going to sleep earlier?
So thinking about people talking about building muscle and loosing fat is only possible for training beginners maybe completely wrong, because simply people are giving their bodies the wrong signals in the wrong time (thinking about the carb loadings in the evening). Because when GH is high you build muscle AND burn fat, you cant help it. But when you blunt the response with carbs all day you of course get the anabolic responce of insulin but inhibit GH, so you get fat along with building muscle.
Ideally in my opinion one must go for carbs in the morning and gradually go low carb/keto in the evening to get the anabolic effect from insulin in the morning AND the muscle building effect and fatburning effect from GH in the evening/night.
And as I am writing this I realize thats what Jack is writing in the Rx for optimal body composition after the Leptin reset, LOL
But the actually question from the beginning remains. So if someone has some idea about it, let it out

They spare their muscles and use their fat as the source provided they are cooled and connected to the ground. This ability goes all the way back to 65 million years ago. Neil Billeaud and I had an interesting discussion about ATP, water, light, DHA and how mammals physiology changes during hibernation or during REM sleep: More complex mammals like humans have shrunk hibernation into some of our nightly sleep cycles Mammals are capable of transforming magnetic flux into a DC electric current using the tensegrity system of piezoelectric semiconductors using the inverse spin Hall effect. People have forgotten that Robert O. Becker wrote about this effect in bone in the 1960's. It directly ties to maintenance and strength of the entire muscle-skeletal system of mammals. The system has different sensitivities based upon the local environment of the mammal and species. This is why poor sleep and poor muscle mass are linked in humans.

# Thread: So stupidly, I recently had cataract surgery
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-stupidly-i-recently-had-cataract-surgery.17817/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### [email redacted] — 2016-04-15T17:16:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-stupidly-i-recently-had-cataract-surgery.17817/post-192755>

Last December.
I am a 62-year-old female. Right after my cataract surgery, I read here about the deleterious UV blocking in the implanted lenses. I'm prediabetic and my fasting blood sugar rose consistently about 10 points after the surgery.
How to cope? Get morning sun on my skin; cold baths; minimize EMF exposure; install some UV light in my house? Anything else? I apologize if the answer to my question is elsewhere in this forum or on Dr. Jack's blogs. I have zero scientific background and get totally befuddled in the blogs.

### nonchalant — 2016-04-15T19:19:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/so-stupidly-i-recently-had-cataract-surgery.17817/post-192770>

Hi Joan, don't fret. You still have UV-sensitive neuropsin in your cornea and skin. You need to be serious about exposure to sunlight all day. It will eventually fix the diabetes also. As for the UV lights, where do you live?
Have you seen these posts?
[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...ses-implanted-fear-not-neuropsin-helps.16972/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/if-your-patients-have-had-lenses-implanted-fear-not-neuropsin-helps.16972/)
[https://www.jackkruse.com/the-dark-knight/](https://www.jackkruse.com/the-dark-knight/)

My parents have both had cataract surgery and their health has really deteriorated afterwards. Wish I could convince them to get more than a few minutes of sunshine daily.

### Inger — 2016-04-20T17:13:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-cold-smoked-salmon-raw.17820/post-193074>

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cortisol-fatigue-and-body-temperature.17821/post-192792>

Hi All,
I'm hoping for some feedback. I have had several saliva tests for cortisol done over the past few years. Although low levels throughout the day, it is a normal cortisol rhythm. Normal being: a higher morning and decreasing as the day goes on. My question is the morning time for me is so hard to get up and get moving, chronic fatigue and I could go back to sleep. Yet as the day goes on I get more energy. At about 5-6pm until 10pm it's the most energy of the day. Is this still considered a circadian mismatch even though the rhythm is normal? Or is something such as leptin playing a role?
I am relatively thin and have a difficult time keeping weight on. Thank you all for any advice!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cortisol-fatigue-and-body-temperature.17821/post-192838>

People are enamored with certainty, they only see what they believe and do not believe what they observe. You do not understand AM sun light therefore you think it is OK to avoid.

Sunlight makes our surfaces relative, and that relativity changes the space/time of our cells and tissues. It shifts blood into the skin by dermal pooling due to eNOS and NO. More than 50% of your blood volume can change your skin surface when sun is present. This can lower your blood pressure. It changes the anatomical structure of the skin by changing the optics of surfaces. It becomes a sensory stimulus for the interoceptive system to induce biochemical substrates via photosynthesis in plants and changes in the ATPase using red light and water in animals. Sunlight increase Vitamin D, histamine, and sulfhydryl groups, while lowering (photolysis) adrenalin, steroids, testosterone, estrogen, thyroid hormone, DNA and RNA. Sunlight induces biochemical reactions via photolysis and it induces coordinated endocrine adaptation effects in the eye and the skin surfaces. It affects the sympathetic and parasympathetic systems. It is the stimulus for the circadian timing mechanism of the body clock via the central retinal pathways.

Melatonin is an antagonist of aromatase so it lowers estrogen in tissues. PCOS in women and high E2 levels in men are signs of sunlight deficiency and a low quantum yield environment. Left untreated, this will cause % heteroplasmy to increase and insulin resistance to rise, glucose control loss, loss of sulfation of the blood plasma, and eventual metabolic syndrome and a change of oxidize lipids with higher mass and inertia in the blood. The answer is not blood pressure medicine, statins, and glucophage…….it is sensible sun exposure. The sun photons makes melatonin initially in the globe, during the day, and its presence in the eye amplifies and generalizes its production throughout the visual systems and into the brain at night when it sensitizes the entire system and shrinks the geometry of the respiratory proteins of mitochondria to regenerate our tissues. Melatonin is the biogenic amine the controls mitochondrial DNA mutations. Since a mitochondria is of bacterial origin it has 5 times the mutation rate of the nuclear genome, making proper melatonin function the key player in health. What is not well known or appreciated is that melatonin is really a solar hormone because of the relationship of its aromatic amino acids backbone that absorbs all frequencies of UV light. UV light is only present during the day and this is why the melanopsin photoreceptors regenerate during daylight when we are not using them.

### caroline — 2016-04-18T06:42:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cortisol-fatigue-and-body-temperature.17821/post-192962>

It is great that your husband understands the significance of light ...for him too!

There is a great little book .... Health and light by John Ott
Dr.K. refers to this book often.

Dr. K. says fowl is foul!

Do you guys eat sushi? you can make your own \[very easy\] and sub cauliflower rice - or real rice if you eat it.
there is a recipe in the recipe section for chicken patties ..... but I use salmon trimmings for it. I get them at our seafood store and they are quite a bit cheaper.
Just lightly grill salmon or seafood of your choice and steam some veggies to go with. I usually grill extra salmon and then we eat it cold with some avocado and home made garlic mayo and green onions. I also add an avocado and some fresh herbs to the mayo and blitz and you have a great dressing.

Do you need an easy recipe for mayo? ask if you want one.

Do you have a crock pot? throw a leg of pork in before you go to bed ...and voila - breakfast!

I also put a couple of grass fed steaks on a wooden board and sprinkle with fresh ground pepper and tumeric and let it sit for a few days. I then slice it very thin and it is a little like salami. I love this and find it very easy to digest - in fact, easier than a cooked steak.

Dr. K. has a cook book.....

Fasting glucose 72
Sodium 141 RR 135-148
Potassium 4.3 RR 3.5-5.3

BUN 16
Creatine 0.72 RR .4-1.4
Bun:creatinine ratio 22. HIGH
GFR 108 RR >60
Calcium 9.6 RR 8.6-10.5
Total protein 6.6 RR 6.4-8.9
Albumin 4.3 Rr 3.5-5.7
Globulin 2.3
A/G 1.9
Alkaline phosphate 77
Ast 19
Alt 14
Bilirubin total 0.7 RR .3-1.2

Uric acid 6.1 RR 2.3-6.6 HIGH (my joints have been swollen in my hands)
HDL 79
LDL 92
Triglycerides 43 RR <150
Lipoprotein 8 RR
Vit b12 833 RR 211-911
Folate 20.5 RR > 5.4
Iron serum 134 RR 50-212
Ferritin 56 RR 10-291 LOW?
Hemoglobin A1c 5.9 HIGH I believe bc I was skipping meals.

Currently on Armour thyroid 1 grain and cytomel
10mcgs. (Two Divided doses throughout day. AM: 1/2 grain armour
5mcgs cytomel.
Afternoon: same.
TSH 0.007 Low RR 0.270-4.200
Free T4 .92. RR .93-1.70
Free T3 3.190

Reverse T3 12.6 RR 9.0-27.0 this is usually elevated without any thyroid AND remains elevated with Armour thyroid.
Only Lowers once adding in the cytomel. Leptin Resistant?
CBC panel is normal.
Differential (polys & lymphs, Eos..) All normal.

Vit D 25-OH 49.8 RR 30-100 LOW. I supplement about 1000iu/day

Homocysteine 5.3 RR 5.0-15.0 Inproved with Thorn b vitamins. Methyl formulation.

Progesterone 0.6 I'm assuming the REf ranges are non applicable as I'm breastfeeding and
Cycle has not yet returned? Usually very depleted on progesterone, I supplement
With bio identical transdermal progesterone.

DHEA s 138 RR 45-270
SHBG 113.4 RR 28-146 (this came down from a 158, 5 months prior)
Testosterone wasn't performed by lab but was requested.
(It's usually quite low.)

Insulin 3 RR <24.4

Estrogens, total 54.6
Pregnenolone 40 RR 15-132
I'm concerned about the SHBG, the BUN creatinine ratio, and of course, the low hormones. But I'm wondering if Breast feeding is a time when SHBG is high, since prolactin is high, not sure.
Hemoglobin A1c is high but fasting glucose and insulin are low.
I'm getting in my AM sunlight and my insomnia is gone!
I do wake up a bit throughout the night but mostly due to my son stirring around.
I'm also getting in much more seafood now.
Trying to stick to the Leptin Rx but I have a terrible habit of
Snacking on green and blacks organic 85% chocolate, I know... ;(it's bad. I'm weaning off.
Any advice or input is greatly appreciated

### Penny — 2016-04-28T02:23:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cortisol-fatigue-and-body-temperature.17821/post-193726>

He means inter/intra cellular water... which would be dehydrated when you are exposed to EMF no matter what kind of water you are drinking - reverse T3 high=>environment still sucketh... BUN/creatine ratio also says the same thing - when you are dehydrated it causes the proteins to misfold and the entrance to the mitochondria (where you process carbs) is screwed up - thus, keto diet with seafood is a great move - you have some more EMF mitigating to do and you should drink more water - also, there could still be flouride in your water which screws everything up - electron flow and iodine - add lots of kelp to your diet - miso soup - remember, iodine is the first to fall... then comes magnesium - because it has no water to bind to as you are dehydrated - I don't know what Berkey is, but you need to drink RO water - they sell them on Amazon - you also might want to add tamarind to your diet - but don't know if that's a hot idea if you are nursing - tamarind helps get rid of flouride - which weirdly is in canned salmon and green tea - chocolate is great, you need something - I would keep that - but not as a snack of course -

I've been drinking around a gallon per day of Berkey water here on LI for 6 years. My bun/c ratio is 7 and I think that's pretty good. If you check the cdc website there's no fluoride in Nassau or Suffolk and I confirmed this personally years ago while doing electrical work in many well houses for many water districts. We do have other pollutants to worry about here especially around the old industrial areas like bethpage and republic airport. I'd like to get other sources of water like mountain valley spring or reverse osmosis in the near future. I'd also like to bury some Berkey water in the ground in a glass carboy in the meantime. Lots of work to do.

I also just cured my insomnia in January with early sun and I also eat more 85% chocolate than I'd like to share. However, it is a fantastic snack to eat during exercise and sports in my experience. I stopped eating green and blacks after reading it has high amounts of lead or something in it. Many brands do, crazy world.
In the last couple months I switched to a high quality lard for cooking eggs in the morning. I was using coconut oil which I learned is not seasonal in winter.
The more lard I eat the less chocolate I crave. I really don't know if one is better than the other, but this is what I have noticed.
As far as I know, there are no smart meters on LI yet but PsychopathicSickEvil&Greedy have plans to roll them out this year.

### Karl K — 2016-04-15T23:54:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reducing-risk-of-and-dealing-with-sunburn.17822/post-192794>

Went on a 45 mile bike ride yesterday out at the lake. Had a great time but now sunburnt on arms. What is the epi-paleo rx for dealing with or reducing risk of sunburn? I am extremely fair skinned and burn easily. Will I still absorb sufficient vit d if arms are covered on bike rides? Should I still be outside getting more sun if already burnt?

### Karl K — 2016-04-16T05:32:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reducing-risk-of-and-dealing-with-sunburn.17822/post-192807>

Dr. Wunsch only touches on the subject of sunscreen as protection very briefly in this video and doesn't even really distinguish what kind of sunscreen would be best- he seems to talk about "shiny sunscreen that stays on the surface". I am looking for more in depth answers here.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-16T05:57:21+0300

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-16T05:58:46+0300

### thomas — 2016-04-16T14:21:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/reducing-risk-of-and-dealing-with-sunburn.17822/post-192827>

your skin isn't equiped for sudden high sun exposure. you gotta take it slow and tan slooooww. the sun takes 6 monthes to gow from it's lowest to highest altitude...you can start to do it earlier in the morning and later in the afternoon to get less dose. I am thinking about all the people that suddenly are on the beach for 2 weeks on july..but never take sun otherwise, that seems dangerous to me. Don't get sun while sunburnt in my opinion your skin is healing and can't function properly. Note.. To me it's impossible to get sunburnt now..and I am a celt, but in the summer my skin is brown man(I am trying to see how brown it can get lol) I gotta try on 21 june to do the full day from dawn to dusk in the sun, maybe that 'll do it lol.

# Thread: Serotonin and sunlight
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/serotonin-and-sunlight.17827/>
**Subforum:** Heal Your Hormones
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Valerie — 2016-04-17T03:30:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/serotonin-and-sunlight.17827/post-192885>

Innov Clin Neurosci. 2013 Jul-Aug; 10(7-8): 20–24.
Published online Jul-Aug 2013.
PMCID: PMC3779905
**Sunshine, Serotonin, and Skin: A Partial Explanation for Seasonal Patterns in Psychopathology**

[Go to:](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3779905/#)
**Abstract**
A number of studies indicate that there can be seasonal variations in the expression of psychiatric phenomena, especially mood and anxiety symptoms, as well as completed suicide. Indeed, in acknowledgement of the potential for seasonal effects in depressive disorders, the _Diagnostic and Statistical Manual of Mental Disorders, Fifth Edition_ indicates the specifier, “with seasonal pattern.” However, the explanations for the relationships between seasonal changes and exacerbations of psychopathology remain unclear, although the empirical literature indicates that an association between sunshine and serotonin is likely. Given that the relationship between sunshine and serotonin is probably a multimediated phenomenon, one contributory facet may be the role of sunshine on human skin. Human skin has an inherent serotonergic system that appears capable of generating serotonin. In this edition of The Interface, we discuss the relationships among psychopathology, sunshine, serotonin, and the skin.

[http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3779905/](http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3779905/)

How is insulin’s action quantized? When energies of electrons are higher because of the action of the photons of the sun, that signal is transduced by the cytochromes in the electron chain transporters. Most electrons from carbohydrates enter at cytochrome one because NADH is a fluorophore protein. It absorbs best at 340 nm range of light which is strongly in the UV range of the solar spectrum. This couples photoelectrically with insulin release from the pancreas.

Photoelectrically? Really? How? In plants sunlight hits a Magnesium atom at the center of chlorophyll to liberate and electron that is immediately ferried away to a reaction center called P680 to liberate electrons from water. The first step in photosynthesis is the oxidation or BURNING of water to liberate electrons. When wood burns in air, oxygen atoms pull electrons from carbon atoms. The electrons in carbon are lovely attached so carbon burns easily. When a plant or animal burns water the process requires a lot of energy. all photosynthetic reactions are unique in that it is the only place in the cosmos where water is burnt to yield electrons. So where do these electrons go in plants? NADPH. Where do they go in animals? NADH. That is the redox couple in cytochrome 1 of mitochondria. These electrons are ferried to oxygen as the terminal electron acceptor in animals. In this quantum dance, protons, are moved, water is charge separated, and ATP is made in two ways. One from a protons force within the mitochondria and another from the penetrating ability of red light in sunlight. If ATP is not made abundantly insulin resistance is the result. Very few people seem to realize that a lack of full spectrum sunlight can drive insulin resistance. Insulin resistance is not a food only problem.

The first step in photosynthesis and sunlight hitting your skin is charge separation of water. Coincidence or quantum design?

This means that an environment with a lowered quantum yield for any reason will also lead to a lowered cortisol level (adrenal fatigue, nocturia, insulin resistance, etc) = high insulin level = insulin resistance = eventual diabetes due to rising heteroplasmy rates.

For 4 billion years bacteria function has been modified by viruses to conform and adapt to environmental change on planet earth. Viral DNA makes up the large majority of human DNA. Our nuclear DNA takes all its direction from the shape size and speed of quantum tunneling that occurs on a mitochondria's respiratory proteins. Viruses play an integral role in shuffling the deck of our nuclear genes when the environment changes but those changes only occur after they are "sensed" by our mitochondria and by things made of bacteria like you microbiome. People forget that that mtDNA haplotype and the microbiome are predominantly inherited from mom by design. Viruses are dominators of particular niche's on Earth that bacteria frequent. They control local zip codes and as such become the change agent of the environment. This is why researchers in India (Nikhal Dhurander) and the USA have linked viral infections to changes in microbiome diversity and obesity. Obesity is not rocket science, it is more complicated because its core is quantum mechanical and nonlinear because of its link to light. How light effects bacteria, mitochondria, and the eukaryotic cell is not equivalent and this is why there are many modes of fatness.

It is also why when energy production is lost in our mitochondria we also get fat. Losses of energy determine size, shape, and evolution of cells. We often lose sight of these directions. Evolutionary biology has generally failed to consider the contribution of viruses to evolutionary history largely because they do not understand the quantum interplay of how the mitochondria and nuclear genome work. This relationship can best be described as a quantum casino where the dealer only deals with probabilities. The cards are quantum tunneling of protons and electrons through our anisotropic structure. What holds the casino all together is sunlight and EZ water. That maintains the sea within us to gives us the life our choices in the environment we allow to happen. Our mitochondrial respiratory proteins change as our decisions about how we live and where we chose to live changes.

Viral genomes can permanently colonize their hosts genomes, adding data and information to host lineages. Some will cause massive alterations to the host and its effect will be reflected in the ancestors genomes. Most viruses are co-apatative and adaptive to a new environment and now that we are in the era of epi-genomics, we have definitive proof that viral imprints and remnants are present all over the human genome.. they are present in all domains of life and the past evidence of viral colonization is the baseline of life and not an exception. All living things are built to be susceptible to viral elements for a quantum reason. Change without change is the reason behind this effect. This becomes the casino dealer inside of every cell for environmental changes that occur on this pale blue dot.

**_Can blue light shape the microbiome because of melanopsin’s location? Yes, it can._** Do we have any data on this? "Here, in a combination of different ipRGC manipulation (melanopsin) mice models and metagenomic analysis, we show that dLAN can induce obesity, hyperglycemia, as well as a shift in microbial composition, abundance, and oscillations through melanopsin and ipRGC-sympathetic nerve circuit (melanopsin). Furthermore, our data suggest that the zeitgeber information that influences microbial rhythmicity is the photic input from ipRGC but not the circadian rhythm of the host. Together, our results suggest that light could shape the architecture of GUT MICROBIOTA, which provides a novel mechanism for dLAN-induced metabolic disorders." ### Jack Kruse — 2016-06-09T14:15:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-first-post-to-read-here-at-jackkruse-com.17829/post-196223>

Something needs to be said about how this information is released using social media and the internet too. I have realized that when people think that this place or science is becoming dogmatic for them they need a gentle shove away to sail on "other winds". People ultimately do not like what nature is teaching us. I biohack nature and not much else. I am not interested in much else. If you come to the conclusion that this site has its own set of dogma and biases you are correct. It is all about Mother Nature and I make no apologies for it. I disclose it to you right here up front. Many along the journey will develop or sense this problem. The reason is simple. Many will not like the reality of the recipe for optimal. Since the medium and mode of content distribution is over the net and done by social media you may not get to have a true human contact with me or the people who truly get where this whole process is headed.

**_I am interested in people with some amount of skin in the game._**

The site is a fountain for you to drink from but it is not a place where I will let you detract from the messages being delivered. Tough love is practiced when I sense you are drifting away from nature and just not reconnecting with what makes us human and work on this pale blue dot.

The human conundrum is how to protect ourselves from and use technology while trying to get well in our modern environment. We should never presume to know a person based on the one-dimensional window of the internet. A soul can’t be defined by critics, enemies or broken ties with family or friends. Neither can it be explained by threads in groups or blogs that lack facial expressions, tone, inflections, or insight into the person’s personality and intent. Technology gives us a unidimensional awareness of others. Until people “get that”, we will forever be a society that thinks 'Beautiful Mind' was a spy movie and every stranger might really be a true friend on Facebook. Most are not. They possess an ambient awareness of who they believe you to be. And you need to realize this and adjust your own perceptions and uses of technology. This 'quantum conundrum' is a direct reflection, not just of me, but my entire networked team of contacts. It's like a recipe a little bit of all of our personalities in it.

I try to use reason with people in my life so they eventually 'get it.' We cannot see UV light, but we also know it is there because your Vitamin D3 levels are its proxy. So would you lay-out in the sun for 8 hours straight? No, you wouldn't do that. What you should expect as you come here to learn:

The perspective here will be very different than most other "position health leaders". They will seek to capitalize on trends, while I will teach you to capitalize on nature's trends solely. They are 4.6 billion years old and are axiomatic for all living systems.

The best lessons you'll get will come with membership and one on one time with me......discussing your N=1 context. That time will cost you.

If you choose to go it alone I will provide a lot of data here on the forum, my Dr. Jack Kruse FB page. You will have to data mine it for yourself and make connections for yourself.

If you choose to have more direction, look into bronze membership which is low-level action in the Patreon blog at [www.patreon.com/DrJackKruse](http://www.patreon.com/DrJackKruse)

If you want the most hand-holding, become a full member. I personally think Gold or above is wise because of the live Q & A's for educations.

Membership will also lead to live events where we can meet in real life and connect.

Membership will allow you to become a full patient at the Kruse Longevity Farm if you choose this option. Again this won't be for everyone because it is labor intensive and will have significant costs associated with it.

In 2018, my goal was to create an ala carte way for you to CHOOSE how you want to interact. The choice is yours. My focus will be on those who decide to go all in. I have done my part for over ten years providing a massive amount of free content to help anyone who wants to help themselves.

Most humans want to belong and look or be like everyone else. While the flaws in this mindset are obvious, when it comes to leadership, it is vital that you NOT fit in and NOT be like everyone else. This is why I am different and always will be.

Please do not expect me to be like everyone else. You'll be very disappointed.

As you assess and sharpen your own leadership, to obtain your own optimal health, forget about fitting in and leading like everyone else. Instead, tap into your own inner leader and trust yourself enough to lead YOUR way – because fitting in is not the goal of this site. It is to do something quite rare in wellness creation.

IF THIS THOUGHT COMES IN YOUR MIND: "I guess those of us that can't afford this are doomed."
I believe you are mistaken and your mindset is also ill...........

Life is all about choices. If my info isn't worth it then you've said something profound about yourself.

Refuse to be a victim of circumstance. That is key symptom of a low dopamine state and a fundamental lack of connection to NATURE.

It is not a pleasant reality. You need to think about this.

Nature doesn't sit down with you and start a discussion.........you need to remember how you were made to exist within her framework and if you do not, a disease is a result! Simple. Biochemists/nutritionists stick with what they know even though it cannot explain the energy flux in a cell. **_Inferior talent can only be graceful when it's carrying inferior ideas._**

You have an obligation to yourself only. You can only change yourself. You can’t let other people tell you who you are. You have to decide that for yourself. Just when the caterpillar thought her world was ending, she turned into a butterfly.........I see the metamorphosis in people before they do because of how I think about them and about things. Health improvements always begin with
I, not we.

### Jack Kruse — 2018-07-07T15:14:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/your-first-post-to-read-here-at-jackkruse-com.17829/post-245210>

Price is what you pay; value is what you get.
People who can't afford or refuse to pay your items are just frustrated that they can't have what they want.

I start by addressing that frustration and explain that I understand and know how it feels.

Next, I explain how I create my content and how long it took me to acquire all this knowledge and how much it cost me in money, time, and blue light hazard to make sense of how medicine should be delivered.

When you break it down for them, it starts to make more sense to the frustrated. If they do not get it, I just cut them from my network and put them out of their misery. If they cannot read the cutting edge info cutting them from my social media presence and info eases their pain because they become unaware of what they are missing.

Instead of apologizing, initially I empathize with them, to see if they are capable of change (dopamine) but I will not bend on my own value. If you want “optimal” you will pay for my time, because that is what I research constantly. You must have skin in the game. Try to get this info anywhere else on the net. Good luck with that. When you are voted off the tribe you will be forced to do it on your own. You will still be able to come back and do it on your own, but you'll learn how hard it was to connect these dots by feeling the pain yourself. For some, that road is the one they SHOULD travel because of their mindset. That is how you’ll begin to appreciate how hard it is to gain this wisdom. You will see how hard it was for me to do. I will teach you why you should appreciate how easy I make it for you these days.

Because people just want to be heard and understood, I finish by thanking them for their email trying to make me feel bad………because their MO will never work on me. I know how hard I worked to get this wisdom. If I am not worth a small fee then I make them live a life without me and my info.
You must weed out all the prospects that don't and won't appreciate you and your contribution to the world. Energy vampires are always looking for the free ride.

It helps sustain what you do and allows you to give so much more.

When you price correctly, you set the foundation of growth for your company.

When you get complaints about your pricing and you know you've done your homework, trust your gut and stick to your guns and you decide…….challenge them or cut them. I give most a chance to change their tact…….but when they don’t I act rapidly.

No one ever regretted buying the best advice of their life. IF you don’t value my advice, I will make you live a life without my participation permanently. That is their opportunity cost.

The more time I spend answering objections on price, the less time I have to focus on my clients--people who have the money to spend on quality service designed to save them time and build their health and extend their life.

I became a lot happier and more successful after I learned how to let go of bad clients and court good ones who valued me, my time, and my information. People who try to haggle on price either just don't have the disposable income to buy your products, or they are pills who know the price of everything and the value of nothing about you. You cannot tolerate their mediocrity.

What I provide is custom-made “health work” just for that client’s N=1. It is not made to be mass-distributed and sold to everyone like a can of soda. It is made specifically just for the client and tailored to the client's mitochondria and environment. Barely anybody else is going to come by and go "wow advice that is not-my-character-at-all nor is it a prescription for a life of an actor in life I want to live like, I want to love my existence forever and hang on to it forever and generalize it to all my family and friends I love."
Anything custom-made or personalized will always be more expensive service. You want custom tailored pants? You gotta pay more for that service and that look. You want your name printed on a can of soda? You have to pay WAY more for that because the person/company is very much going out of their way to make it just for you. If you want to win in the environment you’ve chosen you better to have the right advice to make it happen……..and it won’t come cheap. Price is what you pay; value is what you get. The most important distinction between price and value is the fact that price is arbitrary and value is fundamental.
Finding differences between price and value is by far the most effective investment strategy in your healthy life. Today, going to the doctor is no guarantee of good value on return on equity and today’s disease epidemics are proof of this idea. Not recognizing differences between price and value is also what causes many investors to lose their shirts in life, as services are just as often overpriced as they are underpriced for the value they truly supply to the client. Many are unaware of how this occurs.
So how do you find services that are on sale for less than their true value? The answer is to evaluate them using a set of standards that look beyond the service’s current price tag. I call these standards the four M’s, and they stand for Meaning, Moat, Management and Margin of Safety.
The first step is to make sure the services you invest in has meaning to you and your life. If it does, you'll understand it better, be more likely to research it fully and be more passionate about investing in its concepts. The second step is to choose a service that has a moat. This means that there is something inherently unique about the service that makes it difficult to get the information anywhere else cheaper. The third step is to look at the company's management. Can you trust the fidelity of the advice? If the service you are considering investing in meets all of these qualifications, calculate their margin of safety their services provide you. If the advice you pay for can lead to a huge change in healthspan the margin of safety is large. If what the doctor sells you is an Rx with little hope of helping your situation then you know you are being wallet biopsied. If the functional medicine doctor order 3K worth of labs and none of them have anything to do with your blue light choices you’ve lost a ton of cash and trust. The choice of price to value is not hard to make when the information is unique. When it is a common message it should not cost a lot at all. You get what you pay for in healthcare. The choice is the consumers to make. Who I help is now linked to how they value me. It improves my margin of safety has to deal with a paradigm designed to shut me up. Humans who remain ignorant are the targets of the paradigm. The human conundrum is that often our habitual actions are driven by emotion instead of intellect, especially when it comes to price and value.

I won't let the victims of life grab my fruit. My dopamine networks matters to me deeply.

# Thread: CT different in summer / winter?

### PeVe — 2016-04-17T17:49:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-different-in-summer-winter.17830/post-192928>

Tried to find something with the search function but it didn’t really come up with something to answer my question.

As getting interested in CT in spring, I’m asking myself for considerations of starting CT this time. I’ve read people also do CT throughout the year – Anja Leitz (Better body, better brain) also describes that she uses it throughout the year. Even though she also emphasizes starting it in autumn.

To my understanding: As a starting point a Omega6:Omega3 ratio of 10:1 should be desirable // brain freeze test with ice-cube water. That’s something I fathom.

But, as we also consider circadian and annual rhythms, how does CT fit in here? Here in Germany, we have distinct changes between summer and winter, so could CT during summer lead to … let’s say … a internal signaling misconception? Further I think like during Ice Ages or for Eskimos sun time varied over the year but outside temperatures didn’t vary too much in terms of coldness - does super-cold to cold make a difference? But with a distinct ‘hotness’ in summer, could we expect different physiological responses that could interfere with cold-adaptation processes? Thinking of less DHA in summer as foodsource when nature provided plants and fruits which works well with increased sun-exposure? So if we increase DHA during summer to have a good o6\_o3 ratio but also have sunlight and (maybe) an increased carb intake could that mess up with our internal clocks? Or have an effect? Or are the underlying mechanisms so deep in our genealogy that summer/winter changes do not affect the underlying mechanisms?

Maybe that’s already too much thinking – and I should just do the CT (  )… But what do you think or did you experience? Is there a difference with CT during summer and winter?

### nicld — 2016-04-17T18:57:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-different-in-summer-winter.17830/post-192932>

Then found doing CT then rewarming in the sun felt AMAZING. Made it much easier for me to continue in the winter and moving into deep CT with snow from outside in the tub.

Hi all,

Came across the work of Dr. Kruse a few months ago, and it has been fascinating reading ever since, thanks for all the pioneering work.

### caroline — 2016-04-18T04:19:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-london.17831/post-192960>

### JoshuaLP0 — 2016-04-18T13:32:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-london.17831/post-192967>

Dr. Kruse, I saw your name on the speaker list...I'll keep my eyes peeled for when your confirmed.

Concussion posts are great, I've been working through the protocols.

I actually started a course of low level light therapy over the past month with the Bioflex laser. I've had 3 sessions in total, all fairly short in duration/low intensity - the first two to the frontal lobes and the third one to the brain stem region.

My response to the first was very in line with a typical hormetic curve; immediate boost for 4 hours or so, a 24 hour dip (a Serotonin hangover would be my best description) followed by a leveling out. The latter two treatments haven't followed such an obvious trajectory, triggering a return of headaches (vasodilatory in nature), fatigue, irritability and 'brain fog'. My thinking is that this is an essentially extended hormetic response, with the side effects being triggered by increased nitric oxide (and potential associated inflammation). My thinking is I should let nature takes its course during this re-adjustment period before engaging in further LLLT but potentially use a supplement like PQQ in the meantime. Any thoughts would be appreciated.

\-----

Penny, apologies, I've now adjusted the picture so you can see my face! I work for a software company so lots of EMF exposure on a daily basis. I've been concentrating on drinking non-fluoridated water and want to start incorporate grounding soon (although conscious some of the products can amplify EMF in your environment) so just researching the best way to set this up. Sun is definitely hard to come by in London but am being much more conscious about grabbing exposure when I can, and am looking into getting away somewhere soon.

# Thread: Journey post concussion
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journey-post-concussion.17833/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JoshuaLP0 — 2016-04-18T14:09:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journey-post-concussion.17833/post-192969>

**Goals**
In August 2016 I sustained a concussion (no loss of consciousness) after banging the top of my head in a van. Symptoms of extreme fatigue, dizziness, headaches and brain fog lasted approximately 2 weeks.

Around 2 weeks post, just as symptoms where beginning to clear my girlfriend at the time head butted me in the face accidentally (I hope). This re-triggered the symptoms. 2 weeks after this, just when symptoms were beginning to clear I woke up in the night by banging my head against the wall - since then the symptoms have persisted (although in different permutations). In addition to the above, I have developed anxiety around physical contact (especially with my head) through fear of another concussion.

My goals are to get back to where I was before the concussion (and hopefully even better!).

**Background**
Age -27
Single
154 lbs
6'3 tall

**Family**
Parents in mid 60s with no known health issues
Grandparents - granddad on my fathers side had dementia/grandma on same side passed away at 95 (no serious health issues)
Grandparents on my mothers side still both alive in mid 80s with reasonable health
Sister recently diagnosed with Chohns (likely to have genetic vulnerability due to Ashkenazi background)

**Personal health before concussion**
General health before the concussion was generally ok (although energy wasn't always great, and prone to get headaches). I suffered from mild IBS in my teens through to my early 20s which led to a number of issues with energy/brain fog in my early-mid 20s. Diet changes (no gluten,/dairy) and acupuncture helped alleviate many of the issues.

Previously fractured my eye socket (and concussion) from football injury but with no long lasting effects at the time.

Generally would drink alcohol fairly liberally in social settings before the concussion (but mainly confined to weekends).

**Treatments post concussion**
\-Acupuncture (effective in short term but not necessarily long term)
\-Osteopath (same as above)
\-Cognitive Behaviour Therapy (Effective for anxiety related issues)
\-Floatation tanks (useful for calming me down during anxious periods but not seemingly cumulative in benefit)
Low level light therapy (I started this treatment most recently where I would say I was back to about 70% but was still finding myself getting fatigue headaches after periods of mental exertion). Therefore decided to go ahead with the LLLT:

I've had 3 sessions in total, all fairly short in duration/low intensity - the first two to the frontal lobes and the third one to the brain stem region.

My response to the first was very in line with a typical hormetic curve; immediate boost for 4 hours or so, a 24 hour dip (a Serotonin hangover would be my best description) followed by a leveling out. The latter two treatments haven't followed such an obvious trajectory, triggering a return of headaches (vasodilatory in nature), fatigue, irritability and 'brain fog'. My thinking is that this is an essentially extended hormetic response, with the side effects being triggered by increased nitric oxide (and potential associated inflammation). Symptoms are currently worse than when I started the treatment.

**Supplements:**
Currently:
\-Curcumin (for inflammation)
\-CoQ10 and L-Theanine (for mitochondria and anxiety)
\-Magnesium Taurate (considering switching to Magnesium L Theorenate)
\-Vitamin D3 (400ui)
\-B complex
\-Krill Oil
\-Cordecyps/Reishi (occasional)
\-Chlorella (occasional)

**Diet:**
Generally I am on a more conventional paleo diet but am yet to fully transition to a Leptin reset diet. Try to eat 3 portions of seafood per week + bone brothers + meat offals

**Water:**
Recently made the transition to consciously only drinking un-fluoridated water

**Sun:**
Living in London very poor exposure to natural sunlight (previous Vitamin D tests were deficient, but normalised after supplementation)

**Work/Home Environment:**
Have lived in London my whole life (except 3 years in Manchester)
Work in technology so high exposure to EMF/Blue Light (use flux on an all day basis)
Live in apartment on 5th floor

**Grounding:**
Not yet experimented with it yet

**Sleep**
Generally have always slept well, usually more than 8 hours per night - although was never very disciplined about consistent times (circadian rhythms). Immediately post concussion sleep was between 12-14 hours. This has dropped to 9-10 hours but not waking up feeling particularly refreshed.

### Just Breathe — 2016-04-18T22:41:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journey-post-concussion.17833/post-192988>

Welcome!
It's ok to be deeply concerned about a concussion. You can heal from it! Have faith!

The best tip I have for you is to get sunlight in your eyes in the morning, and in the evening. Catch the sunrise and the sunset every day. Completely avoid bluelight and reading. The faster you do these as strictly as possible, the faster you can heal up! My concussion took 6 weeks to turn around and it was an accidental head-butt from someone else. You can do it!

### Dasha — 2018-03-07T18:41:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journey-post-concussion.17833/post-236590>

Josh - read your symptoms and they are extremely similar to mine. (I fell in October and have been having fatigue headaches just on the side of where I fell since then).
Just wanted to check to how you are doing / if anything has helped alleviate your symptoms since 2016.

Thanks in advance.

Cheers

### JanSz — 2018-03-07T18:53:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journey-post-concussion.17833/post-236593>

@JoshuaLPO
Banging messed up your pituitary gland.
It was not that great to begin with.

Get pituitary MRI.
If MRI is clear, you may have to find Endocrinologist that will test and adjust all your pituitary hormones (that are out of place).
(Good luck finding that endo).

If MRI have found something,
you need twice the luck.

//////////////
It newer hurts to spend lots of time on sunny beach.

.....

### Dasha — 2018-03-09T16:06:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journey-post-concussion.17833/post-236763>

@[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) -- what do you mean to adjust pituitary hormones? Is there something specific that can be taken to readjust them back to normal?
Why does a concussion cause issues with pit gland?

(I had a concussion in Oct 2017 and had 95% of the same symptoms as Josh. I currently still have headaches and fatigue. Headaches my osteopath is telling me because I have TMJ, which I had before, but got worse after the fall.)

### JanSz — 2018-03-09T16:46:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journey-post-concussion.17833/post-236768>

> [Dasha said:](/goto/post?id=236763)
>
> @[JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/) -- what do you mean to adjust pituitary hormones? Is there something specific that can be taken to readjust them back to normal?
> Why does a concussion cause issues with pit gland?
>
> (I had a concussion in Oct 2017 and had 95% of the same symptoms as Josh. I currently still have headaches and fatigue. Headaches my osteopath is telling me because I have TMJ, which I had before, but got worse after the fall.)

Pituitary gland is responsible for majority of body functions.
Usually not all pituitary hormones are messed up due to concussion.
You have to first do the tests and see where you stand.
Problems with pituitary are not always due to concussion.
You can always hope that your pituitary gets back to normal,
but,
in the mean time you have to do your living.
Monitoring and adjusting is the only way I know about.
Monitoring also allows you to find part of the system that do not need any interventions.

Hormones of pituitary
[http://www.biology-pages.info/P/Pituitary.html](http://www.biology-pages.info/P/Pituitary.html)

# Thread: Resetting the Eye Clock
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resetting-the-eye-clock.17834/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael Polito — 2016-04-18T18:24:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resetting-the-eye-clock.17834/post-192976>

How long does it take (days, weeks) before i can get back on track (Circadian) . My sleep sucks, I just started getting out in the sun between 8-9 am (sometimes longer) a few days ago. Eating seafood everyday. Sometimes grass fed beef, and pastured chicken, Dark Leafy Greens, dont eat starch and sugar carbs. Im in great fitness, but cant seem to sleep through the night. I get to bed by 11pm, but up between 2:30 and 4am and toss and turn for 2 hours. I also shut off the lights when it gets dark outside, and use yellow/amber classes if watching tv. Will this take some time before my body kicks in and gets back on track? Thank you for any help.

### Joe Gavin — 2016-04-18T20:24:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resetting-the-eye-clock.17834/post-192978>

It will depend on how long you lived your life in an asynchronous manner, how strict you are with minimizing ambient light at night, and all the other shit going in your life. Play around with it. Try to go to bed earlier and wake up earlier. Eat breakfast while the sun is out (the light will entrain your central clock, and the food will entrain your peripheral clocks--yes, there clock genes everywhere!). Perhaps cut back on working out, and keep it to daylight hours as well (working out at night can mess you up). And definitely do NOT eat after dark; that's when your body is supposed to be resting and regenerating--those programs can't work if you are still digesting food. Unplug your wifi at night and shut off the electricity to your bedroom at a minimum. Then get your barefeet on the ground in the morning, and the AM sunlight in your eyes. Try all that and see how you feel. Doesn't cost a thing except thinking it through a bit.

### Michael Polito — 2016-04-18T20:40:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resetting-the-eye-clock.17834/post-192980>

Good stuff here Joe. I do eat late at night, sometimes because of my schedule. I will try eating before i go to teach my classes.. I love my coffee, but have eliminated that after 12pm. I'm getting it now. I'm a bare footer, so that is cool. Thanks so much Joe.

### Joe Gavin — 2016-04-18T20:49:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resetting-the-eye-clock.17834/post-192982>

and where your skypers/blueblockers to any evening event.

### Mover — 2016-04-18T21:05:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resetting-the-eye-clock.17834/post-192984>

Yes. Just starting doing that 3 days ago. Found a real nice pair of yellow tinted lenses. Shooting glasses color. Round frames. Very cool looking. Also have my blue blocker Amber/orange.

### Michael Polito — 2016-04-19T16:12:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resetting-the-eye-clock.17834/post-193018>

Hey Joe, took your suggestion and did not eat in the evening. Had my last meal at 6pm. Cup a herb tea at night. Kept my yellow lens glasses on while watching the game. Lights off. I slept right through the night till 6:30 am. Perfect ! Thanks man ! I think that was my major problem. Eating too late when the body is supposed to be resting.

### Joe Gavin — 2016-04-21T17:16:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/resetting-the-eye-clock.17834/post-193163>

> [Michael Polito said:](/goto/post?id=193018)

> Hey Joe, took your suggestion and did not eat in the evening. Had my last meal at 6pm. Cup a herb tea at night. Kept my yellow lens glasses on while watching the game. Lights off. I slept right through the night till 6:30 am. Perfect ! Thanks man ! I think that was my major problem. Eating too late when the body is supposed to be resting.

# Thread: CT, Inflammation and Leptin Sensitivity
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-inflammation-and-leptin-sensitivity.17835/>

### Hunger4More — 2016-04-19T01:10:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-inflammation-and-leptin-sensitivity.17835/post-192997>

Hi Guys,

Quick question:

My health background involves a lot of inflammation related issues - excessive joint wear & tear, gut problems, and overall bloating/swelling.

I've been on the Leptin Reset for a little over 3 months now and have been implementing CT for some time as well.

CT in general has been great. With these two strategies, joint and digestive issues are getting much better.

However, my question is this: I've noticed that CT significantly lowers my inflammation -- in fact, the visual difference is quite drastic -- I can go from bloated and puffy, to see six pack abs after my session. However, this effect is only transient - after a few hours, I go back to being bloated and puffy.

Does this effect mean that I am not Leptin Sensitive yet? Should I keep working on this? Do I need to add more and more CT? Or is there possibly something else I'm missing? I'll mention as well that I eat seafood 6-7x per week, have eliminated blue light and have been getting morning sun 5-6x per week.

I'm also going to watch the deep CT webinar -- my apologies if this has been answered in there.

Thanks!

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-19T03:55:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-inflammation-and-leptin-sensitivity.17835/post-193000>

Sounds like you have a problem holding the charge........so that means if you keep going things should improve. I would also say try to get further south or get higher altitude while grounding to increase your quantum yield.

### Michael Polito — 2016-04-19T17:05:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-inflammation-and-leptin-sensitivity.17835/post-193025>

Regarding CT. I have been doing this since the Fall of 2015. As you can see my photo of me in the ice cold swimming hole. Will as little as taking a 5 or 10 min cold shower everyday alone have a beneficial effect.? It makes me feel great. I also follow Wim Hof. He inspired me greatly as well as Dr. Jack.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-19T19:08:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-inflammation-and-leptin-sensitivity.17835/post-193034>

As good no.......but CT always has an effect because it affects mitochondrial respiratory protein geometry

# Thread: Never Be Sick Again
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/never-be-sick-again.17838/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Raiken3712 — 2016-04-20T08:13:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/never-be-sick-again.17838/post-193054>

[http://www.amazon.com/Never-Be-Sick...showViewpoints=0&sortBy=recent#R1IF6QRC64QK9T](http://www.amazon.com/Never-Be-Sick-Again-Health/product-reviews/1558749543/ref=cm_cr_dp_synop?ie=UTF8&showViewpoints=0&sortBy=recent#R1IF6QRC64QK9T)

Anyone read it? I'm curious of opinions. Some of what he says reminded me of Jack. He even mentioned Quantum Physics in there somewhere lol.

There isn't a ton of EMF or water and that kinda thing, but the concept sounded very interesting and logical to me.

There is only one disease cellular dysfunction and two causes deficiency(not enough nutrients), and toxicity.

Than there are six pathways which are Nutrition, Toxin, Mental, Genetic, and Medical.

# Thread: Daily routine
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daily-routine.17839/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 5
