### pascale ahmad — 2016-04-20T16:32:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daily-routine.17839/post-193063>

I have now settled into my daily routine. Sun gazing and grounding from 7.45 to 8.15, while eating my 50 gr protein. Another half an hour of grounding around 5.30/6pm, dinner at 6pm. I don't really feel hungry at lunch time, I usually just have a glass of home made buffalo raw milk kefir and some fresh coconut. I have fish at least once a day (canned tuna or sardines). I love the fact that don't feel like snacking all day the way I used to. I've always been a good sleeper but I sleep ever better now, I think I dream more as well. I feel really sleepy in the evening and have trouble keeping my eyes open past 9.30! I feel great in the morning too, although I was a always a morning person. I have made progress on the EMF front, the husband has agreed to switch off the wifi by 9pm, still watches TV though but I'm going with one battle at a time. Can't wait to receive my blue blocker glasses, I have ordered a pair for the whole family, I'll probably will be the only one wearing them but I shall persevere and lead by example!!!!

### Inger — 2016-04-20T16:55:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daily-routine.17839/post-193072>

Nice    step by step

### Nathan — 2016-04-29T17:31:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daily-routine.17839/post-193821>

Excellent! I've been grounding/sun gazing in the morning for about 3 weeks now and it's amazing how my cravings have completely disappeared.

Keep up the great work!

### pascale ahmad — 2016-04-29T17:50:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/daily-routine.17839/post-193825>

Yes I find that the most amazing thing too. Afternoons use to be torture for me, cravings would kick in about 1.1/2 to 2 hours after lunch, usually for something sweet and coffee. Every morning I would decide that I would be strong and would not succumb and most days I would fail and them I would beat myself up for been so weak. AND NOW I DONT EVEN THINK ABOUT FOOD AT ALL!!!!

# Thread: Here to Keep Hope
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-to-keep-hope.17840/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gerard C. — 2016-04-20T20:08:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-to-keep-hope.17840/post-193089>

Hey Guys,

So happy to become a part of this health community. So much amazing information on this site all of which I am navigating through day by day. It's a lot to digest so I'm taking it bit by bit. Thank You Dr. Kruse for bringing your expertise to people who would otherwise never know it existed.

A little about me and what I'd like to achieve:

I've always been very healthy and very active. Originally from Pennsylvania and now living in New York City, I find myself, at the age of 28, at a bit of a crossroads of health. I know I can't keep breaking my body down.

The past few years I've seen my health declining. This undoubtedly is due to the extremely stressful and judgemental nature of my work, the high-pace life in the city, and the environment itself. Also, bad diet for a few years. Tons of Splenda, Protein Bars, etc.

My biggest gripes have been severe brain fog, daytime fatigue, chronically sleeping til noon (despite not wanting to), and very low emotional states (meaning feeling nothing at all).

Have seen them all: 2 Gastro's, 2 Endo's, Neurologist, 2 Urologists, and 2 Naturopaths (and I'm sure a few more I've forgotten). Most of them have responded with "a little bit of this, a little bit of that" type approach. No one has pointed to a Central Cause as to why this is happening.

The fist few years in NYC were great. Now, I'm having some trouble enjoying everything due to issues I've been having.

My goal is to be energized and clear headed. I like to keep that simple. To the mental and emotional state of a few years ago.

Looking forward to sharing and learning here!

GC

### fitness@home — 2016-04-20T20:37:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-to-keep-hope.17840/post-193092>

GC,

Are you willing to make some changes in your life? Some big - some smaller and easier to implement. Start a journal here for feedback.

You can turn that bus around and feel better (heal). Read the blogs and the threads here on the forum. The information shared can sometimes be daunting to understand, but I've found when I reread a blog a few months later things become clearer and I have a better understanding.

Welcome!

p.s. can you put up a human picture please?

Hey fitness@home! Thanks for responding. I will keep my journal here. My next entry will be some lab results from previous testing to help people get a better idea of the history.

I am willing to make any and all changes. Unfortunately no one thus far has really been able to tell me which direction to go in!

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-20T22:59:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-to-keep-hope.17840/post-193111>

Well the first key is get a plan to get out of the city as much as possible and then make a plan to move eventually......day trips to CT and NE where its rural or hit Central Park and ground on a rock next to Strawberry fields......get the sun without the building shade. There are some great sunny boulders close to the Alice and Wonderland statue.

### caroline — 2016-04-21T03:14:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-to-keep-hope.17840/post-193127>

Welcome Gerard.....

What water are you drinking?
What are you eating? seafood???

nnEMF in your home? in your work place? what do you do?

Do you have blue blockers?

Get all electronics out of your bedroom....

Do you have Dr. K.'s book? The Epi-Paleo RX ....it is the first couple of years the blog

Do you listen to Jack's podcasts? they are a great way to get fast and easier info to get you up to speed.

If you can manage it ....become a "Gold" member and you will have access to all the webinars and the live Q&A every month where Dr. K. answers all questions

### Gerard C. — 2016-04-27T06:44:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/here-to-keep-hope.17840/post-193667>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=193111)
>
> Well the first key is get a plan to get out of the city as much as possible and then make a plan to move eventually......day trips to CT and NE where its rural or hit Central Park and ground on a rock next to Strawberry fields......get the sun without the building shade. There are some great sunny boulders close to the Alice and Wonderland statue.\[/quote
>
> Dr. Kruse and All Other Readers.
>
> I'm going to give you the abridged story here and some labs to lead me in the right direction. Western Med Doctors along with "Alternative" Doctors have all been ambiguous as far as nailing down what's going on. As I've read more and more, I'm not at all surprised. Bare in mind, I'm a 28 year old, former Athlete, currently working in the Entertainment Business.
>
> As for syptoms (in order): Low T, Low Thyroid (Free and Total, mostly Total), Low Total Cholesterol (Most doctors applauded me for this one!), Heartburn, Gas problems, Not getting tired until about 3 or 4am despite laying down to sleep at midnight, tired all day, then WIDE AWAKE at 11pm, weight gain over past year (up 15 lbs). I also have the strangest sensation all day where when I try to speak, I get the feeling like I have to yawn, yet I CAN NEVER COMPLETE A DAMN YAWN! And the feeling remains all day long. My shoulders and quads are both quick to burn out during even light exercise. And finally, the biggest one of all, **BRAIN FOG**. Not like, "I forget my keys" brainfog, but, I feel uncomfortable having a conversation with somebody because I don't feel like I'm "there" at all. Very murky head feeling. Throbbing quite a bit, but not necessarily a headache. Hard to describe.
>
> Treatments Thus Far (Before doing research): Was on Clomid briefly (before the brain fog), worked sort of, but decided to stop due to what I was told was drug dependency. After conventional medicine doctors failed to find an answer, I went to a Naturopath who put me on an anti-candida protocol along with along supps (Tyrosine), Herbs for Adrenals, Natural Thyroid Supplement-- She deduced this treatment based upon a Genova Diagnostics Triad Profile. In all honesty, I don't feel like anything was being "wiped out" of my gut. Did that treatment for 8 weeks now I'm onto the "healing" part of program which included heavy dosing of Glutamine, Colostrum, Licorice and HCL for the heartburn. This seems to be helping more than phase 1.
>
> Most Recent Hormone Panels as Follows (Through Quest):
>
> TSH 1.16
> Free T3: 2.5
> Free T4: 1.0
> Total T3: 55
> Total T4: 4.5
> Cholesterol Total: 117
> Testosterone Free: 67
> Testosterone Total: 567 (highest value in 2 years, old value at 211)
>
> _Also AM Fasting Cortisol (10am) was at 13.7 and Leptin at 0.6._
>
> All of these are out of range (low) or borderline out of range (low).
>
> In the interim, and do to the fact that ABSOLUTELY NOTHING seemed to be working. I started down the mold road. Researching Shoemaker and his protocols. Checking my apartment for Mold. Started to speak to someone who'd been through what I'd been through. So i started on Cholestyramine for 3 weeks and seemed to be improving visually at least. My vision was severely impaired for awhile. "Milky" is the best way to describe it. Ended up spending out of pocket money on testing because I didn't have insurance at the time.
>
> Here are my Shoemaker Labs as follows:
>
> VEGF 1
> C3a 36
> C4a 4475
> MMP9 557
> MSH 14
> TGFBeta1 9640
> Marcons - Negative
> Vasopressin <1
> Osmolality 288
> Lipase 29
> ACTH 31
> VIP <50
>
> I have finished the Cholestyramine because I passed a VCS Test. However, after checking my apartment for mold I found the specimen to be lower than what Shoemaker recommends for mold. Which means my apartment passed the test! I do not work in the same building all the time so it could not be my work either. I'm left somewhat baffled by it all but continue to do treatment: high dosing on fish/fish oil, ozone therapy (breathing through olive oil, ear insufflation, and IV insufflation). Seems to have somewhat of an effect.
>
> A few other notes of full disclosure: For years before all this I was eating protein bars constantly, Monster Energy Drinks, running to exhaustion daily, and stressing the shit out of myself with the demands of my business. I needed to look "thin" so I did whatever it took. During this phase, I was drinking a lot (on an empty stomach) and eating my biggest meal right before bed and ALMOST NEVER, EVER eating breakfast.
>
> **I can provide any other information to paint the rest of this picture if needed.** Or upgrade my account to better guide me. Whatever it takes. I need to save my life and my career. Now is the time to do it.
>
> My main question is: based on all this. What the hell is going ON? KNOWING is what I need right now, once I know, I will do whatever it takes to keep going in the right direction.
>
> Thanks @[Inger](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1057/) and @[caroline](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/888/)

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-21T03:52:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-optics-of-wine.17842/post-193129>

The NAD+ connection (UV light and ketosis) Ketosis needs DHA and WATER to work optimally when we are connected to the local environments solar spectrum and the local magnetic flux through grounding via the inverse Hall spin effect: In 2000, Lenny Guarente's lab reported that NAD+ fuels the activity of sirtuins, including SIRT1—the more NAD+ there is in cells, the more SIRT1 does beneficial things. NAD+ is linked to pseudohypoxia. When oxygen is low ECT is slow and th respirator proteins are further apart and melatonin cycling is low at our surfaces. NAD+/ketosis/IFing are a few those things is to induce formation of new mitochondria from broken down ones. NAD+ can also activate another sirtuin, SIRT3, which is thought to keep mitochondria running smoothly. Caveat: These are all rodent studies........so go slow. I think do it nature's way sans supplements is the key!!!!

Why? humans have massive mitochondrial capacity and rodents don't and they are nocturnal and we are not. They rely heavier on the inverse Hall effect and we rely on the solar spectrum to drive our mammalian version of our battery : SPECIES CONTEXT.

IMPLICATION: if you deliver too much oxygen to a bad engine will it help or make more free radicals that may worsen the issue? It is how oxygen is handled in the mitochondria. You only need a ton of oxygen to match ECT speeds.......which is why ketosis. first makes more sense (Why my book is written as it is)..........but even as ECT speed is increased is no guarantee that a bad mitochondria can use it when % heteroplasmy is amplified (Jimmy Moore)

Why wine's water is key:
The dielectric constant measures how effectively water shields the negative and positive charges from one another and hence reduces the force between them. Generally electric charge and magnetic flux are controlled by a dielectric. Water, is a major constituent of living systems, like us and grapes, is an excellent dielectric because of its large effective dipole moment. The Earth has its own dipole moment too, because of its magnetic field and flux. All living things use the inverse Hall effect to turn magnetic flux to a DC electric current. People forget animals and planets both use the DC electric current to REGENERATE. This occurs in us (not plants) because of how DHA and water interface in a cell membrane using magnetic flux via the inverse Hall effect. It becomes even more critical in the inner mitochondrial membrane where no DHA exists but where we have metal ions that react to magnetic field changes to affect the respiratory proteins geometry. (controls % heteroplasmy)

More complex mammals like humans have shrunk hibernation into some of our nightly sleep cycles. Mammals are capable of transforming magnetic flux into a DC electric current using the tensegrity system of piezoelectric semiconductors using the inverse spin Hall effect. People have forgotten that Robert O. Becker wrote about the Hall effect in bone conduction in the 1960's.The inverse Hall effect could undo the Hall effect in many tissues. This likely explains space osteoporosis and osteoporosis on the surface of Earth that is now microwaved 24/7. Read pages 100-117 in Becker's The Electric Body and you see just what technology is doing to us by affect in how humans use the Hall effect in the Tensegrity system.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-21T03:53:02+0300

People are enamored with certainty, they only see what they believe and do not believe what they observe. nnEMF is everywhere even though you cannot see it your mitochondria do sense it. This is akin to your radio's ability in your car. You cannot see the waves that it sense's but you sure can hear the results of that collision. nnEMF colliding with your respiratory proteins is a crash your brain's mitochondria should always avoid.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-21T03:56:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-optics-of-wine.17842/post-193131>

Human bodies are cooler than birds bodies, and that small difference means their molecules need different sizes and shapes to operate efficiently. Mice also have different temperature ranges because they are nocturnal but biochemists and obesity researcher still think using rodents is wise to understand human biology. My call: BULLSHIT.

What do humans and birds have in common? Both of their ancestors where the only two animals to survive the KT event 65 million years ago. WHY? Both had mitochondrial capacity for different reasons.........one to fly the other to hibernate. Both allow them to disconnect from the sun and Earth for long period of time. When an asteroid hits the planet and the sun is buried by particulate matter you need that capacity to live in a low quantum world. Where did you get food to survive? From the sea........where volcanic vents pumped energy to the ocean to make the marine seafood chain bountiful while it was still cold.............Any see a trend yet that I wrote about in my book? When you know better, you simply crush life. Stop believing bullshit being fed to you...........Question it all.

Mitochondria oscillate at 100hZ = phonons = magnetic flux controls phonons = mitochondria = magnetic flux = inverse Hall spin effect magnetism = DC electric current

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17843/post-193151>

Small success, but just had to share.
My son has old man syndrome - we can't keep him awake past 7:30 and he is up at 4:30am-always.
We just started night light blocking and some CT (disguised as dares ) about 6 weeks ago for my teenage son.
He half-assed used the glasses for the first 1.5 weeks...then he started putting them on...then he started asking that I get them for him closer to sun-down.
The last 2 days I have had to wake him up for school...this is not something that happens...

I am sincerely grateful for this group, Dr Kruse and this site.

### Scompy — 2016-04-23T02:57:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/baby-steps.17843/post-193283>

This time last year, my 7-year old was playing video games at night and was getting allergies, colds and flus like no tomorrow. Very electrosensitive last year. Nearly all of his conditions are totally gone now. The major change is cycling with the sun and wearing blue-blockers, but also that he's not playing on game consoles and I've mitigated and hacked the entire home for nnEMF, and I've been making a point to pump a lot of water into him every day. It's night and day. You can read my timeline over at my journal to see what I did.

# Thread: Seeing the Light!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeing-the-light.17845/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 49
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Purple Haze — 2016-04-22T17:27:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeing-the-light.17845/post-193239>

I’m new to this site…..but I’m beginning to see the light!

I’ve been paleo for a while with some success, but still struggle with insomnia, restless leg syndrome and adrenal fatigue for quite some time.
I thought I was doing everything right over the past decade, eating right, exercising but still always hitting walls and plateaus and not sleeping well.

I’ve been listening to a lot of interviews with Jack on several podcasts and reading as much as I can retain on this site.
Things are really starting to make sense!

Looking back I see what I did wrong and when my issues started to unfold.
Many years ago I used to work outside but was electrocuted with 14,000 volts in a work accident. After that I decided to pursue what I thought was a safer job sitting in an office for 8 hours a day in front of to large computer monitors under crap lighting.

Ironically, I think the office job was more detrimental to my health than the 14,000 volts was…or maybe there’s a connected effect there. Maybe Jack would know what high voltage electric shocked would do to someone's system.

When I started working in an office environment, I stopped going outside as much, but when I did I would wear a hat and sunglasses and slop on the sunscreen…because that’s what modern main stream medicine told us to do. Looking back, this is when my insomnia and restless leg syndrome started…and became progressively worse.

I went paleo, low carb the past few years but when I tried intermittent fasting it revealed I had reactive hypoglycaemia/ adrenal fatigue. I had to go back to some carbs in order to stabilize.
I’m a bit of a carb addict, but am sensitive to carbs as well, so I have to be careful.
Moving to eating a seafood for breakfast this past week has really helped with my carb cravings!
I’m getting out in the sun as much as I can now, no glasses or hat, no sunscreen! Luckily it’s been sunny this week on the 'wet' coast. Starting to drink a lot of mineral-spring water too.

I’ll continue this protocol while looking into getting some purple and/or red lights for my office…cuz I still need to work in one.
thanks for listening.
: )

### Purple Haze — 2016-04-22T19:54:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeing-the-light.17845/post-193242>

Does anyone have a recommendation for the kind of light to use while working on a computer all day (Black/purple light, red light beside the computer)?
thanks

### Rubicon — 2016-04-22T20:27:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeing-the-light.17845/post-193246>

> [Purple Haze said:](/goto/post?id=193242)
>
> Does anyone have a recommendation for the kind of light to use while working on a computer all day (Black/purple light, red light beside the computer)?
> thanks

Hi Purple Haze, welcome.

I believe a lot of members here use a 13W Compact Fluorescent Blacklight like [this Feit one](http://www.amazon.com/Feit-MLBP13T-BLB-Twist-1-Pack/dp/B008YJ89CU). Jack has occasionally done podcasts from his home where he pans the camera and shows a couple of CFL's above his head in a room with aluminium tiles to reflect the UV light.

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Personally, I would recommend starting with minimizing blue light in the office. I use [Flux](https://justgetflux.com/) and set it to be active at all times, and I recently ordered some [Rubylith sheets](https://www.optcorp.com/opt-rubylith-red-masking-film-15-x-20-sheet.html) to put over my screens to really knock down the blue.

### Purple Haze — 2016-04-22T20:35:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeing-the-light.17845/post-193249>

Thanks Rubicon! I'm thinking of attaching a clamp lamp to the top of my monitor and aim the purple light down upon me. Co-workers will think I'm insane, but I'd rather be healthy and sane.
That's a good idea about the rubylith sheet over the screen. I come from the printing-graphics industry...so I actually know what that is. I wouldn't be able to do that at work as I'm dealing with color calibrated graphics on my monitor, but it would be a good idea for my home computer.
thanks again.

### Scompy — 2016-04-23T02:52:08+0300

> thanks again.

I open the windows in the office with my computers n the day and don't use any artificial lighting. Instead, I use colors in the room with purple and reds on the walls. Natural light is a Faraday cage, don't forget that, and even cracking a window can make a difference (186,000 meters/second). After sunset, I use FLux turned down a ton in the settings, but I also have monitor guards too as well as wear blue-blockers. I rarely use any lights at night and have built my own 'torches' that are totally biohacked against all blue-light, plus I have incandenscent/edison bulbs in case I need to turn them on. I try to minimize my time on the computer at night also in general because I'm getting up with the sun each morning.

### AstralJam8 — 2018-02-25T11:40:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeing-the-light.17845/post-235684>

I need to apply some of these hacks. living in western Washington has been difficult, one thing I've noticed is that when the clouds clear up for a few weeks in Summer, if I go outside and look into the blue sky I will 100% sneeze every time, + eyes watering (I don't have pollen allergies, the irritation only occurs when I'm looking at the sky, and it is intense!) during the Fall/Winter/Spring months around 4pm the darkness is such a strain, feels like it's sucking the light/life out of my eyes. I've had CFS ever since I moved here in the early 90's (which later morphed into "Idiopathic Gastroparesis",) so much life wasted in a haze of lethargy, nausea and brain fog, and don't get me started about doctors!

I had some 650nm and 400nm UV LEDs laying around so I wired them up for an EZ Water experiment in another thread, and while I was refilling I tried sweeping the combined beam across my face with my eyes closed, and WOW! it was almost like facing the sun with my eyes closed, I definitely felt a similar lift in energy, yet it was soothing at the same time. Oddly, the light combination was perceived as a white glow through the eyelids, even though it was just red and violet. I can't wait for the ~280nm LEDs to arrive to continue the EZ Water experiment

I'm also thinking about 3D printing a "collar" with the 650nm and 850nm LEDs embedded to shine on the Thyroid gland, as I've definitely had struggles with its "insufficiency" ("normal" ranges can still be insufficient depending on the body's requirements...) Maybe they could be pulsed at 10hz corresponding with mitochondrial signalling pathways as mentioned in this article on Photobiomodulation which shows 650nm and 810nm to have the most pronounced effect:

[https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5066074/](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5066074/)

# Thread: Journal of a Little Starfish
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-little-starfish.17846/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 76
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### lohd2015 — 2016-04-22T21:04:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-little-starfish.17846/post-193257>

Mornings. Never was able to imagine something so intangible can be so powerful yet gentle at the same time.

I love waking up early so that I can be fully immersed in the mornings, whether it is sunny, overcast or even rainy. A feeling of being totally alive and energized, an unexpected continuity from the deep uninterrupted slumber that often takes me to most unusual and exotic dreamscapes. These are experiences totally beyond my comprehension until I entered the magical gardens of this forum last November.

In my daily meanderings in this magical garden, I have yet to meet the creator whom I like to think of as the Omnipresent Benefactor (OB). Not only are the gates to his garden always open to creatures of all varieties, the influence of his largesse is unequivocal. Although unseen, he provides a constant stream of nourishment and knowledge, at just the right time and in the right amounts to every living creature in this garden.

I am often dazed by the beauty and the vibrancy of life in this garden, and humbled by the generosity of all its inhabitants. Many a times I have been invited to rest and partake in the fruits of their labors, and I would often be mesmerized by how beautifully they have tended their habitat. I have yet to explore the farthest corners of this magical garden without being hopelessly lost. I have found this lovely spot with a balance of sun, shade and water which I will call my home. From here, I will be able to enjoy the miraculous bounty bestowed by the OB of this garden, and perhaps start a small garden of my own.

There is another reason why mornings are special to me. It is the time when I receive news from friends who live far away. The intensity of the joy is electrifying when I hear of how well they are doing because of what they have learned from this magical garden. Unlike the euphoria I often share with inhabitants of the garden, this joy touches the deepest and darkest corners of my being. This group of friends are ones I grew up with. We went to the same schools, and yet we had been total strangers for most of the 40 plus years since I left the Fragrant Harbor. There were many sleepless nights when I wondered about their whereabouts and yearned for an opportunity for closure, knowing that it would be an impossibility.....

### lohd2015 — 2016-04-22T21:06:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-little-starfish.17846/post-193258>

This journal will be a story about how the helping of friends attain optimal health help me heal my past and to become the person I was meant to be. The story is ongoing, and I hope I will be getting lots of help and feedback from all of you. Thank you for reading.

### lohd2015 — 2016-04-29T17:40:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-little-starfish.17846/post-193824>

The story continues:

Mornings. I would remember being rattled by the irksome buzz of the alarm clock, and for the briefest moment, could not remember the despair that had engulfed my consciousness the night before. Even though the repetitive motions of preparing for school was comfortably familiar, I was always filled with trepidation, not knowing what pranks nor insults my classmates had planned for me that day.

Annoyed at my lack of response, my dear classmates would often increase the level of provocation, only to be disappointed yet again, as I withdrew deeper into my shell. It became a game to them: how to find the key to dismantle my perfect veneer. Over the next forty years, I often wondered if things would have turned out differently if I had the courage to show them the softness of my vulnerabilities. Life wasn’t always that gloomy until we moved to a new home where my room was dark, and I wasn’t able to play in the sun any more as school started to take up most of the day. As I put on eyeglass and contacts, I became even more disconnected from the light. Night became my sanctuary, where only pillows heard my lonely cries for friendship and acceptance.

I love to wander around in this magical garden. You never know when you will come across a gem left by the OB for you. I was told that there are gems and jewels all over the garden, but it will only reveal itself to you when you are ready to see it. Even more remarkable, the same gem will hold different truths for different beholders. Sometimes these gems will turn into seeds, and if you plant them, they will burgeon into the most amazing vistas. No two vistas are the same. Once a creature inhabits a space, the land takes on its characteristics. Through love, knowledge and effort, the inhabitants sculpt their grounds into the perfect vision of their creations. In doing so, their lands draws and sustains diverse and magnificent life forms, which will further shape and change the vistas. It is indeed a miraculous phenomenon to behold as I wander through this magical garden.

Just the other day, I came across a brilliant drop of crystalline nectar, on it glistened these words: "_We attract what we are prepared to receive. People care more about beliefs than reality or truth because they create their own reality"._

When my life was devoid of light, I attracted darkness, in myriad shapes and forms. Misfortunes, bad decisions, leading to more sadness and adversities. I did not like myself, mistrusted people, and became bulimic. Eventually, my health collapsed and I was drained of all light as I went further into the darkness.

I look around myself now. The sight is so exquisite and captivating it makes my heart ache and fill my eyes with tears of joy and gratitude. This is certainly not the reality of my past. Although I am far from where I would like to be, I am at a place where I never dreamt would be possible....Why am I here?

### lohd2015 — 2016-05-08T18:31:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-little-starfish.17846/post-194377>

Many come to this magical garden, seeking treasures. It is said that the garden will grant all wishes. One must follow one’s own path and no two paths are the same. There are no short cuts either. Many seek a direct path to their goals and often they seem to find themselves traveling in a circuitous path. If they examine closer, they often find that although they seem to have traveled in a circle, and the surroundings look familiar, they are not exactly in the same place as before. If they don’t despair and continue on their paths, they will always find what they seek in this magical garden.

After being reconnected to my long lost classmates in the most serendipitous manner after more than forty years, I found myself in a most unusual position. Instead of being ridiculed and ostracized, I am now trusted, respected and admired. I have a special group of over 20 girls on social media where I share my health experiences with them and help them with their health issues.

I think I was extremely lucky with most of them, as I was able to solve their immediate ailments with simple solutions and a few neat tricks of my own discovery that worked like a charm. I always believed that everyone is different. But I didn’t realize HOW extreme the differences can be. Some of the remedies that work for some absolutely have no effects on others. I started to seek answers as to why that may be. And so I found myself here in this magical garden.

It is said that the paths in this magical garden are like spirals. Every time you make one round, you gain more knowledge to help you find your way to the next level. I think I made it through the first round, and the knowledge I’ve gained is immeasurable and absolutely boundless. To me, they are almost magical, as my friends will report astonishing improvements in their health beyond my wildest imagination (and theirs). I still have some who are skeptical as they have been led to believe in half truths. I hope in time, seeing how well others are doing, they will open their minds and hearts.

I am now at a point where I am immobilized by the height of the spiral paths. It is astonishingly daunting, both in terms of my own journey and that for my friends. I know that I won’t be able to help some of them if I can’t make the journey myself. I keep thinking of the gem left by the OB: “We attract what we are prepared to receive”. Even friends of friends are now asking for my help. I surely don’t have answers like the OB or other inhabitants of this magical garden. So why are they being ‘sent’ to me?

### Sue-UK — 2016-05-08T20:16:10+0300

I know that I won’t be able to help some of them if I can’t make the journey myself. I keep thinking of the gem left by the OB: “We attract what we are prepared to receive”. Even friends of friends are now asking for my help. I surely don’t have answers like the OB or other inhabitants of this magical garden. So why are they being ‘sent’ to me?

Sometimes it's ok to stand still in the garden, treasure the moment, look at how far i've come ...... allow a pause before taking the next step. .. In terms of other people, I have a choice on what I am prepared to receive, which I think affects what I attract. I need to keep paying attention, particularly when I'm leaking energy, because it changes what I attract. Out of the garden if I am asked I can hold out a seed from the garden in the palm of my hand, I can explain how to reach the garden, but they have to pick up the seed and take the first step. I know I can't take them further than I have gone myself, but if they do take the first steps and later pass me in the garden, that would be great.

### lohd2015 — 2016-05-08T20:38:00+0300

I need to keep paying attention, particularly when I'm leaking energy, because it changes what I attract. Out of the garden if I am asked I can hold out a seed from the garden in the palm of my hand, I can explain how to reach the garden, but they have to pick up the seed and take the first step. I know I can't take them further than I have gone myself, but if they do take the first steps and later pass me in the garden, that would be great.

Sue, I totally love your perspective! Would you be able to talk a bit more about leaking energy...like are there signs that tell you when you are leaking energy? Are there things one can do to control the leakage?

So far, I have only 2 girls who have 'picked up the seed'. One is a very good student, the other has quite a natural gift. The rest of 'my' girls are just looking for shortcuts, I don't believe they have any interest in going through the garden paths. But they are all doing rather well under the aegis of the magical garden.

### Sue-UK — 2016-05-08T23:28:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-little-starfish.17846/post-194387>

> [lohd2015 said:](/goto/post?id=194386)
>
> Sue, I totally love your perspective! Would you be able to talk a bit more about leaking energy...like are there signs that tell you when you are leaking energy? Are there things one can do to control the leakage?
>
> So far, I have only 2 girls who have 'picked up the seed'. One is a very good student, the other has quite a natural gift. The rest of 'my' girls are just looking for shortcuts, I don't believe they have any interest in going through the garden paths. But they are all doing rather well under the aegis of the magical garden.

In the past I would spend ages drafting a reply to a post, but when I read it back later I can see through my words I was leaking energy. Or I wouldn't have the confidence to post an idea. I struggled to make decisions... Now it comes and goes ....but I'm more aware of it. I visit my mum in a high nnemf nursing home several times a week, and that is an obvious drain on my energy, but I operate a red, amber, green system with her. If her mental state has her in the red zone, I don't leave until I get her into the green zone. It is best for her, but also for me. It's like a red or amber leaves an energy chord attached to me. On a green, as soon as I get home, I reconnect with nature, by getting outside and grounding, or walking barefoot to the well to get my water. Or I get in the outside tub. I can almost feel myself recharging.

It's much harder recognising less obvious or slower energy leaks, and in the last six months it's meant letting go of several family relationships, friendships, and business opportunities. If there isn't an exchange of energy, I try and choose where and when I offer energy I have, rather than leak it unawares. Still working on that!

### lohd2015 — 2016-05-09T00:17:30+0300

Sue, thank you so much for sharing that with me. It helps me look at what I'm doing with my friends a lot. I can see examples of where I may be leaking energy. Despite that, I still feel an attachment, and haven't been able to totally 'sever' the chords. I don't think I ever can, especially with my classmates. Just think, I felt connected to them even during all the years I lost touch with all of them. It's really some kind of inexplicable entanglement, or karma?

I agree with you totally, that it is an art to be able to be aware and to have control over how you would like your energies to flow. Working on it also. Thanks for helping me, Sue!
Hugs and kisses.

### Sue-UK — 2016-05-09T12:27:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-little-starfish.17846/post-194405>

> [lohd2015 said:](/goto/post?id=194389)
>
> Sue, thank you so much for sharing that with me. It helps me look at what I'm doing with my friends a lot. I can see examples of where I may be leaking energy. Despite that, I still feel an attachment, and haven't been able to totally 'sever' the chords. I don't think I ever can, especially with my classmates. Just think, I felt connected to them even during all the years I lost touch with all of them. It's really some kind of inexplicable entanglement, or karma?

Perhaps it's how we define entanglement and connection.

I can sometimes think of someone, and within hours they have either phoned, or I've met them unexpectedly where I or they wouldn't normally be. But other people can also send out a message to me. I might meet up with someone who tells me they've been thinking about me ..... at that point I don't think it's entanglement. There is an _opportunity_ to entangle, or for a donation or leaking of energy from one to another, or for an exchange of energy that involves one party using money as a form of energy (such as say someone paying for a service or therapy). Maintaining a connection (the chord) is ok, provided both parties benefit from the exchange, or at least the one losing energy to the other accepts the risks (and mitigates wherever possible), like me with my mum. My decision is different with my siblings, 2 cords loosened, one completely cut. Knowing them all my life doesn't mean it's healthy for me to remain connected, and I certainly wasn't entangled ...

I think true entanglement is where an energy exchange takes place but additional energy emerges for both parties, 1 plus 1 equals 3.

>
> I think true entanglement is where an energy exchange takes place but additional energy emerges for both parties, 1 plus 1 equals 3.

I love that, Sue: "1 plus 1 equals 3"!!!

It is similar to some of the ideas I've been working with, like when a connection is made or an event happens, the result usually starts a cascading motion, involving more people/elements in ways you cannot anticipate. (Call it the 'Guides' being totally efficient)

I still haven't quite found the answer to situations where the energy exchange is not 'fair'. For example, the OB has probably saved many lives. How do you value a person's life? How can one ever repay one's Benefactor? I know that is one EXTREME example, as there are not too many OB's in this world.

I do believe our existence is more than just this lifetime. So if you throw that into the mix, it really can complicate how you go about interpreting what is the ultimate/true balance.

Really love exchanging ideas with you.

### caroline — 2016-05-10T06:12:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-little-starfish.17846/post-194461>

I used to view all these quantum entanglements as out of the ordinary .....I can now see that it is the norm ...part of our road to optimal. There isn't a day that goes by now that extraordinary things happen!

positive energy begets positive energy in every possible way.....

Now we are living as we are meant to live ...and enjoying. a cascade of wonderfulness.

My partner just sold his business ...... we were sitting at the ocean not long ago. There were no more picnic tables and so a family asked to sit with us.

After a fun conversation with these folks we discovered that this man is in a related business and was thinking of buying equipment for a big job....

He just picked up most of it last nite.

I can now see clearly that entanglement is way more normal and fruitful ...and really how our worlds connect the positive energy.

I still struggle with past/present relationships that aren't positive .....but we have to let them go.

They cloud our perspective and dull our senses ..... and aren't good for them either. They bring nothing what so ever to the table for anyone.

It is very hard tho when it is family .....but now we know better!

### lohd2015 — 2016-05-11T18:32:21+0300

A gem from the OB today:

> As wellness fades to black, and we get ill, we need to recall life's story has no beginning or end: we arbitrarily choose that moment of experience from which to look back or from which to look ahead. Slowly, with many lost days, we can feel life returning. Rescue is in the air now. The end is in the beginning, and the future lies far ahead. Buckle in, the ride is not over during sickness, it will soon resume. Rage, recover, repeat. Life is a play, we can't afford a replay. Experience life and appreciate the present moment, for this moment, not the replay.

### lohd2015 — 2016-05-12T17:10:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-little-starfish.17846/post-194626>

> Nothing is as wealthy but the inexhaustible wealth of nature. She shows her powers only at cell surfaces, and creates life because she is a million fathoms deep in her complexity. The Earth appears in the path of the sun, receiving an endless bounty light hitting the atoms to program for living; its treasures seem inexhaustible for cells. A star draws on a vast reservoir of energy by means truly unknown to us. This reservoir is built in the subatomic energy which converts to certain forms of light and weird particles; it appears as if evolution dreamt that man's cells would one day learn how to capture this energy and use it for his wellness. To gain control over the sun's light Mother Nature usurped the power of a mitochondria to harness this energy by using oxygen's native powers. Oxygen became life's hero as well as her foe, being not only strong in her desire for electrons, but inexhaustible in its strategies and full of tricks to capture sunlight. Cells became obsessed to call up all of Mother Nature's forces to lay hold of oxygen and make oxygen our cell's prisoner in a prison called a mitochondria. With respect to our lifetimes, we can only know a small portion of what exists. We are built to capture the most important parts of this bounty to create the lives we live.

What happens when sublimation is intensified? You end up with quintessence epitomized in neutrinos, razing through everyone and everything, forever changing them.

Caroline, I am thrilled to hear about the wondrous positive events that are flowing so naturally into your lives. It is truly exciting to feel how things can just happen so naturally, unexpectedly and almost magically, to the benefit of all. I agree with you, being positive and open can invite more positive energies.

Life journeys to me, are very much like health journeys, where n=1.

We must all find the path that works best for us at that particular moment, knowing that the path may be totally different the next day.

I found a musing by the OB on human relationships that is rather succinct: “each person is just an imprint of the many people who have crossed paths with us over the years”.

For every action, there is a reaction. We are never quite the same person after each interaction/event.

I have always been an incurable romantic, and choose to believe that the ‘Universe’ is benevolent, and that we are here, during this lifetime to fulfil a higher purpose (whatever it maybe, even if it is just to have a great time, enjoying the simple joys of life). If that is the case, then everything/person that happens during this time ultimately is part of the path I am supposed to take, and all the changes that take place is part of the ‘grand design’. By accepting that everything has a higher purpose makes it easier to navigate through the difficult times that may challenge us from time to time.

### caroline — 2016-05-13T07:46:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-little-starfish.17846/post-194661>

this is a little story about twins. Jack often speaks about twins and how they can be so totally different.

We stopped at our local butcher shop this morning to get a couple of things. Since we were just out for our morning walk I just stuck $50.00 in my pocket. They had just opened so we would have depleted their small bills. I said I would be back in a couple of hours to go to the green grocer and I would stop and pay.

I tried giving my money to the shop guy and he didn't have a clue what I was talking about. This man has no energy/life emanating from him what so ever - His twin on the other hand is full of positive energy! I am sure I never would have articulated this before ....but now it is so obvious.

### lohd2015 — 2016-05-18T00:01:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-little-starfish.17846/post-194984>

Life is like a web of silk threads. Enchanting when you can follow its patterns, frustrating when they are enmeshed in an inextricable entanglement. Often times, we are distracted by our desires to fully appreciate and experience the elegance of the designs embedded in the chaos.

Nature has a rhythm of her own. Find it and you will be able to dance in harmony. Reject it, your existence will be dislocated by syncopation.

One of my ‘girls’ in the cold country up North has been a great supporter of my EZ group. She is fairly intelligent and curious by nature. She has been overweight since childhood. The freckles from her younger days morphed into melasma when she moved from a lower latitude to where she is now. She has struggled all her life to shed weight. Less than two years ago, she discovered swimming. In combination with a moderate low sugar diet and certain supplements I recommended, she was able to lose a few lbs. and maintain it precariously.

When she started to travel more, and at the same time increased the intensity of her swimming sessions, I tried to explain what I have learned from Doc regarding hormesis and the concept of predator and prey. She did not want to listen. Two months later, she found herself with a persistent lung inflammation, which she was able to ‘control’ with acupuncture, after getting no results from antibiotics. At that point, I decided it was time to share Doc’s great piece on Melasma with her. I had avoided the subject because I knew she wasn’t ready to understand it. And I was correct. So I dropped the topic and quietly blessed her albeit with a heavy heart.

My friend is now in the Far East on vacation. She’s the type who, after about five months of exposure to the teachings of Doc, can still ask me “Isn’t UV supposed to be bad for us?”. I really don’t think I’m that inept at getting Doc’s messages across (or am I??). So it was quite unexpected when she wanted to find out more about why she shouldn't exercise, and why her Melasma has recently taken a turn for the worse. I believe the fact that the friend she is currently staying with has been following Doc’s Leptin Rx with EXCELLENT results prompted her to rethink some of the ideas she previously dismissed. So I gave it another go, really simplifying the science, hoping the message will get through. At the end, I got the usual “thank you very much, will try my best…”.

So imagine my surprise when I received this from her a few days later:

The girl in the photo, who has been on Doc’s Leptin Rx for about two months, has an even more amazing story for me to tell another time….
Apparently, the pair took a long ferry boat ride away from population density to do some sungazing and serious grounding.

I hope this magical journey for them never ends….

### lohd2015 — 2016-05-22T19:12:25+0300

This morning, one of my ‘baby starfish’ who is vacationing in Northern Europe, sent me this message:

“I have to report that I absolutely adjusted my time difference easily. The second day is same like in Toronto.
Thank you for bringing all the useful and life changing info to me and really appreciate your wise advice”.

I replied to my ‘baby starfish’ that I am not worthy of her gratitude, and that I will share her joy and appreciation with the one who truly deserves it.

Thank you Doc, for everything you do, for being who you are, and for changing the lives of so many, in ways that escape the mortal imagination.

### lohd2015 — 2016-05-29T23:04:48+0300

“The whispers of truth are always in our head but we never listen to them until they become shouts.” Jeremy Thomley

I would like to share with you again, news from my friend with Melasma. These photos are quite special for me. Her husband has now joined her in the Far East. They took a long boat ride to a gorgeous outlying island to do sungazing and grounding. For her to convince her extremely skeptical husband to take off his shoes meant that her mind is in a totally different zone.

For my friend, her ‘shout’ moment was when her Melasma worsened. I think she had heard everything I was telling her these past months, but she was not ready to embrace it. She already made the decision to change the moment she asked me why she shouldn’t exercise. I guess she just wanted to hear it from me again. So I used the harshest tone I’ve ever used with her that time. Maybe I didn’t have to, but I wasn’t going to let a golden opportunity like that slip by. It did pain me though, to have to speak to her in such a manner.

I don’t think I need to beat it into her anymore. She got the message, loud and clear.

### lohd2015 — 2016-06-05T18:54:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-little-starfish.17846/post-196010>

I saved a life today with Light, Water and Magnetism.

This morning I noticed a small wasp on my balcony screen. I gently got it inside a tall jar like I normally do, and was ready to take it outside when I noticed it wasn’t doing well. It could hardly stand.

I put on two YouTube videos, one with a pure tone Schumann Resonance at 7.83Hz, the other a Buddhist Chant OM mantra by Tibetan Monks and played them concurrently. I left the wasp there next to the speakers for a while and noticed it was able to stand up. I then gave it some reverse osmosis water. It responded immediately. Then it went back to standing motionless for the next hour. However, when I took the jar outside under the sun, it started to dance with joy, and attempted to fly inside the jar.

After another hour of healing frequencies, I set it free. It flew away with gusto.

### lohd2015 — 2016-06-06T17:03:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-little-starfish.17846/post-196042>

“Why do you bother to save a wasp” I was asked yesterday. The story would have been much lovelier if the wasp had been a bee. For me to decide whether to save the wasp based solely upon the fact that it is ‘nasty’ and stings people, means I have made a judgement on the karma of the wasp. How do I know if the wasp will one day not sting someone/thing that deserves/needs to be stung? And that the sting will not cause a cascade of events that will have great implications for mankind?

To some, it’s called Chaos Theory, to others, it’s quantum entanglement. To me, it’s karma.

From my past experiences, I have learned to be very careful when assigning a value to actions and events. I either accept them, or leave them alone. But one thing that appears to be very clear is that if something shows up at your doorstep, you are not meant to ignore it.

Sue said yesterday in ‘How to Biohack Music’

> I don't feel I'm bio hacking music, it feels like the music is bio hacking me

How true. Now I wonder if it was me who saved the wasp, or if the wasp was sent to help me. After about 2.5 hours of the Buddhist OM mantra mixed with the Schumann Resonance pure tone, I noticed my ears were quiet starting early afternoon. I slept exceptionally well also. (I’m going to biohack this further. I burnt a CD of the two for my month long trip.)

Had I left the wasp alone yesterday, I would have wasted it’s life, and the karmic debt will accrue. You think we’re even now?

### lohd2015 — 2016-06-13T04:06:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-little-starfish.17846/post-196470>

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Meet Melanie, a six year old whose life was forever changed by Light, Water and Magnetism.

Melanie was a premmie, weighing 2 lbs 4 oz. at birth. She had a blood clot in her brain, slight deformity in both hands, one leg is longer than the other. She is also autistic.

When I first talked to her parents in late March, Melanie was very weak, suffering from high fevers frequently, and she was deathly afraid of anything new. Her mother said she would be so scared that she would literally tremble at the sight of a fly.

I told her parents to take away her iPhone, take her out of school which had WiFi. We added seafood to her diet, RO water, and let her play outside in the sun.

I saw Melanie for the first time two days ago in a busy restaurant. She was able to make some eye contact with total strangets, and was quite lively and extremely open with people she knew. At the end of the dinner, she gave me her fist and we 'boomed' a couple of times. Her family said her improvement has been nothing short of miraculous. Her leg is stronger and she is able to walk and run with ease. The use of her hands are better with weekly physical therapy. Her brother said there was an immediate noticeable difference after exposure to sunlight.

The doctors said Melanie will have developmental delays. The girl I met that night was a shy, happy, child who is clever, observant and quick witted.

I have a feeling Melanie will be doing very well.

Thank you Doc.

### lohd2015 — 2016-06-14T21:26:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-little-starfish.17846/post-196567>

Life is always full of surprises. Yesterday was one that will leave a smile on my face for years to come.

The ten of us went to Toogood Pond Park in Unionville. There were plenty of ducks but not much sun. But that didn't matter as the girls really wanted to learn more about Light, Water and Magnetism. So from 9:30 am to about 6 pm, I found myself channeling the OB. I really cannot fathom how I would be able to pull up so much information to totally engage ten minds for such an extended period otherwise.

The twist is, aside from technical terms, I used Cantonese the entire time.

The girls learned all about the mitochondria, the importance of the distance between respiratory proteins, the effect of UV on electrons, the ECT and how IR drives the ATP rotor in complex V. They learned about the Exclusion Zone, photoelectric effect and the difference between animals and plants. I made sure they remember the work of Doug Wallace on mitochondria % heteroplasmy.

I was only able to listen to 11 minutes of the June Webinar last night. Earlier in the day, one of the girls had talked about a very curious case about a relative who just turned 100 this year.

The man is large built, not heavy. His only obvious symptom is serious swelling between the ankles and knees, with discoloration (brown) and occasionally oozing of liquids. He can walk, but he frequently falls down. He is able to get up by himself without any other effects. Doctors are clueless.

What is unusual about this centenarian is he became nocturnal after he moved to Ontario four decades ago. He sleeps for ten hours at 8 am daily.

He is also an active stock investor, trading the Asian markets and doing stock research at night, with ALL the lights OFF. All his computers uses LAN, as he is concerned with security of Wifi networks. Apparently, he has been supporting his family with his trading. In addition, he still completes income tax reportings for his family as well as his children.

I hope to be able to find the answer to this seeming anomaly in the June Webinar. But I will have to wait until I get to Progreso tomorrow. Flight leaves at 9 am. Excited....

I told her what I learned from the Doc, that diseases, especially in the thyroid, are energy issues that originate from mitochondria with high % heteroplasmy.

Life is ultimately not about choices. We make decisions every day and we live with the choices we make. We can be comfortable with those choices because we make all kinds of excuses for them.

To me, life is about the quest for my truth, to find strength and courage to embrace and live my truth. To live behind a veil of falsehoods, in darkness and discomfort, plagued by perpetual fear and uncertainty is not living.

### lohd2015 — 2016-07-05T01:24:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-little-starfish.17846/post-197856>

I saw Melanie a second time the day before I left Toronto. I was shocked by how much and how well she has grown during the two weeks I was in Mexico. She was 2 inches taller and her coloring was extremely healthy. The clothes we bought her in Mexico will barely fit now.

During the four hour Dim Sum luncheon, which happened to be one of the best I've ever experienced, her parents could NOT stop recounting in minute detail, all the changes that has been occurring in their beloved daughter. I am especially intrigued by the following behavioral manifestations:

1\. Melanie is able to fully comprehend, on a deep level, all adult conversations, on whatever subjects and be able to deduce conclusions even in the absence of critical information, such as the name of a person. For example, her brother recently broke off his wedding engagement. The family never mentioned this directly in front of Melanie. One day, Melanie just confronted the ex-fiancee and asked what she did to make her brother split up with her.

2\. To say that Melanie has an affinity for language is an understatement. If you don't want her to learn anything, you'd better not say (or perhaps even think it) in front of her, because not only will she retain the new vocabulary, in Chinese or English, be it technical, slangs or even swear words, she will correctly apply them in the perfect context. Whenever she spoke, she would amaze us with her command of the language, and the subtlety with which she uses some terms. Sometimes she got us laughing so hard.....

3\. Melanie is assertive and creative beyond my imagination. Melanie stands up for what she believes is right, and will argue with you to the bitter end, and like a good lawyer, will employ whatever proper means at her disposal. If the opponent is her father, she will always have the last word because she will resort to using English, knowing full well he doesn't understand a word she's saying.

4\. At the end of the dinner party two weeks ago, I had said to Melanie's mother that I believe when she grows up, Melanie will be doing "big things" (literal translation of the Chinese term I used, which generally means meaningful or important). Melanie was very far away and I can't imagine her having been able to hear it. Her mother told me that from then on, every day, Melanie would announce she would be doing a "big thing" for that day, and would proceed to accomplish her target.

5\. Melanie seems to know instinctively what is good for her. According to her parents, her appetite has been insatiable recently. She is clever and resourceful at finding foods wherever they may be stashed. She also makes her requirements known. The Doc will enjoy this one: lately, she has been asking for salmon, Chinese style steamed whole fish, and lobster. Not just any old lobster mind you, but lobster prepared exclusively at this expensive restaurant which actually does a great job with them. She has also been asking for yogurt, and goat milk. No one knows where she got that idea from. So I told her parents that they should just give her what she wants. I also suggested that they get her some open toe footwear as Melanie has been taking apart the front of all her socks to expose her toes!!

This is the same girl who, a few months ago, would tremble from fear uncontrollably at the sight of dust reflected in the air by light.

I believe Melanie is a genius. She is endowed with exceptional sensory perceptions, and unusual cognitive abilities. She could even be telepathic, for all we know. Because of her physical conditions, the external stimuli proved too much for her body, like an electrical overload that fried her systems incessantly. With Sun, Water, and Magnetism, her body is growing stronger every second of the day and there is no stopping her now.

I feel truly blessed that I can witness this awesome, inspiring and humbling process. The OB has forever liberated a beautiful mind and set her free on the path of greatness to achieve the impossible.

Life is so beautiful.

### lohd2015 — 2016-07-30T01:41:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-little-starfish.17846/post-199131>

**What does N=1 really mean?**

One of my Toronto girls asked me, “You always say N=1, what does it really mean?”

I replied, “Everyone’s mitochondria are different, the distances between the respiratory proteins are different, the % heteroplasmy rates are different, the environments they live in are different, therefore, given the same set of stimulus (i), the results may be very different”.

“What does that mean?”

“It means, if I walked into a room full of strangers and friends and said ‘I love the sun’, everyone will have a different take on what I mean by that statement”.

“What does that mean?”

“It means everyone will interpret the same input differently, or better known as the Rashomon effect, which is the “contradictory interpretations of the same event by different people. The phrase derives from the film [Rashomon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rashomon), where the accounts of the witnesses, suspects, and victims of a murder and possible rape are all different. The Rashomon effect can be defined as "the naming of an [epistemological](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Epistemology) framework - or ways of thinking, knowing, and remembering - required for understanding complex and ambiguous situations." This effect is derived from the film itself, in which there are number of simultaneous factors: "the Rashomon effect is not only about differences in perspective. It occurs particularly where such differences arise in combination with the absence of evidence to elevate or disqualify any version of the truth, plus the social pressure for closure on the question." (Wikipedia)”

“What does that mean?”

“It means we are confined and defined by our consciousness, the foundation of which is in our mitochondria”.

“What does that mean?”

“It means that for most people we can never truly understand another person, because we cannot access their consciousness. We can only try to understand their consciousness through our own consciousness.”

“What does that mean?”

“It means that you can think of any kind of relationship, personal or business, intense or casual, as a game of squash. I call it Life Squash. Unlike a regular squash court that people play in, the walls of the Life Squash court are invisible, and instead of being in the same court together, each person stays within his/her own court with the invisible walls. The two (or more) courts are superimposed, so you have the illusion that both people (or more people) are in the same space simultaneously.

Then you start to play ball. Since each person is confined within his/her walls, the path of the ball is merely a reflection of the person’s own projections.

When two persons are ‘in sync’, or if they share a very similar vibrational level, or if they are so called ‘entangled’, then the ball bounces back and forth between the players very smoothly, and you have the illusion of a great game being played.

When the vibrational levels are not matched, then you end up with a bad game, unless one person ‘steps down’ in energy to match the other person.

For example, do you notice how sometimes it requires very little effort to communicate with someone who is on the same plane with you? You say something like, ‘I’m hungry’. The other person will name the exact dish at the restaurant you are thinking of at that moment. If you’re out of sync, one hour later, you probably find yourself still hungry….

Once in awhile, if you are lucky, you’d engage with a Master in the game of Life Squash. The tolls exacted are extremely high for participation. These Masters are able to change the shape of the courts at will. Think instead of 4 walls of a square or rectangle, you have a circle, triangle, hexagon, octagon, shifting and changing constantly on you. With these Masters, there are no rules of engagement. They can be playing upside down, on the ceiling, or you may find that your ground has totally disappeared. I have only met three Masters in my lifetime. The games are excruciatingly difficult, however, if you manage to survive them, you find that you can tap deep within your consciousness, and find resources you never knew existed. In short, you emerge totally transformed”.

Silence.

“Does that work for everyone?”

“What do you think?”

“......No?”

“And why not?”

“Because N=1?”

“Haha….Good girl!”.

### lohd2015 — 2016-12-25T04:03:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-little-starfish.17846/post-205763>

**FLOURIDATED SALT ALERT!!!!
**
Many members traveling in Mexico are aware of the fact that salt is fluoridated in Mexico. Rubicon, having recently ran out of unfluoridated salt, asked his MIL to bring some Himalayan salt from Columbia when she joins him in PDC. To his surprise, the Himalayan salt is ALSO FLUORIDATED.

This led to the following discovery from a 2006 article: [http://sonic.net/kryptox/nutri/saltGermany.htm](http://sonic.net/kryptox/nutri/saltGermany.htm)

"Globally

English-speaking countries generally have water fluoridation. German-speaking countries have salt fluoridation. Spanish-speaking countries generally have a mixture of water and salt fluoridation. Salt fluoridation has become widespread in Latin America and the Carribean, and more recently in the Middle East (Lebanon, Syria).

We should be wary of describing certain European countries (Nordic countries, Italy, Portugal...) as "unfluoridated". See:

[Prevalence of fluorosis in Norway](http://iadr.confex.com/iadr/2005Balt/techprogram/abstract_61381.htm)
[Use of fluoride tablets in Norway](http://iadr.confex.com/iadr/2003Goteborg/techprogram/abstract_29051.htm)
[Italian dental researchers calling for water fluoridation](http://iadr.confex.com/iadr/2003Goteborg/techprogram/abstract_37383.htm)

At present, more than 63% of salt consumed in Germany is fluoridated. The figure for Switzerland is probably higher, with some cantons implementing 100% salt fluoridation. Fluoridated salt is widespread in Austria, France, Spain, Hungary, Czech Republic and probably many other parts of Europe. With fluoridated salt, there is usually no real consumer choice. Most of the supermarkets in Germany (Aldi, Lidl, PennyMarkt, Norma, for example) stock only fluoridated salt.

In many countries outside Europe the situation is even worse: some governments have ordered that all salt be fluoridated. These include Colombia, Costa Rica and Jamaica. In 1994 the Pan American Health Organization launched a massive program to “fluoridate the entire Region of the Americas”. This project was funded by a multi-year-deal grant from the Kellogg Foundation. (PAHO, 1996)

Consumers of fluoridated salt (at 250 ppm fluoride) typically ingest more fluoride than consumers of fluoridated water. [Fluoridated salt is the most blatant scientific fraud.](http://bruha.com/pfpc/html/salt_facts.html)

In Germany, all fluoridated salt also has iodine added. In German maternity hospitals, mothers are offered fluoride tablets for their babies. Many accept. That's how bad things are. Ignorance reigns.

We have reports of fluoridated salt being on sale in Lidl supermarkets in Britain and Ireland. In Ireland (with mandatory water fluoridation) it is certainly illegal. The difficulty if not impossibility of controlling the distribution of fluoridated salt is further evidence of the lunacy of adding fluoride to anything.

Last year, an Irish public health specialist stated that the Republic of Ireland was borderline iodine-deficient, which is amazing for a maritime nation. It seems to be something that has happened since fluoridation began. Consider "the fluoride-iodine antagonism" as described by PFPC ([http://bruha.com/pfpc/html/thyroid\_history.html](http://bruha.com/pfpc/html/thyroid_history.html))."

This is the link to the original 2005 WHO paper on salt fluoridation: [http://www.who.int/oral\_health/publications/orh\_IDJ\_salt\_fluoration.pdf](http://www.who.int/oral_health/publications/orh_IDJ_salt_fluoration.pdf)

If the practice of salt fluoridation in these countries have not changed, then there are even more hurdles to overcome in the pursuit of optimal health.

Unfluoridated salt will be worth it's weight in gold, just like ancient times.

### lohd2015 — 2016-12-25T04:10:39+0200

**Salt fluoridation in Germany since 1991**

Salt fluoridation and oral health
