"Salt fluoridation has cariostatic potential like water fluoridation (caries reductions up to 50%). In Europe, meaningful percentages of users have been attained only in Germany (67%) and Switzerland (85%). In Latin America, there are more than 100 million users, and several countries have arrived at coverage of 90 to 99%. Salt fluoridation is by far the cheapest method of caries prevention, and billions of people throughout the world could benefit from this method."

### lohd2015 — 2017-01-23T00:33:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/journal-of-a-little-starfish.17846/post-207230>

A deep insight into Confucian classics, by Yvonne Telting:

**'Inspiring', 'Others' by 'Being'**

**Being**

'Being', a small word for an extremely broad concept.

The outside world, the person's system, influencing and being influenced by others, the person, and belief system, the personality. Constant change of characteristics in each layer can give rise to diversity at every level. 'Personality, in individuals and in the field of psychology that studies it, is always in a state of evolution.' \[1\] (Pervin, 1993)

'Being.' We tend to think we know ourselves, but do we? 'How we conduct (behave) ourselves shows us the foundation of our character and our personality' (Wilhelm,1953).

Wilhelm's (1953) interpretation of Confucius \[2\]: 'To conduct': 'No one knows themselves. Only from the consequences of our actions, from the fruits of our work one can derive what one can be expecting.' And: 'To conduct, is not permanent.' So there seem to be actions, consequences and deriving what can be expected and there is nothing guaranteed in advance. \[3\]

Authenticity

Being authentic. What does this mean? Is it being true to oneself? Is it 'what-you-see(-me-doing)-is-what-you-get?' Is it being real? What is real? For who is it real? The word 'authentic' can have a variegation of interpretations.

".. How then should I be?" Honest words in a car ride one day from a highest in hierarchy regarding his actions in the changes his company was undergoing.

How to be 'true' in being? How to be a 'person' or 'personality' in being? Or was his question about: How to be consistent and durable in being. In constant shifting and changes inside and outside ourselves.

'What is durable, is 'the change. Handling change. With a consistency in illustrious virtue \[4\].

Openness

In an illustrated book on this topic, I came across a painting of Ch'en Chu-chung, an early thirteenth century painter. What struck me, were the guiding lines next to the painting:

'... This tiger seems to be carefully watching us, perhaps ready to flee, more likely to attack. His conduct is one of a confident, healthy sovereign, fierce, but holding a tight control on himself until he can survey his situation and then act accordingly.' \[4\]

'... until he can survey his situation and then act accordingly.' Will he attack or will he flee? His focus is out there. He is aware of his strengths and his weaknesses, and seems to be adjusting himself to the situation, without altering himself. We would say: "He / she owns it.." He 'owns' certain behavior. He does not 'need-to-be' a certain way; he wants to be that way.

Without sidetracking this philosophical piece to personality characteristics, correlations, the dimensional structure or taxonomy of personality traits. Openness to his surrounding seems to be key. And, there is a vulnerability at that moment of surveying his surrounding.

Vulnerability

Openness has a lot to do with vulnerability. We may not like vulnerability, when we go out and survey 'our surrounding', or ask others what they think, or involve the employee in decision making.

We like (to be) a confident, active exponent of our ways.

We can use openness, receptiveness to our surrounding, and experience as a means to re-evaluate our beliefs. To handle change. And, be open about it.

We can also use the same openness to inwardly reassert our own righteousness and prefer to be an advocate for our own beliefs. And, whilst attempting to persuade others, we may be maintaining a barrier against outer challenges against ourselves.

Our identity is in ways intertwined with our beliefs. What if the belief structure cracked and began to fall apart? Then, the inner cohesiveness as a personality would too? To some degree most of us may have our self identity defined by our beliefs, opinions, attitudes, thoughts, emotions, us as a person.

Openness in ways, is close to our own personality.

Confucius thoughts on broadness of opinion: "The wise man has / can make enough room (space, capacity) in his own being to let the outside world in."

The designer has the floor: "Creativity and self expression (for innovation) has a lot to do with no longer censoring the raw material that is available to you." - Openness - Creativity - Innovation -

Confucius: 'A grand master knows no useless material.'

A print of a painting by Ch'en Chu - chung,

originate Ming dynasty 1368-1644

The order

'Those who wanted to put the governance of their country in order first regulated their own family.'

'Those who wanted to regulate their own family first cultivated \[6\] themselves.'

'Those who wanted to cultivate themselves first corrected their hearts.

‘Those who wanted to correct their hearts first straightened their will.’

‘Those who wanted to straighten their will first perfected their knowledge.’

‘Growing of wisdom lies in examining things.’

‘Examine \[7\] all things & retain the good.’ ~ Confucius - in the 'Ta Siuè' \[5\]

'Making a Qin' by Gu Kaizhi, ca 345-ca 406

Eastern Jin Dynasty, Palace Museum Beijing

**Inspiring (to) others**
**Influence, inspire and / or ecstasy, enthusiasm**

Parts of the five Confucian Classics are so constructed that it enables us to see a situation from different standpoints. The active side, doing, initiating, creating, and from the receiving side: passive, undergoing. Also, seen from the standpoint of the 'wise one' or the great master.

Influencing, stimulating, enthusiasm goes both ways. '(We are) inspiring others by being' and '(we are) being inspired by others'. Multiple, mutual and recursive. \[3\]

Influence, inspire

One could almost ask: 'What's love got to do with it?'

'Sièn', one of the chapters \[8\] on inspiring and stimulating in one the Confucian Works, relates to the word 'universal', 'on the whole' and means 'mutual charm', 'attraction'. In contrast, to what one might think, reading this chapter, the meaning is broader than pure the word 'attraction'. Just as the beginning of one of the Confucian Works starts with 1. 'creative power' - 'action'- 'the active' and 2. 'natural response'- 'passive' - 'the receiving'. The evolution of this beginning, starts with the fundamentals of all social conduct in: 'Inspiring'. The meaning that is also symbolically described, could or should be considered broader, more general. Symbolically, 'Sièn' describes the courtship of a man and a woman, to symbolise how inspiring and stimulating works, seen from different angles.

'Durable', is an important concept within change and handling change in Confucianism. Therefore, the symbolic meaning of 'Sièn' evolves to 'Continuing'. And, shows us the different angles for a durable concept of inspiring, within constant change. The symbolic description of 'the man' and 'the woman' shows us in more general the conduct of an active party and the receiving party. The leading one and the following one. Highlighted in this symbolic description is how the active, leading party is the subordinate one to the receiving one, and by being leading ànd subordinate, earns the respect of the receiving party, which leads to following. Most essential part in this chapter is: earning leadership by being the subordinate ànd respectful one. With the emphasis placed on respectful, the difference is highlighted between on the one hand inspiring (- mutual charm or attraction), and on the other hand seduction or luring that is seen as lacking this higher moral.

The one in the leading position, who is respectful and voluntarily positioning him/herself under the undergoing party, is a returning theme. While having something great and by being humble and modest, the leading one earns sympathy and willingness that leads to voluntarily following and devotion on the receiving end.

Inspiring others can come along with enthusiasm, or vice versa. Yet, are distinguished within Confucianism as two different matters. Illustrated in another part of these Works, what occurs when a great man harmonizes with his people and acts in accordance.

Ecstasy, enthusiasm

This other part \[9\] of these Works, shows us the fascinating, enchanting effects of striking a chord in someones core. Illustrating what happens when we have feeling for, harmonize, and are in touch with someones soul. Just as how music and rhythm resonates, inspires, and moves, mobilizes, and leads to wanting to join and follow in the enthusiasm.

This phenomenon is symbolised in a historic storytelling referring to a legendary incident, 'The playing of the Qin' or 'Listening to the Qin'. It was told that a great musician, some say it was [Li Kang](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ji_Kang), was visited by a stranger one night while playing the Qin. The stranger taught him an enchanting melody, with such a haunting harmony that it attracted an outer world audience emerging from the underground. The stranger was thought to be visitor from the spirit world. This storytelling is seen in multiple artworks in Chinese history.

'Boya playing a Qin' by Wang Zhenpeng,

Yuan Dynasty (1271-1368), Palace Museum Beijing

Enthusiasm, a reaction / action that leads to voluntarily following and devotion, is in its turn generating enthusiasm. Multiple, mutual and recursive. Crucial point in enthusiasm is being in sync and a unity with the character of the surrounding. Being in sync with the surrounding, makes it possible to inspire others and mobilize them for a task. It is this kind of unity that makes a mass movement with victory possible. All joining together in the same direction. The electricity and energy we feel when we are at a music concert or while chanting at a football match.

Enthusiasm and cheerfulness, have an uplifting and relieving effect. Wilhelm's (1953) interpretation of Confucius illustrates this as following:

'As the thunder, the electrical power, in the beginning of summer re-appears, and the first thunderstorms refresh all of nature, so dissolves a long and accumulated tension. Causing relieve and cheerfulness. The same way music can break tension in our soul and relieve somberness. Enthusiasm in our heart, expresses involuntary in singing, dancing, and in rhythmical movement of our bodies. Throughout human history, people have felt these mysterious moving effects, of invisible sound that moves our hearts and has the power to unite people. The ancient rulers acknowledged the importance of music and gave it an honourable place in leadership.'

How important it exactly is, we may derive out of the fact that one of the five Confucian Classics is 'The Sji Tjing', also called 'The Book of Ode' (also called 'The Book of Songs') \[10\]. The Sji Tjing consists of written down odes, songs (poems) from all over the empire. Songs (poems) of the people, folk, nation, the common people. Songs (poems) made by the people, about what lived in their hearts. Songs with valuable lyrics and messages, to share and reinforcethroughout the nation and years following.

This Book had multiple functions, such as building tradition, building culture, honouring, respecting and awarding great achievements of ancestors. Also, to clarify and illustrate a particular standpoint or situation. And, to reinforce andshare the general accepted ethical-social-cultural standard and norms and values.

Odes, presented in a ritual or sacrifice, to praise the virtue and grace of heroes. Used by rulers, who honoured the divine and greatness of their heroic ancestors, and with this, consolidated the connection between being human and something much higher and greater than us all. With this summarizing Chinese culture, according to Wilhem (1953).

Confucius said the following over this preserving of honouring in ritual:

'The ones who fully understand this sacrifice (you may in my opinion read instead of the word 'sacrifice': 'humbly honouring what was before'), could rule the world, as if he/she was turning around earth, in one's own hand.'

It is using (the positive aspects of) the past, to define and reinforce the future.

### Hairless Samson — 2016-04-23T18:10:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lust-waffels-and-magnesium.17847/post-193308>

Sorry, i just wanted to make you laugh with the title.

My name is Moritz and im from Germany. Ive read and listened to Jack since middle 2014 and my first reaction was...

No way. This can't be. And it's over my head anyways!

Strange though. Why do casual comments of people i worked with and that i didn't pay attention to before, totally overlapped with the input i got from a certain neurosurgeon?

I was working in areal management of a fitness-chain, mostly in educating and training trainers and clubmanagers.

Well now - try to explain some of Dr. Kruse to them in german when they face clients with serious problems...

I am freelancing now and that actually saved me. I've overworked myself after around a decade of working like a madman for 12-14hours daily under blue light, excessive training, no sun because of exhaustion on days off and spending my time indoors away from people (i had enough of em 6 days a week).

I had a thrombosis at the age of 26, systolic bloodpressure in the morning of 170 at the age of 27/28 and felt like i was doing - something wrong? At least i had realised that i wasn't invincible. That might sound funny, but alot of people don't think theyll get hit by disease. Especially in the fitness WOD crossfit survival of the sickest - world. Others do get sick - of course, but not oneself.Seriously!

Jack helped me alot, not knowing about me or the persons i work and worked with. I am grateful that i am able to understand the english language enough to read through his blogs and scientific papers. And i am grateful for you Jack, helping me helping others. Even though my understanding of your work is still not where it should be. Yes - i am afraid of my own created half truths.

Maybe i'll get better by asking (probably) silly questions, reflecting my ignorance or missunderstanding guided by CW. I humbly apologize for that.

Ill stop here and ask for a permission. I don't want to ask about my own journey to optimal on this forum. But i would like to use this platform to ask questions for others. For those who can't speak english, won't believe what they would read (like i did) and are stuck in short term "hey im fine! I don't sleep well, but hey whatever" thinking.

Last but not least i have one picture that wouldn't exist if i would still be stuck in my 26 year old brain (im 29 now). Starnberger See end of march 2016 ;-)

I always have a bias toward thinking the "wrecked athletes" have a built in advantage over others. THE OBSESSIVE COMPULSIVE MENTALITY that pushed us over the edge initially, when correctly focused, seems to do aid us in our new training methods: Sitting around sunning, cold tubing, grounding, eating DHA , are all just the same old same old. ### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-23T18:37:01+0300

We are all wrecked athletes because modern medicine had lots of stories masquerading as facts. Nice pic.

### Hairless Samson — 2016-04-23T22:15:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lust-waffels-and-magnesium.17847/post-193328>

The reason i was spending so many hours at work was because i had the opportunity to help people. I was in a position to do it. I always was giving training hours because i never wanted to lose connection to the members of the gym i was working at. My behavior eventually led me to a choice i had to make. Gyms can be compared to hospitals nowadays. No joke. You see 16yr olds with a double disc herniation. No accident - degenerative. 14 yrs old with DM2 or extremely pain sensitive areas around their neck. People with ms, morbus bechterew, infertility, atherosclerosis, fibromyalgia, hip replacement, semiparesis, osteoporosis, rheuma, athritis, adrenal dysfunction, eating disorders....the list is as long as it could be.

There is no way that normal trainers without a mad mans drive for research and the persistent idea that helping other people is kinda nice will be able to tackle that burden. It is a relieve but also a smack in the face to know, that all those diseases can be tied to a small amount of fundamental things. And to know that pointing this out to people will lead them out of a neolithic comfort, they (thought they) adapted to.

# Thread: Searching for Health in Indiana
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/searching-for-health-in-indiana.17851/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 27
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Gondore — 2016-04-24T04:26:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/searching-for-health-in-indiana.17851/post-193339>

Hello everyone, I am new to this forum but have been on the Facebook group and reading Jack's stuff for a couple weeks now. I wanted to start a journal on here to hopefully help me guide me back to health. I wanted to start off my writing up a somewhat detailed medical history to give you all an idea of where I'm going from. I hope and pray I can learn a lot from you all and return to an optimal state of health!

Here goes.....

I am going to list all symptoms I have noticed over the years, even if they are minor.

I am the oldest of a family of six children. I was raised in a stable Christian home and had a very happy childhood. As a child I did have a few slightly abnormal things. First, I had problems with peeing the bed well into 5th or 6th grade. I also had a significant amount of anxiety when spending the night at friend’s houses. This resulted in my parents having to pick me up in the middle of the night several times. Additional things that were somewhat abnormal included lots of sleep walking and night terrors, and I had a “false” set of front teeth which had to be surgically removed.

Around the age of twelve I was bitten by a tick in the head. I do not remember the type of tick, if I became sick afterwards, and would not have noticed a rash because it would have been hidden by my hair. In my teens I also have a few minor issues. I remember feeling sick a lot in the mornings but then going to school and feeling better by lunch. Occasionally this feeling would cause me to vomit at school but usually I just felt poorly. I did very well in school and always enjoyed learning.

In my late teens and early twenties is when more bothersome health issues started to come to light. Starting around the age of sixteen, I became very interested in weight lifting. I started lifting 1-2 hours per day five days a week. I loved working out and continued to do this until around the age of 22. During this time I tried all kinds of fitness supplements. I had stop lifting at 22 because I started to feel sick for an hour or two every time after lifting.

I started college and was driving about 45 minutes each day to and from school. I started to notice that I would become very fatigued at times. I would be driving and go through periods were almost nothing would keep me awake. I started taking high doses of caffeine while driving so I had fewer issues like this.

Around the age of 22 I started to have rectal bleeding. After seeing my family doctor and trying a couple different creams, I was sent to a GI. The GI diagnosed me with rectal fissures. I was in an extreme amount of pain from these fissures and ended up opting for surgery. The GI doctor performed a fissurectomy. During the surgery the surgeon found several anal fissures alone with several hemorrhoids. The surgeon removed the hemorrhoids and basically cut around the fissures. Needless to say the recovery from the surgery was extremely painful and I ended up having problems with pain after bowel movements for about 5-6 years afterwards. This created a lot of anxiety for me because I worried about going places and having to use the bathroom because it would result in a lot of pain afterwards.

Another problem that developed sometime around this time period was chronic throat clearing. I would get this urge like there was mucus in my throat and that I needed to clear it. What was weird though is that rarely would mucus ever come up. I also started to have periods where it felt like I couldn’t breathe and some chest tightness.

Around 2009 my first child was born, a daughter. For some reason the infant stage was very hard for me. Looking back now, I think I fell into a sort of depression during this period. Sometime during this time period I developed a big cyst on my upper back. It felt like it ruptured at one point and caused me a good amount of pain for weeks. I had a dermatologist look at it and he said he thought it was fine. Since then (probably 4-5 years later) the cyst has never gone away. It is about the size of a silver dollar. I also developed several other cyst like things under my skin. Two of them are on my arms and there is another on the upper part of my leg.

In 2011 my second child was about two months from being born. My episodes of feeling like I couldn’t breathe started to increase. I then went to a GI who said I probably had acid reflux and prescribed me a PPI. I believe the stress of having to go on a PPI for the rest of my life and the PPI itself caused me to become much worse. This is when things really started to go down-hill for me. I developed throat pain, pain when talking, feeling like my throat was going to close at times, apnea type symptoms when falling asleep, pain when turning my head, and just an overall crappy feeling. I also noticed during this time that my joints started popping a lot, I felt worse after exercising, and I started to develop carpel tunnel type symptoms.

Things once again escalated in October of 2013. I was determined to try and get back to 100% health instead of the 70-80% I felt like I was at so I returned to doctors. Around this time the reflux started to worsen again. The throat pain increased and I felt like I had acid coming up my throat at nights. A lot of this time I slept partially sitting up. Since about 2009 I also had episodes where I would wake up at night feeling like I had stopped breathing. Many times I would start to feel very cold and my body would shake. Sometimes I had dumping type symptoms also. The suffocation feeling would usually last anywhere from an hour to a couple hours and then I would get back to sleep.

One day in October 2013, I woke up and this feeling of suffocation was non-stop. During the first couple of months it nearly drove me insane because I couldn’t find anyways to manage it. One day while at work it started to feel like my head was too heavy for my neck to support, my neck hurt, I felt dizzy and the suffocation lead to bad chest tightness. I ended up driving myself to the emergency room and at one point my breathing became so bad I thought I was going to pass out. After getting into the emergency room, they checked my heart and said I was fine. They ended up giving me Ativan and sending me home.

The Ativan helped to take helped a small amount, mainly to just take off the edge. I went back to my family doctor who had been running test on me and he basically ended up saying it was all caused by anxiety. He prescribed Zoloft 50mg and Xanax (as needed). I started taking the Zoloft and the Xanax. After initially starting the Zoloft I began to feel off balance a lot, and like this sensation that my eyes couldn’t keep up with things I was seeing at times. I also experienced pressure behind my eyes. During this time the sleep apnea type symptoms returned. The best way to describe this is that right before falling asleep my body would jerk and wake me back up. This would continue all night long leading to nights of almost no sleep.

During this period I became very depressed and had a lot of suicidal thoughts. The only thing that really kept me going was my wife and two children. I ended up working a lot of days from home because I found it extremely hard to work at my office at work.

After being on the Zoloft for about 3 months and weaned off of it because I couldn’t tell any difference in my condition or my mood. I continued to take the Xanax off and on but only when I really needed it. Driving became very difficult for me because the breathing seemed to be at its worst then and combined with the dizziness I never felt safe driving.

Around the end of December 2013, I had an endoscopy done. They found a hiatal hernia (which was later found to be a false finding) and grade one esophagitis. I was put on a double dose of 40 mg of Protonix (PPI) for three months. During this time I also started to pursue additional testing for my reflux. In January or Fed. I had the 24 PH test and the esophageal manometry test performed. They found that my esophagus functioned normally but that somehow I refluxed about twice as much as an “average” person.

Based on my reflux test results I was a candidate for surgery. I proceeded forward with the surgery on April 25, 2014 because I felt like I really didn’t have any other options. I should also mention that before the surgery, I did see go to an Asthma Center here in Fort Wayne and seen a Pulmonologist. They tested for asthma using a lung function test and said that I improved by 10% when I was on an inhaler. They also said that my inhalation curve was flat, which suggested inflammation of the upper airways. I also seen an ENT during this time who scoped me and diagnosed me with LPR. All of these things lead me to believe that the reflux was the cause of the breathing problems.

My reflux symptoms like tight throat, bad breath, acid taste in mouth, pain when talking, throat going shut feeling, have all improved significantly. I still continue to have the off balance feeling, neck stiffness, slight muscle weakness. I also sometimes get twitching eyelids, sciatic pain, carpel tunnel type feelings in my hands/arms, and I also find that my arms go to sleep a light at night, which wakes me up.

I would like to also speak about my family’s history at this point. My dad’s dad died in an accident that I was witness to when I was about 16. He had no health issues and was 76 old. My dad’s mom is still alive and is 96 and hasn’t been to the hospital since the birth of her last child. My mom’s dad is also very healthy and has few issues at 76. My mom’s mom has had health problems most of her life. She went through a period of pretty significant depression in her 30s, has had her gallbladder removed, a histerconomy, has diverticulitis, and is most recently having significant siatic pain. My dad is in his 50s and hasn’t had any significant health issues. My mom is also in her 50s and has had some problems with her health but nothing major. Out of my five brothers and sisters, one has asthma, and another has felt ill since he was about 4 (he is now 21). One of my other brothers had seizures as a child but grew out of them.

Here is a list of all the symptoms I have had over time. Some of these I still some and some I do not, other come and go:

\- Chronic air hunger/restricted feeling when inhaling

\- Off balance feeling/disoriented when moving worse when moving head

\- Throat clearing

\- Chest tightness

\- Throat pain

\- Twitching eyelids

\- Muscle weakness

\- Cyst/growths under the skin

\- Acne

\- Sores in my nose

\- Sores in my mouth

\- Cold sores

\- Sleep apnea type systems

\- Cold hands and feet

\- Popping joints

\- Hands easily falling asleep at night

\- Tingling/Weakness in hands/arms

\- Pressure behind eyes

\- Sensitivity to bright lights

\- Problems staying asleep

\- Itching in different places during the night with no visible signs of anything on the skin

\- Occasional constipation

\- Occasional diarrhea

\- Anxiety

\- Depression

\- 15 + cavities

\- Siatic pain

\- Exercise intolerance

\- Bony growth on my gums

\- Body tends to shake more than a normal person when I am cold-

\- Cherry angiomas all over my body

\- Moles all over my body

\- Weak feeling in legs

I have quite a bit more to write including some of the results of the hundreds of labs I have had, but I will save that for another post.

**OTHER TESTING
**
\- Extensive testing for asthma concluded my issue was not asthma related (tried several inhalers, methacholine challenge, etc)

\- Have had a CT of lungs/chest along with numerous chest x-rays....found nothing

\- Seen vocal cord experts to see if the issue might be related to Vocal Cord Dysfunction and this was mainly ruled out

\- Have had EKG(s), treadmill stress test, and echocardiogram to help rule out heart issues

\- 24 hr PH test showed twice the reflux as average person

\- Endoscopy showed mild erosion of the esophagus by the lower esophageal sphincter

\- Standard workup by an endocrinologist showed nothing but low testosterone

\- Extensive testing at a balance center showed nothing abnormal to explain my off balance feeling

**THINGS I HAVE TRIED
**
I'm sure I will forget many of the things I have tried over the last 10 years but here are a few of them:

\- Been to see multiple psychiatrist and psychologist (tried several different SSRIs with no results...actually felt worse)

\- Worked with numerous doctors including 2-3 internalists, 2-3 asthma/pulmonologist, 3-4 good functional doctors, 3-4 GI docs, 1 endocrinologist, 2 LLMDs, and probably several others I'm forgetting

\- I have tried all kinds of diets including Paleo, SCD, Autoimmune Paleo, and a couple others

\- Essential Oils

\- Every supplement under the sun including a lot of different probiotics

\- Acupuncture and Massage Therapy

\- Chiropractors

\- Homeopathy

\- All kinds of herbs

\- Energetic testing including SCIO, Zyto, and muscle testing

\- Rife Machines

\- Advanced Cell Therapy

\- Chelation (this may need to be explored more)

\- MTHFR

### Penny — 2016-04-27T22:38:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/searching-for-health-in-indiana.17851/post-193714>

Welcome You might want to check out Scompy's journal and see what he's doing to survive his software job...

You might try to increase magnesium... a lot - and add k2... Sounds like a whole lot of calcium efflux is going on -

Magnesium oil (spray) or foot/bath soak/epsom salts:
[http://www.amazon.com/Collective-We...d=1461785112&sr=8-3&keywords=magnesium+flakes](http://www.amazon.com/Collective-Wellbeing-Magnesium-Flakes-Ounce/dp/B0062A7SRU/ref=sr_1_3_a_it?ie=UTF8&qid=1461785112&sr=8-3&keywords=magnesium+flakes)

and here is a recipe for magnesium water which is a no brainer to do:
[http://www.wheatbellyblog.com/2015/07/magnesium-water-from-wheat-belly-total-health/](http://www.wheatbellyblog.com/2015/07/magnesium-water-from-wheat-belly-total-health/)

Here's the k2 you need 3 times per day:
[http://www.amazon.com/Vitamin-Menat...UTF8&qid=1461784827&sr=8-1&keywords=mk4+15+mg](http://www.amazon.com/Vitamin-Menatetrenone-Mk-4-15mg-vegecapsule/dp/B00GZVM092/ref=sr_1_1_a_it?ie=UTF8&qid=1461784827&sr=8-1&keywords=mk4+15+mg)

And you might want to research the wonders of boron and vitamin d3 - it all works together to take the calcium from your soft tissues and put it back in the bones -

See this blog:
[https://www.jackkruse.com/gnolls-com-opens-the-door-to-obesity-fight/](https://www.jackkruse.com/gnolls-com-opens-the-door-to-obesity-fight/)
[https://www.jackkruse.com/osteoporosis-two-the-vitamin-k2-story/](https://www.jackkruse.com/osteoporosis-two-the-vitamin-k2-story/)

Basically, you want 1000IU of d3 plus 15 mg of k2 plus 3-6 mg of boron plus some amount of magnesium taken all together... your vitamin d level is in the toilet...

You want to eat tons of seafood and embrace the cold because your job is sucking the life out of you - and drink copious amounts of R/O or spring water - Fiji water is great as is coconut water -

You might want to drink green smoothies before/during work also - and attach an RJ45 cable to your router (you can disable the wifi) and stick everything in airplane mode \*all the time\* not just at night - you must recover while at home not expose yourself to more EMF... delete the smart meters and cordless phones also -

Lastly, for $99.00, you can get a research grade stool test that gets the DNA of your gut flora - if you have h pylori, they have a high chance of finding it - [www.americangut.org](http://www.americangut.org) -

If you're going to go regular doctor on this one, I think the h pylori breath test would be your best bet rather than the blood test - which is useless IMHO -

### lohd2015 — 2016-04-27T23:27:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/searching-for-health-in-indiana.17851/post-193716>

> I do currently drink a lot of RO and Kagen water each day.

Hi Gondore!
Just my 2 cents on ionized water in general. You need to be very careful with them. Of course keeping in mind, n = 1 trumps everything.

I had a friend who was using an expensive water ionizer from Japan. She started to develop hay fever like symptoms. It persisted for months EVEN when she was traveling and not drinking the ionized water. I could not figure out why she would be cold, sniffly and sneezy all the time in different environments and settings. So I suggested she stopped the ionized water and just do RO (or distilled). Her symptoms subsided within the first week, and by the second week she's back to normal. She hasn't had a recurrence since.

I don't know how the ionized water may affect you. It is something for you to discover and experiment (amount, timing and context can make a difference). Would love to hear back from you.

### JMO — 2016-05-04T02:38:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/searching-for-health-in-indiana.17851/post-194130>

> [Gondore said:](/goto/post?id=193660)
>
> Jack, I am also working on that. Searching for jobs that allow me to work remotely 100% of the time so I can fully control my environment. It's going to take a little bit, though I do have some leads!

Hi Gondore,

I've been in software development for 35 years. I grew up in the days where we coded with pencil and coding sheets then got some allocated time to work on a computer so all my years weren't staring at a laptop all day and night. Did do my share of nights working thru problems but haven't done much nights for abt 15 years when they started off shoring most of the night calls to India. I've been working part-time from home for prob 8 years (in office the rest) and have been working from home abt the past year. My work environment is the best it has ever been for my entire career and fortunately I am getting closer to retirement. The good news for you is that you have this knowledge while you are young so you can protect yourself. Here are some tips I use.

Laptop hacks

Move your computer as far away from you as possible. I used a body voltage meter to measure. My computer didnt have a plug with grounding so the USB grounding plug makes a really big difference.

Acrylic blue blocking filter for laptop from Low Blue lights - I chose this instead of glasses because I can control my overhead light environment and want to get natural light during the day. [https://www.lowbluelights.com/products.asp?cid=16](https://www.lowbluelights.com/products.asp?cid=16)

USB grounding plug - LessEMF -http://[www.lessemf.com/ground.html](http://www.lessemf.com/ground.html)

External keyboard - also makes a difference because when you touch your laptop keyboard the hard drive makes the magnetic fields higher - external keyboard helps.

Low emf mouse - LessEMF.com

Turn off wireless modem by typing in 192.168.0.1 in url then wireless setup, then disable radio state. Use Cat 5 cable to hardwire to your computer and turn off Bluetooth/wireless on your laptop. Set up your modem on a timer so it goes off at night.

Phone hacks.
Use a landline with speaker phone capability. Use speaker and long 50 ft cord to move outside and take calls when possible.

Lighting

Avoid indoor lighting. Use natural light from windows and leave windows open to let UV light in. Can use some aluminum reflectors to get more UV reflecting in your office. I have an area outside where I can work barefoot to ground and with shorts to expose as much skin as possible to light. In winter, I use some blacklights indoors for UV.

Take breaks/lunch outside to ground and get UV light. I also have an aquarium chiller attached to my bathtub and sometimes do some CT and sun during lunch or after work.

### pascale ahmad — 2016-04-24T13:43:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/social-obligation.17852/post-193360>

Last night I went out to a party for the first time since starting the leptin reset protocol. I was not at all looking forward to it and I was worried that I might go of the rails, but life is such that one has to sometimes do things out of social obligation. As it turned out, it went quite well. I had dinner before going along with a double expressly because I find it difficult to stay up past 9pm! Once there I sipped soda water all evening (pretending I was drinking vodka and tonic!!) and did not eat any of the canapés that were being passed around, fortunately it was a buffet and not a sit down dinner so no one noticed that I wasn't eating. The funny thing was that I was not at all tempted by the food on offer.... The only thing that bottered me was the late night and that by the time I got up the next morning it was already to hot to sit outside and sun gaze. Am I becoming a terrible bore?

### Purple Haze — 2016-04-24T23:27:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-tanning-beds.17853/post-193392>

Hi everyone,
I'm wondering if tanning beds are ok for an occasional UV treatment.
I know nothing can replace the real Sun, but living in Vancouver BC Canada, the Sun can be in hiding for long periods of time. Is it ok to go to UV tanning beds during those dark & gloomy times?
Thank,
Haze

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-25T00:07:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-tanning-beds.17853/post-193393>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=193393)
>
> Very slippery slope for myself........I made the decision based upon what I know that I did not want to use the beds and would rather use the sun by moving to it.

Do you consider it a slippery slope because of potential health risks? I figure if I can use UV black lights in my home and office, it might be ok to use a UV tanning bed on occasion.
Is it the strength and concentration of the UV beds that are a concern? I wouldn't want to use it as substitute for the real Sun, just a winter time supplement.

I'd love to move to a sunnier location one day. In the mean time I want do all I can to recover. I am going to Italy's Amalfi Coast in a few weeks for some Sun & Seafood…and perhaps some cryotherapy in the sea.
I'll think of you when I visit Michelangelo's David. Thanks again Jack for all that you do!

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-25T00:41:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-tanning-beds.17853/post-193397>

I do because Quantum health has taught me that everything is a N=1 situation. That really is the deep meaning of the April 2016 webinar.

### DavidL — 2016-04-25T02:15:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-tanning-beds.17853/post-193403>

I'll make a comment on my own experience. I do like tanning beds and I used on from September 2015 until early April 2016. I buy a monthly pass for 36.99, about the same cost as Vitamin D supplements. I live at 50.45 degrees latitude in Canada. Regina is one of the sunniest places in Canada and winter is sunny most of the time. However, I cannot get UVB rays in my location from Sept to April so I tan.

When I tan, I feel better. I sleep better, I have more energy, and my mood is positive.

The EMF question I have address with lots of grounding pre tanning, and loading up on water. Being very hydrated before tanning is something I swear by.

I have had to alter my diet slightly due to the increased exposure to the tanning.

So far I have not had an ill effects or side effects using the beds. I do protect my face from long exposures. After 3-5 minutes I will shield my face with silken cloth. Day light here is 14+ hours a day so I have reduced my tanning bed exposure in the last 2 weeks, in favor of spending the day outside working. I make sure to expose as much of my spine as I can for as long as I can.

I've installed a golf net outside, my back facing to the south sun. I swing barefoot to get grounded. Being outside grounded in the sun as much as I can is one way I mitigate any negative effects of the tanning bed.

I will again use tanning beds starting in September 2016. Maybe I will make a trip south to get humidity with the sun.

### Purple Haze — 2016-04-25T02:38:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-tanning-beds.17853/post-193404>

Thanks David, as a fellow Canadian I can appreciate your situation. Vancouver is awesome in the Spring and Summer when it's sunny, but as you may know, we can get rain for long periods.
I'm unsure about the UVB here. Vancouver is 49 degrees latitude, so I'll assume the lack of UVB would be the same as Regina, but perhaps even worse because there is not as much sunny days Sept thru April.
I think I'll try the tanning bed to see how it makes me feel. Luckily the past week has been absolutely gorgeous here, so I've been soaking up as much of the real stuff as I can.
Thanks again for the info.

>
> Haze, I'm in Vancouver too and I tan throughout the winter as well as set up some trips for UV. This last winter was the best winter I've had so far. Keeping hydrated is key. And most times I'll do my CT first and then go tan when I'm shivering. Best absorption.

Hi jenaf, it's nice to meet another Vancouverite on here (technically I'm in Langley). You know what we have to deal with here. Thanks for the tips and info. How often do you CT, and where do you do that, in the ocean?

### Curves — 2016-04-27T19:33:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-tanning-beds.17853/post-193707>

Hi @[Purple Haze](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/19042/)! Thought I'd share my tanning bed n=1.
Two years ago I moved from San Francisco to a small rural town in Oregon. That's a move from 37 latitude to 45.
We bought a place with some land and spent much of the summer removing 100s of blackberry bushes which meant I covered my skin so as to avoid injury. Big mistake!
When I had my Vitamin D tested last October it was a abysmal 35.5. I freaked. I thought about supplementing but that didn't feel right so I bought lizard lights with UVB and decided to use a tanning bed once a week for 20 minutes.
I retested a few weeks ago and upped my Vit D to 44.2. Considering the low UV in Oregon during the winter I'm considering this a success.
My goal is to get it up to 60 naturally this summer, plan monthly trips south and rely on the tanning bed January-March.

> I'll make a comment on my own experience. I do like tanning beds and I used on from September 2015 until early April 2016. I buy a monthly pass for 36.99, about the same cost as Vitamin D supplements. I live at 50.45 degrees latitude in Canada. Regina is one of the sunniest places in Canada and winter is sunny most of the time. However, I cannot get UVB rays in my location from Sept to April so I tan.
>
> When I tan, I feel better. I sleep better, I have more energy, and my mood is positive.
>
> The EMF question I have address with lots of grounding pre tanning, and loading up on water. Being very hydrated before tanning is something I swear by.
>
> I have had to alter my diet slightly due to the increased exposure to the tanning.
>
> So far I have not had an ill effects or side effects using the beds. I do protect my face from long exposures. After 3-5 minutes I will shield my face with silken cloth. Day light here is 14+ hours a day so I have reduced my tanning bed exposure in the last 2 weeks, in favor of spending the day outside working. I make sure to expose as much of my spine as I can for as long as I can.
>
> I've installed a golf net outside, my back facing to the south sun. I swing barefoot to get grounded. Being outside grounded in the sun as much as I can is one way I mitigate any negative effects of the tanning bed.
>
> I will again use tanning beds starting in September 2016. Maybe I will make a trip south to get humidity with the sun.

### malc0088 — 2017-01-10T18:03:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-tanning-beds.17853/post-206519>

> [Johnny DeMarco said:](/goto/post?id=206479)
>
> Hi David, You mentioned you change your diet slightly on the days you tan. Other than being very hydrated, and generalities you can share about your macros prior to tanning? Maybe some ascorbate powder ?
> JD

Hey Johnny,
i've used a tanning bed a couple times this winter so far (I live in KS), and I've found a routine that works well. I start the day by locking in with the sun in the AM in the cold with as little clothing as possible, hydrate well on SP all day, load up on DHA from oysters and Sardines, and then I do some form of CT and follow it up with 5-7 min. of a high UV B bed (I would ideally do it in the late morning/noon, matching when the sun would emit UV, but I can't get away from work, so i end up going around 3pm). I've noticed that when i just go tan and I don't load up on DHA, hydrate well, and do CT first, the effects I feel (which are incredibly noticeable) don't stay with me very long and i get really warm in the booth (thought not sweating). With the routine, I go maybe 1x per week for only 5 min. and I absorb the UV very well and feel charged up and I continue to CT in some form everyday which I think aids in holding the UV charge. This has helped to cut back on the trade off of nn-emf exposure. For winter in KS, I've got a decent solar callus, but a tanning bed is not the answer, and I know that. Full Spectrum Sun is the answer.

I tested the routine out when we went to Colorado for NYE, rented a cabin in the mountains, and did my routine, except that I sun bathed at 8500 ft. for about 3-4 hours....I actually got a tan and never burned. This is similar to Scompys video from the PNW where he picked up UV on his meter.

I plan to head south in Feb. to get more real UV from the sun for a few days, and probably back up to altitude one more time before UV is back in KS in March.

I need to get my Vit D labs done to see where I'm at...so far I've gone off of intuition. As I get away from looking to food as the answer and put more effort into the 3 legged stool, I've found my biological antenna is better tuned into nature. Hope this helps.

### Jack Kruse — 2017-01-10T18:04:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-tanning-beds.17853/post-206521>

# Thread: Align to Original Design - Marcus' Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/align-to-original-design-marcus-journal.17856/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Marcus Plating — 2016-04-25T03:16:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/align-to-original-design-marcus-journal.17856/post-193408>

Hi Everyone…I am sitting here in the dark on my laptop with my orange-tinted safety googles over my glasses. I am not sure how I am supposed to do this…So I will follow my intuition…I will share my journey of the last 20 years of chronic energy, weight and stress issues which have been punctuated by blood clotting episodes and recurring periods of progress and setbacks…but wait..is there light at the end of the tunnel?..could it be..is it possible…could the sun really be starting to shine over my life again.

I believe that it was ordained that I came across a YouTube of Dr. Kruse being interviewed and something caught my attention and I ordered his book on Amazon. In the past month, I have listened to hours of Dr. Jack on Podcasts, and read his book through almost twice. I also just started the Fourth Phase of Water..but back to my journey.

I left college with hopes and big dreams and then seemed to get set in a huge 15 year rut that has sapped my energy and strength and my ability to thrive as I am just trying to survive…so here is a quick chronicle.

1994 - Graduate from business school Summa Cum Laude with high hopes and a bright future ahead of me…Out of college, began working in wireless telecommunications (cellular phone) business (at the age of 21) - spent hours per day on a cell phone
1997-2002 - did several juice fasts - 30-50 days in total per year
2000 - Began to do a lot of cycling (100+ miles/week) - to fight propensity to gain weight
1999-2003 - Strict vegan- at a lot of soy during this period of time
2002 - Began to struggle with fatigue issues - they would get so bad I would have to lock my office and nap during the day
2003 - Left vegan lifestyle in hopes of improving fatigue issues
2004 - Get married and stopped regular cycling activities
2004 - Lost two close friends who died of cancer
2005 - Like and idiot, I have my mercury fillings removed by a non-biological dentist.
2006 - Left telecom industry - through downsizing - decide to enter humanitarian work for a few years between USA and Africa
2007- Could no longer wear contact lenses (added this note on May 3, 2016)
2008 Financially things force me back into the tractional workforce so I entered IT industry - began gaining more weight quickly and lack of energy continued to be a persistent issue…ballooned up to 227 pounds. A lot of air travel and long days.
2008 - First daughter is born
2010 - Bilateral pulmonary embolism (Blood clot) hospitalizes me the day after I was supposed to fly across the country but providentially decide to stay home at the last minute and cancel the trip.
2012 - Second daughter is born.
2013 - Blood clot in right leg and was diagnosed with Factor V Leiden and sentenced to near life blood thinners (Warfarin…are you kidding me?)
2015 - Began doing CrossFit 5 times/week to try and lose weight by building muscle - in one year of CrossFit I improved my performance but went from 227 to 232 pounds…was set back by issues with shoulder pain (either impingement or some sort of tear).
2016 - In February went on 40 day water fast for spiritual reasons but also for ancillary health issues - bring weight down 45+ pounds.

Over the past 20 years I have tried almost everything to resolve my persistent health issues…every supplement, every diet, vegetarian, vegan, raw and paleo. I have always struggled with my weight since my early twenties. My energy level has been between terrible and average. I am sure I have spent about $50K over the years on trying natural remedies to fix my energy levels from every advertised supplement and diet known to man.

Here is my shortlist of issues at this point:
Get off of blood-thinners
Deal with my body’s propensity to gain weight quickly
Resolve persistent energy issues
Fix poor sleep problems
Get shoulder pain issue fixed
Get my thinking clearer (and fix this ADD’ish thiking in my mind)
Get rid of edema in my legs

Am now fighting to keep the weigh off and stay where I am. I am not determined to come off of blood thinners, make environmental and dietary changes and really take the next 12-24 months to study the deeper levels of connection of our bodies to environment and nature and nature’s GOD while I systematically make meaningful changes to improve my future and that of my family.

I am quite frustrated but for the first time hopeful…I am not even worried about Optimal right now…I just want to get to some semblance of decent…LOL.

I have already began making some of the changes I need to make:
Eliminating blue light exposure - Wearing orange glasses all evening long
Spending a lot more time in sunlight
Reducing carbs to a minimum (getting ready to do Leptin Rx)
Just ordered my Magnetico sleep pad
Implementing seafood and fish oil (DHA)
Installed a dirty electricity modulator in the house - I would turn off the main breaker but my wife stays up into the wee hours of the night and I can’t wait up for her.
When we have the money I will subscribe to the Gold Membership.

Thank you Dr. Kruse…For the first time my persistence seems to have brought me on a path to true enlightenment. I am excited about what the next several months and years hold.

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*
May 3, 2016 - Updating my original journal post to add some more details - now that my brain is working better..
I believe I have been Leptin resistant for a long time (and not sure how my 40 day water - only fast may have helped to reverse that).

My primary health goals are as follows (I am repeating myself here with a more complete list):

-   Get off of warfarin (blood thinners)!!!
-   Increase energy
-   Keep the weight off (and between 180-185)...am 185 now
-   Resolve the recurring edema in my legs (especially my right leg)
-   Get my mind working better and better (which is starting to happen with more sunlight and wearing my funny orange glasses)
-   Resolve my plantar fasciitis (is this related to warfarin) and shoulder pain issues
-   Sleep better at night (this is slowly starting to happen since my Magnetico came).
-   Get my eyes healthy so I can wear contact lenses again (unless I shouldn't)

_CT is Underway:
I started cold-adapting on May 1 and am shooting to get in 50 degree ice water in my tub within the next 3-4 weeks..have the water down to 59 degrees right now for 30 minutes._

### nonchalant — 2016-04-26T22:37:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/align-to-original-design-marcus-journal.17856/post-193614>

My genetic profile (and maternal grandmother's history) shows a tendency toward DVT. And sure enough, I had some issues in my lower legs, years ago. Large bruises and such. But all those issues are long gone. I'm not sure exactly which of Jack's protocols did the trick, probably a combination. I used to give blood regularly, and I found that the time needed to give a pint of blood became shorter and shorter, as I got healthier. I no longer give blood, but the last time it took a little over 5 minutes. Also when I would get a cut or pick a scab, my blood was thinner and lighter in color. You might want to read where Jack was talking about the zeta potential in blood. It is a negative charge in the RBC's which keep them from sticking together.

I would suggest lots of sunlight to charge the blood, good water without fluoride, seafood, grounding away from power lines, and staying away from technology, especially at night. \[Oh, yeah, that would be a big problem for you.\] A magnetico would be a real help also. And CT... You are trying to increase the energy stored in your bloodstream.

Have you seen those photos of blood cells before and after exposure to smart meters?

### Marcus Plating — 2016-04-29T02:05:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/align-to-original-design-marcus-journal.17856/post-193804>

Hi, I really appreciate your detailed response and you sharing this.  Thank you... I am aware of some of this...I began Earthing / Grounding when I learned about how EMF causes the cells to gather / stick together. Why do we need to stay away from technology at night specifically? ### nonchalant — 2016-04-29T19:34:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/align-to-original-design-marcus-journal.17856/post-193835>

The orange shades are wonderful, I use mine in the early evening and when traveling at night. The blue light at night is the worst thing about technology, especially at night, because of melatonin. But even during the day, you can get an excess of this unbalanced blue light, leading to blue light toxicity and eye problems (like scarring on the retina).

I think I'm more sensitive than many, and blue light on my skin at night also bothers my sleep. So I usually retire early to bed, to read under a red light. Perhaps this sensitivity is due to my past career in IT. Maybe spending hours in the datacenter working on the redundant hardware of active systems so often didn't help, lol. I used to worry about my hearing, but had no idea there were bigger issues. Long sleeves might be helpful at night for blue light on the skin.

I also try to stay away from touching electronics at any time, because I can feel a slight tingling. I think it is stealing electrons from my hands. Especially with the mouse. I never use the self-checkout lanes at the store, because they don't have a stylus to use with the touch screens. I always use the speaker with the cell phone and try not to hold it in my hand. Etc etc etc.

I am aware of some of this...I began Earthing / Grounding when I learned about how EMF causes the cells to gather / stick together. Why do we need to stay away from technology at night specifically? Is that the blue light issue or something else?
>
> I am always wearing my orange shades in the evening especially around technology or light...even driving at night these days...LOL.

I think it is not a good idea to wear those blue blockers at nite? Mine says not to.

# Thread: Should I live in a tent?(half facetious half serious) and other biohacks.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-live-in-a-tent-half-facetious-half-serious-and-other-biohacks.17857/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### StylesGrant — 2016-04-25T04:35:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-live-in-a-tent-half-facetious-half-serious-and-other-biohacks.17857/post-193409>

So let me try to be concise.

Basically I just learned about what DECT handset phones do.

I think they are pretty diabolical, and I think I had, and have had some serious EMF issues on top of the other preexisting health problems I already had, which are significant.

I knew about blue light, I knew some people had EMF sensitivity, I knew I needed blue light blockers, but I just never thought about the damn DECT phone or what it was and what it does. And since I am pretty sure it was boot stomping my health, (symptoms just completely go away when I unplug the phone stations and leave them off for hours or days, or when I go to a family's house that doesn't have a DECT phone).

So now I think I am going to have to back track and start learning about EMF's and circadian stuff in a way I have poo-pooed till now.

It goes without saying, that I am going to get rid of the DECT phone, wire down the wifi net, and get a different roku machine that has an ethernet port.

But before I made the connection to this phone, I was freaking out and looking for mold. Now for what it is worth, I've met many people crippled by mold. I don't take it lightly. Mold is bad. But EMF's can be too, and in my case the link between the symptoms and phone seems pretty certain. But I'm thinking, I've always been sick, and I've always been basically suffering from 'sick house syndrome'.

So why not just live in a tent behind the house? I mean get away from indoor air pollution and fake lights and wires all together. All I need to know is how to ground and how to buy a tent that doesn't offgas crazy chemicals. Right?

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-25T05:22:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/should-i-live-in-a-tent-half-facetious-half-serious-and-other-biohacks.17857/post-193412>

### caroline — 2016-04-26T04:41:23+0300

I can do that .....do I miss stuff? sure do! not for lack of trying - I just overlook the extreme relevance of some very important things.

I thought I have been doing so much right ....but for the last six months I have been outside even more than before when I lived much closer to the ocean.

Now, I am barefoot almost all the time outside with very little clothes on ...and it is making a huge difference.

I knew that before - but now I know it in spades!

There used to be a forum member a few years ago that was doing amazing things for himself with C/T and the leptin reset etc.. He had very real issues ...but - he thought doing all of this at about 80% was more than good enuf and didn't feel he should be told what to do. ### caroline — 2016-04-26T08:43:00+0300

### caroline — 2016-04-26T09:02:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/april-2016-q-a.17858/post-193519>

Something I have been thinking a lot about the last couple of days ...

My mother had dementia in her later years. She loved gardening and would always want to be outside puttering and growing flowers, vegetables etc - you name it.
She certainly didn't have access to electronics of any kind in her day .....and we lived on a farm for a lot of years. She was a great cook and we only ever had home made food.

So wouldn't you think she would have been spared dementia?

Lately JK has added a couple of things to his optimal prescription ....
friends and self confidence.

My mother was a very unhappy and angry woman most of her life. Nothing or no one pleased her .....especially my Dad.

How much does love and joy and being peaceful add to our healthy years? I am thinking if we don't have these things it is going to be a real uphill battle no matter what else we do.

I would like to add one more thing to Dr. K.'s optimal RX if I may....

To love unconditionally - and to be loved unconditionally........and then we can really soar!

BTW ..... This has nothing to do with perfection - it is in spite of and maybe even because of another's imperfections .

### Inger — 2016-04-26T13:07:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/april-2016-q-a.17858/post-193523>

Caroline.... I believe you are spot on here.... Love is the most important thing of all......
but then... everything is tied together..... if you are very disconnected from nature and your brain is not well it might be hard to love unconditionally.... and understand paradox......
but I think it all flows into each other and there is no clear line
some people have huge hearts and love deeply... and do so well health wise even if some things are not optimal
if you miss love... but do everything else perfect.. it is not going to be pretty......

I do think tho.. , love requires a certain amount of energy..... the more you love the more energy... so where do we get that from....... when we have figured that out for ourselves nothing can stop us    we just go to the source and tap

### Sue-UK — 2016-04-26T20:27:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/april-2016-q-a.17858/post-193587>

> [caroline said:](/goto/post?id=193519)
>
> Something I have been thinking a lot about the last couple of days ...
>
> My mother had dementia in her later years. She loved gardening and would always want to be outside puttering and growing flowers, vegetables etc - you name it.
> She certainly didn't have access to electronics of any kind in her day .....and we lived on a farm for a lot of years. She was a great cook and we only ever had home made food.
>
> So wouldn't you think she would have been spared dementia?
>
> Lately JK has added a couple of things to his optimal prescription ....
> friends and self confidence.
>
> My mother was a very unhappy and angry woman most of her life. Nothing or no one pleased her .....especially my Dad.
>
> How much does love and joy and being peaceful add to our healthy years? I am thinking if we don't have these things it is going to be a real uphill battle no matter what else we do.
>
> I would like to add one more thing to Dr. K.'s optimal RX if I may....
>
> To love unconditionally - and to be loved unconditionally........and then we can really soar!
>
> BTW ..... This has nothing to do with perfection - it is in spite of and maybe even because of another's imperfections .

I watched a TV programme a few years ago about dementia and at the end the expert was asked what was the most important thing to do to avoid dementia and he basically said be sociable, maintain good friendships, interact with other people.

Now after 6 months in a nnemf hell nursing home, through a UK winter, the dementia train for my mum is going at high speed. In terms of care and comfort, the place is great. Mum won't try and make friends, because she thinks she is making a fool of herself because she is deaf, (she has to try and hear or lip read, process that, think of a response and then answer, by which time either the conversation has moved on, or she has misheard and answers a different question). But what I've observed is that others who are the more sociable seem to withstand the same nnemf environment and lack of the 3 legged stool much better. Not immunity to dementia, but there seems to be more resilience somehow .... They look forward to things, enjoy each other's company .....Dopamine from sociability slowing the dementia train?

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-26T21:15:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/april-2016-q-a.17858/post-193590>

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-26T21:16:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/april-2016-q-a.17858/post-193592>

If you could use some extra cash and don't mind trading your health for it, enter into a contract to host one on your property. This pastor will earn $1500-5000/mo for this one. Once it's installed, think you can change your mind? Oops, think again, it's a 30-40 year contract. Want to sell your property? Try doing that with a cell tower installed that can't be removed. Decisions come with trade-offs. Heavily monetized decisions come with big costs that you may not see upfront. The County approved this, but guess what they couldn't consider when approving or denying a new cell tower? Health. That's right, the Telecommunications Act of 1996 in the USA made it illegal to consider health effects when approving or denying cell towers. But, some communities are wising up and finding work arounds a by objecting for other reasons, such as property values. You might be shocked to know that Big Telco targets old age homes because they need the revenue too!!!

[http://www.wesh.com/…/another-cellphone-tower-dis…/37092812…](http://www.wesh.com/news/another-cellphone-tower-disguised-as-cross-nearly-finished/37092812#.Vx-fJ70ooJc.facebook)

That congregation needs the Quantlet now a whole lot more than church FYI

[https://www.indiegogo.com/…/the-quantlet-your-workout-welln…](https://www.indiegogo.com/projects/the-quantlet-your-workout-wellness-optimized#/)
Today science and religion have us worshiping "things" we cannot sense or see well, and both are having interesting and massive effect on how we live our life. People should at least be given the ability to worship an invisible force that actually does something positive for their lives........you perspective depends how your view the problem.

### Penny — 2016-04-27T01:58:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/april-2016-q-a.17858/post-193629>

**Mother Teresa's Rules**
"Every moment in time gives us the chance to love, to learn, to dream, and to become more than we ever imagined"
People are often unreasonable, illogical and self- centered.
Forgive them anyway.

If you are kind, people may accuse you of selfish, ulterior motives.
Be kind anyway.

If you are successful, you will win some false friends and some true enemies.
Succeed anyway.

If you are honest and frank, people may cheat you.
Be honest and frank anyway.

What you spend your years building, someone could destroy overnight.
Build anyway.

If you find serenity and happiness, they may be jealous.
Be happy anyway.

The good you do today, people will often forget tomorrow.
Do good anyway.

Give the world the best you have and it may never be enough.
Give the world the best you have anyway.

You see, if the final analysis, it is between you and God. It was never between you and them anyway.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-28T23:57:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/april-2016-q-a.17858/post-193790>

Einstein employed thought experiments called Gedankenexperiments (conceptual) in his mind before he actually materialized his theories.

If a light ray is bent in an accelerated system, then, according to the principle of equivalence, light should also be bent by gravity, contradicting the everyday expectation that light will travel in a straight line (unless it passes from one medium to another). If its path is curved by gravity, that must mean that “straight line” has a different meaning near a massive gravitational body such as a star than it does in empty space. This was a hint that gravity should be treated as a geometric phenomenon.

Now think about what was said about gravity and geometry in April 2016?

Einstein did get something wrong though. Do you know what it was?

### Jack Kruse — 2016-06-16T01:51:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/april-2016-q-a.17858/post-196655>

All thermodynamic relationships depend on scale. This is a deep lesson of the April webinar if you have not figured this out. If you double the sides of a box, then you get four times the area, but you get eight times the volume. Volume factors scale much faster than area factors in scaling. Now think about these factors when we go from a large scale to that of 30-50 Angstroms in your inner mitochondrial membrane where the cristae are as water is heated up to shrink the respiratory proteins. Think about the June 2016 webinar. An elephant faces an entirely different set of problems than a mouse, based entirely on the relative effects of scale. And a mitochondria takes full advantage of scale as it shrinks because of QED.

Einstein got time wrong........as you will see this coming year.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-08-11T20:27:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/april-2016-q-a.17858/post-199519>

So with the August 2016 Q & A coming up Sunday I thought if you like Frauhofer lines and how they linked to mitochondria you might like this link too since you've already been exposed to the April 2016 webinar. Might neutrino's affect elements on Earth via molecular resonant energy transfers? If so could neutrino's effect us the same way? April's radical idea was how could a neutrino effect a mitochondria was it not? What this paper suggests is the exact same thing but with a twist. They believe now that something that doesn't really interact with anything is changing something that can't be changed. I chuckled when I read it. Welcome to the party. I love when sceince catches up to crazy. THE STRANGE CASE OF FLARES AND RADIOACTIVE ELEMENTS
It's a mystery that presented itself unexpectedly: The radioactive decay of some elements sitting quietly in laboratories on Earth seemed to be influenced by activities inside the sun, 93 million miles away.
Is this possible?
Researchers from Stanford and Purdue University believe it is. But their explanation of how it happens opens the door to yet another mystery.
There is even an outside chance that this unexpected effect is brought about by a previously unknown particle emitted by the sun. "That would be truly remarkable," said Peter Sturrock, Stanford professor emeritus of applied physics and an expert on the inner workings of the sun. [http://news.stanford.edu/news/2010/august/sun-082310.html](http://news.stanford.edu/news/2010/august/sun-082310.html)

### Jack Kruse — 2016-12-03T15:22:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/april-2016-q-a.17858/post-204705>

So members you thought that epic April 2016 webinar on the end game of mitochondria was intense? Well this relaity might be more ground breaking to you: Now we have others going down the same path as I did ten years ago when I came up my my thesis that a mitochodria is capable of nuclear reactions using neutrinos. If I am correct in my speculation based upon my data collection and research we should find proof that elements below atomic number 26 can under go transmutation in cells (because of what occurs in mito) obviating the need for many raw materials of atoms for key enzymes and proteins from food. This means what is today believed to be essential no longer is. In other words: sunlight can be turned directly into matter based upon what the cell needs when it needs it. Lavoisier has established a mass conservation law which is valid in chemistry. Now we know that it is NOT true when nuclear reactions are involved. The review of more than two centuries of research demonstrates that this is not true in biology. It appears that all living organisms can under some circumstances produce nuclear reactions. However, there is an important need of finding an adequate theory to explain these results. It is highly probable that such a theory should also be capable of explaining Cold Fusion, or more generally, nuclear reactions in condensed matter. Another point is the irreproducibility of some experiments. Probably, in order to produce significant transmutation of an element, it is necessary that another element be missing. It seems that nature has a tendency to find ways to transmute an element into another to provide the necessary ingredients for the healthy growth of the four kingdoms of bacteria, fungi, plants and animals, including human beings. [http://www.e-catworld.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/09/JCMNS-Biberian.pdf](http://www.e-catworld.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/09/JCMNS-Biberian.pdf) It appears this picture needs to be updated for mitochondria now.

### Sharla — 2016-04-25T05:41:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/newish.17859/post-193415>

I've been following Jack for a while, trying to incorporate some of his ideas into my suburban life. Intuitively, I know he's on to something. I want a little more accountability, which is why I'm introducing myself to this forum. For a long time "Understand Jack Kruse" has been on my goal list. Life has got in the way of this goal, but now I'm recommitting.

I have a north facing patio. Until about April, I see no sun in the morning. So now that it's April, for the last week, I've been sitting out on my patio in the morning for 20 minutes to drink my coffee. And I've dunked my face in ice water a couple of times, because my face is on fire from its sensitivity (lots of healing to do!). My dreams have been more memorable. I live in a hot climate, so morning sun like this (6:45-7:30am) is good to get the sunlight that I know makes me happier, without the tiredness that comes from the heat.

Right now I'm watching Mother Nature put on a show of rainbows, lightning, and thunder. It's a bit unusual around these parts, so always a special treat. I'm hoping you can support me through all this and find my way into the intricacies of nature's wisdom. Thank you!

# Thread: April webinar meets Sulfur and Selenium.

### Jack Kruse — 2016-04-25T17:43:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/april-webinar-meets-sulfur-and-selenium.17861/post-193446>

Many people think seafood is toxic because of heavy metals and they always forget that seafood is loaded with Selenium. After hearing the April 2016 webinar have a look at the periodic table. What do you notice about the relationship between selenium and sulfur? Seafood has both Se and S in high amounts.

“isothiocyanates have been the basis of a drug in development that replaces the sulfur bonds with selenium, with far stronger potency against melanoma.”
Selenium is just below sulfur on the periodic table so I’m assuming the noted increase in potency is a quantum effect as the outer shell of electrons of selenium are more loosely held than those of sulfur – right? My ANSWER: Replacing sulfur with selenium makes a tissue more likely to refill itself with UV light. This is why selenoproteins are so anticancer like in effect. It is also why seafood is strongly associated with an anticancer effect. Why? Selenium is a semiconductor that can make electricity when saltwater is poured over it and light hits it. Why? Because selenium can convert light into electricity, photoelectrically. The DC electric current is the key to biologic regeneration. Selenium is used in industry for solar panels because of this ability. It is a wide band semiconductor. Selenium’s ability to conduct electricity increases as its exposure to light increases. This means it produces more DC electric current when it absorb UV light. Remember learning long ago in one of my early blogs about the anti carcinogenic value of selenium and Vit D3? Food with the highest selenium level…....oysters, followed by tuna. It turns out they also have a ton of sulfur. How can you help your own cancer diagnosis with this information? Stop allowing your cells to leak light to become cancer cells. Chronic blue light ROS without healing UV and IR = epi-oncogenesis. from leaking excessive amounts of ELF-UV light from cells. When you stop the leak and add back UV/IR you begin to engage the regeneration programs in biology designed to refill cells with UV. Its pretty simple. Pseudohypoxia is a sign of ELF-UV release. Read Roeland van Wick book for all the details. Cancer growth is uncontrolled. What controls it? ELF-UV light increase mitosis. Growth is necessary in biology but uncontrolled growth is not; but any kind of growth is always stimulated by damage (ROS), but it is normally under feedback control of UV/IR light to stimulate regeneration.

Food is just data on sunlight’s photonic power that it packs into food as it travels around our star. We need an adequate balanced diet of light. That light is carried on the back of electrons and the said electrons are delivered to your mitochondria.
